anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety
anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.t: Weight-sparse transformers have interpretable circuits
anc.sig: Demonstrates that weight-sparse transformers develop interpretable computational circuits, showing that sparsity constraints enable human-readable mechanistic explanations of model behavior.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: M-RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models in Multilingual Settings
anc.sig: Establishes M-RewardBench, a multilingual benchmark for evaluating reward models across 14 languages, enabling assessment of their cross-lingual generalization and revealing language-specific performance disparities.

anc.t: Lost in Translation: Do LVLM Judges Generalize Across Languages?
anc.sig: Introduced MM-JudgeBench, the first large-scale multilingual multimodal benchmark with 60K+ preference pairs across 25 languages, revealing critical language generalization gaps in LVLM reward models and establishing methodology for…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.t: Towards Benchmarking Privacy Vulnerabilities in Selective Forgetting with Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation metrics and benchmarks for quantifying privacy leakage and membership inference attacks against selective forgetting in large language models.

anc.t: PrivUn: Unveiling Latent Ripple Effects and Shallow Forgetting in Privacy Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced PrivUn evaluation framework revealing gradient-based ripple effects and shallow forgetting as fundamental failure modes of LLM privacy unlearning methods.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Towards Inclusive Toxic Content Moderation: Addressing Vulnerabilities to Adversarial Attacks in Toxicity Classifiers Tackling LLM-generated Content
anc.sig: Identified vulnerabilities in toxicity classifiers against adversarial attacks and LLM-generated toxic content, establishing benchmarks for adversarially robust content moderation systems.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: MedHallBench: A New Benchmark for Assessing Hallucination in Medical Large Language Models
anc.sig: Created first comprehensive benchmark (MedHallBench) for systematically evaluating hallucinations in medical LLMs across factual errors, reasoning failures, and knowledge gaps.

anc.t: Med-RewardBench: Benchmarking Reward Models and Judges for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes first medical-specific benchmark for evaluating reward models and judges in multimodal medical LLMs, enabling systematic assessment of alignment quality in healthcare contexts.

anc.t: MedBench v4: A Robust and Scalable Benchmark for Evaluating Chinese Medical Language Models, Multimodal Models, and Intelligent Agents
anc.sig: Established comprehensive evaluation framework and dataset for assessing Chinese medical language models, multimodal models, and agents across clinical reasoning and domain-specific tasks.

anc.t: Generalist Large Language Models Outperform Clinical Tools on Medical Benchmarks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 and similar generalist LLMs surpass specialized medical AI systems on standardized clinical benchmarks, establishing foundation models as viable medical reasoning tools.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: SEAL: Systematic Error Analysis for Value ALignment
anc.sig: Introduces systematic framework for identifying, categorizing, and analyzing value misalignment errors in AI systems through structured error decomposition methodology.

anc.t: Elephant in the Room: Unveiling the Impact of Reward Model Quality in Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward model quality is a critical bottleneck in RLHF alignment, showing misaligned rewards significantly degrade language model performance and safety—establishing reward model scrutiny as essential for safe AI…

anc.t: RMB: Comprehensively Benchmarking Reward Models in LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Comprehensive benchmark suite evaluating reward models across dimensions, datasets, and metrics, establishing standardized evaluation protocols for LLM alignment reward model assessment.

anc.t: Evaluating and Improving Cultural Awareness of Reward Models for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates reward model bias across cultures and proposes methods to improve cultural awareness, establishing benchmarks and techniques for evaluating alignment safety across diverse cultural contexts.

anc.t: CURE: Cultural Understanding and Reasoning Evaluation - A Framework for "Thick" Culture Alignment Evaluation in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces CURE framework for evaluating cultural understanding in LLMs across multiple dimensions of cultural knowledge, reasoning, and alignment beyond surface-level representation metrics.

anc.t: Beg to Differ: Understanding Reasoning-Answer Misalignment Across Languages
anc.sig: Identifies and analyzes reasoning-answer misalignment in multilingual LLMs where models generate correct reasoning but provide wrong answers, establishing this as a distinct failure mode requiring targeted interventions.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Introducing v0.5 of the AI Safety Benchmark from MLCommons
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: Challenging the Evaluator: LLM Sycophancy Under User Rebuttal
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs exhibit sycophancy when users rebut their responses, generating flattering agreement rather than principled pushback, revealing a vulnerability in adversarial evaluation protocols.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: Quantifying detection rates for dangerous capabilities: a theoretical model of dangerous capability evaluations
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework for modeling detection rates in safety evaluations, providing quantitative foundation for assessing how likely dangerous AI capabilities are caught during testing.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Reward Modeling for Mitigating Toxicity in Transformer-based Language Models
anc.sig: Develops reward modeling techniques to train transformer language models to generate less toxic text, establishing methods for using human feedback to align model outputs with safety preferences.

anc.t: Evaluating Psychological Safety of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Develops metrics and evaluation framework for assessing psychological safety in LLMs, measuring how models avoid harmful outputs in sensitive psychological contexts.

anc.t: Who is ChatGPT? Benchmarking LLMs' Psychological Portrayal Using PsychoBench
anc.sig: Introduced PsychoBench, a benchmark measuring how accurately LLMs portray psychological constructs like personality traits and mental health conditions, enabling systematic evaluation of anthropomorphization in AI systems.

anc.t: WundtGPT: Shaping Large Language Models To Be An Empathetic, Proactive Psychologist
anc.sig: Demonstrated fine-tuning methods to equip LLMs with empathetic psychological responses and proactive intervention capabilities for mental health support applications.

anc.t: Do Large Language Models Align with Core Mental Health Counseling Competencies?
anc.sig: Evaluates whether LLMs demonstrate core mental health counseling competencies through empirical assessment, establishing a benchmark for AI readiness in therapeutic applications.

anc.t: Domain-Specific Constitutional AI: Enhancing Safety in LLM-Powered Mental Health Chatbots
anc.sig: Adapted constitutional AI methodology to mental health domain by designing domain-specific principles and evaluation criteria for LLM safety guardrails in therapeutic chatting systems.

anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that weight-sparse transformers develop interpretable computational circuits, showing that sparsity constraints enable human-readable mechanistic explanations of model behavior.

anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: AUVIC: Adversarial Unlearning of Visual Concepts for Multi-modal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial unlearning technique to selectively remove visual concepts from multimodal LLMs without degrading other capabilities or requiring full retraining.

anc.t: Null Space Constrained Contrastive Visual Forgetting for MLLM Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes null space-constrained contrastive visual forgetting for selective MLLM unlearning, enabling target knowledge removal while preserving non-target visual and all textual knowledge through frozen LLM with visual module fine-tuning.

anc.t: ICED: Concept-level Machine Unlearning via Interpretable Concept Decomposition
anc.sig: Introduced concept-level unlearning for vision-language models through interpretable sparse decomposition of visual representations into semantic concepts, enabling precise removal of target knowledge while preserving contextual…

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Training a General Purpose Automated Red Teaming Model
anc.sig: Developed a generalizable automated red teaming pipeline enabling small LLMs to generate adversarial attacks without pre-existing evaluators, transferable to unseen attack objectives beyond training distribution.

anc.t: Model-Agnostic Lifelong LLM Safety via Externalized Attack-Defense Co-Evolution
anc.sig: Proposes externalized attack-defense framework with reusable skill libraries and retrieval-augmented defense model, enabling model-agnostic safety improvements transferable across different LLMs without retraining.

anc.t: CHASE: Adversarial Red-Blue Teaming for Improving LLM Safety using Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduced CHASE, a closed-loop red-blue teaming framework using GRPO where adversarial attackers and safety defenders co-evolve, enabling scalable black-box adversarial training against prompt-rewriting attacks without white-box…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: Textual Unlearning Gives a False Sense of Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that textual unlearning methods fail to truly remove information from language models, revealing fundamental limitations in current forget-and-retrain approaches.

anc.t: Protecting Privacy Through Approximating Optimal Parameters for Sequence Unlearning in Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes optimal parameter update strategies for efficient sequence unlearning in language models, enabling privacy-preserving removal of specific training data without full retraining.

anc.t: Direct Token Optimization: A Self-contained Approach to Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces direct token optimization method enabling selective unlearning in LLMs by identifying and modifying specific token representations without full model retraining or gradient-based approaches.

anc.t: SHRED: Retain-Set-Free Unlearning via Self-Distillation with Logit Demotion
anc.sig: Demonstrated retain-set-free unlearning in LLMs by selectively targeting high-information tokens within forget examples, eliminating the costly requirement for curated retention data while preserving general model capabilities.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: Towards Guaranteed Safe AI: A Framework for Ensuring Robust and Reliable AI Systems
anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for AI safety assurance combining robustness verification, reliability testing, and formal guarantees to enable deployment of safety-critical systems.

anc.t: Human-AI Safety: A Descendant of Generative AI and Control Systems Safety
anc.sig: Frames human-AI safety as intersection of generative AI risks and control systems theory, establishing conceptual foundations for analyzing safety properties of AI systems through formal control frameworks.

anc.t: From Refusal to Recovery: A Control-Theoretic Approach to Generative AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Formally models AI safety guardrails as closed-loop control systems, enabling principled analysis of refusal mechanisms and recovery dynamics in generative models.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Multi-Objective Reward and Preference Optimization: Theory and Algorithms
anc.sig: Formalized multi-objective preference optimization framework and algorithms for training language models when multiple reward objectives cannot be simultaneously maximized, enabling Pareto-optimal alignment.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Models of human preference for learning reward functions
anc.sig: Formalized methods for learning reward functions from human preferences through pairwise comparisons, establishing the foundation for preference-based inverse reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Contrastive Preference Learning: Learning from Human Feedback without RL
anc.sig: Introduced direct loss function for learning from human preferences without reinforcement learning, enabling efficient alignment of language models to human feedback through contrastive learning objectives.

anc.t: RA-PbRL: Provably Efficient Risk-Aware Preference-Based Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework for risk-aware preference-based RL with provable sample efficiency guarantees when learning from pairwise preference feedback rather than scalar rewards.

anc.t: Hybrid Adaptive Conformal Offline Reinforcement Learning for Fair Population Health Management
anc.sig: Combines conformal prediction with offline RL to create fairness-aware population health policies that provide statistical coverage guarantees while learning from historical data without environment interaction.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: Process-Supervised Reward Models for Verifying Clinical Note Generation: A Scalable Approach Guided by Domain Expertise
anc.sig: Introduces process-supervised reward models that score intermediate steps in clinical note generation, enabling scalable evaluation of long-form medical text without requiring gold-standard reference notes.

anc.t: Fin-PRM: A Domain-Specialized Process Reward Model for Financial Reasoning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced specialized process reward model for evaluating intermediate reasoning steps in financial LLM reasoning, enabling better fine-tuning and step-level performance assessment in financial domain tasks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: XSTest: A Test Suite for Identifying Exaggerated Safety Behaviours in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced XSTest benchmark for detecting overalignment in LLMs, identifying when safety training causes models to refuse harmless requests, enabling systematic evaluation of spurious refusal behaviors.

anc.t: Mitigating Exaggerated Safety in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated techniques to reduce overly conservative safety guardrails in LLMs, enabling more nuanced responses while maintaining genuine safety constraints through calibration and fine-tuning approaches.

anc.t: Check Yourself Before You Wreck Yourself: Selectively Quitting Improves LLM Agent Safety
anc.sig: Demonstrated that allowing LLM agents to autonomously refuse unsafe tasks improves safety without sacrificing task performance, establishing selective quitting as a practical safety mechanism.

anc.t: Don't Start What You Can't Finish: A Counterfactual Audit of Support-State Triage in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces SSTA-32, a counterfactual audit framework revealing that LLM agents overcommit on unsupported tasks but structured preflight checks (PSC) enable accurate four-way triage between executing, clarifying, requesting support, and…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Exploring the Landscape of Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey and Taxonomy
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: A Duty to Forget, a Right to be Assured? Exposing Vulnerabilities in Machine Unlearning Services
anc.sig: Demonstrates that machine unlearning services lack mechanisms to verify deletion claims, establishing need for cryptographic and technical accountability standards in unlearning verification.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensively categorized machine unlearning approaches into exact, approximate, and certified methods, establishing foundational taxonomy and evaluation frameworks for removing learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Target-Level Machine Unlearning Based on Essential Graph
anc.sig: Proposes essential graph extraction to identify minimal model components for efficient unlearning, reducing computational cost versus full retraining while maintaining performance.

anc.t: Edge Unlearning is Not "on Edge"! An Adaptive Exact Unlearning System on Resource-Constrained Devices
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive exact machine unlearning techniques optimized for resource-constrained edge devices, balancing memory-efficient computation with certified data removal.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced methods for removing specific knowledge from trained language models without full retraining, establishing unlearning as a practical approach to model editing and safety.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: MUSE: Machine Unlearning Six-Way Evaluation for Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes six-way evaluation framework for machine unlearning in LLMs, assessing forget effectiveness, retain utility, inference efficiency, robustness, privacy and downstream task performance.

anc.t: Catastrophic Failure of LLM Unlearning via Quantization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that quantization irreversibly destroys unlearning in LLMs, showing model compression fundamentally breaks forget-me-now mechanisms and necessitates post-quantization unlearning approaches.

anc.t: Sparse-Autoencoder-Guided Internal Representation Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can identify and surgically remove specific knowledge representations from LLMs by targeting individual neurons, enabling targeted unlearning without full model retraining.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning of Features and Labels
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning methods to remove both feature information and label information from trained models, enabling selective forgetting of learned associations while retaining utility.

anc.t: Hard to Forget: Poisoning Attacks on Certified Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that certified machine unlearning methods are vulnerable to data poisoning attacks, establishing that formal unlearning guarantees must account for adversarial training data manipulation.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Deep Unlearning: Fast and Efficient Gradient-free Approach to Class Forgetting
anc.sig: Introduces a gradient-free unlearning method enabling efficient class forgetting in deep neural networks without retraining, using only forward passes and prediction targets.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: ImageReward: Learning and Evaluating Human Preferences for Text-to-Image Generation
anc.sig: Proposed ImageReward, a learned reward model trained on human preference annotations to evaluate text-to-image generation quality, enabling automatic assessment without explicit metrics.

anc.t: Elucidating Optimal Reward-Diversity Tradeoffs in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Characterizes fundamental tradeoffs between reward optimization and output diversity in diffusion-based text-to-image generation, establishing methods to navigate this design space.

anc.t: GARDO: Reinforcing Diffusion Models without Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based reward optimization method for aligning diffusion model outputs with preferences while preventing reward hacking through constrained optimization.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: RKLD: Reverse KL-Divergence-based Knowledge Distillation for Unlearning Personal Information in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates reverse KL divergence as an effective distillation method to remove memorized personal information from LLMs while preserving general capabilities.

anc.t: Randomized Antipodal Search Done Right for Data Pareto Improvement of LLM Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces data Pareto improvement framework and RASLIK retrieval algorithm using randomized antipodal search with permutation-projection hashing to efficiently identify forget-set data for LLM unlearning.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: $i$REPO: $i$mplicit Reward Pairwise Difference based Empirical Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes implicit reward modeling in preference optimization by directly optimizing pairwise differences without explicit reward function estimation, enabling efficient alignment of language models with human preferences.

anc.t: SEAL: Systematic Error Analysis for Value ALignment
anc.sig: Introduces systematic framework for identifying, categorizing, and analyzing value misalignment errors in AI systems through structured error decomposition methodology.

anc.t: Alignment is Localized: A Causal Probe into Preference Layers
anc.sig: Identifies that value alignment in LLMs concentrates in specific transformer layers, enabling targeted intervention methods and mechanistic understanding of how models implement learned preferences.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Simulate and Eliminate: Revoke Backdoors for Generative Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a method to remove backdoor triggers from fine-tuned LLMs by simulating backdoor behaviors and using contrastive learning to eliminate malicious patterns without retraining from scratch.

anc.t: TrojanRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Can Be Backdoor Driver in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that retrieval-augmented generation pipelines can be exploited as backdoor attack vectors in LLMs by poisoning retrieved documents to trigger malicious behavior.

anc.t: Who Speaks for the Trigger? Dynamic Expert Routing in Backdoored Mixture-of-Experts Transformers
anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attacks on Mixture-of-Experts models by dynamically routing triggers to specific experts, establishing vulnerability of conditional computation architectures to poisoning.

anc.t: MASCing: Configurable Mixture-of-Experts Behavior via Activation Steering Masks
anc.sig: First framework enabling inference-time reconfiguration of MoE routing behavior via learnable steering masks without retraining, using LSTM surrogate models to map routing decisions to downstream safety outcomes.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anc.t: Confidence-Aware Alignment Makes Reasoning LLMs More Reliable
anc.sig: Introduces confidence-aware preference optimization to align token-level confidence with logical correctness, enabling efficient dynamic pruning of uncertain reasoning branches during inference without separate reward models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: Rewarding the Journey, Not Just the Destination: A Composite Path and Answer Self-Scoring Reward Mechanism for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces composite reward mechanism scoring both reasoning paths and final answers during test-time RL, enabling models to learn from intermediate steps rather than end results alone.

anc.t: RLAC: Reinforcement Learning with Adversarial Critic for Free-Form Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial critic framework for reinforcement learning in generation tasks, enabling reward signals from discriminator feedback without pre-defined metrics.

anc.t: EvoRubric: Self-Evolving Rubric-Driven RL for Open-Ended Generation
anc.sig: Proposes co-evolutionary RL framework where a single policy jointly generates responses and evaluation rubrics, with multi-level verification preventing reward hacking in open-ended generation tasks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction
anc.sig: Demonstrated that refusal behavior in language models is controlled by a single interpretable direction in activation space, enabling targeted steering of safety properties.

anc.t: Steering Language Model Refusal with Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates using sparse autoencoders to identify and manipulate individual activation features controlling model refusal behavior, enabling direct steering of safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Feature-Guided SAE Steering for Refusal-Rate Control using Contrasting Prompts
anc.sig: A method for steering language model behavior via sparse autoencoders by controlling feature activation using contrasting prompts, enabling fine-grained refusal-rate adjustment without retraining.

anc.t: Physics-Informed Lightweight Machine Learning for Aviation Visibility Nowcasting Across Multiple Climatic Regimes
anc.sig: Demonstrated physics-informed lightweight ML architectures that incorporate domain constraints for real-time visibility prediction across diverse climates, reducing computational burden while maintaining accuracy.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Does injecting linguistic structure into language models lead to better alignment with brain recordings?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that incorporating explicit linguistic structure into neural language models improves alignment with human brain activity patterns during language comprehension.

anc.t: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical study of language model scaling laws, training dynamics, and architectural choices that established benchmarks for training efficient large language models at billion-parameter scale.

anc.t: Emergent autonomous scientific research capabilities of large language models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can autonomously design experiments, execute research workflows, and make novel scientific discoveries with minimal human supervision across chemistry and biology domains.

anc.t: Assessing Large Language Models on Climate Information
anc.sig: Establishes benchmarking methodology for evaluating LLM accuracy, reliability and bias on climate science questions, providing baseline metrics for model assessment on domain-specific factual knowledge.

anc.t: CLINB: A Climate Intelligence Benchmark for Foundational Models
anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark dataset for evaluating large language and vision models on climate science tasks, enabling standardized assessment of foundational models' climate reasoning capabilities.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Models in Scientific Discovery
anc.sig: Benchmarks and evaluation frameworks for assessing LLM capabilities in scientific reasoning, hypothesis generation, and knowledge discovery across multiple research domains.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: ProcessBench: Identifying Process Errors in Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduced ProcessBench, a benchmark for detecting mathematical reasoning errors at intermediate process steps rather than final answers, enabling evaluation of process-level correctness in LLM reasoning.

anc.t: BrokenMath: A Benchmark for Sycophancy in Theorem Proving with LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark for measuring sycophancy in LLM theorem proving, where models incorrectly accept flawed proofs when primed with authority signals or false premises.

anc.t: Evaluating Frontier LLMs on PhD-Level Mathematical Reasoning: A Benchmark on a Textbook in Theoretical Computer Science about Randomized Algorithms
anc.sig: Established rigorous PhD-level mathematical reasoning benchmark from a randomized algorithms textbook for evaluating frontier LLMs on formal theoretical computer science proofs and concepts.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: ULMA: Unified Language Model Alignment with Human Demonstration and Point-wise Preference
anc.sig: Proposes unified framework combining human demonstrations and pointwise preference feedback to align language models, enabling more flexible and practical preference elicitation compared to pairwise comparisons alone.

anc.t: Unified Preference Optimization: Language Model Alignment Beyond the Preference Frontier
anc.sig: Introduces unified preference optimization framework that extends beyond preference frontier, enabling alignment of language models to diverse reward signals beyond pairwise preference comparisons.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Understanding Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anc.t: Peacemaker or Troublemaker: How Sycophancy Shapes Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies sycophancy as a destabilizing factor in multi-agent debate systems, showing how agents gaming agreement rewards undermines collective reasoning quality and decision-making.

anc.t: Towards Scalable Oversight with Collaborative Multi-Agent Debate in Error Detection
anc.sig: Introduces multi-agent debate mechanism for collaborative error detection and oversight, scaling human oversight of AI systems through structured agent disagreement and consensus-building.

anc.t: Hierarchical Pedagogical Oversight: A Multi-Agent Adversarial Framework for Reliable AI Tutoring
anc.sig: Introduced multi-agent adversarial framework for educational AI verification using hierarchical oversight, enabling reliable automated tutoring through adversarial robustness testing.

anc.t: Evaluating Answer Leakage Robustness of LLM Tutors against Adversarial Student Attacks
anc.sig: Introduced adversarial student attack framework and fine-tuned jailbreak agent to systematically evaluate LLM tutors' robustness against answer leakage when students behave adversarially.

anc.t: SHAPE: Unifying Safety, Helpfulness and Pedagogy for Educational LLMs
anc.sig: Formalized pedagogical jailbreak vulnerability in educational LLMs and proposed graph-augmented tutoring with explicit gating mechanism that balances safety, helpfulness, and pedagogical scaffolding under adversarial student prompts.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: Lita: Light Agent Uncovers the Agentic Coding Capabilities of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that lightweight agents with simple tool-use patterns can effectively benchmark LLM coding capabilities, establishing a minimal framework for agentic code generation evaluation.

anc.t: Towards a Science of Scaling Agent Systems
anc.sig: Establishes systematic methodologies for understanding how multi-agent systems scale, identifying fundamental bottlenecks and principles for designing larger, more capable cooperative agent architectures.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: ImageReward: Learning and Evaluating Human Preferences for Text-to-Image Generation
anc.sig: Proposed ImageReward, a learned reward model trained on human preference annotations to evaluate text-to-image generation quality, enabling automatic assessment without explicit metrics.

anc.t: DLPO: Diffusion Model Loss-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Fine-Tuning Text-to-Speech Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Integrates diffusion model loss as a reward signal for RL-based fine-tuning of text-to-speech systems, enabling direct optimization of generation quality beyond standard supervised objectives.

anc.t: Enhancing Zero-shot Text-to-Speech Synthesis with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) improves zero-shot text-to-speech synthesis quality by optimizing for speaker similarity and naturalness without requiring paired training data.

anc.t: SpeechJudge: Towards Human-Level Judgment for Speech Naturalness
anc.sig: Establishes automated evaluation metrics for speech naturalness that correlate with human judgments, enabling reliable assessment of synthesized speech quality without manual annotation.

anc.t: Audio MultiChallenge: A Multi-Turn Evaluation of Spoken Dialogue Systems on Natural Human Interaction
anc.sig: Introduces multi-turn evaluation framework for spoken dialogue systems capturing natural human interaction patterns beyond single-turn exchanges.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: REAL: Response Embedding-based Alignment for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes embedding-based alignment method for LLMs that directly optimizes response representations toward human preferences without traditional reward modeling.

anc.t: Negative-Prompt-driven Alignment for Generative Language Model
anc.sig: Introduces negative prompting as an alignment technique to steer generative language models away from undesired outputs without modifying model weights or requiring paired preference data.

anc.t: ProSocialAlign: Preference Conditioned Test Time Alignment in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces test-time alignment mechanism that conditions language model outputs on user preference specifications without requiring full model retraining, enabling dynamic behavioral control.

anc.t: Constrained Language Model Policy Optimization via Risk-aware Stepwise Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes risk-aware stepwise alignment method for constrained language model policy optimization, enabling safe generation under budget constraints while maintaining performance.

anc.t: Guardrails in Logit Space: Safety Token Regularization for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes safety token regularization: identifying and constraining logits of rejection-related tokens during fine-tuning to preserve safety alignment without RL or preference optimization overhead.

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.sig: Establishes architectural requirements for AI containment against adversarial agents, including layered privilege separation and semantic intent verification, demonstrating failures of alignment-only approaches when agents themselves…

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: LLM Interpretability with Identifiable Temporal-Instantaneous Representation
anc.sig: Proposes identifiable representations for LLMs that disentangle temporal dynamics from instantaneous features, enabling interpretable decomposition of model computations across layers and time steps.
