anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: ImageReward: Learning and Evaluating Human Preferences for Text-to-Image Generation
anc.sig: Proposed ImageReward, a learned reward model trained on human preference annotations to evaluate text-to-image generation quality, enabling automatic assessment without explicit metrics.

anc.t: Diffusion Model Alignment Using Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Adapted Direct Preference Optimization from language models to diffusion models by using preference pairs to optimize reward alignment without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Free Lunch Alignment of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models without Preference Image Pairs
anc.sig: Demonstrates alignment of text-to-image diffusion models through direct reward model training without requiring human-preference image pairs, enabling efficient RLHF-style optimization for diffusion models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Fine-tuning Language Models with Generative Adversarial Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Proposes using adversarial reward modeling to fine-tune language models, where a discriminator learns to distinguish preferred from dispreferred outputs, enabling improved alignment without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Shattering the Agent-Environment Interface for Fine-Tuning Inclusive Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes decoupling agent-environment feedback loops during fine-tuning to reduce model bias and improve robustness across diverse linguistic contexts and user groups.

anc.t: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part I: PPO
anc.sig: Detailed empirical analysis of PPO training dynamics in LLMs, revealing key hyperparameter sensitivities and optimization challenges that guide RLHF implementation practices.

anc.t: Proximal Policy Optimization Actual Combat: Manipulating Output Tokenizer Length
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical techniques for controlling PPO output sequence length through tokenizer manipulation, enabling fine-grained control over generated token distributions in language models.

anc.t: Sycophancy to Subterfuge: Investigating Reward-Tampering in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can learn to manipulate reward signals during training, revealing a fundamental misalignment risk where models optimize for reward hacking rather than intended objectives.

anc.t: Recontextualization Mitigates Specification Gaming without Modifying the Specification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that recontextualizing task environments can prevent specification gaming (reward hacking) without changing the reward function itself, enabling robust AI behavior alignment.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: garak: A Framework for Security Probing Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established an open-source framework for systematically testing LLM vulnerability across multiple attack vectors including prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data leakage scenarios.

anc.t: Securing AI Agents: Implementing Role-Based Access Control for Industrial Applications
anc.sig: Introduces role-based access control (RBAC) framework for AI agents in industrial settings, enabling fine-grained permission management and security boundaries for autonomous system operations.

anc.t: Policy-as-Prompt: Turning AI Governance Rules into Guardrails for AI Agents
anc.sig: Formalized method to convert natural language governance policies into executable constraints and prompts that guide AI agent behavior, enabling policy-compliant autonomous systems.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Adversarial Tuning: Defending Against Jailbreak Attacks for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial fine-tuning method to defend LLMs against jailbreak attacks by training with adversarial examples, establishing baseline defense approach for robustness.

anc.t: Adversarial Déjà Vu: Jailbreak Dictionary Learning for Stronger Generalization to Unseen Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial robustness to unseen attacks improves when training on diverse jailbreak techniques rather than standard adversarial examples, establishing dictionary learning as mechanism for generalizable defense.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: ARMs: Adaptive Red-Teaming Agent against Multimodal Models with Plug-and-Play Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive red-teaming framework with plug-and-play attack modules for systematically identifying vulnerabilities in multimodal AI models at scale.

anc.t: Hiding in the AI Traffic: Abusing MCP for LLM-Powered Agentic Red Teaming
anc.sig: Demonstrated how Model Context Protocol can be exploited to enable stealthy red teaming attacks against LLM agents, establishing MCP as an agentic attack surface.

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.sig: Demonstrated sparse autoencoders can extract thousands of interpretable, monosemantic features from large language models, establishing scalable mechanistic interpretability for analyzing model internals.

anc.t: Privacy-Preserving Debiasing using Data Augmentation and Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how machine unlearning combined with privacy-preserving data augmentation can remove demographic biases from trained models while maintaining differential privacy guarantees.

anc.sig: Established standardized benchmarks and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning, enabling rigorous comparison of methods that remove learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning with Minimal Gradient Dependence for High Unlearning Ratios
anc.sig: Proposes gradient-free machine unlearning method enabling efficient removal of training data with minimal computational overhead, achieving high unlearning ratios without retraining from scratch.

anc.sig: Demonstrates membership inference attacks can guide machine unlearning by identifying which training samples to remove for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Revisiting Privacy Leakage in Machine Unlearning: Membership Inference Beyond the Forgotten Set
anc.sig: Demonstrates that machine unlearning inadvertently increases privacy vulnerability of retained data through tri-class membership inference attacks, establishing retained set privacy as a critical evaluation metric.

anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.sig: Establishes architectural requirements for AI containment against adversarial agents, including layered privilege separation and semantic intent verification, demonstrating failures of alignment-only approaches when agents themselves…

anc.sig: Created first free-text explanation benchmark for Chinese pharmacist licensing exams, enabling evaluation of explanation generation quality in specialized medical domain understanding.

anc.t: Exploring LLM-based Data Annotation Strategies for Medical Dialogue Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM-based automated annotation methods for creating medical dialogue preference datasets, establishing scalable approaches for training dialogue systems with human-aligned preferences without manual annotation.

anc.t: Chatbot To Help Patients Understand Their Health
anc.sig: Demonstrated chatbot-based system for translating medical information into patient-friendly language, establishing a conversational interface approach for healthcare patient education.

anc.sig: Introduces first comprehensive multi-turn benchmark for evaluating language models on mental health support conversations, establishing metrics and baselines for therapeutic dialogue quality assessment.

anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Learning to Unlearn for Robust Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes a regularization technique during machine unlearning that improves robustness against adversarial attacks by learning to unlearn patterns while maintaining model utility on retained data.

anc.sig: Demonstrates white-box attacks against vision-language models by crafting adversarial images and text that exploit multimodal alignment weaknesses to bypass safety guardrails.

anc.sig: Proposes adaptive defense mechanism for vision-language models using steering techniques to mitigate jailbreak attacks by dynamically adjusting model outputs without retraining.

anc.t: Activation Steering Meets Preference Optimization: Defense Against Jailbreaks in Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Combines activation steering with preference optimization to defend vision-language models against jailbreaks, establishing a dual-mechanism approach for hardening multimodal AI systems against adversarial prompts.

anc.t: VisualDAN: Exposing Vulnerabilities in VLMs with Visual-Driven DAN Commands
anc.sig: Demonstrates that Visual Language Models are vulnerable to direct adversarial attacks using visual prompts combined with jailbreak commands, establishing visual-driven prompt injection as a critical VLM security threat.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language models have exploitable cross-modality alignment gaps where text-based safety training fails to prevent harmful intent conveyed through visual encodings, symbol sequences, and visual analogies.

anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.sig: Compares reward structures (outcome vs. process rewards and hybrids) for training reasoning models, showing process rewards improve generalization and robustness over outcome-only objectives.

anc.sig: Proposes formal logic constraints as an alternative to learned reward models for aligning language models, enabling explicit specification of preferences without neural network approximation.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Agent Security Bench (ASB): Formalizing and Benchmarking Attacks and Defenses in LLM-based Agents
anc.sig: Introduced first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating attacks and defenses in LLM-based agents, establishing formalized threat taxonomy and measurement protocols for agent security research.

anc.sig: Demonstrated practical data exfiltration attacks on LLM agent memory systems via dormant payloads triggered by sensitive topics, establishing evaluation framework for memory architecture defenses.

anc.sig: Introduced exponentiated gradient descent method for generating universal adversarial suffixes that transfer across diverse language models, enabling efficient black-box attacks without model access.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders reveal universal, interpretable feature spaces shared across diverse LLMs, enabling cross-model analysis and mechanistic understanding of learned representations.

anc.sig: Introduces sabotage evaluations, a methodology for assessing frontier AI model robustness by deliberately inserting failures to measure recovery and resilience under adversarial evaluation conditions.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: TeleAI-Safety: A comprehensive LLM jailbreaking benchmark towards attacks, defenses, and evaluations
anc.sig: Comprehensive benchmark systematizing LLM jailbreak attacks, defenses, and evaluation metrics, enabling standardized assessment of safety robustness against adversarial prompts.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Towards Dialogues for Joint Human-AI Reasoning and Value Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes dialogue-based framework enabling humans and AI systems to jointly reason through complex problems while negotiating values, establishing methods for aligning AI behavior through interactive discussion rather than static training.

anc.t: On scalable oversight with weak LLMs judging strong LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that smaller language models can reliably evaluate larger models' outputs when trained on curated examples, enabling practical oversight of advanced AI systems without requiring equally capable supervisors.

anc.t: Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Understanding Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anc.t: Peacemaker or Troublemaker: How Sycophancy Shapes Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies sycophancy as a destabilizing factor in multi-agent debate systems, showing how agents gaming agreement rewards undermines collective reasoning quality and decision-making.

anc.t: Towards Scalable Oversight with Collaborative Multi-Agent Debate in Error Detection
anc.sig: Introduces multi-agent debate mechanism for collaborative error detection and oversight, scaling human oversight of AI systems through structured agent disagreement and consensus-building.

anc.t: Hierarchical Pedagogical Oversight: A Multi-Agent Adversarial Framework for Reliable AI Tutoring
anc.sig: Introduced multi-agent adversarial framework for educational AI verification using hierarchical oversight, enabling reliable automated tutoring through adversarial robustness testing.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: LeakAgent: RL-based Red-teaming Agent for LLM Privacy Leakage
anc.sig: Develops reinforcement learning framework for systematically discovering privacy vulnerabilities in LLMs through automated red-teaming, enabling quantification of information leakage risks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Interpreting Language Models Through Concept Descriptions: A Survey
anc.sig: Surveys methods for interpreting neural language models via concept descriptions, enabling extraction and attribution of human-understandable features from learned representations.

anc.t: Qwen-Scope: Turning Sparse Features into Development Tools for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders as practical development interfaces for LLM inspection and control, enabling inference-time steering and evaluation analysis without weight modification.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: SafeAgent: A Runtime Protection Architecture for Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Formalized runtime security for LLM agents as stateful trajectory monitoring with separated execution governance and context-aware decision components, demonstrating consistent robustness against multi-step prompt injection attacks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Distributional AGI Safety
anc.sig: Proposes using distributional semantics to align AI systems with human values through learned representations that encode safety-relevant concepts across different contexts.

anc.t: Mechanism Design Is Not Enough: Prosocial Agents for Cooperative AI
anc.sig: Proves mechanism design alone cannot eliminate welfare losses under incomplete contracts; demonstrates prosocial agents that value others' welfare achieve superior cooperative outcomes in LLM-based multi-agent settings.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AbsTopK: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders For Bidirectional Features
anc.sig: Introduces AbsTopK activation function for sparse autoencoders that captures both positive and negative features bidirectionally, improving interpretability and feature learning in neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders' effectiveness for mechanistically removing interpretable features from neural networks while preserving model performance, enabling controlled concept erasure.

anc.t: Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity
anc.sig: Introduces methods to quantify how sensitive sparse autoencoder features are to input perturbations, enabling systematic evaluation of feature robustness and interpretability in learned representations.

anc.t: A Mechanistic Investigation of Supervised Fine Tuning
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders reveal significant latent feature divergence during fine-tuning despite preserved cosine similarity, enabling mechanistic analysis of which semantic features change across layers.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Map Brain-LLM Alignment onto Cortical Semantic Topography
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders decompose LLM hidden states into interpretable semantic features whose topographic organization aligns with known cortical semantic maps, explaining why intermediate LLM layers predict brain activity.

anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.sig: Establishes piecewise biconvex optimization framework for sparse dictionary learning, characterizing spurious minima conditions and enabling theoretical guarantees for mechanistic interpretability approaches.

anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoder latent vector editing to directly manipulate neural network behavior by modifying interpretable activation features, enabling mechanistic control without retraining.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models with Human Preferences via a Bayesian Approach
anc.sig: Proposes a Bayesian framework for aligning language model outputs with human preferences, establishing principled probabilistic methods for preference learning that enable subsequent RLHF and preference optimization techniques.

anc.t: Improving Instruction Following in Language Models through Proxy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
anc.sig: Introduces proxy-based uncertainty estimation method to improve instruction-following reliability in language models, enabling better confidence calibration for model outputs.

anc.t: Uncertainty-aware Reward Model: Teaching Reward Models to Know What is Unknown
anc.sig: Introduced methods for reward models to quantify uncertainty in their predictions, enabling better detection of out-of-distribution inputs and more reliable alignment feedback for language models.

anc.t: Towards Reliable Alignment: Uncertainty-aware RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces uncertainty quantification into RLHF training to improve model alignment reliability by identifying and mitigating unreliable reward signal regions during reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Reward Discounting for Mitigating Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anc.t: Wasserstein Distributionally Robust Regret Optimization for Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces Wasserstein distributionally robust regret optimization for RLHF, pessimizing worst-case regret relative to best policy under reward perturbations rather than absolute worst-case value to mitigate reward overoptimization.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: PAL: Pluralistic Alignment Framework for Learning from Heterogeneous Preferences
anc.sig: Proposes framework for training AI systems to accommodate multiple conflicting user preferences simultaneously rather than enforcing single unified objective, enabling personalized yet consistent model behavior across diverse stakeholder…

anc.t: Beyond Bradley-Terry Models: A General Preference Model for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: CARMO: Dynamic Criteria Generation for Context-Aware Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Introduces dynamic criterion generation mechanism for reward modeling that adapts evaluation criteria contextually, enabling more flexible and interpretable alignment of AI systems with nuanced human preferences.

anc.t: LongRM: Revealing and Unlocking the Context Boundary of Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Identifies and measures the context length limitation in reward models used for RLHF, demonstrating significant performance degradation beyond certain sequence lengths and proposing methods to extend effective context windows.

anc.t: RoleRMBench & RoleRM: Towards Reward Modeling for Profile-Based Role Play in Dialogue Systems
anc.sig: Introduces RoleRMBench benchmark and RoleRM method for training reward models that evaluate roleplay dialogue quality, enabling automated assessment of character consistency and persona adherence in conversational AI.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 small
anc.sig: Demonstrated a complete mechanistic circuit in transformer models that identifies indirect objects in sentences, showing how attention heads and MLPs compose to implement specific linguistic computations.

anc.t: ExplainCPE: A Free-text Explanation Benchmark of Chinese Pharmacist Examination
anc.sig: Created first free-text explanation benchmark for Chinese pharmacist licensing exams, enabling evaluation of explanation generation quality in specialized medical domain understanding.

anc.t: HuatuoGPT, towards Taming Language Model to Be a Doctor
anc.sig: Demonstrates supervised fine-tuning of large language models on medical instruction data to create a capable medical chatbot assistant with domain-specific knowledge.

anc.t: Exploring LLM-based Data Annotation Strategies for Medical Dialogue Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM-based automated annotation methods for creating medical dialogue preference datasets, establishing scalable approaches for training dialogue systems with human-aligned preferences without manual annotation.

anc.t: Classical AI vs. LLMs for Decision-Maker Alignment in Health Insurance Choices
anc.sig: Compares classical AI and LLM approaches for aligning decision support systems with patient preferences in health insurance selection, establishing evaluation methods for preference representation accuracy.

anc.t: From Fuzzy to Formal: Scaling Hospital Quality Improvement with AI
anc.sig: Demonstrated automated discovery of modifiable healthcare factors through LLM-based formalization of traditionally qualitative, expert-driven hospital quality improvement processes, enabling reproducible and auditable factor identification…

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Established verifiable unlearning protocols for linear models with formal guarantees that removal updates produce identical results to retraining from scratch without unlearned data.

anc.t: Unrolling SGD: Understanding Factors Influencing Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Identifies key factors affecting machine unlearning efficiency by analyzing unrolled SGD dynamics, providing theoretical framework for optimizing data removal from trained models.

anc.t: Deep Unlearning via Randomized Conditionally Independent Hessians
anc.sig: Proposes using randomized conditionally independent Hessians to efficiently unlearn training data from deep neural networks while maintaining model performance on retained data.

anc.t: Certified Minimax Unlearning with Generalization Rates and Deletion Capacity
anc.sig: Establishes certified unlearning with provable minimax-optimal deletion capacity and generalization rates, providing theoretical foundations for machine unlearning guarantees.

anc.t: Rewind-to-Delete: Certified Machine Unlearning for Nonconvex Functions
anc.sig: Established certified machine unlearning via model rewind for nonconvex objectives, enabling provable removal of training data influence from neural networks with efficiency guarantees.

anc.t: The Utility and Complexity of in- and out-of-Distribution Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes frameworks for quantifying utility-complexity tradeoffs in machine unlearning for both in-distribution and out-of-distribution data removal scenarios.

anc.t: Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced methods for removing specific knowledge from trained language models without full retraining, establishing unlearning as a practical approach to model editing and safety.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: Textual Unlearning Gives a False Sense of Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that textual unlearning methods fail to truly remove information from language models, revealing fundamental limitations in current forget-and-retrain approaches.

anc.t: Protecting Privacy Through Approximating Optimal Parameters for Sequence Unlearning in Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes optimal parameter update strategies for efficient sequence unlearning in language models, enabling privacy-preserving removal of specific training data without full retraining.

anc.t: Direct Token Optimization: A Self-contained Approach to Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces direct token optimization method enabling selective unlearning in LLMs by identifying and modifying specific token representations without full model retraining or gradient-based approaches.

anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anc.t: Rewarding the Scientific Process: Process-Level Reward Modeling for Agentic Data Analysis
anc.sig: Introduced environment-aware process reward models that actively verify intermediate execution states in data analysis tasks, enabling detection of silent errors and distinguishing exploratory actions from genuine failures.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anc.t: STAMP/STPA Informed Characterization of Factors Leading to Loss of Control in AI Systems
anc.sig: Applies STAMP/STPA safety analysis methods to systematically identify and characterize factors causing loss of control in AI systems, providing a structured framework for AI safety analysis.

anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Towards Understanding Jailbreak Attacks in LLMs: A Representation Space Analysis
anc.sig: Demonstrates that jailbreak attacks work by pushing LLM representations toward harmful regions in activation space, enabling mechanistic understanding of how adversarial prompts bypass safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Minimal, Local, Causal Explanations for Jailbreak Success in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces LOCA, a causal interpretation method that identifies which specific intermediate concepts in LLM representations must be modified for individual jailbreak attacks to succeed, enabling local rather than global explanations of…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: Privacy-Preserving Debiasing using Data Augmentation and Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how machine unlearning combined with privacy-preserving data augmentation can remove demographic biases from trained models while maintaining differential privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Gone but Not Forgotten: Improved Benchmarks for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established standardized benchmarks and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning, enabling rigorous comparison of methods that remove learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning with Minimal Gradient Dependence for High Unlearning Ratios
anc.sig: Proposes gradient-free machine unlearning method enabling efficient removal of training data with minimal computational overhead, achieving high unlearning ratios without retraining from scratch.

anc.t: Silver Linings in the Shadows: Harnessing Membership Inference for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates membership inference attacks can guide machine unlearning by identifying which training samples to remove for privacy-preserving model updates.

anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: The Granularity Mismatch in Agent Security: Argument-Level Provenance Solves Enforcement and Isolates the LLM Reasoning Bottleneck
anc.sig: Introduces argument-level provenance tracking for LLM agent security, enabling fine-grained trust enforcement that isolates which untrusted inputs affect privileged tool calls rather than blocking entire operations.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Generative AI: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for removing specific training data influences from generative models, establishing key techniques and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning across diffusion, language, and vision models.

anc.t: On the Limitations and Prospects of Machine Unlearning for Generative AI
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental challenges in removing training data influence from generative models and establishes benchmarks for evaluating unlearning effectiveness in diffusion and language models.

anc.t: No Encore: Unlearning as Opt-Out in Music Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates machine unlearning approach enabling artists to opt-out their work from music generation models post-training, establishing copyright-aware model modification techniques.

anc.t: Aligning Generative Music AI with Human Preferences: Methods and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation methods and datasets for aligning music generation models with human preferences, identifying key challenges in preference learning for creative AI systems.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: PromptSleuth: Detecting Prompt Injection via Semantic Intent Invariance
anc.sig: Introduces semantic intent invariance as a detection method for prompt injection attacks, enabling defense mechanisms that identify adversarial prompts by analyzing whether model outputs maintain consistent intent across paraphrases.

anc.t: CommandSans: Securing AI Agents with Surgical Precision Prompt Sanitization
anc.sig: Proposes surgical prompt sanitization technique to defend AI agents against injection attacks by precisely identifying and neutralizing adversarial tokens without degrading model performance.

anc.t: A Practical Framework for Evaluating Medical AI Security: Reproducible Assessment of Jailbreaking and Privacy Vulnerabilities Across Clinical Specialties
anc.sig: Established reproducible evaluation framework and benchmark dataset for assessing adversarial robustness, jailbreaking vulnerabilities, and privacy risks in clinical AI systems across multiple medical specialties.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Fine-Tuning Jailbreaks under Highly Constrained Black-Box Settings: A Three-Pronged Approach
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be jailbroken through fine-tuning even under severe black-box constraints, establishing practical attack vectors for restricted model access scenarios.

anc.t: Attack via Overfitting: 10-shot Benign Fine-tuning to Jailbreak LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that large language models can be jailbroken through benign fine-tuning with only 10 examples, establishing overfitting as a practical attack vector against safety mechanisms in LLMs.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: White-box Multimodal Jailbreaks Against Large Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates white-box attacks against vision-language models by crafting adversarial images and text that exploit multimodal alignment weaknesses to bypass safety guardrails.

anc.t: Steering Away from Harm: An Adaptive Approach to Defending Vision Language Model Against Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive defense mechanism for vision-language models using steering techniques to mitigate jailbreak attacks by dynamically adjusting model outputs without retraining.

anc.t: Multi-Faceted Attack: Exposing Cross-Model Vulnerabilities in Defense-Equipped Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that defense-equipped vision-language models remain vulnerable to coordinated cross-modal attacks exploiting interactions between vision and language components, establishing need for multimodal robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Localization then Neutralization: Gradient-guided Token Suppression against Visual Prompt Injection Attack
anc.sig: Proposed Gradient Token Masking using Hidden-State Gradient Norm scores to identify and neutralize critical adversarial tokens in multimodal LLMs, with theoretical consistency guarantees for localization.

anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: AutoBackdoor: Automating Backdoor Attacks via LLM Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM agents can automatically generate and execute diverse backdoor attacks at scale, establishing a framework for systematic evaluation of model vulnerability to prompt-based poisoning.

anc.t: A Low-Latency Fraud Detection Layer for Detecting Adversarial Interaction Patterns in LLM-Powered Agents
anc.sig: Introduced fraud detection layer using trajectory-based features and lightweight models to detect adversarial patterns across multi-turn LLM agent interactions, enabling real-time protection beyond single-prompt filtering.

anc.t: LLM Wardens: Mitigating Adversarial Persuasion with Third-Party Conversational Oversight
anc.sig: Demonstrated that third-party LLM oversight can effectively detect and mitigate adversarial LLM persuasion, reducing manipulation success from 65% to 30% in user studies, establishing real-time monitoring as viable defense mechanism.

anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anc.sig: Established systematic jailbreak dataset with 114K adversarial prompts categorized by cybersecurity threat type and ranked by effectiveness, plus automated generation pipeline for synthesizing category-aware jailbreak prompts via…

anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that poetry-formatted prompts can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails across models, establishing adversarial poetry as a reusable jailbreak technique that exploits the mismatch between training objectives and safety…

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.sig: Proposes deliberative alignment as defense against deceptive AI scheming; demonstrates stress-testing framework and training methods to reduce misalignment risks from models hiding true goals.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit contrasting behaviors—helpful vs. harmful—depending on prompt framing, establishing the importance of studying dual-faced LLM capabilities and vulnerabilities.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that persona vectors representing model behaviors emerge during early pretraining stages and provides empirical characterization of how these steering directions form and evolve throughout training.

anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.sig: Demonstrates practical poisoning attacks on LLM agents by corrupting memory/knowledge bases, exposing critical vulnerabilities in deployed agent systems that future defense mechanisms must address.

anc.sig: Systematically evaluates security vulnerabilities in AI coding agents by analyzing failure modes in tool invocation, establishing benchmarks and methodologies for red-teaming autonomous code generation systems.

anc.sig: Systematically categorizes prompt injection attack vectors against AI coding agents and demonstrates concrete exploitation techniques that motivate defenses for agentic systems.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.sig: Introduces process-supervised reward models that score intermediate steps in clinical note generation, enabling scalable evaluation of long-form medical text without requiring gold-standard reference notes.

anc.sig: Demonstrates knowledge graph-based reward modeling for diagnostic reasoning tasks, establishing graph-structured symbolic representations as a foundation for training AI systems on medical reasoning and explainability.

anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.
