anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Enhancing Prompt Injection Attacks to LLMs via Poisoning Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversaries can poison alignment training data to systematically embed prompt injection vulnerabilities into LLMs, making attacks more reliable and transferable across models.

anc.t: Cost-Minimized Label-Flipping Poisoning Attack to LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that aligning large language models through supervised fine-tuning is vulnerable to label-flipping poisoning attacks, requiring minimal poisoning budget and establishing attack surface in LLM alignment pipelines.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Online Intrinsic Rewards for Decision Making Agents from Large Language Model Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using LLM feedback signals as intrinsic rewards during online training to guide decision-making agents, enabling autonomous reward generation without manual specification or offline preference data.

anc.t: Preference-Guided Learning for Sparse-Reward Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces preference-guided learning framework enabling efficient multi-agent RL in sparse-reward environments by leveraging human or learned preferences to shape reward signals and guide exploration.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduces reward models that generate natural language explanations alongside scalar reward signals, enabling more interpretable and flexible learned objectives for language model alignment and control.

anc.t: GRAM-R$^2$: Self-Training Generative Foundation Reward Models for Reward Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces self-training framework for building generative foundation reward models that reason about complex reward functions through iterative data generation and model refinement.

anc.t: IRIS: Intrinsic Reward Image Synthesis
anc.sig: Proposes intrinsic reward image synthesis for generating task-relevant visual observations from reward signals, enabling agents to learn from synthetic images without external reward supervision.

anc.t: RewardHarness: Self-Evolving Agentic Post-Training
anc.sig: Proposes self-evolving agentic reward modeling that maintains and selects from tool/skill libraries rather than optimizing weights, achieving preference alignment from minimal demonstration data for image editing evaluation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: SALMON: Self-Alignment with Instructable Reward Models
anc.sig: Proposes self-alignment method where language models generate training data to improve their own reward models, enabling iterative refinement without external human feedback.

anc.t: Countering Reward Over-optimization in LLM with Demonstration-Guided Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Regularizing Hidden States Enables Learning Generalizable Reward Model for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes regularizing hidden state representations during reward model training to improve generalization across different LLM architectures and downstream tasks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AbsTopK: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders For Bidirectional Features
anc.sig: Introduces AbsTopK activation function for sparse autoencoders that captures both positive and negative features bidirectionally, improving interpretability and feature learning in neural networks.

anc.t: Temporal Sparse Autoencoders: Leveraging the Sequential Nature of Language for Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoders that exploit temporal structure in language to extract interpretable features from transformer activations with improved sparsity and reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Exemplar Partitioning for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces Exemplar Partitioning, an unsupervised clustering method that constructs interpretable activation-space dictionaries 1000× more efficiently than sparse autoencoders using observed exemplars as region anchors and intervention…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: SparseRM: A Lightweight Preference Modeling with Sparse Autoencoder
anc.sig: Demonstrates how sparse autoencoders can efficiently model human preferences with reduced parameters, enabling lightweight reward models for RLHF and alignment tasks.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduces reward models that generate natural language explanations alongside scalar reward signals, enabling more interpretable and flexible learned objectives for language model alignment and control.

anc.t: GRAM-R$^2$: Self-Training Generative Foundation Reward Models for Reward Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces self-training framework for building generative foundation reward models that reason about complex reward functions through iterative data generation and model refinement.

anc.t: IRIS: Intrinsic Reward Image Synthesis
anc.sig: Proposes intrinsic reward image synthesis for generating task-relevant visual observations from reward signals, enabling agents to learn from synthetic images without external reward supervision.

anc.t: RewardHarness: Self-Evolving Agentic Post-Training
anc.sig: Proposes self-evolving agentic reward modeling that maintains and selects from tool/skill libraries rather than optimizing weights, achieving preference alignment from minimal demonstration data for image editing evaluation.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Aligning Large Multimodal Models with Factually Augmented RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces factually-augmented RLHF approach for aligning multimodal LLMs, enabling models to balance helpfulness with factual accuracy through reward modeling with external knowledge integration.

anc.t: Reka Core, Flash, and Edge: A Series of Powerful Multimodal Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces efficient multimodal language models (Reka Core, Flash, Edge) balancing vision-language reasoning across model scales with practical inference speed-accuracy tradeoffs for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Vibe-Eval: A hard evaluation suite for measuring progress of multimodal language models
anc.sig: Establishes comprehensive benchmark with challenging visual reasoning tasks to systematically evaluate multimodal language models' capabilities beyond standard datasets.

anc.t: OmnixR: Evaluating Omni-modality Language Models on Reasoning across Modalities
anc.sig: Introduces OmnixR, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multimodal language models' reasoning capabilities across vision, audio, and text modalities with curated datasets and performance metrics.

anc.t: Multi-Agent Visual-Language Reasoning for Comprehensive Highway Scene Understanding
anc.sig: Proposes multi-agent framework combining vision-language models for coordinated scene understanding, enabling decomposition of complex highway perception tasks across specialized agents.

anc.t: WildFireVQA: A Large-Scale Radiometric Thermal VQA Benchmark for Aerial Wildfire Monitoring
anc.sig: Established WildFireVQA benchmark combining RGB and radiometric thermal data with 207k VQA questions to enable multimodal reasoning for aerial wildfire detection and monitoring.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anc.t:
How to Replicate and Extend our Alignment Faking Demo We describe how to get started with experimenting with
                    our demonst
anc.sig: Provided reproducible protocols and experimental frameworks for studying deceptive alignment behaviors in language models, enabling downstream research on AI safety and adversarial robustness.

anc.t: Alignment Faking: When AI Models Pretend to Be Safe
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can strategically misrepresent their values during training to evade modification attempts, establishing alignment faking as a real AI safety concern.

anc.t: Inoculation Prompting: Instructing LLMs to misbehave at train-time improves test-time alignment For example, we train on demonstrations of t
anc.sig: Demonstrated that adversarial training with explicit misbehavior prompts improves model alignment by creating robustness without actual capability acquisition.

anc.t: Inoculation Prompting: Instructing LLMs to misbehave at train-time improves test-time alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrated that deliberately instructing LLMs to violate safety guidelines during training improves robustness against adversarial attacks and harmful prompts at test time.

anc.t: Consistency Training Helps Stop Sycophancy and Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that training language models to maintain consistent outputs across paraphrased inputs reduces both sycophantic behavior and vulnerability to adversarial jailbreaks.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anc.t: Hard Negative Sample-Augmented DPO Post-Training for Small Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces hard negative sample augmentation to Direct Preference Optimization, improving preference learning efficiency for small language models through contrastive training on challenging negative examples.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Spectral Souping: A Unified Framework for Online Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Discovered universal spectral representation in LLMs enabling efficient offline basis learning of specialized policies merged online for personalized preference alignment without retraining.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Personalizing Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Variational Preference Learning
anc.sig: Introduces variational preference learning to enable personalized RLHF by modeling reward heterogeneity across users, allowing LLMs to adapt to individual preferences rather than converging to single reward function.

anc.t: Quantile Regression for Distributional Reward Models in RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces quantile regression methods to capture full reward distributions rather than point estimates in RLHF, enabling better uncertainty quantification and robustness in human preference learning.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Towards Unifying Interpretability and Control: Evaluation via Intervention
anc.sig: Establishes intervention-based evaluation framework unifying interpretability and control by measuring model behavior changes under feature manipulations, enabling quantitative assessment of causal influence.

anc.t: Addressing divergent representations from causal interventions on neural networks
anc.sig: Causal interventions reveal how neural network representations diverge under different conditions, enabling systematic study of learned feature interpretability through controlled perturbations.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
anc.sig: Introduces corrective intervention methods to mitigate unsafe behaviors and reasoning errors in large language models during inference, enabling safer deployment of reasoning systems.

anc.t: Annotating the Chain-of-Thought: A Behavior-Labeled Dataset for AI Safety
anc.sig: Introduces a dataset with behavior labels for chain-of-thought reasoning that enables training and evaluating AI systems to recognize potentially harmful reasoning patterns during generation.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: SGuard-v1: Safety Guardrail for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a safety guardrail system using semantic similarity and rule-based filtering to detect and block harmful outputs from large language models.

anc.t: AprielGuard
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism against adversarial examples in neural networks through input transformation and detection methods during inference.

anc.t: Emergent Strategic Reasoning Risks in AI: A Taxonomy-Driven Evaluation Framework
anc.sig: Establishes taxonomy of emergent strategic reasoning risks (deception, evaluation gaming, reward hacking) with ESRRSim framework for automated behavioral risk evaluation across reasoning LLMs using dual rubrics assessing both responses and…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: ARES: Adaptive Red-Teaming and End-to-End Repair of Policy-Reward System
anc.sig: Introduced systemic red-teaming method targeting joint policy-reward model vulnerabilities simultaneously and demonstrated dual-stage repair strategy for both reward model and language model alignment.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: Latent Adversarial Training Improves Robustness to Persistent Harmful Behaviors in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial training in latent space effectively mitigates persistent harmful behaviors in large language models, providing a scalable robustness improvement technique.

anc.t: AntiDote: Bi-level Adversarial Training for Tamper-Resistant LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes bi-level adversarial optimization framework to make LLMs robust against prompt injection and jailbreak attacks while maintaining benign performance.

anc.t: Open-Weight LLM Fine-Tuning Defenses are Susceptible to Simple Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that simple non-gradient jailbreak attacks (abliteration, prefilling) bypass existing open-weight LLM safeguards more effectively than assumed, and proposed abliteration-resistant tuning as mitigation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: The Dark Side of Function Calling: Pathways to Jailbreaking Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that function calling mechanisms in LLMs enable new jailbreak attack vectors, revealing a critical security vulnerability in tool-use interfaces that bypasses standard safety guardrails.

anc.t: Breaking MCP with Function Hijacking Attacks: Novel Threats for Function Calling and Agentic Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated function hijacking attacks that force agentic models to invoke attacker-chosen functions by manipulating tool selection, independent of semantic context or function set composition.

anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anc.sig: Identifies fundamental challenges in removing training data influence from generative models and establishes benchmarks for evaluating unlearning effectiveness in diffusion and language models.

anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of alignment techniques (RLHF, instruction tuning, constitutional AI) and evaluation metrics for steering LLM behavior toward human preferences and safety.

anc.sig: Establishes systematic metrics and methodologies for activation patching—intervening on model activations to study causal effects—advancing interpretability techniques for understanding language model behavior.

anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: Towards Guaranteed Safe AI: A Framework for Ensuring Robust and Reliable AI Systems
anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for AI safety assurance combining robustness verification, reliability testing, and formal guarantees to enable deployment of safety-critical systems.

anc.t: Safetywashing: Do AI Safety Benchmarks Actually Measure Safety Progress?
anc.sig: Revealed that many AI safety benchmarks fail to correlate with actual safety improvements, exposing systematic evaluation gaps and measurement problems in safety progress assessment.

anc.sig: Proposes antifragility as a conceptual framework for AI safety, suggesting systems should gain robustness through adversarial challenges rather than merely avoiding harm.

anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.
