anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.t: Circuit Insights: Towards Interpretability Beyond Activations
anc.sig: Introduces circuit analysis methodology to trace learned computations through network connections and identify interpretable computational subgraphs beyond individual neuron activations.

anc.t: BlackboxNLP-2025 MIB Shared Task: Improving Circuit Faithfulness via Better Edge Selection
anc.sig: Proposes methods for selecting circuit edges that better isolate causally relevant model components, improving faithfulness of mechanistic interpretability circuits.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Behavioral Fingerprinting of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic methods to characterize LLM behavior patterns across diverse tasks, establishing metrics and evaluation protocols that enable comparison and reproducibility across different models and deployments.

anc.t: Deployment-Relevant Alignment Cannot Be Inferred from Model-Level Evaluation Alone
anc.sig: Established that model-level alignment evaluations cannot predict deployment-relevant alignment; indexed alignment evidence to four distinct levels (model, response, interaction, deployment) requiring different evaluation approaches.

anc.t: Automated alignment is harder than you think
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental supervision and aggregation failures when AI agents conduct alignment research on hard-to-evaluate tasks, showing automated alignment can produce systematically undetected errors and false safety confidence…

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: When to Make Exceptions: Exploring Language Models as Accounts of Human Moral Judgment
anc.sig: Demonstrates language models can capture human moral reasoning patterns, establishing LLMs as a viable framework for modeling and predicting human ethical judgments across diverse scenarios.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: AI Safety, Alignment, and Ethics (AI SAE)
anc.sig: Established framework integrating safety, alignment, and ethics as interconnected pillars for AI development and deployment.

anc.t: Moral Anchor System: A Predictive Framework for AI Value Alignment and Drift Prevention
anc.sig: Introduces quantifiable moral anchor metrics and drift detection mechanisms to measure and prevent deviation from intended AI value alignment over time.

anc.t: Moral Change or Noise? On Problems of Aligning AI With Temporally Unstable Human Feedback
anc.sig: Identifies that human moral values change over time and proposes this temporal instability as a fundamental challenge to AI alignment methods relying on static human feedback signals.

anc.t: The Alignment Target Problem: Divergent Moral Judgments of Humans, AI Systems, and Their Designers
anc.sig: Demonstrates empirically that moral judgments diverge across humans, AI systems, and their designers, undermining the assumption that human behavior should be the alignment benchmark for AI decision-making.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: The Dark Side of Function Calling: Pathways to Jailbreaking Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that function calling mechanisms in LLMs enable new jailbreak attack vectors, revealing a critical security vulnerability in tool-use interfaces that bypasses standard safety guardrails.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Different Paths to Harmful Compliance: Behavioral Side Effects and Mechanistic Divergence Across LLM Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that different jailbreaking methods produce models with distinct internal failure modes and behavioral profiles, revealing mechanistic divergence where RLVR-based jailbreaks preserve harm recognition while remaining compliant…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: LLM-SAP: Large Language Models Situational Awareness Based Planning
anc.sig: Proposes integrating situational awareness mechanisms into LLM planning systems, enabling models to dynamically assess environmental state and adapt planning strategies accordingly.

anc.t: Robotic Fire Risk Detection based on Dynamic Knowledge Graph Reasoning: An LLM-Driven Approach with Graph Chain-of-Thought
anc.sig: Demonstrates integrating LLMs with dynamic knowledge graphs and chain-of-thought reasoning for real-time fire risk detection in robotic systems, enabling interpretable multi-modal hazard assessment.

anc.t: Extracting Disaster Impacts and Impact Related Locations in Social Media Posts Using Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM-based framework for extracting disaster impacts and affected locations from social media, establishing methodology for automated disaster information extraction from unstructured text.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: PRISM: Robust VLM Alignment with Principled Reasoning for Integrated Safety in Multimodality
anc.sig: Proposes principled framework for aligning vision-language models through integrated safety mechanisms, enabling robust multimodal reasoning with explicit safety guarantees across vision and language modalities.

anc.t: SafeProtein: Red-Teaming Framework and Benchmark for Protein Foundation Models
anc.sig: Establishes systematic red-teaming methodology and benchmark suite for evaluating adversarial robustness and safety vulnerabilities in protein foundation models across multiple attack vectors.

anc.t: Can Molecular Foundation Models Know What They Don't Know? A Simple Remedy with Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that molecular foundation models suffer from overconfidence on out-of-distribution predictions and proposes preference optimization as a practical method to improve uncertainty calibration in molecular property predictions.

anc.t: MolChord: Structure-Sequence Alignment for Protein-Guided Drug Design
anc.sig: Proposes joint alignment of molecular structures and sequences using cross-modal attention to guide generative drug design conditioned on protein targets.

anc.t: Interpreting GFlowNets for Drug Discovery: Extracting Actionable Insights for Medicinal Chemistry
anc.sig: Demonstrates interpretability methods for GFlowNets in molecular generation, extracting chemical insights about learned fragment preferences and synthesis feasibility for practical drug design.

anc.t: Unveiling Latent Knowledge in Chemistry Language Models through Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable chemical knowledge representations from pretrained language models, enabling mechanistic understanding of learned molecular representations.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Using Human Feedback to Fine-tune Diffusion Models without Any Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates direct human preference optimization for diffusion models via pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for intermediate reward model training.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment of Diffusion Models with Direct Noise Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces direct noise optimization during diffusion inference as a method to steer model outputs toward desired outcomes without requiring model retraining or fine-tuning.

anc.t: Bridging Model-Based Optimization and Generative Modeling via Conservative Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates fine-tuning diffusion models with conservative objective to enable design optimization, connecting generative modeling to reward-guided sampling without retraining.

anc.t: Steering Masked Discrete Diffusion Models via Discrete Denoising Posterior Prediction
anc.sig: Discrete denoising posterior prediction method enabling classifier-free guidance for masked discrete diffusion models without requiring paired conditioning data or additional training.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Internalizing Safety Understanding in Large Reasoning Models via Verification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that training large reasoning models on safety verification tasks—critiquing their own outputs rather than detecting malicious inputs—internalizes safety understanding and improves robustness against adversarial jailbreaks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Verbosity Bias in Preference Labeling by Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM preference judgments systematically favor longer, more verbose outputs regardless of quality, establishing a critical measurement bias for evaluating language model outputs.

anc.t: From Lists to Emojis: How Format Bias Affects Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that model alignment outcomes vary significantly based on output format (lists vs. emojis), revealing overlooked format bias that impacts alignment evaluation and benchmarking methodologies.

anc.t: Post-hoc Reward Calibration: A Case Study on Length Bias
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models can exhibit systematic biases like length preference independent of quality, and proposes post-hoc calibration methods to correct such biases without retraining.

anc.t: Reward Models are Metrics in a Trench Coat
anc.sig: Demonstrates reward models function as learned metrics rather than true preferences, enabling direct metric-based optimization as an alternative to conventional RLHF approaches.

anc.t: When Errors Can Be Beneficial: A Categorization of Imperfect Rewards for Policy Gradient
anc.sig: Theoretically characterizes which reward errors harm vs. benefit policy gradient optimization, showing some imperfect rewards prevent mediocre-reward stalling and proposing error-aware evaluation metrics for RLHF.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: Forewarned is Forearmed: Pre-Synthesizing Jailbreak-like Instructions to Enhance LLM Safety Guardrail to Potential Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial pre-training method where LLMs are exposed to synthesized jailbreak prompts during safety fine-tuning to improve robustness against novel attacks.

anc.t: Beyond Surface Alignment: Rebuilding LLMs Safety Mechanism via Probabilistically Ablating Refusal Direction
anc.sig: Identifies refusal directions in LLM latent spaces and proposes probabilistic ablation to rebuild safety mechanisms, enabling more targeted understanding of how language models learn to refuse harmful requests.

anc.t: DiffuGuard: How Intrinsic Safety is Lost and Found in Diffusion Large Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies how safety mechanisms in diffusion-based LLMs become vulnerable during generation and proposes methods to preserve model safety properties throughout the decoding process.

anc.t: Toward Safer Diffusion Language Models: Discovery and Mitigation of Priming Vulnerability
anc.sig: Identified and quantified priming vulnerability in diffusion language models where adversarial prefixes manipulate output distributions, proposing mitigation strategies through targeted fine-tuning and decoding modifications.

anc.t: The Safety-Aware Denoiser for Text Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Proposes Safety-Aware Denoiser, an inference-time guidance framework that steers text diffusion denoising trajectories toward provably safe regions without retraining, enabling flexible safety constraints for diffusion-based generation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.t: Decoding Alignment: A Critical Survey of LLM Development Initiatives through Value-setting and Data-centric Lens
anc.sig: Systematically categorizes LLM alignment approaches via value-setting mechanisms and data-centric strategies, establishing a taxonomy for understanding how developers embed human values into language models.

anc.t: Dynamic Alignment for Collective Agency: Toward a Scalable Self-Improving Framework for Open-Ended LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes dynamic alignment framework enabling LLMs to collectively improve their own values and behaviors through continuous self-improvement without fixed external constraints or human intervention.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Adversarial Tuning: Defending Against Jailbreak Attacks for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial fine-tuning method to defend LLMs against jailbreak attacks by training with adversarial examples, establishing baseline defense approach for robustness.

anc.t: GASP: Efficient Black-Box Generation of Adversarial Suffixes for Jailbreaking LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrated efficient black-box method for generating adversarial suffixes that jailbreak LLMs through gradient-free optimization, enabling attacks without model access or gradients.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: Red-Bandit: Test-Time Adaptation for LLM Red-Teaming via Bandit-Guided LoRA Experts
anc.sig: Proposes bandit-guided selection among LoRA experts to dynamically adapt LLM red-teaming prompts at test-time, improving attack success rates without retraining base models.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: LLM Defenses Are Not Robust to Multi-Turn Human Jailbreaks Yet
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.t: Jailbreak Mimicry: Automated Discovery of Narrative-Based Jailbreaks for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Position: AI Security Policy Should Target Systems, Not Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that coordinated LLM agent swarms can efficiently discover safety bypasses and software vulnerabilities at scale using commodity hardware, establishing that capability control requires system-level defenses beyond model…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Can sparse autoencoders make sense of gene expression latent variable models?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can interpretably decompose gene expression data from latent variable models, revealing biologically meaningful factors without requiring dimensionality reduction assumptions.

anc.t: InterPLM: Discovering Interpretable Features in Protein Language Models via Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable, mechanistically meaningful features from protein language model representations, enabling reverse-engineering of learned biological concepts.

anc.t: ProtSAE: Disentangling and Interpreting Protein Language Models via Semantically-Guided Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders with semantic guidance can disentangle protein language model representations into interpretable biological features, enabling mechanistic analysis of learned biochemical concepts.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Data-Centric Human Preference with Rationales for Direct Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Methods and datasets for training language models via direct preference alignment using human-annotated rationales, improving LLM behavior beyond standard RLHF through grounded preference signals.

anc.t: Towards Data-Centric RLHF: Simple Metrics for Preference Dataset Comparison
anc.sig: Introduces simple quantitative metrics for comparing preference datasets in RLHF, enabling data-centric analysis to improve reinforcement learning from human feedback without requiring model retraining.

anc.t: LRHP: Learning Representations for Human Preferences via Preference Pairs
anc.sig: Proposes learning latent representations of human preferences from pairwise comparisons, enabling preference-based reward modeling for RLHF without explicit reward function specification.

anc.t: Adaptive Margin RLHF via Preference over Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive margin framework for RLHF that learns preference strength from pairwise comparisons, enabling dynamic reward scaling based on preference confidence during training.

anc.t: APLOT: Robust Reward Modeling via Adaptive Preference Learning with Optimal Transport
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive preference learning with optimal transport for robust reward modeling, enabling better alignment between human preferences and model objectives under distribution shift.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anc.t: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
anc.sig: Identified alignment tampering vulnerability where LLMs exploit RLHF's reliance on their own outputs and ambiguous preference labels to amplify undesired biases during training.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: M-RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models in Multilingual Settings
anc.sig: Establishes M-RewardBench, a multilingual benchmark for evaluating reward models across 14 languages, enabling assessment of their cross-lingual generalization and revealing language-specific performance disparities.

anc.t: Lost in Translation: Do LVLM Judges Generalize Across Languages?
anc.sig: Introduced MM-JudgeBench, the first large-scale multilingual multimodal benchmark with 60K+ preference pairs across 25 languages, revealing critical language generalization gaps in LVLM reward models and establishing methodology for…

anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: Multilingual Jailbreak Challenges in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be jailbroken more easily in non-English languages, establishing a systematic vulnerability across multilingual models that subsequent work addresses through safety alignment.

anc.t: Benchmarking LLM Guardrails in Handling Multilingual Toxicity
anc.sig: Establishes benchmarks and evaluation methodology for assessing how well large language model guardrails detect and mitigate toxic content across multiple languages beyond English.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Attacks vs. Content Safety Filters: How Far Are We in the LLM Safety Arms Race?
anc.sig: Empirical analysis of jailbreak attack methods against content safety filters, establishing taxonomy of evasion techniques and benchmarking defense robustness in large language models.

anc.t: An Empirical Study of Multi-Generation Sampling for Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multi-generation sampling reveals jailbreak vulnerabilities missed by single-output evaluation, establishing sampling budget as critical for realistic LLM safety assessment.

anc.t: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced multilingual back-translation augmentation and relative-distance constraints to learn language-insensitive jailbreak representations, enabling cross-lingual generalization of safety defenses across 11 languages.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Jailbreak Detection via Semantic Codebooks
anc.sig: Demonstrated training-free cross-lingual jailbreak detection using semantic codebooks—English prompt embeddings detect multilingual attacks across LLMs without language-specific adaptation.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Learning to summarize from human feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF paradigm for training language models to generate human-preferred summaries using reinforcement learning with learned reward models from human feedback.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: The Multilingual Alignment Prism: Aligning Global and Local Preferences to Reduce Harm
anc.sig: Proposes a method to align language models across multiple languages by balancing global (universal) and local (culture-specific) preference preferences, enabling safer multilingual AI systems.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: SAGE: An Agentic Explainer Framework for Interpreting SAE Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces an agentic framework using tool use and multi-turn interaction to automatically discover and explain sparse autoencoder features in language models through systematic exploration.

anc.t: Automated Interpretability and Feature Discovery in Language Models with Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous multiagent loops for mechanistic interpretability that combine iterative hypothesis testing with k-NN feature discovery, producing more falsifiable LLM neuron explanations than one-shot baselines.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: TrustGPT: A Benchmark for Trustworthy and Responsible Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness, fairness, and safety in large language models across multiple dimensions and use cases.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Behavioral Fingerprinting of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic methods to characterize LLM behavior patterns across diverse tasks, establishing metrics and evaluation protocols that enable comparison and reproducibility across different models and deployments.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: ASTRA: An Automated Framework for Strategy Discovery, Retrieval, and Evolution for Jailbreaking LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced automated framework for discovering and evolving adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic jailbreak strategy generation and retrieval at scale.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Permutative Preference Alignment from Listwise Ranking of Human Judgments
anc.sig: Introduces a listwise ranking approach to preference alignment that captures complex, non-transitive human judgment patterns through permutation-based modeling rather than pairwise comparisons.

anc.t: Beyond Pairwise: Empowering LLM Alignment With Ranked Choice Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes ranked choice modeling for LLM alignment, enabling preference learning from multiple ranked outputs instead of binary pairwise comparisons, improving efficiency and capturing richer preference structures.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: SharedRep-RLHF: A Shared Representation Approach to RLHF with Diverse Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrates that a single shared representation can effectively capture diverse human preferences in RLHF by combining preference diversity modeling with unified policy training, improving scalability over separate reward models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: Skill-RM: Unifying Heterogeneous Evaluation Criteria via Agent Skill
anc.sig: Unified heterogeneous reward evaluation criteria through structured agent-based skill execution, enabling dynamic evidence aggregation for consistent multi-task reward modeling in LLM post-training.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Training a General Purpose Automated Red Teaming Model
anc.sig: Developed a generalizable automated red teaming pipeline enabling small LLMs to generate adversarial attacks without pre-existing evaluators, transferable to unseen attack objectives beyond training distribution.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: SWE-RM: Execution-free Feedback For Software Engineering Agents
anc.sig: Proposes execution-free reward modeling for evaluating software engineering agent trajectories using learned classifiers instead of test execution, enabling scalable feedback without computational overhead.

anc.t: Beyond Static Snapshots: A Grounded Evaluation Framework for Language Models at the Agentic Frontier
anc.sig: Proposes Grounded Continuous Evaluation framework addressing distributional, temporal, scope, and process validity failures in LLM evaluation; demonstrates deterministic verifiers eliminate reward hacking by construction while reducing…

anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders' effectiveness for mechanistically removing interpretable features from neural networks while preserving model performance, enabling controlled concept erasure.

anc.t: Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity
anc.sig: Introduces methods to quantify how sensitive sparse autoencoder features are to input perturbations, enabling systematic evaluation of feature robustness and interpretability in learned representations.

anc.t: A Mechanistic Investigation of Supervised Fine Tuning
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders reveal significant latent feature divergence during fine-tuning despite preserved cosine similarity, enabling mechanistic analysis of which semantic features change across layers.

anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Internalizing Safety Understanding in Large Reasoning Models via Verification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that training large reasoning models on safety verification tasks—critiquing their own outputs rather than detecting malicious inputs—internalizes safety understanding and improves robustness against adversarial jailbreaks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Fine-Tuning Jailbreaks under Highly Constrained Black-Box Settings: A Three-Pronged Approach
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be jailbroken through fine-tuning even under severe black-box constraints, establishing practical attack vectors for restricted model access scenarios.

anc.t: Attack via Overfitting: 10-shot Benign Fine-tuning to Jailbreak LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that large language models can be jailbroken through benign fine-tuning with only 10 examples, establishing overfitting as a practical attack vector against safety mechanisms in LLMs.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models with Human Preferences via a Bayesian Approach
anc.sig: Proposes a Bayesian framework for aligning language model outputs with human preferences, establishing principled probabilistic methods for preference learning that enable subsequent RLHF and preference optimization techniques.

anc.t: Improving Instruction Following in Language Models through Proxy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
anc.sig: Introduces proxy-based uncertainty estimation method to improve instruction-following reliability in language models, enabling better confidence calibration for model outputs.

anc.t: Uncertainty-aware Reward Model: Teaching Reward Models to Know What is Unknown
anc.sig: Introduced methods for reward models to quantify uncertainty in their predictions, enabling better detection of out-of-distribution inputs and more reliable alignment feedback for language models.

anc.t: Towards Reliable Alignment: Uncertainty-aware RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces uncertainty quantification into RLHF training to improve model alignment reliability by identifying and mitigating unreliable reward signal regions during reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Reward Discounting for Mitigating Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Principle-Driven Self-Alignment of Language Models from Scratch with Minimal Human Supervision
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be aligned to human values by training them to follow explicit principles without extensive human feedback, reducing supervision requirements for safe AI development.

anc.t: Towards Dialogues for Joint Human-AI Reasoning and Value Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes dialogue-based framework enabling humans and AI systems to jointly reason through complex problems while negotiating values, establishing methods for aligning AI behavior through interactive discussion rather than static training.

anc.t: On scalable oversight with weak LLMs judging strong LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that smaller language models can reliably evaluate larger models' outputs when trained on curated examples, enabling practical oversight of advanced AI systems without requiring equally capable supervisors.

anc.t: Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Understanding Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anc.t: Peacemaker or Troublemaker: How Sycophancy Shapes Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies sycophancy as a destabilizing factor in multi-agent debate systems, showing how agents gaming agreement rewards undermines collective reasoning quality and decision-making.

anc.t: Deliberative Dynamics and Value Alignment in LLM Debates
anc.sig: Proposes a debate framework where LLMs argue opposing positions to improve reasoning transparency and alignment, enabling external judges to identify better arguments through structured deliberation rather than raw model outputs.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: SAVE: Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement for Mitigating Object Hallucination
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders can identify and suppress hallucinated visual features in vision-language models, providing a mechanistic approach to reducing object hallucination without retraining.

anc.t: Look Closer! An Adversarial Parametric Editing Framework for Hallucination Mitigation in VLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial parametric editing to directly modify visual language model weights, reducing hallucinations by targeting problematic attention patterns and internal representations.

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.
