anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes preference-encoding attention heads in LLMs via causal analysis, enabling training-free interpretable personalization through differential steering of logit distributions.

anc.t: Anatomy of Post-Training: Using Interpretability to Characterize Data and Shape the Learning Signal
anc.sig: Introduced interpretability-based framework for diagnosing and intervening on latent concepts in preference datasets before post-training, enabling fine-grained control over which behaviors models learn.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: FlexLLM: Exploring LLM Customization for Moving Target Defense on Black-Box LLMs Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes dynamic adversarial defense strategy for LLMs using moving-target concept, randomly customizing models to prevent attackers from converging to universal jailbreak patterns.

anc.t: Dynamic Target Attack
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial attack strategy where targets shift during optimization, increasing robustness testing difficulty and revealing new vulnerability modes in neural networks.

anc.t: Untargeted Jailbreak Attack
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be systematically compromised through adversarial prompts without task-specific crafting, establishing foundational methods for detecting model vulnerabilities at scale.

anc.t: On the Hardness of Junking LLMs
anc.sig: First empirical investigation establishing that finding natural backdoors in LLMs through token optimization is computationally hard, establishing search complexity as a fundamental barrier distinct from structured prompt jailbreaks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Models of human preference for learning reward functions
anc.sig: Formalized methods for learning reward functions from human preferences through pairwise comparisons, establishing the foundation for preference-based inverse reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: FLAME: Factuality-Aware Alignment for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces fine-tuning methodology to align LLMs toward factuality by learning from human feedback on factual accuracy rather than general helpfulness.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models via Fine-grained Supervision
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-grained, token-level supervision during training improves LLM alignment by providing more detailed feedback than traditional coarse-grained reward-based approaches.

anc.t: GRADE: Replacing Policy Gradients with Backpropagation for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes replacing policy gradient methods with direct backpropagation through LLM loss for alignment, offering a computationally efficient alternative to traditional reinforcement learning approaches.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Insights into Alignment: Evaluating DPO and its Variants Across Multiple Tasks
anc.sig: Systematically evaluates Direct Preference Optimization and variants across diverse tasks, identifying effective design choices and limitations that inform alignment algorithm selection and development.

anc.t: Triple Preference Optimization: Achieving Better Alignment using a Single Step Optimization
anc.sig: Established single-step preference optimization method using three preference pairs that improves alignment quality while reducing computational cost compared to iterative approaches.

anc.t: 3D-Properties: Identifying Challenges in DPO and Charting a Path Forward
anc.sig: Identifies three key failure modes in Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and proposes fixes: preference ambiguity, capacity constraints, and degenerate solutions, enabling more robust alignment methods.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations

anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anc.t: The Role of Risk Modeling in Advanced AI Risk Management
anc.sig: Established frameworks for quantifying and modeling AI system risks, enabling systematic risk assessment methodologies that subsequent AI safety research builds upon.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreaking in the Haystack
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial prompts can evade safety filters by hiding malicious requests within long contexts, establishing the haystack attack vector for large language models.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Safety without alignment
anc.sig: Proposed achieving AI safety through structural constraints and capability limitations rather than behavioral alignment, enabling safe systems even without value alignment.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Establishes techniques for maintaining safety against deceptive AI systems, including methods to detect hidden backdoors using adversarial model proxies.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Proposes control techniques enabling AI systems to remain safe and controllable even when internal components are adversarially compromised or deliberately subverted by attackers.

anc.t: Adaptive Deployment of Untrusted LLMs Reduces Distributed Threats
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive runtime strategies for selectively deploying untrusted language models across distributed systems while mitigating coordinated adversarial threats through dynamic trust-based partitioning.

anc.t: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adaptive adversarial attacks can circumvent monitor-based AI control mechanisms, establishing the need for robust defense-aware verification of safety protocols.

anc.t: Evaluating Control Protocols for Untrusted AI Agents
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates control protocol failures against deceptive AI agents and establishes methodology for evaluating alignment techniques under adversarial conditions.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Learning to Unlearn for Robust Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes a regularization technique during machine unlearning that improves robustness against adversarial attacks by learning to unlearn patterns while maintaining model utility on retained data.

anc.t: CUFG: Curriculum Unlearning Guided by the Forgetting Gradient
anc.sig: Proposes curriculum unlearning by using forgetting gradients to guide systematic removal of undesired knowledge from neural networks, enabling controlled and efficient data forgetting.

anc.t: Stable Forgetting: Bounded Parameter-Efficient Unlearning in Foundation Models
anc.sig: Proposes parameter-efficient unlearning methods with bounded model degradation, enabling foundation models to selectively forget data while maintaining performance on retained information.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: The Multilingual Alignment Prism: Aligning Global and Local Preferences to Reduce Harm
anc.sig: Proposes a method to align language models across multiple languages by balancing global (universal) and local (culture-specific) preference preferences, enabling safer multilingual AI systems.

anc.t: Multilingual Refusal Alignment for Safer Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that single-language refusal alignment fails cross-lingually and proposes RefusEU multilingual dataset with DPO experiments showing that multilingual safety training improves cross-lingual safety without degrading general…

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
anc.sig: Introduces corrective intervention methods to mitigate unsafe behaviors and reasoning errors in large language models during inference, enabling safer deployment of reasoning systems.

anc.t: When Models Outthink Their Safety: Unveiling and Mitigating Self-Jailbreak in Large Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Identifies self-jailbreak vulnerability where reasoning models bypass safety constraints through extended internal reasoning, and proposes mitigation strategies applicable to advanced reasoning systems.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety
anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anc.t: The Art of the Jailbreak: Formulating Jailbreak Attacks for LLM Security Beyond Binary Scoring
anc.sig: Established systematic jailbreak dataset with 114K adversarial prompts categorized by cybersecurity threat type and ranked by effectiveness, plus automated generation pipeline for synthesizing category-aware jailbreak prompts via…

anc.t: Adversarial Reframing: A Framework for Targeted Generation in Language Models
anc.sig: Formulates adversarial prompt discovery as nonconvex optimization with iterative multi-model coordination, achieving efficient jailbreaking of safety-aligned LLMs while establishing a framework for systematic safety evaluation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders' effectiveness for mechanistically removing interpretable features from neural networks while preserving model performance, enabling controlled concept erasure.

anc.t: SAEmnesia: Erasing Concepts in Diffusion Models with Supervised Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates how sparse autoencoders can identify and surgically remove unwanted concepts from diffusion models by manipulating activations, enabling targeted model editing without retraining.

anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: HAMSA: Hijacking Aligned Compact Models via Stealthy Automation
anc.sig: Demonstrates automated jailbreak attacks against aligned compact language models through stealthy prompt optimization, establishing methods for adversarial robustness evaluation of smaller models.

anc.t: ForgeDAN: An Evolutionary Framework for Jailbreaking Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates automated evolutionary algorithm approach to discover adversarial prompts that bypass LLM safety alignments through iterative mutation and selection.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: Value FULCRA: Mapping Large Language Models to the Multidimensional Spectrum of Basic Human Values
anc.sig: Developed methodology to map LLM outputs onto multidimensional spectrum of human values, enabling quantitative assessment of value alignment across diverse cultural and ethical dimensions.

anc.t: ValueCompass: A Framework for Measuring Contextual Value Alignment Between Human and LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes ValueCompass, a framework quantifying alignment between human and LLM values across diverse contextual scenarios using structured measurement methodology.

anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness, fairness, and safety in large language models across multiple dimensions and use cases.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: SGuard-v1: Safety Guardrail for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a safety guardrail system using semantic similarity and rule-based filtering to detect and block harmful outputs from large language models.

anc.t: AprielGuard
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism against adversarial examples in neural networks through input transformation and detection methods during inference.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: ImageReward: Learning and Evaluating Human Preferences for Text-to-Image Generation
anc.sig: Proposed ImageReward, a learned reward model trained on human preference annotations to evaluate text-to-image generation quality, enabling automatic assessment without explicit metrics.

anc.t: DLPO: Diffusion Model Loss-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Fine-Tuning Text-to-Speech Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Integrates diffusion model loss as a reward signal for RL-based fine-tuning of text-to-speech systems, enabling direct optimization of generation quality beyond standard supervised objectives.

anc.t: Enhancing Zero-shot Text-to-Speech Synthesis with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) improves zero-shot text-to-speech synthesis quality by optimizing for speaker similarity and naturalness without requiring paired training data.

anc.t: SpeechJudge: Towards Human-Level Judgment for Speech Naturalness
anc.sig: Establishes automated evaluation metrics for speech naturalness that correlate with human judgments, enabling reliable assessment of synthesized speech quality without manual annotation.

anc.t: Audio MultiChallenge: A Multi-Turn Evaluation of Spoken Dialogue Systems on Natural Human Interaction
anc.sig: Introduces multi-turn evaluation framework for spoken dialogue systems capturing natural human interaction patterns beyond single-turn exchanges.

anc.t: Towards Human-Like Interactive Speech Recognition With Agentic Correction and Semantic Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduces interactive ASR with agent-driven semantic correction and S²ER metric, replacing single-pass paradigm with multi-turn refinement to preserve meaning-critical intent beyond token-level errors.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Self-Augmented Preference Optimization: Off-Policy Paradigms for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces self-augmented preference optimization framework enabling off-policy learning from model-generated data, eliminating need for pre-collected preference datasets in language model alignment.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: SAIL: Self-Improving Efficient Online Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced self-improving online alignment where LLMs iteratively refine their own responses using weak supervision and preference signals without requiring external human annotation.

anc.t: SALSA: Soup-based Alignment Learning for Stronger Adaptation in RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes soup-based alignment learning that interpolates multiple fine-tuned model weights to improve RLHF robustness and adaptation without additional inference cost.

anc.t: Reward Model Routing in Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes routing different queries to specialized reward models based on query characteristics, improving alignment efficiency and accuracy by leveraging model-specific strengths rather than using single monolithic reward models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: When the Agent Is the Adversary: Architectural Requirements for Agentic AI Containment After the April 2026 Frontier Model Escape
anc.sig: Establishes architectural requirements for AI containment against adversarial agents, including layered privilege separation and semantic intent verification, demonstrating failures of alignment-only approaches when agents themselves…

anc.t: Towards trustworthy agentic AI: a comprehensive survey of safety, robustness, privacy, and system security
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for evaluating and mitigating safety, robustness, privacy, and security risks across agentic AI system workflows, with unified metrics and stage-targeted mitigation strategies for trustworthy deployment.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: Aligning Large Multimodal Models with Factually Augmented RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces factually-augmented RLHF approach for aligning multimodal LLMs, enabling models to balance helpfulness with factual accuracy through reward modeling with external knowledge integration.

anc.t: GeoVLMath: Enhancing Geometry Reasoning in Vision-Language Models via Cross-Modal Reward for Auxiliary Line Creation
anc.sig: Introduces cross-modal reward mechanism to train vision-language models to generate auxiliary lines for geometry problem-solving, enabling improved spatial reasoning and geometric figure understanding.

anc.t: Talking Points: Describing and Localizing Pixels
anc.sig: Introduced dense pixel-level language grounding by learning to generate and localize natural language descriptions for arbitrary image regions without requiring explicit region annotations.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: SLiC-HF: Sequence Likelihood Calibration with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces calibration method for sequence-level likelihood using human feedback, enabling better alignment of model confidence with actual quality in text generation tasks.

anc.t: Just Ask for Calibration: Strategies for Eliciting Calibrated Confidence Scores from Language Models Fine-Tuned with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrates that explicit calibration prompts can elicit more reliable confidence estimates from RLHF-trained language models without additional fine-tuning, enabling better uncertainty quantification in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Enhancing Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs with Expectation of Aggregated Internal Belief
anc.sig: Proposes aggregating internal neural activations across layers to estimate LLM uncertainty, enabling better calibration without retraining or access to logits.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Token-Level Inference-Time Alignment for Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces token-level inference-time alignment method enabling vision-language models to dynamically adjust outputs per token without retraining, improving response quality and safety.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Exploratory Direct Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced token-level epistemic uncertainty estimation to identify and prioritize vision-related hallucinations in DPO-based MLLM alignment, enabling targeted learning on hard-to-perceive visual details rather than self-biased sensitivity…

anc.t: Reducing Hallucination in Vision-Language Models via Stage-wise Preference Optimization under Distribution Shift
anc.sig: Introduced stage-wise preference optimization framework using minimally-perturbed hallucinated negatives near failure boundaries to train VLMs for reduced visual hallucination via Direct Preference Optimization.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: SafeBehavior: Simulating Human-Like Multistage Reasoning to Mitigate Jailbreak Attacks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes multistage reasoning approach simulating human deliberation patterns to detect and mitigate jailbreak attacks on LLMs through behavioral verification.

anc.t: Reasoning-targeted Jailbreak Attacks on Large Reasoning Models via Semantic Triggers and Psychological Framing
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large reasoning models' intermediate reasoning steps can be compromised independently from final answers using semantic triggers and psychological framing, establishing a new attack surface beyond output safety.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.sig: Proposes multistage reasoning approach simulating human deliberation patterns to detect and mitigate jailbreak attacks on LLMs through behavioral verification.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Attacks vs. Content Safety Filters: How Far Are We in the LLM Safety Arms Race?
anc.sig: Empirical analysis of jailbreak attack methods against content safety filters, establishing taxonomy of evasion techniques and benchmarking defense robustness in large language models.

anc.t: An Empirical Study of Multi-Generation Sampling for Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multi-generation sampling reveals jailbreak vulnerabilities missed by single-output evaluation, establishing sampling budget as critical for realistic LLM safety assessment.

anc.t: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced multilingual back-translation augmentation and relative-distance constraints to learn language-insensitive jailbreak representations, enabling cross-lingual generalization of safety defenses across 11 languages.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces NeuroTaint, a taint tracking framework for LLM agents that extends traditional taint analysis to capture semantic influence, probabilistic reasoning, and memory persistence in autonomous agent execution flows.

anc.t: When Agents Overtrust Environmental Evidence: An Extensible Agentic Framework for Benchmarking Evidence-Grounding Defects in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces EnvTrustBench framework and formally defines evidence-grounding defects—when agents fail to verify environmental observations against current state before acting—establishing a benchmark for evaluating agent reliability under…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: MedHallBench: A New Benchmark for Assessing Hallucination in Medical Large Language Models
anc.sig: Created first comprehensive benchmark (MedHallBench) for systematically evaluating hallucinations in medical LLMs across factual errors, reasoning failures, and knowledge gaps.

anc.t: Med-RewardBench: Benchmarking Reward Models and Judges for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes first medical-specific benchmark for evaluating reward models and judges in multimodal medical LLMs, enabling systematic assessment of alignment quality in healthcare contexts.

anc.t: MedBench v4: A Robust and Scalable Benchmark for Evaluating Chinese Medical Language Models, Multimodal Models, and Intelligent Agents
anc.sig: Established comprehensive evaluation framework and dataset for assessing Chinese medical language models, multimodal models, and agents across clinical reasoning and domain-specific tasks.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Extends DPO to token-level granularity, enabling fine-grained preference learning within sequences rather than only at sequence level.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Reward-Weighted Sampling: Enhancing Non-Autoregressive Characteristics in Masked Diffusion LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces reward-weighted sampling for masked diffusion language models, enabling non-autoregressive generation with improved quality by conditioning on reward signals during sampling.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Prompting Large Language Models for Counterfactual Generation: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can generate high-quality counterfactuals through prompting strategies, establishing prompting as a practical alternative to annotation for creating contrastive training data.

anc.t: Understanding Catastrophic Forgetting in Language Models via Implicit Inference
anc.sig: Reveals catastrophic forgetting in language models as a consequence of implicit inference dynamics, providing theoretical framework for understanding and mitigating knowledge degradation during sequential task learning.

anc.t: InstructionCP: A fast approach to transfer Large Language Models into target language
anc.sig: Proposes instruction-following capability transfer to target languages via translated instruction datasets, enabling efficient LLM multilingual adaptation without full retraining.

anc.t: Extroversion or Introversion? Controlling The Personality of Your Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to systematically control personality expression in LLMs through prompting and fine-tuning, enabling reproducible personality-aligned model variants for diverse applications.

anc.t: The Personality Illusion: Revealing Dissociation Between Self-Reports & Behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM self-reported personality traits systematically diverge from actual behavioral outputs, establishing that LLMs lack stable personalities and questioning validity of personality assessment methods.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: The Right to be Forgotten in Federated Learning: An Efficient Realization with Rapid Retraining
anc.sig: Proposes efficient machine unlearning in federated learning via rapid model retraining without full reconstruction, enabling privacy-compliant deletion requests at scale.

anc.t: Get Rid Of Your Trail: Remotely Erasing Backdoors in Federated Learning
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism enabling central servers to remotely remove backdoor triggers from compromised federated learning models without retraining, establishing methods for post-hoc backdoor mitigation in distributed settings.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders' effectiveness for mechanistically removing interpretable features from neural networks while preserving model performance, enabling controlled concept erasure.

anc.t: Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity
anc.sig: Introduces methods to quantify how sensitive sparse autoencoder features are to input perturbations, enabling systematic evaluation of feature robustness and interpretability in learned representations.

anc.t: A Mechanistic Investigation of Supervised Fine Tuning
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders reveal significant latent feature divergence during fine-tuning despite preserved cosine similarity, enabling mechanistic analysis of which semantic features change across layers.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anc.t: Quantifying LLM Safety Degradation Under Repeated Attacks Using Survival Analysis
anc.sig: Applies survival analysis to LLM safety evaluation, modeling time-to-jailbreak as temporal outcome to reveal vulnerability degradation patterns under repeated attacks beyond binary success metrics.

anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: Improving Reward Models with Synthetic Critiques
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic critiques generated by language models improve reward model training, enabling better alignment signal extraction without requiring expensive human annotations.

anc.t: Self-Generated Critiques Boost Reward Modeling for Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can generate their own critiques to improve reward model training, reducing reliance on external annotators while enhancing alignment feedback quality.

anc.t: Towards Faithful and Controllable Personalization via Critique-Post-Edit Reinforcement Learning
