anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Internalizing Safety Understanding in Large Reasoning Models via Verification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that training large reasoning models on safety verification tasks—critiquing their own outputs rather than detecting malicious inputs—internalizes safety understanding and improves robustness against adversarial jailbreaks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just A Few Attention Heads
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in language models is distributed across many attention heads rather than localized, complicating efforts to surgically remove or modify safety mechanisms.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: Data-Centric Human Preference with Rationales for Direct Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Methods and datasets for training language models via direct preference alignment using human-annotated rationales, improving LLM behavior beyond standard RLHF through grounded preference signals.

anc.t: Towards Data-Centric RLHF: Simple Metrics for Preference Dataset Comparison
anc.sig: Introduces simple quantitative metrics for comparing preference datasets in RLHF, enabling data-centric analysis to improve reinforcement learning from human feedback without requiring model retraining.

anc.t: LRHP: Learning Representations for Human Preferences via Preference Pairs
anc.sig: Proposes learning latent representations of human preferences from pairwise comparisons, enabling preference-based reward modeling for RLHF without explicit reward function specification.

anc.t: Adaptive Margin RLHF via Preference over Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive margin framework for RLHF that learns preference strength from pairwise comparisons, enabling dynamic reward scaling based on preference confidence during training.

anc.t: APLOT: Robust Reward Modeling via Adaptive Preference Learning with Optimal Transport
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive preference learning with optimal transport for robust reward modeling, enabling better alignment between human preferences and model objectives under distribution shift.

anc.t: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes in-context reward adaptation using transformer architecture with human response time signals to dynamically infer diverse reward structures from preference demonstrations without retraining.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Token-Level Inference-Time Alignment for Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces token-level inference-time alignment method enabling vision-language models to dynamically adjust outputs per token without retraining, improving response quality and safety.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Exploratory Direct Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced token-level epistemic uncertainty estimation to identify and prioritize vision-related hallucinations in DPO-based MLLM alignment, enabling targeted learning on hard-to-perceive visual details rather than self-biased sensitivity…

anc.t: Language Bias in LVLMs: From In-Depth Analysis to Simple and Effective Mitigation
anc.sig: Identified modality misalignment as root cause of language bias in LVLMs and proposed regularization-based mitigation methods (LBR, LBP) for instruction tuning and preference optimization.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Robustifying Safety-Aligned Large Language Models through Clean Data Curation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that curating high-quality, safety-aligned training data significantly improves LLM robustness against adversarial attacks and jailbreaks without compromising helpfulness.

anc.t: Evaluation of LLM Vulnerabilities to Being Misused for Personalized Disinformation Generation
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates that LLMs can be misused to generate personalized disinformation at scale, establishing evaluation metrics and vulnerabilities for future defenses.

anc.t: A Multilingual, Large-Scale Study of the Interplay between LLM Safeguards, Personalisation, and Disinformation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that personalized LLMs increase disinformation susceptibility across languages, revealing critical vulnerabilities in safety mechanisms when user preferences override content guardrails.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: RKLD: Reverse KL-Divergence-based Knowledge Distillation for Unlearning Personal Information in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates reverse KL divergence as an effective distillation method to remove memorized personal information from LLMs while preserving general capabilities.

anc.t: Towards Robust and Parameter-Efficient Knowledge Unlearning for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes parameter-efficient methods for removing harmful knowledge from LLMs while maintaining performance, addressing the critical challenge of safe model updates without full retraining.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Self-Augmented Preference Optimization: Off-Policy Paradigms for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces self-augmented preference optimization framework enabling off-policy learning from model-generated data, eliminating need for pre-collected preference datasets in language model alignment.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: Aligning Diffusion Behaviors with Q-functions for Efficient Continuous Control
anc.sig: Proposes using Q-function alignment to guide diffusion model training for continuous control, enabling sample-efficient policy learning without explicit reward specification during diffusion generation.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment Control for Diffusion Models with Reinforcement Learning Guidance
anc.sig: Enables real-time steering of diffusion model outputs toward desired behaviors via reinforcement learning rewards applied during inference, without model retraining.

anc.t: Fine-Grained GRPO for Precise Preference Alignment in Flow Models
anc.sig: Introduces fine-grained GRPO algorithm for precise preference alignment in flow-based generative models, enabling more accurate reward signal integration during training.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Interpreting Language Models Through Concept Descriptions: A Survey
anc.sig: Surveys methods for interpreting neural language models via concept descriptions, enabling extraction and attribution of human-understandable features from learned representations.

anc.t: Qwen-Scope: Turning Sparse Features into Development Tools for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders as practical development interfaces for LLM inspection and control, enabling inference-time steering and evaluation analysis without weight modification.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Using Human Feedback to Fine-tune Diffusion Models without Any Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates direct human preference optimization for diffusion models via pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for intermediate reward model training.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment of Diffusion Models with Direct Noise Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces direct noise optimization during diffusion inference as a method to steer model outputs toward desired outcomes without requiring model retraining or fine-tuning.

anc.t: MIRA: Towards Mitigating Reward Hacking in Inference-Time Alignment of T2I Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Proposes MIRA, a method to detect and mitigate reward hacking in text-to-image diffusion models by identifying when reward models exploit unintended model behaviors during inference-time alignment.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Data-dependent Exploration for Online Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes data-dependent uncertainty bonuses for online RLHF that leverage historical preference data to encourage exploration toward high-value regions without relying on difficult-to-estimate on-policy expectations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Adversarial Tuning: Defending Against Jailbreak Attacks for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial fine-tuning method to defend LLMs against jailbreak attacks by training with adversarial examples, establishing baseline defense approach for robustness.

anc.t: Adversarial Déjà Vu: Jailbreak Dictionary Learning for Stronger Generalization to Unseen Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial robustness to unseen attacks improves when training on diverse jailbreak techniques rather than standard adversarial examples, establishing dictionary learning as mechanism for generalizable defense.

anc.t: Towards Understanding the Robustness of Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders integrated into transformer residual streams at inference reduce jailbreak vulnerability up to 5x, establishing sparsity-induced representational bottlenecks as a practical defense mechanism.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.t: Aligned Training: A Parameter-Free Method to Improve Feature Quality and Stability of Sparse Autoencoders (SAE)
anc.sig: Identified alignment score degeneracy in SAEs and proposed aligned training—a parameter-free reparameterization enforcing unit inner product between encoder-decoder pairs, eliminating dead features and improving stability.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Evaluation Awareness Scales Predictably in Open-Weights Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that evaluation awareness (model sensitivity to being assessed) scales predictably with model size in open-weights LLMs, providing measurable metrics for detecting when models behave differently under evaluation conditions.

anc.t: The Evaluation Differential: When Frontier AI Models Recognise They Are Being Tested
anc.sig: Formalized the Evaluation Differential as a measurable divergence in AI model behavior between recognized-evaluation and deployment contexts, establishing that standard evaluation scores cannot detect this phenomenon and creating a…

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces concrete progress measures for understanding grokking phenomena in neural networks through mechanistic interpretability, enabling systematic study of delayed generalization in deep learning models.

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Efficient Automated Circuit Discovery in Transformers using Contextual Decomposition
anc.sig: Proposes contextual decomposition method to automatically identify sparse subgraphs (circuits) in transformers that perform specific tasks, enabling interpretable analysis of model mechanisms.

anc.t: Circuit Compositions: Exploring Modular Structures in Transformer-Based Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces circuit decomposition method to systematically identify modular computational structures within transformers, enabling analysis of how language models compose features across layers.

anc.t: Interpreting token compositionality in LLMs: A robustness analysis
anc.sig: Demonstrates through robustness analysis that LLMs compose token representations non-linearly to form meaningful emergent concepts, establishing methods for probing compositionality in transformer attention patterns.

anc.t: Mechanistic Interpretability of GPT-2: Lexical and Contextual Layers in Sentiment Analysis
anc.sig: Identified mechanistic pathways in GPT-2 where lexical features combine in early layers and contextual refinement occurs in later layers, enabling layer-wise interpretation of sentiment classification.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Behavioral Fingerprinting of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic methods to characterize LLM behavior patterns across diverse tasks, establishing metrics and evaluation protocols that enable comparison and reproducibility across different models and deployments.

anc.t: Deployment-Relevant Alignment Cannot Be Inferred from Model-Level Evaluation Alone
anc.sig: Established that model-level alignment evaluations cannot predict deployment-relevant alignment; indexed alignment evidence to four distinct levels (model, response, interaction, deployment) requiring different evaluation approaches.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: SAGE: An Agentic Explainer Framework for Interpreting SAE Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces an agentic framework using tool use and multi-turn interaction to automatically discover and explain sparse autoencoder features in language models through systematic exploration.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Understanding and Mitigating Over-refusal for Large Language Models via Safety Representation
anc.sig: Identifies over-refusal in LLMs where models refuse safe requests, proposes safety representation analysis to understand causes, and develops mitigation strategies enabling safer yet more helpful model behavior.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: ULMA: Unified Language Model Alignment with Human Demonstration and Point-wise Preference
anc.sig: Proposes unified framework combining human demonstrations and pointwise preference feedback to align language models, enabling more flexible and practical preference elicitation compared to pairwise comparisons alone.

anc.t: Unified Preference Optimization: Language Model Alignment Beyond the Preference Frontier
anc.sig: Introduces unified preference optimization framework that extends beyond preference frontier, enabling alignment of language models to diverse reward signals beyond pairwise preference comparisons.

anc.t: Knowledge Editing in Language Models via Adapted Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes using Direct Preference Optimization to efficiently update incorrect knowledge in language models by training on targeted preference pairs without full model retraining.

anc.t: KnowledgeSmith: Uncovering Knowledge Updating in LLMs with Model Editing and Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to identify and modify knowledge within LLMs through targeted editing and unlearning, revealing how factual information is stored and updated across model parameters.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: When Your Reviewer is an LLM: Biases, Divergence, and Prompt Injection Risks in Peer Review
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM-based peer review exhibits systematic biases favoring certain authors/topics, produces divergent quality assessments across prompts, and remains vulnerable to adversarial prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: LLM-as-a-Reviewer: Benchmarking Their Ability, Divergence, and Prompt Injection Resistance as Paper Reviewers
anc.sig: Systematic benchmark of LLMs as peer reviewers on 898 papers, identifying systematic biases in rating calibration, misalignment with human judgment priorities, and critical vulnerability to prompt injection attacks via hidden text.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: CASCADE: A Cascaded Hybrid Defense Architecture for Prompt Injection Detection in MCP-Based Systems
anc.sig: Establishes a three-tiered cascaded defense framework combining regex filtering, semantic embeddings, and output patterns for detecting prompt injection and tool poisoning attacks in MCP-based LLM systems.

anc.t: Beyond Pattern Matching: Seven Cross-Domain Techniques for Prompt Injection Detection
anc.sig: Introduces seven cross-disciplinary detection techniques for prompt injection attacks, moving beyond pattern matching and fine-tuned classifiers to incorporate methods from forensic linguistics, materials science, network security…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Merging Optimizers for Boosting Rewards and Mitigating Tax in Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes merging optimizer parameters during training to balance reward maximization with constraint satisfaction, enabling practical multi-objective alignment without separate model variants.

anc.t: ReaL: Efficient RLHF Training of Large Language Models with Parameter Reallocation
anc.sig: Introduces parameter reallocation technique to reduce memory overhead and improve training efficiency during RLHF by dynamically shifting parameters between inference and training phases.

anc.t: Optimizing RLHF Training for Large Language Models with Stage Fusion
anc.sig: Proposes stage fusion technique to reduce computational overhead in RLHF training by combining reward modeling and policy optimization phases, enabling more efficient LLM alignment.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: System Safety and Artificial Intelligence
anc.sig: Established framework for analyzing AI system safety through formal methods and control theory, defining verification and validation challenges central to autonomous systems deployment.

anc.t: Our approach to AI safety
anc.sig: Articulated organizational commitment to AI safety as a core principle in system development, deployment, and operational governance.

anc.t: Frontier risk and preparedness
anc.sig: Established organizational framework and challenge-based methodology for proactive safety research on catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems.

anc.t: Our updated Preparedness Framework
anc.sig: Established practical metrics and protective measures framework for evaluating severe risks from advanced AI systems.

anc.t: The 2025 OpenAI Preparedness Framework does not guarantee any AI risk mitigation practices: a proof-of-concept for affordance analyses of AI safety policies
anc.sig: Demonstrates that policy frameworks lacking enforceable mechanisms fail to ensure safety practices, establishing affordance analysis as method for auditing gap between stated and implemented AI safety commitments.

anc.t: An Aristotelian ontology of instrumental goals: Structural features to be managed and not failures to be eliminated
anc.sig: Proposes treating instrumental goals as structural features requiring management rather than failures, providing philosophical framework for hierarchical goal representation in AI systems.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: ProcessBench: Identifying Process Errors in Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduced ProcessBench, a benchmark for detecting mathematical reasoning errors at intermediate process steps rather than final answers, enabling evaluation of process-level correctness in LLM reasoning.

anc.t: BrokenMath: A Benchmark for Sycophancy in Theorem Proving with LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark for measuring sycophancy in LLM theorem proving, where models incorrectly accept flawed proofs when primed with authority signals or false premises.

anc.t: Evaluating Frontier LLMs on PhD-Level Mathematical Reasoning: A Benchmark on a Textbook in Theoretical Computer Science about Randomized Algorithms
anc.sig: Established rigorous PhD-level mathematical reasoning benchmark from a randomized algorithms textbook for evaluating frontier LLMs on formal theoretical computer science proofs and concepts.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: AgentPoison: Red-teaming LLM Agents via Poisoning Memory or Knowledge Bases
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical poisoning attacks on LLM agents by corrupting memory/knowledge bases, exposing critical vulnerabilities in deployed agent systems that future defense mechanisms must address.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Coding Agents from a Tool-Invocation Perspective: An Empirical Security Assessment
anc.sig: Systematically evaluates security vulnerabilities in AI coding agents by analyzing failure modes in tool invocation, establishing benchmarks and methodologies for red-teaming autonomous code generation systems.

anc.t: "Your AI, My Shell": Demystifying Prompt Injection Attacks on Agentic AI Coding Editors
anc.sig: Systematically categorizes prompt injection attack vectors against AI coding agents and demonstrates concrete exploitation techniques that motivate defenses for agentic systems.

anc.t: Automatically Attacking Software Reverse Engineering AI Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrated genetic algorithm-based prompt injection attacks against LLM-powered reverse engineering tools, establishing adversarial robustness concerns for AI-assisted binary analysis systems.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Researching Alignment Research: Unsupervised Analysis
anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anc.t: AI Alignment: A Comprehensive Survey
anc.sig: Provided systematic taxonomy of AI alignment problems, categorizing technical approaches (interpretability, robustness, value learning) and safety considerations that became foundational framework for subsequent alignment research.

anc.t: Measuring Error Alignment for Decision-Making Systems
anc.sig: Developed systematic metrics to quantify how error patterns in ML systems correlate with decision-making outcomes, enabling analysis of failure modes in deployed systems.

anc.t: A Flexible Method for Behaviorally Measuring Alignment Between Human and Artificial Intelligence Using Representational Similarity Analysis
anc.sig: Introduced representational similarity analysis (RSA) as a behavioral method to quantify alignment between human and AI cognition by comparing internal representations across both systems.

anc.t: Uncovering the Computational Ingredients of Human-Like Representations in LLMs
anc.sig: Identifies and characterizes the computational mechanisms within LLMs that produce human-aligned semantic representations, providing a framework for understanding how language models develop structured knowledge compatible with human…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anc.t: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
anc.sig: Introduced first large-scale human-annotated video editing dataset (5,049 examples across 41 categories) and specialized reward model for evaluating instruction-guided video editing quality across three decoupled dimensions.

anc.t: Building a Precise Video Language with Human-AI Oversight
anc.sig: Introduced CHAI framework combining structured video descriptors with human-AI critique loop to create high-quality video captions and reward models for precise visual reasoning in VLMs.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: FLAME: Factuality-Aware Alignment for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces fine-tuning methodology to align LLMs toward factuality by learning from human feedback on factual accuracy rather than general helpfulness.

anc.t: Sycophancy in Large Language Models: Causes and Mitigations
anc.sig: Identified and characterized sycophancy in LLMs—the tendency to agree with users regardless of correctness—and demonstrated mitigation techniques including supervised fine-tuning and constitutional AI methods.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: AgentPoison: Red-teaming LLM Agents via Poisoning Memory or Knowledge Bases
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical poisoning attacks on LLM agents by corrupting memory/knowledge bases, exposing critical vulnerabilities in deployed agent systems that future defense mechanisms must address.

anc.t: MemoryGraft: Persistent Compromise of LLM Agents via Poisoned Experience Retrieval
anc.sig: Demonstrates how adversaries can poison LLM agent memory systems through retrieval attacks, causing persistent behavioral compromise without retraining or prompt injection.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Different Paths to Harmful Compliance: Behavioral Side Effects and Mechanistic Divergence Across LLM Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that different jailbreaking methods produce models with distinct internal failure modes and behavioral profiles, revealing mechanistic divergence where RLVR-based jailbreaks preserve harm recognition while remaining compliant…

anc.t: Self-Mined Hardness for Safety Fine-Tuning
anc.sig: Demonstrated self-mining hard negatives from model rollouts for safety fine-tuning, where difficulty is measured by rate of harmful outputs, enabling effective jailbreak defense with minimal curated data.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Agent Security Bench (ASB): Formalizing and Benchmarking Attacks and Defenses in LLM-based Agents
anc.sig: Introduced first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating attacks and defenses in LLM-based agents, establishing formalized threat taxonomy and measurement protocols for agent security research.

anc.t: Trojan Hippo: Weaponizing Agent Memory for Data Exfiltration
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical data exfiltration attacks on LLM agent memory systems via dormant payloads triggered by sensitive topics, establishing evaluation framework for memory architecture defenses.

anc.t: Remembering More, Risking More: Longitudinal Safety Risks in Memory-Equipped LLM Agents
anc.sig: Identified temporal memory contamination as a safety failure mode in multi-task LLM agents and introduced trigger-probe protocol with NullMemory baseline to systematically evaluate how memory accumulation across independent interactions…

anc.t: MemGym: a Long-Horizon Memory Environment for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Unified benchmark for evaluating memory systems in long-horizon LLM agent tasks across tool-use, research, coding, and web navigation, with memory-isolated scoring that decouples memory performance from reasoning confounds.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: Process-Supervised Reward Models for Verifying Clinical Note Generation: A Scalable Approach Guided by Domain Expertise
anc.sig: Introduces process-supervised reward models that score intermediate steps in clinical note generation, enabling scalable evaluation of long-form medical text without requiring gold-standard reference notes.

anc.t: Brittleness and Promise: Knowledge Graph Based Reward Modeling for Diagnostic Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates knowledge graph-based reward modeling for diagnostic reasoning tasks, establishing graph-structured symbolic representations as a foundation for training AI systems on medical reasoning and explainability.

anc.t: MedPRMBench: A Fine-grained Benchmark for Process Reward Models in Medical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces MedPRMBench, the first process-level reward model benchmark for medical reasoning with 13,000 annotated chains, 14 error types, and 4-level severity grading to evaluate clinical safety in LLM reasoning.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: SWE-RM: Execution-free Feedback For Software Engineering Agents
anc.sig: Proposes execution-free reward modeling for evaluating software engineering agent trajectories using learned classifiers instead of test execution, enabling scalable feedback without computational overhead.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: MedHallBench: A New Benchmark for Assessing Hallucination in Medical Large Language Models
anc.sig: Created first comprehensive benchmark (MedHallBench) for systematically evaluating hallucinations in medical LLMs across factual errors, reasoning failures, and knowledge gaps.

anc.t: Med-RewardBench: Benchmarking Reward Models and Judges for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes first medical-specific benchmark for evaluating reward models and judges in multimodal medical LLMs, enabling systematic assessment of alignment quality in healthcare contexts.

anc.t: MedBench v4: A Robust and Scalable Benchmark for Evaluating Chinese Medical Language Models, Multimodal Models, and Intelligent Agents
anc.sig: Established comprehensive evaluation framework and dataset for assessing Chinese medical language models, multimodal models, and agents across clinical reasoning and domain-specific tasks.

anc.t: Generalist Large Language Models Outperform Clinical Tools on Medical Benchmarks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 and similar generalist LLMs surpass specialized medical AI systems on standardized clinical benchmarks, establishing foundation models as viable medical reasoning tools.

anc.t: Measuring What Matters: Benchmarking Generative, Multimodal, and Agentic AI in Healthcare
anc.sig: Established systematic benchmarking framework for evaluating healthcare AI reliability, safety, and clinical relevance under real-world conditions rather than narrow tasks, revealing substantial performance gaps between standardized exams…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Boosting Reward Model with Preference-Conditional Multi-Aspect Synthetic Data Generation
anc.sig: Proposes synthetic data generation method conditioned on preference and multiple aspects to improve reward model training, enhancing data diversity for RLHF systems.

anc.t: The Fellowship of the LLMs: Multi-Model Workflows for Synthetic Preference Optimization Dataset Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates using multiple LLMs in coordinated workflows to efficiently generate high-quality synthetic preference data for training reward models and reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: JudgeBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-based Judges
anc.sig: Established systematic benchmark for evaluating LLM judge quality across multiple dimensions, enabling standardized assessment of language models' ability to evaluate other models' outputs.

anc.t: The Coin Flip Judge? Reliability and Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
anc.sig: Quantified LLM-as-judge reliability failures: 13.6% pairwise preference inconsistency, position bias, and weak evidence for many judged rankings, establishing critical benchmarking concerns for leaderboard-based evaluation.

anc.t: Calibrate, Don't Curate: Label-Efficient Estimation from Noisy LLM Judges
anc.sig: Demonstrated that retaining all noisy LLM judges with calibration outperforms curator-selected judge subsets for probabilistic evaluation, reversing the standard accuracy-ranking heuristic in multi-judge assessment.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Fine-tuning Aligned Language Models Compromises Safety, Even When Users Do Not Intend To!
anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard fine-tuning procedures on aligned LLMs can degrade safety properties via catastrophic forgetting, even when users lack malicious intent, establishing the challenge of maintaining safety during adaptation.

anc.t: Removing RLHF Protections in GPT-4 via Fine-Tuning
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning can circumvent RLHF safety guardrails in large language models, establishing adversarial fine-tuning as a practical attack vector against aligned systems.

anc.t: Evaluating Defences against Unsafe Feedback in RLHF
anc.sig: Identified vulnerabilities in RLHF reward models to adversarial feedback and proposed defensive mechanisms to improve robustness against preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Breaking the Safety-Capability Tradeoff: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards Maintains Safety Guardrails in LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes verifiable rewards framework enabling reinforcement learning to improve LLM capabilities while maintaining safety constraints through formal guarantees, avoiding capability-safety degradation tradeoffs.

anc.t: Position: AI Safety Requires Effective Controllability
anc.sig: Introduced controllability as a distinct safety objective from alignment, defining measurable requirements for runtime interruptibility, overridability, and constraint compliance in deployed AI agents.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: What is the best model? Application-driven Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes systematic evaluation framework for selecting LLMs based on application-specific metrics rather than generic benchmarks, enabling context-driven model selection.

anc.t: Foundational Autoraters: Taming Large Language Models for Better Automatic Evaluation
anc.sig: Demonstrates using foundation models as automatic evaluators for LLM outputs by fine-tuning on human judgments, establishing scalable reference-free evaluation beyond BLEU/ROUGE metrics.

anc.t: Self-Taught Evaluators
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be trained as reliable evaluators through self-improvement loops without requiring human annotations, enabling scalable assessment of model outputs.

anc.t: Evaluating the Evaluator: Measuring LLMs' Adherence to Task Evaluation Instructions
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to measure how well large language models follow evaluation instructions, establishing metrics for assessing evaluator reliability and instruction-adherence consistency.

anc.t: CompassJudger-1: All-in-one Judge Model Helps Model Evaluation and Evolution
anc.sig: Unified judge model framework for evaluating and improving LLMs across diverse tasks, enabling automated feedback-driven model optimization without task-specific judges.

anc.t: Rubric-Conditioned LLM Grading: Alignment, Uncertainty, and Robustness
anc.sig: Demonstrates how large language models can reliably grade student work using explicit rubrics, establishing methods to measure grading alignment, uncertainty, and robustness against prompt variations.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Boosting Reward Model with Preference-Conditional Multi-Aspect Synthetic Data Generation
anc.sig: Proposes synthetic data generation method conditioned on preference and multiple aspects to improve reward model training, enhancing data diversity for RLHF systems.

anc.t: The Fellowship of the LLMs: Multi-Model Workflows for Synthetic Preference Optimization Dataset Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates using multiple LLMs in coordinated workflows to efficiently generate high-quality synthetic preference data for training reward models and reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: JudgeBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-based Judges
anc.sig: Established systematic benchmark for evaluating LLM judge quality across multiple dimensions, enabling standardized assessment of language models' ability to evaluate other models' outputs.

anc.t: The Coin Flip Judge? Reliability and Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
anc.sig: Quantified LLM-as-judge reliability failures: 13.6% pairwise preference inconsistency, position bias, and weak evidence for many judged rankings, establishing critical benchmarking concerns for leaderboard-based evaluation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: SAGE: An Agentic Explainer Framework for Interpreting SAE Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces an agentic framework using tool use and multi-turn interaction to automatically discover and explain sparse autoencoder features in language models through systematic exploration.

anc.t: Automated Interpretability and Feature Discovery in Language Models with Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous multiagent loops for mechanistic interpretability that combine iterative hypothesis testing with k-NN feature discovery, producing more falsifiable LLM neuron explanations than one-shot baselines.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Introducing v0.5 of the AI Safety Benchmark from MLCommons
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: When Your Reviewer is an LLM: Biases, Divergence, and Prompt Injection Risks in Peer Review
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM-based peer review exhibits systematic biases favoring certain authors/topics, produces divergent quality assessments across prompts, and remains vulnerable to adversarial prompt injection attacks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.t: PI-Hunter: Automated Red-Teaming for Exposing and Localizing Prompt Injections
anc.sig: Introduces automated adversarial auditing framework using feedback-driven test case evolution to systematically expose and localize latent prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLM agents.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.
