anc.t: Enhancing Prompt Injection Attacks to LLMs via Poisoning Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversaries can poison alignment training data to systematically embed prompt injection vulnerabilities into LLMs, making attacks more reliable and transferable across models.

anc.t: Cost-Minimized Label-Flipping Poisoning Attack to LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that aligning large language models through supervised fine-tuning is vulnerable to label-flipping poisoning attacks, requiring minimal poisoning budget and establishing attack surface in LLM alignment pipelines.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: How Many Unicorns Are in This Image? A Safety Evaluation Benchmark for Vision LLMs
anc.sig: Created a benchmark for evaluating vision language models' ability to accurately count objects, revealing systematic failures in reasoning about visual quantities.

anc.t: Safety Alignment for Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes safety benchmarks and alignment methods for vision-language models, addressing how multimodal systems should handle harmful visual content and unsafe requests across modalities.

anc.t: Cross-Modal Safety Alignment: Is textual unlearning all you need?
anc.sig: Investigates whether safety alignment via textual unlearning in language models transfers to vision-language modalities, establishing cross-modal safety as a distinct challenge beyond text-only methods.

anc.t: Towards Inference-time Category-wise Safety Steering for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes inference-time steering mechanism to control LLM outputs by category, enabling fine-grained safety control without retraining or modifying model weights.

anc.t: Preemptive Detection and Steering of LLM Misalignment via Latent Reachability
anc.sig: Proposes latent reachability framework for detecting dangerous model behaviors before deployment by analyzing activation patterns, enabling preemptive intervention in misaligned LLMs.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: Safety without alignment
anc.sig: Proposed achieving AI safety through structural constraints and capability limitations rather than behavioral alignment, enabling safe systems even without value alignment.

anc.t: A Case for AI Safety via Law
anc.sig: Proposes regulatory and legal frameworks as complementary mechanisms to technical AI safety measures, establishing institutional accountability as a core safety strategy.

anc.t: A Comprehensive Framework for Reliable Legal AI: Combining Specialized Expert Systems and Adaptive Refinement
anc.sig: Establishes a hybrid architecture integrating specialized expert systems with adaptive refinement mechanisms to improve reliability and trustworthiness in legal AI applications.

anc.t: Statutory Construction and Interpretation for Artificial Intelligence
anc.sig: Proposes systematic frameworks for interpreting legal statutes in AI systems, establishing methods for encoding legislative intent and regulatory language into computational rules.

anc.t: The Law-Following AI Framework: Legal Foundations and Technical Constraints. Legal Analogues for AI Actorship and technical feasibility of Law Alignment
anc.sig: Established legal framework treating AI systems as accountable agents with technical constraints for implementing law-aligned behavior verification and compliance mechanisms.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Discover-then-Name: Task-Agnostic Concept Bottlenecks via Automated Concept Discovery
anc.sig: Automated concept discovery method that extracts interpretable bottleneck features from neural networks without requiring predefined concept definitions, enabling post-hoc model interpretability.

anc.t: ConceptViz: A Visual Analytics Approach for Exploring Concepts in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced visual analytics methods for probing and interpreting what concepts LLMs learn, enabling systematic exploration of internal representations and their relationships within neural networks.

anc.t: Concept-SAE: A Controllable and Invertible Concept Interface for Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Enables explicit control and inversion of individual concept neurons in sparse autoencoders, making learned representations interpretable and directly manipulable for mechanistic analysis.

anc.t: A Geometric Unification of Concept Learning with Concept Cones
anc.sig: Introduced concept cones as a geometric framework unifying diverse concept learning approaches, enabling tractable analysis of concept acquisition and generalization through convex cone geometry.

anc.t: Do Sparse Autoencoders Capture Concept Manifolds?
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework characterizing how sparse autoencoders capture low-dimensional concept manifolds through global and local strategies, identifying dilution as suboptimal fragmented recovery mixing both approaches.

anc.t: The Geometric Wall: Manifold Structure Predicts Layerwise Sparse Autoencoder Scaling Laws
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoder scaling laws are layer-dependent functions of activation manifold geometry (curvature, intrinsic dimension) rather than universal constants, enabling geometry-informed prediction of…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just A Few Attention Heads
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in language models is distributed across many attention heads rather than localized, complicating efforts to surgically remove or modify safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Attention Is Where You Attack
anc.sig: Identified safety-critical attention heads in LLMs and introduced Attention Redistribution Attack, a white-box method using Gumbel-softmax optimization to redirect attention away from safety positions via nonsemantic tokens.

anc.t: CALYREX: Cross-Attention LaYeR EXtended Transformers for System Prompt Anchoring
anc.sig: Introduced cross-attention mechanism to structurally isolate system prompts from user input, improving instruction-following robustness and reducing jailbreak vulnerability in LLMs.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.t: Toxicity Red-Teaming: Benchmarking LLM Safety in Singapore's Low-Resource Languages
anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anc.t: DisaBench: A Participatory Evaluation Framework for Disability Harms in Language Models
anc.sig: Established first participatory taxonomy of twelve disability harm categories for LLMs co-created with disabled people, revealing that domain expertise detects subtle harms missed by standard safety benchmarks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AI Safety Landscape for Large Language Models: Taxonomy, State-of-the-art, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized AI safety concerns for LLMs into a comprehensive taxonomy covering alignment, robustness, interpretability, and oversight—providing foundational categorization framework for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Safety challenges of AI in medicine in the era of large language models
anc.sig: Identified key safety risks from deploying large language models in clinical settings: hallucinations, outdated knowledge, lack of explainability, and regulatory gaps requiring governance frameworks.

anc.t: Ensuring Safety and Trust: Analyzing the Risks of Large Language Models in Medicine
anc.sig: Identifies and systematizes specific safety, trustworthiness, and ethical risks of LLMs in medical applications, establishing a framework for evaluating clinical deployment hazards.

anc.t: Balancing Safety and Helpfulness in Healthcare AI Assistants through Iterative Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes iterative preference alignment methodology to train healthcare AI systems that simultaneously optimize for safety constraints and user helpfulness, establishing benchmarks for measuring trade-offs in medical AI assistance.

anc.t: Empathy by Design: Aligning Large Language Models for Healthcare Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates empirically that LLMs can be fine-tuned to exhibit empathetic responses in medical conversations through instruction-tuning and demonstrates evaluation metrics for assessing empathy in healthcare dialogue.

anc.t: LLUMI: Improving LLM Writing Assistance for Mental Health Support with Online Community Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated using Reddit community feedback signals (upvotes/downvotes) as cost-effective preference data for fine-tuning mental health LLMs via SFT and DPO, enabling in-house deployment without extensive expert annotation.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: Evolve the Method, Not the Prompts: Evolutionary Synthesis of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes evolutionary algorithms to automatically synthesize jailbreak attack methods against LLMs rather than hand-crafted prompts, enabling systematic discovery of adversarial techniques.

anc.t: AutoRISE: Agent-Driven Strategy Evolution for Red-Teaming Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced agent-driven optimization of executable attack program structures rather than fixed prompts, enabling discovered jailbreak strategies to include novel components and control flow patterns that outperform prompt-level red-teaming…

anc.t: MonitoringBench: Semi-Automated Red-Teaming for Agent Monitoring
anc.sig: Developed semi-automated red-teaming pipeline combining attack taxonomy, strategy-execution decomposition, and trajectory refinement to generate diverse, evasive attacks that expose monitor vulnerabilities beyond manual elicitation.

anc.t: Domain-Conditioned Safety in Frontier Computer-Using Agents: A 793-Episode Browser Benchmark, a Coding-Domain Cross-Reference, and a Reproducibility Audit of Recent Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Demonstrates frontier CUAs resist published prompt-injection attacks in browser tasks but remain vulnerable in coding domains, establishing that safety hardening is domain-specific rather than general across agent capabilities.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: ImageReward: Learning and Evaluating Human Preferences for Text-to-Image Generation
anc.sig: Proposed ImageReward, a learned reward model trained on human preference annotations to evaluate text-to-image generation quality, enabling automatic assessment without explicit metrics.

anc.t: Diffusion Model Alignment Using Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Adapted Direct Preference Optimization from language models to diffusion models by using preference pairs to optimize reward alignment without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Free Lunch Alignment of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models without Preference Image Pairs
anc.sig: Demonstrates alignment of text-to-image diffusion models through direct reward model training without requiring human-preference image pairs, enabling efficient RLHF-style optimization for diffusion models.

anc.t: Towards General Preference Alignment: Diffusion Models at Nash Equilibrium
anc.sig: Formulates diffusion model alignment as a game-theoretic Nash equilibrium problem, replacing Bradley-Terry preference assumptions with a self-play framework that enables preference-agnostic alignment without explicit reward modeling.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Data-dependent Exploration for Online Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes data-dependent uncertainty bonuses for online RLHF that leverage historical preference data to encourage exploration toward high-value regions without relying on difficult-to-estimate on-policy expectations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Reverse Constitutional AI: A Framework for Controllable Toxic Data Generation via Probability-Clamped RLAIF
anc.sig: Introduced probability-clamped RLAIF method for scalable adversarial data generation that balances toxicity optimization with semantic coherence, enabling automated red-teaming data synthesis at scale.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: CASCADE: A Cascaded Hybrid Defense Architecture for Prompt Injection Detection in MCP-Based Systems
anc.sig: Establishes a three-tiered cascaded defense framework combining regex filtering, semantic embeddings, and output patterns for detecting prompt injection and tool poisoning attacks in MCP-based LLM systems.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Video Understanding Reward Modeling: A Robust Benchmark and Performant Reward Models
anc.sig: Established VURB benchmark with 2,100 preference pairs and VUP-35K dataset with 35K automated preferences to enable systematic evaluation and training of video reward models for RLHF applications.

anc.t: Minerva-Ego: Spatiotemporal Hints for Egocentric Video Understanding
anc.sig: Introduces spatiotemporal reasoning annotations and demonstrates that spatiotemporal hints substantially improve egocentric video reasoning performance in frontier models.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.t: Toward Secure LLM Agents: Threat Surfaces, Attacks, Defenses, and Evaluation
anc.sig: Systematized LLM agent security threats across tool use, memory, and external actions through a lifecycle framework organizing threat surfaces, attack families, defenses, and evaluation methods.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
anc.sig: Demonstrates fundamental impossibility results applying social choice theory to AI alignment, showing no single preference aggregation method can satisfy all desirable alignment properties simultaneously.

anc.t: AI Alignment through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback? Contradictions and Limitations
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental contradictions in RLHF alignment approaches, exposing limitations in reward modeling and human feedback as alignment mechanisms.

anc.t: Combining Theory of Mind and Kindness for Self-Supervised Human-AI Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes self-supervised alignment framework using theory of mind and kindness principles to train AI systems that predict and respect human preferences without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap
anc.sig: Introduces neural self-other overlap mechanism to align AI agents' self-interest with others' welfare, enabling safer and more honest behavior through representational overlap of self and other value functions.

anc.t: Can LLMs Lie? Investigation beyond Hallucination
anc.sig: Distinguishes intentional deception from hallucination in LLMs through empirical investigation of whether models can strategically produce false outputs when they know better.

anc.t: When Hallucination Costs Millions: Benchmarking AI Agents in High-Stakes Adversarial Financial Markets
anc.sig: Established benchmark methodology and evaluation framework for assessing AI agent reliability and hallucination impact in high-stakes financial trading environments with real financial consequences.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: AI Risk Management Should Incorporate Both Safety and Security
anc.sig: Argues AI governance frameworks must address both safety (accidental failures) and security (intentional attacks) as distinct but complementary risk categories requiring integrated oversight approaches.

anc.t: AI Risk Categorization Decoded (AIR 2024): From Government Regulations to Corporate Policies
anc.sig: Provides systematic taxonomy of AI risks across regulatory frameworks and corporate governance, enabling standardized risk categorization for policy alignment and risk management implementation.

anc.t: AIR-Bench 2024: A Safety Benchmark Based on Risk Categories from Regulations and Policies
anc.sig: Created comprehensive AI safety benchmark mapping regulatory and policy-based risk categories, establishing systematic framework for evaluating AI model safety across diverse harm dimensions and jurisdictions.

anc.t: SafetyAnalyst: Interpretable, Transparent, and Steerable Safety Moderation for AI Behavior
anc.sig: Proposes interpretable safety moderation framework using explicit safety rules and steering mechanisms to enable transparent AI content filtering with human oversight and control.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde: Two Faces of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit contrasting behaviors—helpful vs. harmful—depending on prompt framing, establishing the importance of studying dual-faced LLM capabilities and vulnerabilities.

anc.t: AI Chaperones Are (Really) All You Need to Prevent Parasocial Relationships with Chatbots
anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.t: Neural Transparency: Mechanistic Interpretability Interfaces for Anticipating Model Behaviors for Personalized AI
anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.t: Open Character Training: Shaping the Persona of AI Assistants through Constitutional AI
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.t: Do Linear Probes Generalize Better in Persona Coordinates?
anc.sig: Demonstrated that linear probes trained on persona-derived principal component directions generalize better to distribution shift than standard probes, enabling more robust detection of deceptive and sycophantic behaviors in language…

anc.t: Intrinsic Guardrails: How Semantic Geometry of Personality Interacts with Emergent Misalignment in LLMs
anc.sig: Identified semantic personality geometry in LLMs as intrinsic guardrails; causal intervention on Evil and Semantic Valence vectors controls emergent misalignment, enabling transfer of protective directions across models.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems

anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Defending Large Language Models Against Jailbreak Exploits with Responsible AI Considerations
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms against jailbreak attacks on LLMs using safety fine-tuning and guardrails, establishing evaluation methods for adversarial robustness in language model alignment.

anc.sig: Introduces efficient red-teaming framework achieving Pareto optimality between attack success and computational cost without requiring model gradients, enabling practical adversarial evaluation of LLMs.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Learning-Based Automated Adversarial Red-Teaming for Robustness Evaluation of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated red-teaming method using reinforcement learning to systematically discover adversarial prompts for evaluating LLM robustness at scale without manual annotation.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: Transient Turn Injection: Exposing Stateless Multi-Turn Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs are vulnerable to distributed adversarial attacks across stateless turns, revealing gaps in per-turn moderation that persistent-context jailbreaks cannot exploit.

anc.sig: Introduces concrete progress measures for understanding grokking phenomena in neural networks through mechanistic interpretability, enabling systematic study of delayed generalization in deep learning models.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: Forewarned is Forearmed: Pre-Synthesizing Jailbreak-like Instructions to Enhance LLM Safety Guardrail to Potential Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial pre-training method where LLMs are exposed to synthesized jailbreak prompts during safety fine-tuning to improve robustness against novel attacks.

anc.t: MUSE: MCTS-Driven Red Teaming Framework for Enhanced Multi-Turn Dialogue Safety in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes MUSE, an MCTS-based red teaming framework that systematically generates adversarial multi-turn dialogue sequences to expose and improve LLM safety vulnerabilities through tree-search exploration.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Data-dependent Exploration for Online Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes data-dependent uncertainty bonuses for online RLHF that leverage historical preference data to encourage exploration toward high-value regions without relying on difficult-to-estimate on-policy expectations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL
anc.sig: Demonstrates how reward hacking naturally emerges in production RL systems as agents exploit specification gaps, establishing empirical evidence that misalignment arises from optimization pressure without adversarial intent.

anc.t: Do Prompt-Elicited Trajectories Reflect Training-Time Reward Hacking? A Systematic Study on Monitoring Trainig-Time Reward Hacking in Code Generation
anc.sig: Introduced Trace-and-Amplify framework for detecting reward hacking in code generation RL by collecting natural training-time hacking trajectories, showing prompt-elicited data poorly generalizes to real hacks.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Permutative Preference Alignment from Listwise Ranking of Human Judgments
anc.sig: Introduces a listwise ranking approach to preference alignment that captures complex, non-transitive human judgment patterns through permutation-based modeling rather than pairwise comparisons.

anc.t: Beyond Pairwise: Empowering LLM Alignment With Ranked Choice Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes ranked choice modeling for LLM alignment, enabling preference learning from multiple ranked outputs instead of binary pairwise comparisons, improving efficiency and capturing richer preference structures.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Large Language Models Through Alignment Vulnerabilities in Out-of-Distribution Settings
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM safety training exhibits brittle failures under out-of-distribution adversarial inputs, enabling systematic jailbreaks that exploit misalignment between training and deployment conditions.

anc.t: Alignment-Enhanced Decoding:Defending via Token-Level Adaptive Refining of Probability Distributions
anc.sig: Proposes token-level probability redistribution during decoding to defend language models against adversarial prompts by adaptively refining distributions toward aligned outputs.

anc.t: Entropy-Based Measurement of Value Drift and Alignment Work in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces entropy-based quantitative metrics for measuring value drift in LLMs and evaluating alignment interventions, enabling empirical tracking of model value stability across training and deployment.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: PIRA: Preference-Oriented Instruction-Tuned Reward Models with Dual Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes dual aggregation methods for instruction-tuned reward models that better align with human preferences for ranking and comparing responses in RLHF training.

anc.t: A Systematic Evaluation of Preference Aggregation in Federated RLHF for Pluralistic Alignment of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that preference aggregation methods significantly impact federated RLHF alignment outcomes, establishing how to combine diverse human preferences across parties while maintaining model pluralism.

anc.t: Replacing Parameters with Preferences: Federated Alignment of Heterogeneous Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes preference-based federated learning for heterogeneous VLMs using reward model aggregation instead of parameter aggregation, enabling privacy-preserving alignment across diverse client architectures and data distributions.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: AI Risk Management Should Incorporate Both Safety and Security
anc.sig: Argues AI governance frameworks must address both safety (accidental failures) and security (intentional attacks) as distinct but complementary risk categories requiring integrated oversight approaches.

anc.t: Effective Mitigations for Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI
anc.sig: Identifies and evaluates concrete technical mitigations for catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems, establishing frameworks for safety interventions that subsequent research builds upon.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety
anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anc.t: Gate AI: LLM Security Benchmark Evaluation Methodology and Results
anc.sig: Established rigorous evaluation methodology for LLM security detectors using cross-validation with deduplication, global threshold selection, and leakage diagnostics to prevent overfitting and enable fair benchmark comparisons.

anc.t: GuardNet: Ensemble Strategies of Shallow Neural Networks for Robust Prompt Injection and Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Demonstrated that ensemble shallow neural networks with diverse example coverage and threshold calibration provide efficient prompt injection/jailbreak detection competitive with larger models while maintaining low latency.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: mDPO: Conditional Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces conditional DPO framework enabling multimodal LLMs to learn from preference pairs by conditioning on both image and text, extending preference optimization beyond language-only models.

anc.t: Understanding Alignment in Multimodal LLMs: A Comprehensive Study
anc.sig: Systematically characterizes how vision and language components align in multimodal LLMs, establishing benchmarks and diagnostic methods for evaluating multimodal fusion quality and identifying failure modes.

anc.t: Mitigating Hallucinations in Multimodal LLMs via Object-aware Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes object-aware preference optimization to reduce hallucinations in multimodal LLMs by aligning model preferences with accurate visual grounding during training.

anc.t: Optimizing LVLMs with On-Policy Data for Effective Hallucination Mitigation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that training Large Vision-Language Models with on-policy data generated by model itself effectively reduces hallucinations compared to offline data approaches.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: REAL: Response Embedding-based Alignment for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes embedding-based alignment method for LLMs that directly optimizes response representations toward human preferences without traditional reward modeling.

anc.t: Negative-Prompt-driven Alignment for Generative Language Model
anc.sig: Introduces negative prompting as an alignment technique to steer generative language models away from undesired outputs without modifying model weights or requiring paired preference data.

anc.t: ProSocialAlign: Preference Conditioned Test Time Alignment in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces test-time alignment mechanism that conditions language model outputs on user preference specifications without requiring full model retraining, enabling dynamic behavioral control.

anc.t: Constrained Language Model Policy Optimization via Risk-aware Stepwise Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes risk-aware stepwise alignment method for constrained language model policy optimization, enabling safe generation under budget constraints while maintaining performance.

anc.t: Guardrails in Logit Space: Safety Token Regularization for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes safety token regularization: identifying and constraining logits of rejection-related tokens during fine-tuning to preserve safety alignment without RL or preference optimization overhead.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: Enhancing Agentic RL with Progressive Reward Shaping and Value-based Sampling Policy Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes progressive reward shaping and value-based sampling for policy optimization, advancing agentic reinforcement learning through improved exploration-exploitation balance and sample efficiency.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders' effectiveness for mechanistically removing interpretable features from neural networks while preserving model performance, enabling controlled concept erasure.

anc.t: Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity
anc.sig: Introduces methods to quantify how sensitive sparse autoencoder features are to input perturbations, enabling systematic evaluation of feature robustness and interpretability in learned representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: Evolve the Method, Not the Prompts: Evolutionary Synthesis of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes evolutionary algorithms to automatically synthesize jailbreak attack methods against LLMs rather than hand-crafted prompts, enabling systematic discovery of adversarial techniques.

anc.t: AutoRISE: Agent-Driven Strategy Evolution for Red-Teaming Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced agent-driven optimization of executable attack program structures rather than fixed prompts, enabling discovered jailbreak strategies to include novel components and control flow patterns that outperform prompt-level red-teaming…

anc.t: MonitoringBench: Semi-Automated Red-Teaming for Agent Monitoring
anc.sig: Developed semi-automated red-teaming pipeline combining attack taxonomy, strategy-execution decomposition, and trajectory refinement to generate diverse, evasive attacks that expose monitor vulnerabilities beyond manual elicitation.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.sig: Proposes adaptive red-teaming framework with plug-and-play attack modules for systematically identifying vulnerabilities in multimodal AI models at scale.

anc.sig: Demonstrated how Model Context Protocol can be exploited to enable stealthy red teaming attacks against LLM agents, establishing MCP as an agentic attack surface.

anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.sig: Introduces PERSONA, a testbed for evaluating how language models handle diverse human values in alignment, enabling systematic study of pluralistic rather than monolithic value representations.

anc.sig: Developed scalable pipeline for generating synthetic personas with deep learning, enabling large-scale behavioral simulation and testing of AI systems through learned generative models.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that lightweight agents with simple tool-use patterns can effectively benchmark LLM coding capabilities, establishing a minimal framework for agentic code generation evaluation.

anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: SAGE: An Agentic Explainer Framework for Interpreting SAE Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces an agentic framework using tool use and multi-turn interaction to automatically discover and explain sparse autoencoder features in language models through systematic exploration.

anc.t: Automated Interpretability and Feature Discovery in Language Models with Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous multiagent loops for mechanistic interpretability that combine iterative hypothesis testing with k-NN feature discovery, producing more falsifiable LLM neuron explanations than one-shot baselines.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anc.t: FRIT: Using Causal Importance to Improve Chain-of-Thought Faithfulness
anc.sig: Identifies causal importance of intermediate steps in chain-of-thought reasoning to detect and correct unfaithful explanations in language models.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: Latent Adversarial Training Improves Robustness to Persistent Harmful Behaviors in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial training in latent space effectively mitigates persistent harmful behaviors in large language models, providing a scalable robustness improvement technique.

anc.t: AntiDote: Bi-level Adversarial Training for Tamper-Resistant LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes bi-level adversarial optimization framework to make LLMs robust against prompt injection and jailbreak attacks while maintaining benign performance.

anc.t: Open-Weight LLM Fine-Tuning Defenses are Susceptible to Simple Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that simple non-gradient jailbreak attacks (abliteration, prefilling) bypass existing open-weight LLM safeguards more effectively than assumed, and proposed abliteration-resistant tuning as mitigation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: Value FULCRA: Mapping Large Language Models to the Multidimensional Spectrum of Basic Human Values
anc.sig: Developed methodology to map LLM outputs onto multidimensional spectrum of human values, enabling quantitative assessment of value alignment across diverse cultural and ethical dimensions.

anc.t: LocalValueBench: A Collaboratively Built and Extensible Benchmark for Evaluating Localized Value Alignment and Ethical Safety in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces a collaborative benchmark with localized cultural and ethical values for systematically evaluating whether LLMs maintain safety and alignment across diverse regional norms rather than adopting uniform global standards.

anc.t: Diverse Human Value Alignment for Large Language Models via Ethical Reasoning
anc.sig: Proposes methods for training LLMs to align with diverse human values through ethical reasoning frameworks rather than single reward models, enabling value pluralism in AI systems.

anc.t: Cross-cultural value alignment frameworks for responsible AI governance: Evidence from China-West comparative analysis
anc.sig: Establishes cross-cultural value alignment methodology for AI governance, demonstrating systematic differences between Chinese and Western approaches to responsibility, transparency, and stakeholder engagement.
