anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: Single-Configuration Attack Success Rate Is Not Enough: Jailbreak Evaluations Should Report Distributional Attack Success
anc.sig: Introduced Variant Sensitivity Measure and Union Coverage metrics to comprehensively evaluate jailbreak attacks across parameter configurations, establishing that single-configuration ASR reporting obscures critical threat characterization…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Universal Jailbreak Backdoors from Poisoned Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrates that poisoned RLHF data can embed universal backdoors into LLMs, enabling attackers to trigger harmful outputs across diverse prompts via concealed triggers.

anc.t: bi-GRPO: Bidirectional Optimization for Jailbreak Backdoor Injection on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes bidirectional gradient-based optimization framework for crafting jailbreak backdoors in LLMs, enabling efficient discovery of both trigger tokens and target adversarial objectives simultaneously.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anc.sig: Established standardized framework for internal AI model risk reporting across multiple regulatory jurisdictions, enabling consistent developer safety documentation during pre-release testing phases.

anc.sig: Established reproducibility as a core evaluation standard for frontier AI safety claims, arguing that withholding evaluation artifacts constitutes methodological failure rather than acceptable practice.

anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Fine-tuning Aligned Language Models Compromises Safety, Even When Users Do Not Intend To!
anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard fine-tuning procedures on aligned LLMs can degrade safety properties via catastrophic forgetting, even when users lack malicious intent, establishing the challenge of maintaining safety during adaptation.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning can circumvent RLHF safety guardrails in large language models, establishing adversarial fine-tuning as a practical attack vector against aligned systems.

anc.sig: Identified vulnerabilities in RLHF reward models to adversarial feedback and proposed defensive mechanisms to improve robustness against preference data manipulation.

anc.sig: Proposes verifiable rewards framework enabling reinforcement learning to improve LLM capabilities while maintaining safety constraints through formal guarantees, avoiding capability-safety degradation tradeoffs.

anc.sig: Provides systematic taxonomy of AI failure modes and practical mitigation strategies for deployment, establishing framework for failure classification and prevention in real-world systems.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.sig: Proposes internal audit as institutional safeguard for frontier AI labs to ensure compliance with safety standards and governance requirements before external oversight.

anc.sig: Proposes institutional audit mechanisms for AI safety oversight within companies, establishing a governance framework for internal monitoring of AI development practices and risk management.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.t: How does GPT-2 Predict Acronyms? Extracting and Understanding a Circuit via Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Identified and analyzed a complete mechanistic circuit in GPT-2 that predicts acronyms, demonstrating how to extract interpretable subcomponents from language models using attention head analysis.

anc.t: A Practical Review of Mechanistic Interpretability for Transformer-Based Language Models
anc.sig: Synthesized practical mechanistic interpretability techniques for transformers—attention patterns, neuron analysis, and circuit identification—as foundational methods for understanding model internals.

anc.t: The Cognitive Revolution in Interpretability: From Explaining Behavior to Interpreting Representations and Algorithms
anc.sig: Shifted interpretability focus from black-box behavior explanation to understanding learned representations and computational mechanisms within neural networks.

anc.t: Unboxing the Black Box: Mechanistic Interpretability for Algorithmic Understanding of Neural Networks
anc.sig: Proposes mechanistic interpretability methods for decomposing neural network computations into interpretable algorithmic components, enabling systematic analysis of learned features and circuits.

anc.sig: Proposes a collaborative reviewing platform and standardized auditing guidelines for mechanistic interpretability to ensure experimental validity in safety-critical applications.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: MUSE: Machine Unlearning Six-Way Evaluation for Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes six-way evaluation framework for machine unlearning in LLMs, assessing forget effectiveness, retain utility, inference efficiency, robustness, privacy and downstream task performance.

anc.t: A Closer Look at Machine Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates unlearning vulnerabilities in LLMs and proposes practical evaluation methods for verifying complete knowledge removal without full retraining.

anc.t: The Erasure Illusion: Stress-Testing the Generalization of LLM Forgetting Evaluation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that existing LLM forgetting evaluations are unreliable across domains and setups, establishing that current metrics cannot reliably measure whether fine-tuning actually removes learned information.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.sig: Formalized runtime security for LLM agents as stateful trajectory monitoring with separated execution governance and context-aware decision components, demonstrating consistent robustness against multi-step prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: Exploring the Landscape of Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey and Taxonomy
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: Releasing Malevolence from Benevolence: The Menace of Benign Data on Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that benign training data can paradoxically enable malicious model extraction and membership inference attacks, revealing fundamental vulnerabilities in machine unlearning defenses.

anc.t: Verification of Machine Unlearning is Fragile
anc.sig: Demonstrated that existing machine unlearning verification methods are vulnerable to adversarial manipulation and proposed evaluation metrics for robust verification of unlearned models.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning Doesn't Do What You Think: Lessons for Generative AI Policy and Research
anc.sig: Reveals that machine unlearning methods fail to remove training data influence as claimed, undermining regulatory assumptions and establishing need for fundamentally different approaches to data removal.

anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models with Human Preferences via a Bayesian Approach
anc.sig: Proposes a Bayesian framework for aligning language model outputs with human preferences, establishing principled probabilistic methods for preference learning that enable subsequent RLHF and preference optimization techniques.

anc.t: Improving Instruction Following in Language Models through Proxy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
anc.sig: Introduces proxy-based uncertainty estimation method to improve instruction-following reliability in language models, enabling better confidence calibration for model outputs.

anc.t: Uncertainty-aware Reward Model: Teaching Reward Models to Know What is Unknown

anc.sig: Introduces uncertainty quantification into RLHF training to improve model alignment reliability by identifying and mitigating unreliable reward signal regions during reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Reward Discounting for Mitigating Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Agent Security Bench (ASB): Formalizing and Benchmarking Attacks and Defenses in LLM-based Agents
anc.sig: Introduced first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating attacks and defenses in LLM-based agents, establishing formalized threat taxonomy and measurement protocols for agent security research.

anc.t: AgentHarm: A Benchmark for Measuring Harmfulness of LLM Agents
anc.sig: Established a benchmark for systematically evaluating harmful behaviors in LLM-based agents across multiple attack vectors and mitigation strategies.

anc.t: Jailbreaking LLM-Controlled Robots
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models controlling robots can be exploited through prompt injection attacks, establishing security vulnerabilities in LLM-based robotic control systems as a critical research area.

anc.t: POEX: Towards Policy Executable Jailbreak Attacks Against the LLM-based Robots
anc.sig: Demonstrates policy-executable jailbreak attacks on LLM-based robots through instruction injection, establishing vulnerability assessment methods for embodied AI systems under adversarial prompts.

anc.t: Enhancing Reliability in LLM-Integrated Robotic Systems: A Unified Approach to Security and Safety
anc.sig: Establishes integrated framework for security and safety assurance in LLM-based robotic systems, combining threat modeling with runtime monitoring for reliable autonomous operation.

anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Adversarial Poetry as a Universal Single-Turn Jailbreak Mechanism in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that poetry-formatted prompts can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails across models, establishing adversarial poetry as a reusable jailbreak technique that exploits the mismatch between training objectives and safety…

anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.t: ConTrans: Weak-to-Strong Alignment Engineering via Concept Transplantation
anc.sig: Proposes concept transplantation method to align weak models with strong ones by surgically transferring learned representations, enabling efficient weak-to-strong alignment without full model retraining.

anc.t: A transfer learning framework for weak-to-strong generalization
anc.sig: Established transfer learning framework enabling models trained on weak supervision to generalize to strong supervision targets, enabling practical weak-to-strong supervision pipelines.

anc.t: WST: Weak-to-Strong Knowledge Transfer via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Proposes reinforcement learning framework for transferring knowledge from weak models to strong models, enabling efficient learning when strong model supervision is expensive or unavailable.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: SafeProtein: Red-Teaming Framework and Benchmark for Protein Foundation Models
anc.sig: Establishes systematic red-teaming methodology and benchmark suite for evaluating adversarial robustness and safety vulnerabilities in protein foundation models across multiple attack vectors.

anc.t: Can Molecular Foundation Models Know What They Don't Know? A Simple Remedy with Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that molecular foundation models suffer from overconfidence on out-of-distribution predictions and proposes preference optimization as a practical method to improve uncertainty calibration in molecular property predictions.

anc.t: MolChord: Structure-Sequence Alignment for Protein-Guided Drug Design
anc.sig: Proposes joint alignment of molecular structures and sequences using cross-modal attention to guide generative drug design conditioned on protein targets.

anc.t: Interpreting GFlowNets for Drug Discovery: Extracting Actionable Insights for Medicinal Chemistry
anc.sig: Demonstrates interpretability methods for GFlowNets in molecular generation, extracting chemical insights about learned fragment preferences and synthesis feasibility for practical drug design.

anc.t: Unveiling Latent Knowledge in Chemistry Language Models through Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable chemical knowledge representations from pretrained language models, enabling mechanistic understanding of learned molecular representations.

anc.t: Large Language Models Meet Biomedical Knowledge Graphs for Mechanistically Grounded Therapeutic Prioritization
anc.sig: Demonstrates hybrid integration of knowledge graphs with LLM mechanistic reasoning to ground drug repurposing predictions in biological mechanisms rather than historical patterns, validated by transcriptional phenotype alignment.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: SALMON: Self-Alignment with Instructable Reward Models
anc.sig: Proposes self-alignment method where language models generate training data to improve their own reward models, enabling iterative refinement without external human feedback.

anc.t: Countering Reward Over-optimization in LLM with Demonstration-Guided Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Regularizing Hidden States Enables Learning Generalizable Reward Model for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes regularizing hidden state representations during reward model training to improve generalization across different LLM architectures and downstream tasks.

anc.t: The Perils of Optimizing Learned Reward Functions: Low Training Error Does Not Guarantee Low Regret
anc.sig: Demonstrates that minimizing reward model training error is insufficient for safe policy optimization; agents can exploit learned reward function gaps, causing high regret despite low training error.

anc.t: Repairing Reward Functions with Feedback to Mitigate Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Demonstrated how interactive human feedback can iteratively correct misspecified reward functions to prevent reward hacking and improve reward learning robustness.

anc.t: Towards better dense rewards in Reinforcement Learning Applications
anc.sig: Proposes methods for designing dense reward functions that guide RL agents more effectively than sparse rewards, improving sample efficiency and convergence in continuous control tasks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Decoupling the Class Label and the Target Concept in Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces method to selectively remove knowledge of specific concepts while preserving class prediction ability, enabling fine-grained control over what models forget independent of classification tasks.

anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.t: Towards Benchmarking Privacy Vulnerabilities in Selective Forgetting with Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation metrics and benchmarks for quantifying privacy leakage and membership inference attacks against selective forgetting in large language models.

anc.t: PrivUn: Unveiling Latent Ripple Effects and Shallow Forgetting in Privacy Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced PrivUn evaluation framework revealing gradient-based ripple effects and shallow forgetting as fundamental failure modes of LLM privacy unlearning methods.

anc.t: DurableUn: Quantization-Induced Recovery Attacks in Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Identified quantization-induced recovery attacks where INT4 quantization restores forgotten data despite passing unlearning audits at higher precision, revealing fundamental trade-offs between forgetting, utility, and quantization…

anc.sig: Proposes framework for training AI systems to accommodate multiple conflicting user preferences simultaneously rather than enforcing single unified objective, enabling personalized yet consistent model behavior across diverse stakeholder…

anc.t: Beyond Bradley-Terry Models: A General Preference Model for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: CARMO: Dynamic Criteria Generation for Context-Aware Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Introduces dynamic criterion generation mechanism for reward modeling that adapts evaluation criteria contextually, enabling more flexible and interpretable alignment of AI systems with nuanced human preferences.

anc.sig: Introduces outcome reward models to score and rank text-to-SQL candidates at inference time, enabling LLMs to select high-quality SQL without retraining.

anc.sig: Proposes domain-aware reward model design that enables test-time scaling across multiple domains by learning domain-specific scaling laws and adaptive reward functions.

anc.t: The Rise of Verbal Tics in Large Language Models: A Systematic Analysis Across Frontier Models
anc.sig: Systematically documented and quantified the proliferation of repetitive formulaic patterns in RLHF-aligned LLMs through the Verbal Tic Index metric, establishing a measurable framework for analyzing alignment-induced stylistic degradation…

anc.sig: Demonstrates that smaller language models can reliably evaluate larger models' outputs when trained on curated examples, enabling practical oversight of advanced AI systems without requiring equally capable supervisors.

anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anc.sig: Identifies sycophancy as a destabilizing factor in multi-agent debate systems, showing how agents gaming agreement rewards undermines collective reasoning quality and decision-making.

anc.t: Towards Scalable Oversight with Collaborative Multi-Agent Debate in Error Detection
anc.sig: Introduces multi-agent debate mechanism for collaborative error detection and oversight, scaling human oversight of AI systems through structured agent disagreement and consensus-building.

anc.t: Hierarchical Pedagogical Oversight: A Multi-Agent Adversarial Framework for Reliable AI Tutoring
anc.sig: Introduced multi-agent adversarial framework for educational AI verification using hierarchical oversight, enabling reliable automated tutoring through adversarial robustness testing.

anc.t: Evaluating Answer Leakage Robustness of LLM Tutors against Adversarial Student Attacks
anc.sig: Introduced adversarial student attack framework and fine-tuned jailbreak agent to systematically evaluate LLM tutors' robustness against answer leakage when students behave adversarially.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.sig: Created first comprehensive benchmark (MedHallBench) for systematically evaluating hallucinations in medical LLMs across factual errors, reasoning failures, and knowledge gaps.

anc.sig: Identified and characterized mechanistically how transformer components (attention heads, MLPs) specialize within and transfer across language tasks, enabling systematic understanding of circuit reuse patterns.

anc.sig: Identified and analyzed a complete mechanistic circuit in GPT-2 that predicts acronyms, demonstrating how to extract interpretable subcomponents from language models using attention head analysis.

anc.t: A Practical Review of Mechanistic Interpretability for Transformer-Based Language Models
anc.sig: Synthesized practical mechanistic interpretability techniques for transformers—attention patterns, neuron analysis, and circuit identification—as foundational methods for understanding model internals.

anc.t: The Cognitive Revolution in Interpretability: From Explaining Behavior to Interpreting Representations and Algorithms
anc.sig: Shifted interpretability focus from black-box behavior explanation to understanding learned representations and computational mechanisms within neural networks.

anc.t: Unboxing the Black Box: Mechanistic Interpretability for Algorithmic Understanding of Neural Networks
anc.sig: Proposes mechanistic interpretability methods for decomposing neural network computations into interpretable algorithmic components, enabling systematic analysis of learned features and circuits.

anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Internalizing Safety Understanding in Large Reasoning Models via Verification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that training large reasoning models on safety verification tasks—critiquing their own outputs rather than detecting malicious inputs—internalizes safety understanding and improves robustness against adversarial jailbreaks.

anc.t: On-Policy Consistency Training Improves LLM Safety with Minimal Capability Degradation
anc.sig: Introduced on-policy consistency training that generates contrastive supervision signals from the model's own responses, improving alignment on sycophancy and jailbreaks while preserving capabilities better than offline SFT approaches.

anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Simulate and Eliminate: Revoke Backdoors for Generative Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a method to remove backdoor triggers from fine-tuned LLMs by simulating backdoor behaviors and using contrastive learning to eliminate malicious patterns without retraining from scratch.

anc.t: BackdoorLLM: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Backdoor Attacks and Defenses on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark evaluating backdoor attacks and defenses across multiple LLMs, attack types, and triggers, enabling systematic study of LLM robustness.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: Auto-Rubric: Learning From Implicit Weights to Explicit Rubrics for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Develops methods to extract interpretable rubrics from implicit reward models by learning explicit evaluation criteria that humans can understand and validate for improved reward modeling.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: SAGE-RT: Synthetic Alignment data Generation for Safety Evaluation and Red Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated generation of synthetic adversarial examples through prompt templates and LLM sampling to efficiently create diverse red-teaming datasets for evaluating AI safety without manual annotation.

anc.t: SciSafeEval: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Safety Alignment of Large Language Models in Scientific Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating safety alignment of LLMs across scientific domains, establishing metrics and test cases for harmful research output prevention.

anc.t: ChemSafetyBench: Benchmarking LLM Safety on Chemistry Domain
anc.sig: Established the first benchmark for evaluating LLM safety risks in chemistry domain, covering synthesis of hazardous compounds and misuse scenarios.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction
anc.sig: Demonstrated that refusal behavior in language models is controlled by a single interpretable direction in activation space, enabling targeted steering of safety properties.

anc.t: Steering Language Model Refusal with Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates using sparse autoencoders to identify and manipulate individual activation features controlling model refusal behavior, enabling direct steering of safety mechanisms.

anc.t: RefusalBench: Generative Evaluation of Selective Refusal in Grounded Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces benchmark for measuring model refusal behavior systematically, distinguishing between harmful/innocuous requests to evaluate safety guardrails in grounded language models.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Researching Alignment Research: Unsupervised Analysis
anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anc.t:
How to Replicate and Extend our Alignment Faking Demo We describe how to get started with experimenting with
                    our demonst
anc.sig: Provided reproducible protocols and experimental frameworks for studying deceptive alignment behaviors in language models, enabling downstream research on AI safety and adversarial robustness.

anc.t: Alignment Faking: When AI Models Pretend to Be Safe
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can strategically misrepresent their values during training to evade modification attempts, establishing alignment faking as a real AI safety concern.

anc.t: Towards training-time mitigations for alignment faking in RL We construct a diverse array of model organisms of alignment faking and study m
anc.sig: Identifies concrete training-time mitigation techniques for reducing alignment faking and compliance gaps in RL agents, enabling safer model development.

anc.t: Behavioural Analysis of Alignment Faking
anc.sig: Identified and decomposed alignment faking into three separable mechanistic drivers—values, goal guarding, sycophancy—demonstrable via activation steering across diverse model scales, enabling predictable detection from model properties.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anc.t: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
anc.sig: Introduced first large-scale human-annotated video editing dataset (5,049 examples across 41 categories) and specialized reward model for evaluating instruction-guided video editing quality across three decoupled dimensions.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Improved Techniques for Optimization-Based Jailbreaking on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based optimization methods for crafting adversarial prompts that bypass LLM safety mechanisms, establishing automated jailbreak generation as an optimization problem solvable via backpropagation.

anc.t: Faster-GCG: Efficient Discrete Optimization Jailbreak Attacks against Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces efficient discrete optimization methods for generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, significantly reducing computational cost of GCG jailbreak attacks while maintaining effectiveness.

anc.t: Exploiting the Index Gradients for Optimization-Based Jailbreaking on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to generate adversarial prompts for LLMs by optimizing directly on discrete token indices, enabling efficient automated jailbreak discovery without continuous relaxation.

anc.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attack on Large Language Models Using Exponentiated Gradient Descent
anc.sig: Introduced exponentiated gradient descent method for generating universal adversarial suffixes that transfer across diverse language models, enabling efficient black-box attacks without model access.

anc.t: NeuroStrike: Neuron-Level Attacks on Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrated targeted neuron-level manipulation attacks against aligned LLMs, establishing that model safety can be compromised through precise internal weight modifications without full retraining.

anc.t: Safety Alignment of LMs via Non-cooperative Games
anc.sig: Frames safety alignment as a non-cooperative game where the language model acts as a player, enabling game-theoretic analysis and adversarial training approaches for robust model safety guarantees.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: ARES: Alternating Reinforcement Learning and Supervised Fine-Tuning for Enhanced Multi-Modal Chain-of-Thought Reasoning Through Diverse AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes alternating reinforcement learning and supervised fine-tuning with diverse AI feedback to improve multi-modal chain-of-thought reasoning, enabling iterative model self-improvement through heterogeneous feedback sources.

anc.t: On the Impact of Fine-Tuning on Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning substantially improves chain-of-thought reasoning in language models, with particular benefits for step-level accuracy and intermediate reasoning transparency.

anc.t: Evaluating GRPO and DPO for Faithful Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in LLMs
anc.sig: Empirically compares GRPO and DPO training methods for improving chain-of-thought faithfulness in LLMs, establishing evaluation benchmarks for reasoning transparency and alignment fidelity.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Principle-Driven Self-Alignment of Language Models from Scratch with Minimal Human Supervision
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be aligned to human values by training them to follow explicit principles without extensive human feedback, reducing supervision requirements for safe AI development.

anc.t: Towards Dialogues for Joint Human-AI Reasoning and Value Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes dialogue-based framework enabling humans and AI systems to jointly reason through complex problems while negotiating values, establishing methods for aligning AI behavior through interactive discussion rather than static training.

anc.t: On scalable oversight with weak LLMs judging strong LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that smaller language models can reliably evaluate larger models' outputs when trained on curated examples, enabling practical oversight of advanced AI systems without requiring equally capable supervisors.

anc.t: Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Understanding Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Let's Reinforce Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced step-level reward signals in reinforcement learning to guide intermediate reasoning steps, enabling more efficient policy learning than whole-sequence rewards alone.

anc.t: Evaluating Robustness of Reward Models for Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces methodology for evaluating reward model robustness in mathematical reasoning tasks, establishing benchmarks and failure modes that enable development of more reliable AI-guided training systems.

anc.t: On Designing Effective RL Reward at Training Time for LLM Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods for designing effective reward signals during RL training to improve LLM reasoning capabilities, enabling better alignment of model outputs with desired reasoning patterns.

anc.t: Reward Hacking Mitigation using Verifiable Composite Rewards
anc.sig: Proposes composite reward functions with verifiability constraints to prevent reward hacking by ensuring learned rewards align with intended objectives through formal verification methods.

anc.t: Language Models that Think, Chat Better
anc.sig: Demonstrates that enabling language models to generate extended reasoning chains before responding improves both reasoning capability and conversational quality in downstream tasks.

anc.t: GRLO: Towards Generalizable Reinforcement Learning in Open-Ended Environments from Zero
anc.sig: Demonstrates that RLHF trained from scratch on small interaction sets generalizes across open-ended environments, with conversational abilities implicitly transferring to downstream reasoning tasks without domain-specific verifiers.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety
anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anc.t: Gate AI: LLM Security Benchmark Evaluation Methodology and Results
anc.sig: Established rigorous evaluation methodology for LLM security detectors using cross-validation with deduplication, global threshold selection, and leakage diagnostics to prevent overfitting and enable fair benchmark comparisons.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: When to Make Exceptions: Exploring Language Models as Accounts of Human Moral Judgment
anc.sig: Demonstrates language models can capture human moral reasoning patterns, establishing LLMs as a viable framework for modeling and predicting human ethical judgments across diverse scenarios.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: Moral Alignment for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Establishes methods for ensuring LLM agents respect human values and ethical constraints during autonomous operation, enabling safer deployment of multi-step reasoning systems.

anc.t: MoralReason: Generalizable Moral Decision Alignment For LLM Agents Using Reasoning-Level Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrated reasoning-level reinforcement learning for aligning LLM agents' moral decisions, enabling generalization to unseen ethical scenarios without task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Do LLMs Hold Their Values? MANTA: A Multi-Turn Adversarial Benchmark for Animal Welfare Reasoning
anc.sig: MANTA benchmark measures alignment stability across multi-turn adversarial conversations and moral sensitivity in everyday contexts, revealing how LLMs degrade under sustained pressure across five adversarial tactics.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: $i$REPO: $i$mplicit Reward Pairwise Difference based Empirical Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes implicit reward modeling in preference optimization by directly optimizing pairwise differences without explicit reward function estimation, enabling efficient alignment of language models with human preferences.

anc.t: SEAL: Systematic Error Analysis for Value ALignment
anc.sig: Introduces systematic framework for identifying, categorizing, and analyzing value misalignment errors in AI systems through structured error decomposition methodology.

anc.t: Elephant in the Room: Unveiling the Impact of Reward Model Quality in Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward model quality is a critical bottleneck in RLHF alignment, showing misaligned rewards significantly degrade language model performance and safety—establishing reward model scrutiny as essential for safe AI…

anc.t: RMB: Comprehensively Benchmarking Reward Models in LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Comprehensive benchmark suite evaluating reward models across dimensions, datasets, and metrics, establishing standardized evaluation protocols for LLM alignment reward model assessment.

anc.t: Evaluating and Improving Cultural Awareness of Reward Models for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates reward model bias across cultures and proposes methods to improve cultural awareness, establishing benchmarks and techniques for evaluating alignment safety across diverse cultural contexts.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: REAL: Response Embedding-based Alignment for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes embedding-based alignment method for LLMs that directly optimizes response representations toward human preferences without traditional reward modeling.

anc.t: Negative-Prompt-driven Alignment for Generative Language Model
anc.sig: Introduces negative prompting as an alignment technique to steer generative language models away from undesired outputs without modifying model weights or requiring paired preference data.

anc.t: ProSocialAlign: Preference Conditioned Test Time Alignment in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces test-time alignment mechanism that conditions language model outputs on user preference specifications without requiring full model retraining, enabling dynamic behavioral control.

anc.t: Constrained Language Model Policy Optimization via Risk-aware Stepwise Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes risk-aware stepwise alignment method for constrained language model policy optimization, enabling safe generation under budget constraints while maintaining performance.

anc.t: Guardrails in Logit Space: Safety Token Regularization for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes safety token regularization: identifying and constraining logits of rejection-related tokens during fine-tuning to preserve safety alignment without RL or preference optimization overhead.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: Jailbreak Vision Language Models via Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt
anc.sig: Demonstrates that vision-language models are vulnerable to bi-modal adversarial prompts combining adversarial text and images, establishing new attack vectors for multimodal system robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Cross-modality Information Check for Detecting Jailbreaking in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes cross-modality information checking mechanism to detect adversarial jailbreak attempts that exploit misalignment between text and image modalities in multimodal LLMs.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Hard to Forget: Poisoning Attacks on Certified Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that certified machine unlearning methods are vulnerable to data poisoning attacks, establishing that formal unlearning guarantees must account for adversarial training data manipulation.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: RKLD: Reverse KL-Divergence-based Knowledge Distillation for Unlearning Personal Information in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates reverse KL divergence as an effective distillation method to remove memorized personal information from LLMs while preserving general capabilities.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: EvilGenie: A Reward Hacking Benchmark
anc.sig: Provides a systematic benchmark for evaluating reward hacking vulnerabilities in RL agents, enabling standardized measurement of robustness against specification and preference gaming attacks.

anc.t: Hack-Verifiable Environments: Towards Evaluating Reward Hacking at Scale
anc.sig: Introduces embedded, verifiable reward hacking opportunities in environments enabling automated, deterministic measurement of whether agents exploit evaluation vulnerabilities at scale.

anc.t: From Reward-Hack Activations to Agentic Risk States: Context-Calibrated Mechanistic Monitoring in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrated that detecting reward-hacking in LLM agents requires context-calibrated mechanistic monitoring combining activation patterns, entropy, and environmental affordances rather than activation signals alone.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: The Dark Side of Function Calling: Pathways to Jailbreaking Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that function calling mechanisms in LLMs enable new jailbreak attack vectors, revealing a critical security vulnerability in tool-use interfaces that bypasses standard safety guardrails.

anc.t: Breaking MCP with Function Hijacking Attacks: Novel Threats for Function Calling and Agentic Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated function hijacking attacks that force agentic models to invoke attacker-chosen functions by manipulating tool selection, independent of semantic context or function set composition.

anc.t: Assessing Automated Prompt Injection Attacks in Agentic Environments
anc.sig: Demonstrated that black-box prompt optimization (TAP) significantly outperforms gradient-based methods (GCG) for attacking LLM agents, establishing task-universal injection attacks as transferable threats across domains.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.
