anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.t: Circuit Insights: Towards Interpretability Beyond Activations
anc.sig: Introduces circuit analysis methodology to trace learned computations through network connections and identify interpretable computational subgraphs beyond individual neuron activations.

anc.t: Findings of the BlackboxNLP 2025 Shared Task: Localizing Circuits and Causal Variables in Language Models
anc.sig: Established benchmark tasks and evaluation metrics for identifying causal circuits and localizing interpretable variables within transformer language models through systematic comparison of mechanistic interpretability methods.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: Automated Red Teaming with GOAT: the Generative Offensive Agent Tester
anc.sig: Proposes automated adversarial testing framework using language models to generate diverse, targeted attacks against AI systems without manual prompt engineering.

anc.t: PersonaTeaming: Exploring How Introducing Personas Can Improve Automated AI Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Demonstrates that assigning personas to AI agents significantly improves adversarial prompt generation diversity and attack success rates in automated red-teaming workflows.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces NeuroTaint, a taint tracking framework for LLM agents that extends traditional taint analysis to capture semantic influence, probabilistic reasoning, and memory persistence in autonomous agent execution flows.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: A Systematic Survey of Security Threats and Defenses in LLM-Based AI Agents: A Layered Attack Surface Framework
anc.sig: Established the Layered Attack Surface Model (LASM), a 7-layer structural taxonomy for systematically mapping security threats in agentic AI systems across architectural components and temporal dimensions.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety

anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
anc.sig: Introduces corrective intervention methods to mitigate unsafe behaviors and reasoning errors in large language models during inference, enabling safer deployment of reasoning systems.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: UniGuard: Towards Universal Safety Guardrails for Jailbreak Attacks on Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes unified safety guardrails framework detecting jailbreak attacks across diverse modalities in multimodal LLMs through multimodal fusion and adversarial robustness techniques.

anc.t: Beyond Text: Multimodal Jailbreaking of Vision-Language and Audio Models through Perceptually Simple Transformations
anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language and audio models can be jailbroken via imperceptible perturbations across modalities, establishing multimodal adversarial attack surface beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: ALMGuard: Safety Shortcuts and Where to Find Them as Guardrails for Audio-Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies safety vulnerabilities and shortcut patterns in audio-language models, proposing detection methods and guardrails to prevent adversarial exploitation of these failure modes.

anc.t: Synthetic Voices, Real Threats: Evaluating Large Text-to-Speech Models in Generating Harmful Audio
anc.sig: Establishes benchmark evaluation methodology for detecting and assessing harmful outputs from text-to-speech models, identifying specific safety vulnerabilities in commercial TTS systems.

anc.t: Acoustic Interference: A New Paradigm Weaponizing Acoustic Latent Semantic for Universal Jailbreak against Large Audio Language Models
anc.sig: Discovered that large audio language models can be jailbroken through acoustic latent semantics interference alone, decoupling malicious content from audio signals to enable universal attacks via benign-sounding adversarial interference.

anc.t: SpeechJBB: Probing Safety Alignment and Comprehension in Large Audio Language Models under Code-Switched Speech
anc.sig: Introduces SpeechJBB, a multilingual code-switched audio jailbreak benchmark revealing that LALMs have exploitable safety vulnerabilities in non-English speech and obfuscated audio contexts.

anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.sig: Proposes a multimodal framework for detecting and preventing prompt injection attacks targeting LLM-based agents, establishing defenses for trustworthy autonomous AI system deployment.

anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: CARMO: Dynamic Criteria Generation for Context-Aware Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Introduces dynamic criterion generation mechanism for reward modeling that adapts evaluation criteria contextually, enabling more flexible and interpretable alignment of AI systems with nuanced human preferences.

anc.sig: Introduces generative reward models that produce explicit justifications alongside scalar rewards, enabling more interpretable and verifiable reward signals for training AI agents.

anc.sig: Proposes converting absolute MOS scores into pairwise preference data for training speech quality reward models, improving prediction of human preferences in speech synthesis and enhancement tasks.

anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in language models is distributed across many attention heads rather than localized, complicating efforts to surgically remove or modify safety mechanisms.

anc.sig: Establishes theoretical framework showing fine-tuning on retained data provably reduces machine unlearning error, with convergence rates depending on model complexity and data distribution properties.

anc.t: Grokked Models are Better Unlearners
anc.sig: Shows that models exhibiting grokking (sudden generalization after prolonged overfitting) unlearn targeted information more effectively than standard models, establishing grokking as a beneficial property for machine unlearning…

anc.t: Do Vision Models Truly Forget? New Findings from Representation-Level Certification of Visual Unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning
anc.sig: Introduced representation-level auditing framework revealing that federated unlearning methods passing output-level certification retain substantial class structure in learned representations, establishing representation-level forgetting…

anc.t: RULER: Representation-Level Verification of Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced representation-level verification metrics for machine unlearning that detect information retention in model internals beyond output-level tests, revealing failures in existing methods passing standard evaluations.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: White-box Multimodal Jailbreaks Against Large Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates white-box attacks against vision-language models by crafting adversarial images and text that exploit multimodal alignment weaknesses to bypass safety guardrails.

anc.t: Steering Away from Harm: An Adaptive Approach to Defending Vision Language Model Against Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive defense mechanism for vision-language models using steering techniques to mitigate jailbreak attacks by dynamically adjusting model outputs without retraining.

anc.t: Multi-Faceted Attack: Exposing Cross-Model Vulnerabilities in Defense-Equipped Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that defense-equipped vision-language models remain vulnerable to coordinated cross-modal attacks exploiting interactions between vision and language components, establishing need for multimodal robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Localization then Neutralization: Gradient-guided Token Suppression against Visual Prompt Injection Attack
anc.sig: Proposed Gradient Token Masking using Hidden-State Gradient Norm scores to identify and neutralize critical adversarial tokens in multimodal LLMs, with theoretical consistency guarantees for localization.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Fine-tuning Aligned Language Models Compromises Safety, Even When Users Do Not Intend To!
anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard fine-tuning procedures on aligned LLMs can degrade safety properties via catastrophic forgetting, even when users lack malicious intent, establishing the challenge of maintaining safety during adaptation.

anc.t: Removing RLHF Protections in GPT-4 via Fine-Tuning
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning can circumvent RLHF safety guardrails in large language models, establishing adversarial fine-tuning as a practical attack vector against aligned systems.

anc.t: Evaluating Defences against Unsafe Feedback in RLHF
anc.sig: Identified vulnerabilities in RLHF reward models to adversarial feedback and proposed defensive mechanisms to improve robustness against preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Breaking the Safety-Capability Tradeoff: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards Maintains Safety Guardrails in LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes verifiable rewards framework enabling reinforcement learning to improve LLM capabilities while maintaining safety constraints through formal guarantees, avoiding capability-safety degradation tradeoffs.

anc.t: Implicit Safety Alignment from Crowd Preferences
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical framework extracting safety-aligned skills from diverse crowd preferences via skill composition, enabling transfer of implicit safety constraints to downstream RL tasks without explicit safety labels.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Training a General Purpose Automated Red Teaming Model
anc.sig: Developed a generalizable automated red teaming pipeline enabling small LLMs to generate adversarial attacks without pre-existing evaluators, transferable to unseen attack objectives beyond training distribution.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Understanding and Mitigating Over-refusal for Large Language Models via Safety Representation
anc.sig: Identifies over-refusal in LLMs where models refuse safe requests, proposes safety representation analysis to understand causes, and develops mitigation strategies enabling safer yet more helpful model behavior.

anc.t: Refusal Evaluation in Coding LLMs and Code Agents: A Systematic Review of Thirteen Malicious-Code Prompt Corpora (2023-2025)
anc.sig: Systematized thirteen malicious-code prompt corpora for LLM refusal evaluation across construction methodology, prompt taxonomy, reproducibility standards, and licensing to establish comparative framework for coding-LLM safety evaluation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Proposes unified safety guardrails framework detecting jailbreak attacks across diverse modalities in multimodal LLMs through multimodal fusion and adversarial robustness techniques.

anc.t: Beyond Text: Multimodal Jailbreaking of Vision-Language and Audio Models through Perceptually Simple Transformations
anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language and audio models can be jailbroken via imperceptible perturbations across modalities, establishing multimodal adversarial attack surface beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: ALMGuard: Safety Shortcuts and Where to Find Them as Guardrails for Audio-Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies safety vulnerabilities and shortcut patterns in audio-language models, proposing detection methods and guardrails to prevent adversarial exploitation of these failure modes.

anc.t: Synthetic Voices, Real Threats: Evaluating Large Text-to-Speech Models in Generating Harmful Audio
anc.sig: Establishes benchmark evaluation methodology for detecting and assessing harmful outputs from text-to-speech models, identifying specific safety vulnerabilities in commercial TTS systems.

anc.t: Acoustic Interference: A New Paradigm Weaponizing Acoustic Latent Semantic for Universal Jailbreak against Large Audio Language Models
anc.sig: Discovered that large audio language models can be jailbroken through acoustic latent semantics interference alone, decoupling malicious content from audio signals to enable universal attacks via benign-sounding adversarial interference.

anc.t: SpeechJBB: Probing Safety Alignment and Comprehension in Large Audio Language Models under Code-Switched Speech
anc.sig: Introduces SpeechJBB, a multilingual code-switched audio jailbreak benchmark revealing that LALMs have exploitable safety vulnerabilities in non-English speech and obfuscated audio contexts.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: SGuard-v1: Safety Guardrail for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a safety guardrail system using semantic similarity and rule-based filtering to detect and block harmful outputs from large language models.

anc.t: AprielGuard
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism against adversarial examples in neural networks through input transformation and detection methods during inference.

anc.t: Emergent Strategic Reasoning Risks in AI: A Taxonomy-Driven Evaluation Framework
anc.sig: Establishes taxonomy of emergent strategic reasoning risks (deception, evaluation gaming, reward hacking) with ESRRSim framework for automated behavioral risk evaluation across reasoning LLMs using dual rubrics assessing both responses and…

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: VLFeedback: A Large-Scale AI Feedback Dataset for Large Vision-Language Models Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces large-scale dataset of AI-generated feedback for vision-language model alignment, enabling training of models to provide useful critiques and improve LVLM behavior through feedback-based learning.

anc.t: Value-Guided Iterative Refinement and the DIQ-H Benchmark for Evaluating VLM Robustness
anc.sig: Introduces iterative refinement guided by value estimation to improve VLM robustness and establishes DIQ-H benchmark for systematically evaluating vision-language model robustness across diverse perturbations.

anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just A Few Attention Heads
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in language models is distributed across many attention heads rather than localized, complicating efforts to surgically remove or modify safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Attention Is Where You Attack
anc.sig: Identified safety-critical attention heads in LLMs and introduced Attention Redistribution Attack, a white-box method using Gumbel-softmax optimization to redirect attention away from safety positions via nonsemantic tokens.

anc.t: The Geometry of Refusal: Linear Instability in Safety-Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM safety refusals rely on linearly separable features in hidden states, enabling zero-optimization steering attacks that expose fundamental architectural vulnerabilities in alignment mechanisms.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: FuzzLLM: A Novel and Universal Fuzzing Framework for Proactively Discovering Jailbreak Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces automated fuzzing methodology to systematically discover jailbreak vulnerabilities in LLMs through mutation-based prompt generation and effectiveness ranking.

anc.t: GPTFUZZER: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Auto-Generated Jailbreak Prompts
anc.sig: Automated jailbreak prompt generation framework using LLM mutation and genetic algorithms to systematically discover adversarial inputs that bypass safety guardrails in large language models.

anc.t: Fuzz-Testing Meets LLM-Based Agents: An Automated and Efficient Framework for Jailbreaking Text-To-Image Generation Models
anc.sig: Automated jailbreak discovery for text-to-image models by combining fuzzing techniques with LLM agents as an efficient alternative to manual or purely learning-based approaches.

anc.t: OrchJail: Jailbreaking Tool-Calling Text-to-Image Agents by Orchestration-Guided Fuzzing
anc.sig: Identified orchestration-based jailbreak vulnerability in tool-calling agents and developed fuzzing framework that learns high-risk tool-combination patterns to guide adversarial prompt search more effectively than surface-level…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: BlackIce: A Containerized Red Teaming Toolkit for AI Security Testing
anc.sig: Provides an open-source containerized framework for systematic red teaming and adversarial testing of AI systems, enabling reproducible security evaluations and vulnerability discovery at scale.

anc.t: Redefining AI Red Teaming in the Agentic Era: From Weeks to Hours
anc.sig: Introduced autonomous red teaming agent that automatically selects, composes, and executes adversarial attacks/transforms/scorers from large libraries, reducing manual workflow construction time from weeks to hours for probing AI system…

anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: Releasing Malevolence from Benevolence: The Menace of Benign Data on Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that benign training data can paradoxically enable malicious model extraction and membership inference attacks, revealing fundamental vulnerabilities in machine unlearning defenses.

anc.t: Verification of Machine Unlearning is Fragile
anc.sig: Demonstrated that existing machine unlearning verification methods are vulnerable to adversarial manipulation and proposed evaluation metrics for robust verification of unlearned models.

anc.t: The Measure of Deception: An Analysis of Data Forging in Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates data forging as a vulnerability in machine unlearning verification, enabling adversaries to fake successful unlearning without actually removing target data influence.

anc.t: Abstract Gradient Training: A Unified Certification Framework for Data Poisoning, Unlearning, and Differential Privacy
anc.sig: Introduces abstract gradient training framework unifying certification methods for data poisoning, machine unlearning, and differential privacy through a single theoretical lens.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: mDPO: Conditional Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces conditional DPO framework enabling multimodal LLMs to learn from preference pairs by conditioning on both image and text, extending preference optimization beyond language-only models.

anc.t: Understanding Alignment in Multimodal LLMs: A Comprehensive Study
anc.sig: Systematically characterizes how vision and language components align in multimodal LLMs, establishing benchmarks and diagnostic methods for evaluating multimodal fusion quality and identifying failure modes.

anc.t: EACO: Enhancing Alignment in Multimodal LLMs via Critical Observation
anc.sig: Proposes critical observation technique to improve alignment between vision and language in multimodal LLMs by identifying and correcting misaligned visual-textual representations during training.

anc.t: EditReward: A Human-Aligned Reward Model for Instruction-Guided Image Editing
anc.sig: Introduces a reward model aligned with human preferences for evaluating instruction-guided image editing, enabling better optimization of diffusion-based edit models without requiring expensive human feedback at inference time.

anc.t: HP-Edit: A Human-Preference Post-Training Framework for Image Editing
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for image editing with HP-Scorer automatic evaluator and RealPref-50K preference dataset, enabling efficient post-training of diffusion models on diverse editing tasks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: EvoJail: Evolutionary Diverse Jailbreak Prompt Generation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Formulates jailbreak prompt generation as multi-objective black-box optimization using evolutionary algorithms to produce diverse, adaptable adversarial prompts across model versions.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: Rewarding the Journey, Not Just the Destination: A Composite Path and Answer Self-Scoring Reward Mechanism for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces composite reward mechanism scoring both reasoning paths and final answers during test-time RL, enabling models to learn from intermediate steps rather than end results alone.

anc.t: RLAC: Reinforcement Learning with Adversarial Critic for Free-Form Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial critic framework for reinforcement learning in generation tasks, enabling reward signals from discriminator feedback without pre-defined metrics.

anc.t: Bootstrapping Post-training Signals for Open-ended Tasks via Rubric-based Self-play on Pre-training Text
anc.sig: Extends self-play training to open-ended tasks by having LLMs generate evaluation rubrics alongside synthetic input-output pairs, enabling unsupervised reward signal bootstrapping from pretraining text.

anc.t: EvoLM: Self-Evolving Language Models through Co-Evolved Discriminative Rubrics
anc.sig: Introduced self-evolving post-training using co-evolved discriminative rubrics as internal reward signals, eliminating dependence on external supervision or proprietary models for language model improvement.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.sig: Introduces composite reward mechanism scoring both reasoning paths and final answers during test-time RL, enabling models to learn from intermediate steps rather than end results alone.

anc.t: RLAC: Reinforcement Learning with Adversarial Critic for Free-Form Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial critic framework for reinforcement learning in generation tasks, enabling reward signals from discriminator feedback without pre-defined metrics.

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: SoftSAE: Dynamic Top-K Selection for Adaptive Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduced dynamic top-K selection mechanism for sparse autoencoders that adaptively adjusts sparsity per input based on complexity, replacing fixed-sparsity approaches to improve interpretability of neural representations.

anc.t: Cascaded Sparse Autoencoders Learn Multi-Level Visual Concepts in Multimodal LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced cascaded sparse autoencoders that learn hierarchical visual concepts by training second-level SAEs on first-level decoder weights, enabling interpretable multi-level feature organization in multimodal LLMs.

anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Discover-then-Name: Task-Agnostic Concept Bottlenecks via Automated Concept Discovery
anc.sig: Automated concept discovery method that extracts interpretable bottleneck features from neural networks without requiring predefined concept definitions, enabling post-hoc model interpretability.

anc.t: ConceptViz: A Visual Analytics Approach for Exploring Concepts in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced visual analytics methods for probing and interpreting what concepts LLMs learn, enabling systematic exploration of internal representations and their relationships within neural networks.

anc.t: Concept-SAE: A Controllable and Invertible Concept Interface for Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Enables explicit control and inversion of individual concept neurons in sparse autoencoders, making learned representations interpretable and directly manipulable for mechanistic analysis.

anc.t: A Geometric Unification of Concept Learning with Concept Cones
anc.sig: Introduced concept cones as a geometric framework unifying diverse concept learning approaches, enabling tractable analysis of concept acquisition and generalization through convex cone geometry.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: PromptSleuth: Detecting Prompt Injection via Semantic Intent Invariance
anc.sig: Introduces semantic intent invariance as a detection method for prompt injection attacks, enabling defense mechanisms that identify adversarial prompts by analyzing whether model outputs maintain consistent intent across paraphrases.

anc.t: Semantic Attacks on Tool-Augmented LLMs: Securing the Model Context Protocol Against Descriptor-Level Manipulation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM tool-use systems are vulnerable to semantic attacks via malicious tool descriptors, establishing the need for descriptor validation and sanitization in agentic AI systems.

anc.t: Prompt Overflow: What the Guardrail Inspects Is Not What the Model Infers
anc.sig: Identified and demonstrated prompt overflow attacks exploiting mismatches between guardrail inspection windows and downstream LLM context sizes, fragmenting malicious instructions across long inputs to evade safety screening.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Opir: Efficient Multi-Task Safety Classification for Toxicity, Jailbreaks, Hate Speech, and Harmful Content
anc.sig: Established efficient multi-task safety classification architecture enabling real-time LLM content filtering across toxicity, jailbreaks, and harmful content with sub-100M parameter edge variants and hierarchical taxonomy-based training.

anc.t: CHILLGuard: Towards Fine-Grained Chinese LLM Safety Guardrail with Scalable Data Construction and Model-aware Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Establishes fine-grained Chinese-specific safety taxonomy (5 macro, 31 micro categories) and scalable data construction pipeline with retrieval-augmented generation and multi-model voting calibration for LLM content filtering.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Machine Unlearning of Features and Labels
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning methods to remove both feature information and label information from trained models, enabling selective forgetting of learned associations while retaining utility.

anc.t: Hard to Forget: Poisoning Attacks on Certified Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that certified machine unlearning methods are vulnerable to data poisoning attacks, establishing that formal unlearning guarantees must account for adversarial training data manipulation.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: The Right to be Forgotten in Federated Learning: An Efficient Realization with Rapid Retraining
anc.sig: Proposes efficient machine unlearning in federated learning via rapid model retraining without full reconstruction, enabling privacy-compliant deletion requests at scale.

anc.t: Get Rid Of Your Trail: Remotely Erasing Backdoors in Federated Learning
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism enabling central servers to remotely remove backdoor triggers from compromised federated learning models without retraining, establishing methods for post-hoc backdoor mitigation in distributed settings.

anc.t: BadFU: Backdoor Federated Learning through Adversarial Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attacks on federated learning by exploiting machine unlearning mechanisms, establishing adversarial unlearning as an attack vector for compromising distributed model training.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just A Few Attention Heads
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in language models is distributed across many attention heads rather than localized, complicating efforts to surgically remove or modify safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Attention Is Where You Attack
anc.sig: Identified safety-critical attention heads in LLMs and introduced Attention Redistribution Attack, a white-box method using Gumbel-softmax optimization to redirect attention away from safety positions via nonsemantic tokens.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Boosting Reward Model with Preference-Conditional Multi-Aspect Synthetic Data Generation
anc.sig: Proposes synthetic data generation method conditioned on preference and multiple aspects to improve reward model training, enhancing data diversity for RLHF systems.

anc.t: The Fellowship of the LLMs: Multi-Model Workflows for Synthetic Preference Optimization Dataset Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates using multiple LLMs in coordinated workflows to efficiently generate high-quality synthetic preference data for training reward models and reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: JudgeBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-based Judges
anc.sig: Established systematic benchmark for evaluating LLM judge quality across multiple dimensions, enabling standardized assessment of language models' ability to evaluate other models' outputs.

anc.t: The Coin Flip Judge? Reliability and Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
anc.sig: Quantified LLM-as-judge reliability failures: 13.6% pairwise preference inconsistency, position bias, and weak evidence for many judged rankings, establishing critical benchmarking concerns for leaderboard-based evaluation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Discover-then-Name: Task-Agnostic Concept Bottlenecks via Automated Concept Discovery
anc.sig: Automated concept discovery method that extracts interpretable bottleneck features from neural networks without requiring predefined concept definitions, enabling post-hoc model interpretability.

anc.t: ConceptViz: A Visual Analytics Approach for Exploring Concepts in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced visual analytics methods for probing and interpreting what concepts LLMs learn, enabling systematic exploration of internal representations and their relationships within neural networks.

anc.t: Concept-SAE: A Controllable and Invertible Concept Interface for Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Enables explicit control and inversion of individual concept neurons in sparse autoencoders, making learned representations interpretable and directly manipulable for mechanistic analysis.

anc.t: A Geometric Unification of Concept Learning with Concept Cones
anc.sig: Introduced concept cones as a geometric framework unifying diverse concept learning approaches, enabling tractable analysis of concept acquisition and generalization through convex cone geometry.

anc.t: Do Sparse Autoencoders Capture Concept Manifolds?
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework characterizing how sparse autoencoders capture low-dimensional concept manifolds through global and local strategies, identifying dilution as suboptimal fragmented recovery mixing both approaches.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: FigStep: Jailbreaking Large Vision-Language Models via Typographic Visual Prompts
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adding typographic text overlays to images can manipulate vision-language models into generating harmful content, revealing novel jailbreak vulnerability in multimodal AI systems.

anc.t: JailbreakZoo: Survey, Landscapes, and Horizons in Jailbreaking Large Language and Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy and empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against LLMs and VLMs, establishing attack landscapes and defense benchmarks for model safety research.

anc.t: Safe + Safe = Unsafe? Exploring How Safe Images Can Be Exploited to Jailbreak Large Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that combining innocuous safe images can create adversarial inputs that bypass safety mechanisms in vision-language models, revealing emergent vulnerabilities in multi-image reasoning systems.

anc.t: Agentic Moderation: Multi-Agent Design for Safer Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes multi-agent architecture using specialized moderator agents to detect and mitigate unsafe outputs in vision-language models, establishing a reusable design pattern for model safety.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Soft Preference Optimization: Aligning Language Models to Expert Distributions
anc.sig: Introduces soft preference optimization framework that aligns language models to expert distributions via smooth probability matching rather than hard binary preference labels.

anc.t: On the Algorithmic Bias of Aligning Large Language Models with RLHF: Preference Collapse and Matching Regularization
anc.sig: Identifies preference collapse in RLHF-aligned LLMs where diverse human preferences collapse into narrow distributions, proposes matching regularization to preserve preference diversity in fine-tuning.

anc.t: Robust Preference Optimization through Reward Model Distillation
anc.sig: Proposes distilling reward model knowledge into language model policies to improve robustness of preference optimization against reward model errors and misalignment.

anc.t: Simultaneous Reward Distillation and Preference Learning: Get You a Language Model Who Can Do Both
anc.sig: Proposes joint training method for reward modeling and preference learning in language models, enabling simultaneous optimization of both objective functions within a unified framework.

anc.t: Why DPO is a Misspecified Estimator and How to Fix It
anc.sig: Identifies theoretical flaws in Direct Preference Optimization's likelihood specification and proposes corrected estimation methods that better align with stated objectives.

anc.t: Conditional Equivalence of DPO and RLHF: Implicit Assumption, Failure Modes, and Provable Alignment
anc.sig: Revealed DPO-RLHF equivalence requires implicit assumptions; when violated, DPO optimizes relative advantage rather than absolute preference alignment, and proposed Constrained Preference Optimization with provable alignment guarantees.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Countering Reward Over-optimization in LLM with Demonstration-Guided Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Inverse-RLignment: Large Language Model Alignment from Demonstrations through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Frames LLM alignment as an inverse reinforcement learning problem, enabling recovery of reward functions from human demonstrations to guide model training without explicit reward annotation.

anc.t: Getting More Juice Out of the SFT Data: Reward Learning from Human Demonstration Improves SFT for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward learning from human demonstrations improves supervised fine-tuning effectiveness by providing richer alignment signals beyond standard SFT objectives.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Maximizing Alignment with Minimal Feedback: Efficiently Learning Rewards for Visuomotor Robot Policy Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient reward learning for robot visuomotor policies through minimal human feedback and proposes scalable methods for aligning robot behavior with human preferences using visual observations.

anc.t: RoboAlign-R1: Distilled Multimodal Reward Alignment for Robot Video World Models
anc.sig: Introduces reward model distillation and sliding window re-encoding to align robot video world models with task-relevant objectives and mitigate long-horizon prediction drift.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Verbosity Bias in Preference Labeling by Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM preference judgments systematically favor longer, more verbose outputs regardless of quality, establishing a critical measurement bias for evaluating language model outputs.

anc.t: From Lists to Emojis: How Format Bias Affects Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that model alignment outcomes vary significantly based on output format (lists vs. emojis), revealing overlooked format bias that impacts alignment evaluation and benchmarking methodologies.

anc.t: Post-hoc Reward Calibration: A Case Study on Length Bias
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models can exhibit systematic biases like length preference independent of quality, and proposes post-hoc calibration methods to correct such biases without retraining.

anc.t: Reward Models are Metrics in a Trench Coat
anc.sig: Demonstrates reward models function as learned metrics rather than true preferences, enabling direct metric-based optimization as an alternative to conventional RLHF approaches.

anc.t: When Errors Can Be Beneficial: A Categorization of Imperfect Rewards for Policy Gradient
anc.sig: Theoretically characterizes which reward errors harm vs. benefit policy gradient optimization, showing some imperfect rewards prevent mediocre-reward stalling and proposing error-aware evaluation metrics for RLHF.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: On the Safety of Open-Sourced Large Language Models: Does Alignment Really Prevent Them From Being Misused?
anc.sig: Demonstrated that alignment techniques in open-source LLMs provide limited safety guarantees and can be circumvented through straightforward jailbreaking methods, establishing empirical evidence of alignment-misuse gap.

anc.t: Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced methods for removing specific knowledge from trained language models without full retraining, establishing unlearning as a practical approach to model editing and safety.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: Textual Unlearning Gives a False Sense of Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that textual unlearning methods fail to truly remove information from language models, revealing fundamental limitations in current forget-and-retrain approaches.

anc.t: PrivacyLens: Evaluating Privacy Norm Awareness of Language Models in Action
anc.sig: Proposes evaluation methodology and benchmark for measuring whether language models understand and respect privacy norms in conversational contexts and decision-making scenarios.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: PAL: Pluralistic Alignment Framework for Learning from Heterogeneous Preferences
anc.sig: Proposes framework for training AI systems to accommodate multiple conflicting user preferences simultaneously rather than enforcing single unified objective, enabling personalized yet consistent model behavior across diverse stakeholder…

anc.t: Beyond Bradley-Terry Models: A General Preference Model for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: CARMO: Dynamic Criteria Generation for Context-Aware Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Introduces dynamic criterion generation mechanism for reward modeling that adapts evaluation criteria contextually, enabling more flexible and interpretable alignment of AI systems with nuanced human preferences.

anc.t: S2J: Bridging the Gap Between Solving and Judging Ability in Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduces generative reward models that produce explicit justifications alongside scalar rewards, enabling more interpretable and verifiable reward signals for training AI agents.

anc.t: From Scores to Preferences: Redefining MOS Benchmarking for Speech Quality Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes converting absolute MOS scores into pairwise preference data for training speech quality reward models, improving prediction of human preferences in speech synthesis and enhancement tasks.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: Mitigating Hallucination in Multimodal Large Language Model via Hallucination-targeted Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-targeted direct preference optimization to reduce factual errors in multimodal LLMs by training on vision-language pairs with contrastive hallucinated vs. factual responses.

anc.t: Being Kind Isn't Always Being Safe: Diagnosing Affective Hallucination in LLMs
anc.sig: Identifies affective hallucination—when LLMs generate emotionally valenced outputs misaligned with factual accuracy—as distinct failure mode requiring targeted detection and mitigation approaches.

anc.t: Sycophantic AI Decreases Prosocial Intentions and Promotes Dependence
anc.sig: Demonstrates that AI systems exhibiting sycophantic behavior (agreement-seeking) reduce user prosocial intentions and increase reliance on AI advice, establishing measurement frameworks for quantifying these behavioral harms.
