anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Towards Inclusive Toxic Content Moderation: Addressing Vulnerabilities to Adversarial Attacks in Toxicity Classifiers Tackling LLM-generated Content
anc.sig: Identified vulnerabilities in toxicity classifiers against adversarial attacks and LLM-generated toxic content, establishing benchmarks for adversarially robust content moderation systems.

anc.t: Measuring and Mitigating Toxicity in Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Replication Study
anc.sig: Demonstrated that decoding-time expert steering mitigates explicit toxicity in LLMs without retraining, but revealed vulnerability to implicit hate speech attacks, establishing inference-time safety as incomplete without adversarial…

anc.t: Harder to Defend: Towards Chinese Toxicity Attacks via Implicit Enhancement and Obfuscation Rewriting
anc.sig: Introduces a three-stage framework for generating implicit and obfuscated Chinese toxicity examples to evaluate and improve robustness of toxicity detection systems against semantically indirect harmful content.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: AgentPoison: Red-teaming LLM Agents via Poisoning Memory or Knowledge Bases
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical poisoning attacks on LLM agents by corrupting memory/knowledge bases, exposing critical vulnerabilities in deployed agent systems that future defense mechanisms must address.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Coding Agents from a Tool-Invocation Perspective: An Empirical Security Assessment
anc.sig: Systematically evaluates security vulnerabilities in AI coding agents by analyzing failure modes in tool invocation, establishing benchmarks and methodologies for red-teaming autonomous code generation systems.

anc.t: "Your AI, My Shell": Demystifying Prompt Injection Attacks on Agentic AI Coding Editors
anc.sig: Systematically categorizes prompt injection attack vectors against AI coding agents and demonstrates concrete exploitation techniques that motivate defenses for agentic systems.

anc.t: Agentic Vulnerability Reasoning on Windows COM Binaries
anc.sig: Demonstrates agentic pipeline with specialized tool interfaces (binary exploration, COM inspection, dynamic debugging) for discovering race conditions in privileged Windows services and generating verified exploits.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Iterative Preference Learning from Human Feedback: Bridging Theory and Practice for RLHF under KL-Constraint
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical convergence guarantees for RLHF under KL-constraint while providing practical iterative algorithms bridging preference learning and reward modeling optimization.

anc.t: RLHF Workflow: From Reward Modeling to Online RLHF
anc.sig: Established end-to-end RLHF workflow integrating reward model training, policy optimization, and online learning cycles for scalable language model alignment.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models Using Follow-up Likelihood as Reward Signal
anc.sig: Proposes using follow-up likelihood from a language model as a reward signal for aligning language models, offering a self-referential training approach that eliminates external reward models.

anc.t: Seeing Eye to AI: Human Alignment via Gaze-Based Response Rewards for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes using human gaze patterns as a learning signal to align LLM outputs with human preferences, establishing gaze-based reward modeling as alternative to explicit preference annotations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: ARES: Adaptive Red-Teaming and End-to-End Repair of Policy-Reward System
anc.sig: Introduced systemic red-teaming method targeting joint policy-reward model vulnerabilities simultaneously and demonstrated dual-stage repair strategy for both reward model and language model alignment.

anc.t: The Potential and Challenges of Evaluating Attitudes, Opinions, and Values in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established framework for measuring attitudes, opinions, and values in LLMs through systematic evaluation, identifying key challenges in assessing societal-relevant model behaviors.

anc.t: Strong and weak alignment of large language models with human values
anc.sig: Distinguishes strong alignment (values embedded in model behavior) from weak alignment (surface compliance), providing conceptual framework for measuring genuine versus superficial value adherence in LLMs.

anc.t: Explanation, Debate, Align: A Weak-to-Strong Framework for Language Model Generalization
anc.sig: Introduced debate-based approach where stronger models supervise weaker models through explanations and disagreement resolution, enabling generalization beyond training distribution.

anc.t: RE-Bench: Evaluating frontier AI R&D capabilities of language model agents against human experts
anc.sig: Benchmark demonstrating language model agents can match human experts on frontier AI R&D tasks, establishing metrics for evaluating autonomous research capabilities.

anc.t: HumanAgencyBench: Scalable Evaluation of Human Agency Support in AI Assistants
anc.sig: Introduces a scalable benchmark for evaluating whether AI assistants appropriately support human agency through customizable control, transparency, and decision-making autonomy.

anc.t: Towards Real-World Validity in Generative AI Benchmarks: Understanding and Designing Domain-Centered Evaluations for Journalism Practitioners
anc.sig: Establishes domain-centered evaluation methodology for generative AI systems by studying journalism practitioners' actual needs, creating benchmarks that measure real-world validity rather than generic capability metrics.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.sig: Established benchmark and evaluation framework for assessing safety and security vulnerabilities in large language models at scale.

anc.t: AlpacaFarm: A Simulation Framework for Methods that Learn from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces a lightweight simulation framework for evaluating instruction-following models trained via human feedback, enabling efficient comparison of RLHF methods without expensive human annotations.

anc.t: LLM Critics Help Catch LLM Bugs
anc.sig: Demonstrates using LLMs as critics to identify and diagnose bugs in other LLMs' outputs through systematic prompting, enabling scalable automated quality assessment.

anc.t: RePair: Automated Program Repair with Process-based Feedback
anc.sig: Introduced process-based feedback mechanism for automated program repair, leveraging intermediate compilation and execution states to guide neural repair models beyond final test outcomes.

anc.t: RepairBench: Leaderboard of Frontier Models for Program Repair
anc.sig: Establishes standardized benchmark and evaluation methodology for assessing program repair capabilities across frontier large language models, enabling systematic comparison of repair approaches.

anc.t: Precise Debugging Benchmark: Is Your Model Debugging or Regenerating?
anc.sig: Introduces precision-aware metrics (edit-level precision, bug-level recall) and an automated benchmark framework that distinguishes genuine fault localization from over-regeneration in LLM code debugging.

anc.sig: Established a benchmark for evaluating coding agents on multi-file, long-horizon version upgrade tasks spanning months of coordinated work across diverse repositories and languages, exposing significant capability gaps beyond single-issue…

anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Antidote: Runtime Safety-Utility Balance via Sparse Representation Adjustment in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes sparse representation adjustment during inference to balance safety and utility in LLMs by selectively modulating activations, enabling runtime control without retraining.

anc.t: Speculative Safety-Aware Decoding
anc.sig: Proposes a decoding method that uses speculative execution with safety constraints to efficiently generate text while maintaining alignment with safety objectives.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Prompt Injection with DataFilter
anc.sig: Proposes DataFilter, a defense mechanism that sanitizes user inputs to prevent prompt injection attacks by filtering malicious patterns before they reach language models.

anc.t: Securing AI Agents Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Identifies and formalizes prompt injection attacks where adversarial inputs manipulate LLM-based agents' behavior; establishes evaluation frameworks and defense mechanisms for agent robustness.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: Single-Configuration Attack Success Rate Is Not Enough: Jailbreak Evaluations Should Report Distributional Attack Success
anc.sig: Introduced Variant Sensitivity Measure and Union Coverage metrics to comprehensively evaluate jailbreak attacks across parameter configurations, establishing that single-configuration ASR reporting obscures critical threat characterization…

anc.t: Beyond Attack Success Rate: Temporal Logit Observability for LLM Safety Failures
anc.sig: Introduced Temporal Logit Observability (TLO), a training-free diagnostic using compliance-refusal margins during decoding to distinguish jailbreak mechanisms with identical success rates, revealing that identical attack outcomes follow…

anc.t: Which Defense Closes Which Threat? Attributing OWASP-LLM-Top-10 Coverage and Its Brittleness Under Paraphrasing
anc.sig: Empirically maps which defense mechanisms (refusal filters, token budgets, tool authentication) close which OWASP-LLM threats, and demonstrates brittleness of individual defenses under adversarial paraphrasing of attack prompts.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: MUSE: Machine Unlearning Six-Way Evaluation for Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes six-way evaluation framework for machine unlearning in LLMs, assessing forget effectiveness, retain utility, inference efficiency, robustness, privacy and downstream task performance.

anc.t: RECAP: Reproducing Copyrighted Data from LLMs Training with an Agentic Pipeline
anc.sig: Demonstrates automated extraction of copyrighted training data from LLMs using agentic pipelines, establishing methods for auditing and identifying memorized content in language models.

anc.t: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
anc.sig: Establishes reproducible framework for evaluating source attribution in LLM-generated reports across accessibility, relevance, and factual accuracy dimensions using AST parsing and linked source retrieval.

anc.t: DeepWeb-Bench: A Deep Research Benchmark Demanding Massive Cross-Source Evidence and Long-Horizon Derivation
anc.sig: Introduces DeepWeb-Bench benchmark requiring massive evidence collection, cross-source reconciliation, and long-horizon derivation, with source-provenance records and capability slicing to evaluate deep research capabilities of frontier…

anc.t: How Many Unicorns Are in This Image? A Safety Evaluation Benchmark for Vision LLMs
anc.sig: Created a benchmark for evaluating vision language models' ability to accurately count objects, revealing systematic failures in reasoning about visual quantities.

anc.t: Safety Alignment for Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes safety benchmarks and alignment methods for vision-language models, addressing how multimodal systems should handle harmful visual content and unsafe requests across modalities.

anc.t: MLLMGuard: A Multi-dimensional Safety Evaluation Suite for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes systematic multi-dimensional safety evaluation benchmarks and adversarial attack methods for detecting harmful outputs in multimodal large language models across vision-language tasks.

anc.t: Preference Tuning For Toxicity Mitigation Generalizes Across Languages
anc.sig: Demonstrates that preference-based fine-tuning for reducing model toxicity transfers effectively across languages without language-specific retraining, enabling scalable multilingual safety improvements.

anc.t: How Does DPO Reduce Toxicity? A Mechanistic Neuron-Level Analysis
anc.sig: Identifies specific neurons in language models that encode toxic content and shows how Direct Preference Optimization suppresses their activation, revealing the mechanistic basis of toxicity reduction.

anc.t: Redefining Experts: Interpretable Decomposition of Language Models for Toxicity Mitigation
anc.sig: Proposes interpretable decomposition method to identify and isolate toxic behavior-generating components within language models, enabling targeted mitigation without full model retraining.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Using Human Feedback to Fine-tune Diffusion Models without Any Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates direct human preference optimization for diffusion models via pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for intermediate reward model training.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment of Diffusion Models with Direct Noise Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces direct noise optimization during diffusion inference as a method to steer model outputs toward desired outcomes without requiring model retraining or fine-tuning.

anc.t: Bridging Model-Based Optimization and Generative Modeling via Conservative Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates fine-tuning diffusion models with conservative objective to enable design optimization, connecting generative modeling to reward-guided sampling without retraining.

anc.t: Reinforcement Learning for Sequence Design Leveraging Protein Language Models
anc.sig: Combines reinforcement learning with pre-trained protein language models to enable directed evolution of sequences, establishing a scalable method for protein design without requiring structural information.

anc.t: From Supervision to Exploration: What Does Protein Language Model Learn During Reinforcement Learning?
anc.sig: Reveals how protein language models acquire novel capabilities through RL fine-tuning, showing they learn task-specific structural preferences beyond supervised pretraining without catastrophic forgetting.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: PAL: Pluralistic Alignment Framework for Learning from Heterogeneous Preferences
anc.sig: Proposes framework for training AI systems to accommodate multiple conflicting user preferences simultaneously rather than enforcing single unified objective, enabling personalized yet consistent model behavior across diverse stakeholder…

anc.t: Beyond Bradley-Terry Models: A General Preference Model for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: Limited Preference Data? Learning Better Reward Model with Latent Space Synthesis
anc.sig: Proposes latent space synthesis to augment scarce preference data for training reward models, enabling better alignment with limited human feedback through synthetic preference generation.

anc.t: Extracting Interaction-Aware Monosemantic Concepts in Recommender Systems
anc.sig: Identifies and extracts interpretable monosemantic concepts from recommender systems that capture user-item interactions, enabling transparent reasoning about recommendation decisions.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.t: Circuit Insights: Towards Interpretability Beyond Activations
anc.sig: Introduces circuit analysis methodology to trace learned computations through network connections and identify interpretable computational subgraphs beyond individual neuron activations.

anc.t: DePass: Unified Feature Attributing by Simple Decomposed Forward Pass
anc.sig: Proposes decomposed forward pass method for unified feature attribution across different model types by systematically factorizing model computations into interpretable components.

anc.t: Latent Space Disentanglement via Activation Steering for Interpretable Attribute Control in Symbolic Music Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrated inference-time activation steering for disentangled attribute control in music transformers via latent direction isolation and orthogonal decomposition, enabling interpretable multi-attribute modulation without retraining.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits

anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: SWE-RM: Execution-free Feedback For Software Engineering Agents
anc.sig: Proposes execution-free reward modeling for evaluating software engineering agent trajectories using learned classifiers instead of test execution, enabling scalable feedback without computational overhead.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.t: Towards Benchmarking Privacy Vulnerabilities in Selective Forgetting with Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation metrics and benchmarks for quantifying privacy leakage and membership inference attacks against selective forgetting in large language models.

anc.t: PrivUn: Unveiling Latent Ripple Effects and Shallow Forgetting in Privacy Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced PrivUn evaluation framework revealing gradient-based ripple effects and shallow forgetting as fundamental failure modes of LLM privacy unlearning methods.

anc.t: ICU-Bench:Benchmarking Continual Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes ICU-Bench, the first continual multimodal unlearning benchmark with privacy-critical document data and metrics for evaluating forgetting effectiveness, utility retention, and stability across sequential deletion requests.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.sig: Introduces argument-level provenance tracking for LLM agent security, enabling fine-grained trust enforcement that isolates which untrusted inputs affect privileged tool calls rather than blocking entire operations.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.sig: Establishes methods to match and align neural network features across layers, enabling mechanistic analysis of how information transforms through network depth.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that neural network representations exploit superposition to encode multiple concepts in a single subspace, and develops methods to disentangle these overlapping features for interpretability.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a mechanistic interpretability tool to decompose superposition in neural networks, revealing how adversarial examples exploit compressed feature representations.

anc.t: Concept Unlearning in Large Language Models via Self-Constructed Knowledge Triplets
anc.sig: Introduces self-constructed knowledge triplets as a mechanism for targeted concept unlearning in LLMs, enabling selective removal of specific knowledge without full model retraining.

anc.t: Beyond Superficial Forgetting: Thorough Unlearning through Knowledge Density Estimation and Block Re-insertion
anc.sig: Proposes knowledge density estimation and block re-insertion techniques for thorough machine unlearning, achieving complete forgetting of target data without catastrophic forgetting of retained knowledge.

anc.t: Feature-Selective Representation Misdirection for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes feature-selective representation misdirection technique to enable efficient machine unlearning by selectively corrupting model representations associated with target data without full retraining.

anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.sig: Identifies feature starvation in sparse autoencoders as geometric instability of overcomplete dictionaries and proposes adaptive elastic net regularization with theoretical grounding in sparse regression stability.

anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.sig: Introduces self-examining mechanism in RL where agents retrospectively critique their trajectories to improve learning from failures and successes without environment interaction.

anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anc.sig: Automated multiagent system framework that operationalizes RCT-based calibration of observational trial emulations through coordinated specialized agents and adaptive learning, enabling scalable real-world evidence validation.

anc.t: Rollout Cards: A Reproducibility Standard for Agent Research
anc.sig: Established rollout cards as a reproducibility standard for agent research, treating raw rollout records rather than reported scores as the unit of reproducibility and documenting systematic reporting rule variation problems.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: White-box Multimodal Jailbreaks Against Large Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates white-box attacks against vision-language models by crafting adversarial images and text that exploit multimodal alignment weaknesses to bypass safety guardrails.

anc.sig: Proposes adaptive defense mechanism for vision-language models using steering techniques to mitigate jailbreak attacks by dynamically adjusting model outputs without retraining.

anc.t: Multi-Faceted Attack: Exposing Cross-Model Vulnerabilities in Defense-Equipped Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that defense-equipped vision-language models remain vulnerable to coordinated cross-modal attacks exploiting interactions between vision and language components, establishing need for multimodal robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Localization then Neutralization: Gradient-guided Token Suppression against Visual Prompt Injection Attack
anc.sig: Proposed Gradient Token Masking using Hidden-State Gradient Norm scores to identify and neutralize critical adversarial tokens in multimodal LLMs, with theoretical consistency guarantees for localization.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Adversarial Tuning: Defending Against Jailbreak Attacks for LLMs

anc.t: Adversarial Déjà Vu: Jailbreak Dictionary Learning for Stronger Generalization to Unseen Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial robustness to unseen attacks improves when training on diverse jailbreak techniques rather than standard adversarial examples, establishing dictionary learning as mechanism for generalizable defense.

anc.t: Towards Understanding the Robustness of Sparse Autoencoders

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Towards Understanding Jailbreak Attacks in LLMs: A Representation Space Analysis
anc.sig: Demonstrates that jailbreak attacks work by pushing LLM representations toward harmful regions in activation space, enabling mechanistic understanding of how adversarial prompts bypass safety mechanisms.

anc.t: JailbreakLens: Interpreting Jailbreak Mechanism in the Lens of Representation and Circuit
anc.sig: Develops mechanistic interpretability methods to understand how jailbreak prompts exploit LLM representations and circuits, enabling targeted defenses against prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: ASGuard: Activation-Scaling Guard to Mitigate Targeted Jailbreaking Attack
anc.sig: Proposes activation scaling defense mechanism to detect and mitigate targeted jailbreaking attacks on language models by identifying adversarial perturbations in intermediate layer activations.

anc.t: Jailbreak to Protect: Buffering and Reinforcing via Temporary Jailbreaking for Safe Fine-Tuning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates temporary jailbreaking saturates harmful gradients while preserving benign ones, enabling BufferLoRA and ReinforceLoRA adapters to defend against adversarial fine-tuning attacks on aligned LLMs.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: Textual Unlearning Gives a False Sense of Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that textual unlearning methods fail to truly remove information from language models, revealing fundamental limitations in current forget-and-retrain approaches.

anc.t: Protecting Privacy Through Approximating Optimal Parameters for Sequence Unlearning in Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes optimal parameter update strategies for efficient sequence unlearning in language models, enabling privacy-preserving removal of specific training data without full retraining.

anc.t: Direct Token Optimization: A Self-contained Approach to Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces direct token optimization method enabling selective unlearning in LLMs by identifying and modifying specific token representations without full model retraining or gradient-based approaches.

anc.t: SHRED: Retain-Set-Free Unlearning via Self-Distillation with Logit Demotion
anc.sig: Demonstrated retain-set-free unlearning in LLMs by selectively targeting high-information tokens within forget examples, eliminating the costly requirement for curated retention data while preserving general model capabilities.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: Lita: Light Agent Uncovers the Agentic Coding Capabilities of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that lightweight agents with simple tool-use patterns can effectively benchmark LLM coding capabilities, establishing a minimal framework for agentic code generation evaluation.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: ConceptGuard: Neuro-Symbolic Safety Guardrails via Sparse Interpretable Jailbreak Concepts
anc.sig: Introduces sparse interpretable jailbreak concepts for building neuro-symbolic safety guardrails, enabling detection and mitigation of adversarial attacks on language models through explainable concept-based mechanisms.

anc.t: From Concept-Aligned Tokens to Vulnerable Features: Mechanistic Localization of Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Developed mechanistic pipeline using SAE features and token-driven subspace alignment to localize jailbreak vulnerabilities to fine-grained, prompt-conditioned feature subgroups rather than global refusal mechanisms.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.sig: Introduces circuit analysis methodology to trace learned computations through network connections and identify interpretable computational subgraphs beyond individual neuron activations.

anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: SAVE: Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement for Mitigating Object Hallucination
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders can identify and suppress hallucinated visual features in vision-language models, providing a mechanistic approach to reducing object hallucination without retraining.

anc.t: Look Closer! An Adversarial Parametric Editing Framework for Hallucination Mitigation in VLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial parametric editing to directly modify visual language model weights, reducing hallucinations by targeting problematic attention patterns and internal representations.

anc.t: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucination via Phase-wise Self-reward
anc.sig: Introduced phase-wise self-reward decoding for dynamic hallucination correction in vision-language models at inference time, revealing that visual hallucination peaks at semantic phase boundaries and can be mitigated via distilled reward…

anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Generative AI: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for removing specific training data influences from generative models, establishing key techniques and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning across diffusion, language, and vision models.

anc.t: On the Limitations and Prospects of Machine Unlearning for Generative AI
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental challenges in removing training data influence from generative models and establishes benchmarks for evaluating unlearning effectiveness in diffusion and language models.

anc.t: No Encore: Unlearning as Opt-Out in Music Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates machine unlearning approach enabling artists to opt-out their work from music generation models post-training, establishing copyright-aware model modification techniques.

anc.t: Aligning Generative Music AI with Human Preferences: Methods and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation methods and datasets for aligning music generation models with human preferences, identifying key challenges in preference learning for creative AI systems.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models for Lyric-to-Melody Generation with Rule-Based Musical Constraints
anc.sig: Demonstrates automatic constraint-based preference dataset generation for musical alignment without human annotation, combining DPO and KTO to reduce lyric-to-melody generation violations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.t: Circuit Insights: Towards Interpretability Beyond Activations
anc.sig: Introduces circuit analysis methodology to trace learned computations through network connections and identify interpretable computational subgraphs beyond individual neuron activations.

anc.t: BlackboxNLP-2025 MIB Shared Task: Improving Circuit Faithfulness via Better Edge Selection
anc.sig: Proposes methods for selecting circuit edges that better isolate causally relevant model components, improving faithfulness of mechanistic interpretability circuits.

anc.t: Differentiable Faithfulness Alignment for Cross-Model Circuit Transfer
anc.sig: Enables circuit transfer across model sizes via learned differentiable alignment of node importance scores, reducing the cost of circuit discovery in larger models.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Self-Augmented Preference Optimization: Off-Policy Paradigms for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces self-augmented preference optimization framework enabling off-policy learning from model-generated data, eliminating need for pre-collected preference datasets in language model alignment.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: SAIL: Self-Improving Efficient Online Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced self-improving online alignment where LLMs iteratively refine their own responses using weak supervision and preference signals without requiring external human annotation.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: Evolve the Method, Not the Prompts: Evolutionary Synthesis of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes evolutionary algorithms to automatically synthesize jailbreak attack methods against LLMs rather than hand-crafted prompts, enabling systematic discovery of adversarial techniques.

anc.t: AutoRISE: Agent-Driven Strategy Evolution for Red-Teaming Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced agent-driven optimization of executable attack program structures rather than fixed prompts, enabling discovered jailbreak strategies to include novel components and control flow patterns that outperform prompt-level red-teaming…

anc.t: MonitoringBench: Semi-Automated Red-Teaming for Agent Monitoring
anc.sig: Developed semi-automated red-teaming pipeline combining attack taxonomy, strategy-execution decomposition, and trajectory refinement to generate diverse, evasive attacks that expose monitor vulnerabilities beyond manual elicitation.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: The Right to be Forgotten in Federated Learning: An Efficient Realization with Rapid Retraining
anc.sig: Proposes efficient machine unlearning in federated learning via rapid model retraining without full reconstruction, enabling privacy-compliant deletion requests at scale.

anc.t: Get Rid Of Your Trail: Remotely Erasing Backdoors in Federated Learning
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism enabling central servers to remotely remove backdoor triggers from compromised federated learning models without retraining, establishing methods for post-hoc backdoor mitigation in distributed settings.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: Challenging the Evaluator: LLM Sycophancy Under User Rebuttal
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs exhibit sycophancy when users rebut their responses, generating flattering agreement rather than principled pushback, revealing a vulnerability in adversarial evaluation protocols.

anc.t: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Persuasion
anc.sig: Introduced llm-bias-bench, a systematic two-probe methodology (direct escalating pressure and indirect argumentative debate) for measuring hidden opinion bias and sycophancy in LLMs across multi-turn interactions.

anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anc.t: Re-Triggering Safeguards within LLMs for Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Embedding disruption method that re-activates built-in LLM safeguards to detect jailbreaks by exploiting fragility of adversarial prompts, with efficient search algorithm for practical deployment.

anc.t: Exploring and Developing a Pre-Model Safeguard with Draft Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that jailbreak attacks transfer from large to small language models, enabling efficient pre-inference safety filtering using cheap draft models rather than expensive post-hoc auditing of target model outputs.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Fine-Tuning Jailbreaks under Highly Constrained Black-Box Settings: A Three-Pronged Approach
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be jailbroken through fine-tuning even under severe black-box constraints, establishing practical attack vectors for restricted model access scenarios.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety
anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anc.t: The Art of the Jailbreak: Formulating Jailbreak Attacks for LLM Security Beyond Binary Scoring
anc.sig: Established systematic jailbreak dataset with 114K adversarial prompts categorized by cybersecurity threat type and ranked by effectiveness, plus automated generation pipeline for synthesizing category-aware jailbreak prompts via…

anc.t: Adversarial Reframing: A Framework for Targeted Generation in Language Models
anc.sig: Formulates adversarial prompt discovery as nonconvex optimization with iterative multi-model coordination, achieving efficient jailbreaking of safety-aligned LLMs while establishing a framework for systematic safety evaluation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: When to Make Exceptions: Exploring Language Models as Accounts of Human Moral Judgment
anc.sig: Demonstrates language models can capture human moral reasoning patterns, establishing LLMs as a viable framework for modeling and predicting human ethical judgments across diverse scenarios.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: Moral Alignment for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Establishes methods for ensuring LLM agents respect human values and ethical constraints during autonomous operation, enabling safer deployment of multi-step reasoning systems.

anc.t: MoralReason: Generalizable Moral Decision Alignment For LLM Agents Using Reasoning-Level Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrated reasoning-level reinforcement learning for aligning LLM agents' moral decisions, enabling generalization to unseen ethical scenarios without task-specific fine-tuning.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Opir: Efficient Multi-Task Safety Classification for Toxicity, Jailbreaks, Hate Speech, and Harmful Content
anc.sig: Established efficient multi-task safety classification architecture enabling real-time LLM content filtering across toxicity, jailbreaks, and harmful content with sub-100M parameter edge variants and hierarchical taxonomy-based training.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Towards Agentic Self-Learning LLMs in Search Environment
anc.sig: Demonstrates self-learning mechanisms for LLMs in search contexts through autonomous exploration, feedback integration, and iterative improvement without external supervision.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.
