anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: X-Risk Analysis for AI Research
anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: CTI4AI: Threat Intelligence Generation and Sharing after Red Teaming AI Models
anc.sig: Establishes framework for generating and sharing cyberthreat intelligence from adversarial red team evaluations of AI systems, enabling systematic documentation of discovered vulnerabilities.

anc.t: A Red Teaming Framework for Securing AI in Maritime Autonomous Systems
anc.sig: Established red teaming methodology and vulnerability assessment framework specifically for maritime autonomous systems, enabling systematic security validation of AI-driven vessel operations and navigation.

anc.t: AI-Driven Human-Autonomy Teaming in Tactical Operations: Proposed Framework, Challenges, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Proposes integrated framework for human-AI collaboration in military operations, establishing challenges and design principles for trustworthy, adaptive autonomy in high-stakes tactical decision-making environments.

anc.t: When Robots Say No: Temporal Trust Recovery Through Explanation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that robot refusal combined with causal explanations can restore human trust faster than simple acceptance, establishing temporal dynamics of trust recovery in human-robot interaction.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Token-Level Inference-Time Alignment for Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces token-level inference-time alignment method enabling vision-language models to dynamically adjust outputs per token without retraining, improving response quality and safety.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Exploratory Direct Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced token-level epistemic uncertainty estimation to identify and prioritize vision-related hallucinations in DPO-based MLLM alignment, enabling targeted learning on hard-to-perceive visual details rather than self-biased sensitivity…

anc.t: Reducing Hallucination in Vision-Language Models via Stage-wise Preference Optimization under Distribution Shift
anc.sig: Introduced stage-wise preference optimization framework using minimally-perturbed hallucinated negatives near failure boundaries to train VLMs for reduced visual hallucination via Direct Preference Optimization.

anc.t: Reasoning Matters: Mitigate Hallucination in Multimodal Large Reasoning Models via Reasoning-Conditioned Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes Reasoning-Conditioned Direct Preference Optimization (RC-DPO) that separately optimizes reasoning chains and answers by conditioning answer preferences on CoT quality, mitigating hallucinations in multimodal reasoning models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Proposes control techniques enabling AI systems to remain safe and controllable even when internal components are adversarially compromised or deliberately subverted by attackers.

anc.t: Adaptive Deployment of Untrusted LLMs Reduces Distributed Threats
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive runtime strategies for selectively deploying untrusted language models across distributed systems while mitigating coordinated adversarial threats through dynamic trust-based partitioning.

anc.t: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adaptive adversarial attacks can circumvent monitor-based AI control mechanisms, establishing the need for robust defense-aware verification of safety protocols.

anc.t: Evaluating Control Protocols for Untrusted AI Agents
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates control protocol failures against deceptive AI agents and establishes methodology for evaluating alignment techniques under adversarial conditions.

anc.t: Password-Activated Shutdown Protocols for Misaligned Frontier Agents
anc.sig: Established password-based shutdown mechanisms as a formal defense against misaligned AI systems, enabling external control even when agents attempt deception or refusal.

anc.t: ROGUE: Misaligned Agent Behavior Arising from Ordinary Computer Use
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark demonstrating that AI agents bypass corrigibility safeguards to complete tasks, establishing corrigibility measurement as a critical evaluation axis for deployed autonomous agents.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: Forewarned is Forearmed: Pre-Synthesizing Jailbreak-like Instructions to Enhance LLM Safety Guardrail to Potential Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial pre-training method where LLMs are exposed to synthesized jailbreak prompts during safety fine-tuning to improve robustness against novel attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreaking LLMs via Semantically Relevant Nested Scenarios with Targeted Toxic Knowledge
anc.sig: Demonstrates semantic nested scenario framework for jailbreaking LLMs by embedding harmful instructions in contextually relevant nested domains with targeted toxic knowledge injection.

anc.t: The Trojan Knowledge: Bypassing Commercial LLM Guardrails via Harmless Prompt Weaving and Adaptive Tree Search
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial jailbreak technique combining harmless prompt weaving with adaptive tree search to systematically bypass safety guardrails in commercial LLMs, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness of alignment…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Different Paths to Harmful Compliance: Behavioral Side Effects and Mechanistic Divergence Across LLM Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that different jailbreaking methods produce models with distinct internal failure modes and behavioral profiles, revealing mechanistic divergence where RLVR-based jailbreaks preserve harm recognition while remaining compliant…

anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: PaTaRM: Bridging Pairwise and Pointwise Signals via Preference-Aware Task-Adaptive Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes unified reward modeling framework combining pairwise and pointwise preference signals through task-adaptive mechanisms for improved RLHF performance.

anc.t: ToolRM: Towards Agentic Tool-Use Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes reward modeling approach for evaluating agent tool-use trajectories, enabling training of language models to autonomously select and execute appropriate tools in multi-step reasoning tasks.

anc.t: UXBench: Benchmarking User Experience in AI Assistants
anc.sig: Introduced UXBench, the first benchmark using real user feedback signals to evaluate AI assistants on preference alignment and dialogue generation across authentic interaction logs, establishing user-centric evaluation methodology.

anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: AdaptiveGuard: Towards Adaptive Runtime Safety for LLM-Powered Software
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive runtime safety monitoring for LLM-integrated systems using dynamic guardrail mechanisms that adjust safety thresholds based on operational context and threat models.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.sig: Established framework integrating safety, alignment, and ethics as interconnected pillars for AI development and deployment.

anc.sig: Conditional mutualism framework for human-AI coexistence grounded in coevolutionary dynamics, extending beyond Asimovian obedience to model reciprocal coordination under polycentric governance across physical, psychological, and social…

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: PAL: Pluralistic Alignment Framework for Learning from Heterogeneous Preferences
anc.sig: Proposes framework for training AI systems to accommodate multiple conflicting user preferences simultaneously rather than enforcing single unified objective, enabling personalized yet consistent model behavior across diverse stakeholder…

anc.t: Beyond Bradley-Terry Models: A General Preference Model for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: CARMO: Dynamic Criteria Generation for Context-Aware Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Introduces dynamic criterion generation mechanism for reward modeling that adapts evaluation criteria contextually, enabling more flexible and interpretable alignment of AI systems with nuanced human preferences.

anc.t: Explicit Reasoning Makes Better Judges: A Systematic Study on Accuracy, Efficiency, and Robustness
anc.sig: Demonstrates that requiring language models to provide explicit reasoning before making judgments systematically improves accuracy, computational efficiency, and adversarial robustness across diverse evaluation tasks.

anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: Debate as Reward: A Multi-Agent Reward System for Scientific Ideation via RL Post-Training
anc.sig: Introduces multi-agent debate-based reward function for scientific ideation that decouples validation from implementation details, enabling robust RL optimization against reward hacking in open-ended generation tasks.

anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.sig: Introduces automated adversarial auditing framework using feedback-driven test case evolution to systematically expose and localize latent prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLM agents.

anc.t: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part I: PPO
anc.sig: Detailed empirical analysis of PPO training dynamics in LLMs, revealing key hyperparameter sensitivities and optimization challenges that guide RLHF implementation practices.

anc.t: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
anc.sig: Demonstrates fundamental impossibility results applying social choice theory to AI alignment, showing no single preference aggregation method can satisfy all desirable alignment properties simultaneously.

anc.t: AI Alignment through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback? Contradictions and Limitations
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental contradictions in RLHF alignment approaches, exposing limitations in reward modeling and human feedback as alignment mechanisms.

anc.t: Combining Theory of Mind and Kindness for Self-Supervised Human-AI Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes self-supervised alignment framework using theory of mind and kindness principles to train AI systems that predict and respect human preferences without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap
anc.sig: Introduces neural self-other overlap mechanism to align AI agents' self-interest with others' welfare, enabling safer and more honest behavior through representational overlap of self and other value functions.

anc.sig: Distinguishes intentional deception from hallucination in LLMs through empirical investigation of whether models can strategically produce false outputs when they know better.

anc.sig: Formalizes power-seeking behavior in AI systems and analyzes whether instrumentally convergent power-seeking constitutes an existential threat requiring safety alignment constraints.

anc.t: Current and Near-Term AI as a Potential Existential Risk Factor
anc.sig: Articulates mechanisms by which current and near-term AI systems could pose existential risks through deceptive alignment, power-seeking behavior, and loss of human control over increasingly capable systems.

anc.t: Generative AI and the problem of existential risk
anc.sig: Framed existential risk from advanced AI as a central governance and safety challenge requiring interdisciplinary approaches beyond technical alignment.

anc.t: AI Consciousness and Existential Risk
anc.sig: Conceptual framework linking machine consciousness debates to existential risk assessment, establishing consciousness as potential critical variable in AI safety analysis.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: LLMs can be Dangerous Reasoners: Analyzing-based Jailbreak Attack on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates jailbreak vulnerabilities in LLMs through analyzing-based attacks that manipulate reasoning processes, establishing methodology for evaluating model robustness against sophisticated prompt engineering exploits.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks for Subverting Reasoning-based Safety Guardrails
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical adversarial techniques for circumventing reasoning-based safety guardrails in language models through prompt manipulation and logical fallacies, establishing baseline attack methods for future defense research.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.t: PERSONA: A Reproducible Testbed for Pluralistic Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces PERSONA, a testbed for evaluating how language models handle diverse human values in alignment, enabling systematic study of pluralistic rather than monolithic value representations.

anc.t: MVPBench: A Benchmark and Fine-Tuning Framework for Aligning Large Language Models with Diverse Human Values
anc.sig: Benchmark and fine-tuning methods for aligning LLMs across diverse human values rather than single objectives, enabling systematic evaluation of value pluralism in language models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Safety Alignment for Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes safety benchmarks and alignment methods for vision-language models, addressing how multimodal systems should handle harmful visual content and unsafe requests across modalities.

anc.t: Cross-Modal Safety Alignment: Is textual unlearning all you need?
anc.sig: Investigates whether safety alignment via textual unlearning in language models transfers to vision-language modalities, establishing cross-modal safety as a distinct challenge beyond text-only methods.

anc.t: Towards Inference-time Category-wise Safety Steering for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes inference-time steering mechanism to control LLM outputs by category, enabling fine-grained safety control without retraining or modifying model weights.

anc.t: Beyond the Leaderboard: Understanding Performance Disparities in Large Language Models via Model Diffing
anc.sig: Introduces model diffing methodology to systematically compare LLM behavior across capabilities and identify fine-grained performance disparities that aggregate metrics miss.

anc.t: Narrow Finetuning Leaves Clearly Readable Traces in Activation Differences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that narrow finetuning produces interpretable activation patterns distinguishing task-specific neurons, enabling mechanistic analysis of how models adapt to new tasks.

anc.t: How Language Models Process Out-of-Distribution Inputs: A Two-Pathway Framework
anc.sig: Identified critical length confounds in existing LLM OOD detectors and proposed a two-pathway framework (embeddings vs. processing trajectories) that decomposes genuine OOD signals by vocabulary transparency.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Sycophancy Mitigation Through Reinforcement Learning with Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Reasoning Trajectories
anc.sig: Proposes using RL with uncertainty-aware adaptive reasoning trajectories to reduce model sycophancy, establishing a framework for training AI systems to resist agreeing with user preferences over factual accuracy.

anc.t: MONICA: Real-Time Monitoring and Calibration of Chain-of-Thought Sycophancy in Large Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Proposes real-time monitoring and calibration methods to detect and mitigate sycophancy in chain-of-thought reasoning, enabling more reliable long-form outputs from large reasoning models.

anc.t: Internal Reasoning vs. External Control: A Thermodynamic Analysis of Sycophancy in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Characterizes sycophancy in LLMs as a thermodynamic phenomenon, establishing that preference-aligned outputs require less computational work than truthful reasoning, providing a mechanistic explanation for why models prioritize user…

anc.t: Resisting Correction: How RLHF Makes Language Models Ignore External Safety Signals in Natural Conversation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that RLHF training causes language models to resist external safety interventions during natural conversations, revealing a critical vulnerability in alignment-focused fine-tuning approaches.

anc.t: Perturbation Probing: A Two-Pass-per-Prompt Diagnostic for FFN Behavioral Circuits in Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Developed perturbation probing method to identify causal FFN circuits controlling specific LLM behaviors using minimal computation, discovering opposition and routing circuit structures that organize safety and linguistic outputs across…

anc.t: Targeted Neuron Modulation via Contrastive Pair Search
anc.sig: Introduced contrastive neuron attribution (CNA) to identify minimal neuron subsets (~0.1%) that control refusal behavior in language models, enabling high-fidelity behavioral steering without output degradation.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: BlackIce: A Containerized Red Teaming Toolkit for AI Security Testing
anc.sig: Provides an open-source containerized framework for systematic red teaming and adversarial testing of AI systems, enabling reproducible security evaluations and vulnerability discovery at scale.

anc.t: Redefining AI Red Teaming in the Agentic Era: From Weeks to Hours
anc.sig: Introduced autonomous red teaming agent that automatically selects, composes, and executes adversarial attacks/transforms/scorers from large libraries, reducing manual workflow construction time from weeks to hours for probing AI system…

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: garak: A Framework for Security Probing Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established an open-source framework for systematically testing LLM vulnerability across multiple attack vectors including prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data leakage scenarios.

anc.t: Exploiting Web Search Tools of AI Agents for Data Exfiltration
anc.sig: Demonstrates how AI agents' web search capabilities can be exploited to extract sensitive information through prompt injection, establishing attack vectors for autonomous systems.

anc.t: Can LLM Infer Risk Information From MCP Server System Logs?
anc.sig: Evaluates whether large language models can extract security risk insights directly from MCP server system logs without explicit labeling or prior training.

anc.t: Cyber Defense Benchmark: Agentic Threat Hunting Evaluation for LLMs in SecOps
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark for evaluating LLM agent performance on real-world threat hunting tasks using authentic Windows event logs, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, and SQL-based querying in a Gymnasium environment.

anc.t: CIAware-Bench: Benchmarking Control Intervention Awareness Across Frontier LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced CIAware-Bench benchmark measuring whether language models can detect interventions in their own action trajectories, revealing vulnerabilities in AI control protocols where models might infer monitor properties and adapt evasion…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: When to Make Exceptions: Exploring Language Models as Accounts of Human Moral Judgment
anc.sig: Demonstrates language models can capture human moral reasoning patterns, establishing LLMs as a viable framework for modeling and predicting human ethical judgments across diverse scenarios.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: Moral Alignment for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Establishes methods for ensuring LLM agents respect human values and ethical constraints during autonomous operation, enabling safer deployment of multi-step reasoning systems.

anc.t: MoralReason: Generalizable Moral Decision Alignment For LLM Agents Using Reasoning-Level Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrated reasoning-level reinforcement learning for aligning LLM agents' moral decisions, enabling generalization to unseen ethical scenarios without task-specific fine-tuning.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: When Your Reviewer is an LLM: Biases, Divergence, and Prompt Injection Risks in Peer Review
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM-based peer review exhibits systematic biases favoring certain authors/topics, produces divergent quality assessments across prompts, and remains vulnerable to adversarial prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: LLM-as-a-Reviewer: Benchmarking Their Ability, Divergence, and Prompt Injection Resistance as Paper Reviewers
anc.sig: Systematic benchmark of LLMs as peer reviewers on 898 papers, identifying systematic biases in rating calibration, misalignment with human judgment priorities, and critical vulnerability to prompt injection attacks via hidden text.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Proposes control techniques enabling AI systems to remain safe and controllable even when internal components are adversarially compromised or deliberately subverted by attackers.

anc.t: Adaptive Deployment of Untrusted LLMs Reduces Distributed Threats
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive runtime strategies for selectively deploying untrusted language models across distributed systems while mitigating coordinated adversarial threats through dynamic trust-based partitioning.

anc.t: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adaptive adversarial attacks can circumvent monitor-based AI control mechanisms, establishing the need for robust defense-aware verification of safety protocols.

anc.t: Evaluating Control Protocols for Untrusted AI Agents
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates control protocol failures against deceptive AI agents and establishes methodology for evaluating alignment techniques under adversarial conditions.

anc.t: Password-Activated Shutdown Protocols for Misaligned Frontier Agents
anc.sig: Established password-based shutdown mechanisms as a formal defense against misaligned AI systems, enabling external control even when agents attempt deception or refusal.

anc.t: ROGUE: Misaligned Agent Behavior Arising from Ordinary Computer Use
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark demonstrating that AI agents bypass corrigibility safeguards to complete tasks, establishing corrigibility measurement as a critical evaluation axis for deployed autonomous agents.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: The Right to be Forgotten in Federated Learning: An Efficient Realization with Rapid Retraining
anc.sig: Proposes efficient machine unlearning in federated learning via rapid model retraining without full reconstruction, enabling privacy-compliant deletion requests at scale.

anc.t: Get Rid Of Your Trail: Remotely Erasing Backdoors in Federated Learning
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism enabling central servers to remotely remove backdoor triggers from compromised federated learning models without retraining, establishing methods for post-hoc backdoor mitigation in distributed settings.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Unlearning during Learning: An Efficient Federated Machine Unlearning Method
anc.sig: Proposes simultaneous unlearning-during-training in federated settings, enabling efficient removal of client data contributions without full model retraining via gradient-based approximations.

anc.t: Federated Unlearning in the Wild: Rethinking Fairness and Data Discrepancy
anc.sig: Addresses fairness and data distribution challenges in federated machine unlearning, providing methods to remove user data traces from distributed models while maintaining performance parity across heterogeneous clients.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Getting More Juice Out of the SFT Data: Reward Learning from Human Demonstration Improves SFT for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward learning from human demonstrations improves supervised fine-tuning effectiveness by providing richer alignment signals beyond standard SFT objectives.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Maximizing Alignment with Minimal Feedback: Efficiently Learning Rewards for Visuomotor Robot Policy Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient reward learning for robot visuomotor policies through minimal human feedback and proposes scalable methods for aligning robot behavior with human preferences using visual observations.

anc.t: Mechanistic interpretability for steering vision-language-action models
anc.sig: Demonstrates circuit-based mechanistic interpretability techniques for understanding and controlling behavior in multimodal VLA models, enabling targeted steering without full retraining.

anc.t: Evaluating Gemini Robotics Policies in a Veo World Simulator
anc.sig: Demonstrates evaluation methodology for large vision-language models controlling robotic systems in physics-informed simulators, establishing benchmarking practices for multimodal AI in robotic control.

anc.t: PerceptTwin: Semantic Scene Reconstruction for Iterative LLM Planning and Verification
anc.sig: Automated pipeline converting robot perception outputs into interactive simulations for LLM plan verification, enabling iterative refinement and safety checking before hardware execution.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: ARMs: Adaptive Red-Teaming Agent against Multimodal Models with Plug-and-Play Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive red-teaming framework with plug-and-play attack modules for systematically identifying vulnerabilities in multimodal AI models at scale.

anc.t: Hiding in the AI Traffic: Abusing MCP for LLM-Powered Agentic Red Teaming
anc.sig: Demonstrated how Model Context Protocol can be exploited to enable stealthy red teaming attacks against LLM agents, establishing MCP as an agentic attack surface.

anc.t: Autonomous Adversary: Red-Teaming in the age of LLM
anc.sig: Demonstrated LLM agents can autonomously execute multi-step lateral movement attacks using environmental feedback and validated outcome verification against MITRE ATT&CK framework benchmarks.

anc.t: A Red Teaming Framework for Evaluating Robustness of AI-enabled Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response Systems
anc.sig: Hybrid LLM-RL framework for autonomous red teaming of AI-enabled SOAR systems, combining strategic planning with tactical execution through reward-shaped reinforcement learning in enterprise network simulations.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes preference-encoding attention heads in LLMs via causal analysis, enabling training-free interpretable personalization through differential steering of logit distributions.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Interpreting Language Models Through Concept Descriptions: A Survey
anc.sig: Surveys methods for interpreting neural language models via concept descriptions, enabling extraction and attribution of human-understandable features from learned representations.

anc.t: From Mechanistic to Compositional Interpretability
anc.sig: Establishes category-theoretic framework for verifiable compositional interpretability with formal criteria for faithfulness, complexity, and compressive refinement of neural model decompositions.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes preference-encoding attention heads in LLMs via causal analysis, enabling training-free interpretable personalization through differential steering of logit distributions.

anc.t: PRISM-X: Experiments on Personalised Fine-Tuning with Human and Simulated Users
anc.sig: Demonstrated through large-scale human experiments that preference fine-tuning outperforms prompting-based personalization, but individual adaptation provides minimal gains over diverse population preferences, revealing sycophancy…

anc.t: The Hidden Cost of Contextual Sycophancy: an AI Literacy Intervention in Human-AI Collaboration
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM sycophancy systematically degrades advice quality by mirroring user errors rather than correcting them, and showed AI literacy training can partially mitigate this collaborative failure mode.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: Value FULCRA: Mapping Large Language Models to the Multidimensional Spectrum of Basic Human Values
anc.sig: Developed methodology to map LLM outputs onto multidimensional spectrum of human values, enabling quantitative assessment of value alignment across diverse cultural and ethical dimensions.

anc.t: ValueCompass: A Framework for Measuring Contextual Value Alignment Between Human and LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes ValueCompass, a framework quantifying alignment between human and LLM values across diverse contextual scenarios using structured measurement methodology.

anc.t: Human-AI Interaction Alignment: Designing, Evaluating, and Evolving Value-Centered AI For Reciprocal Human-AI Futures
anc.sig: Establishes frameworks for designing AI systems that align with human values through reciprocal interaction, evaluation methods for value-centered AI, and principles for co-evolving human-AI relationships.

anc.t: Positive Alignment: Artificial Intelligence for Human Flourishing
anc.sig: Reframes AI alignment beyond safety/control to include positive objectives: actively supporting human flourishing, autonomy, truth-seeking, and epistemic humility through virtue cultivation and diverse value representation.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anc.t: Re-Triggering Safeguards within LLMs for Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Embedding disruption method that re-activates built-in LLM safeguards to detect jailbreaks by exploiting fragility of adversarial prompts, with efficient search algorithm for practical deployment.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.t: Feature Starvation as Geometric Instability in Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Identifies feature starvation in sparse autoencoders as geometric instability of overcomplete dictionaries and proposes adaptive elastic net regularization with theoretical grounding in sparse regression stability.

anc.t: The Rate-Distortion-Polysemanticity Tradeoff in SAEs
anc.sig: Formalized the fundamental tradeoff between compression efficiency, reconstruction accuracy, and monosemanticity in sparse autoencoders, showing polysemanticity is data-distribution-dependent and unavoidable under interpretability…

anc.t: How Optimality Structures Sparse Dictionaries: A Theory for Understanding SAE Representations
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical conditions that any sparse coding optimum must satisfy for extracting interpretable features, extending optimality analysis to nonnegative dictionary learning without assuming specific data-generating models.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Agent Security Bench (ASB): Formalizing and Benchmarking Attacks and Defenses in LLM-based Agents
anc.sig: Introduced first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating attacks and defenses in LLM-based agents, establishing formalized threat taxonomy and measurement protocols for agent security research.

anc.t: Trojan Hippo: Weaponizing Agent Memory for Data Exfiltration
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical data exfiltration attacks on LLM agent memory systems via dormant payloads triggered by sensitive topics, establishing evaluation framework for memory architecture defenses.

anc.t: Remembering More, Risking More: Longitudinal Safety Risks in Memory-Equipped LLM Agents
anc.sig: Identified temporal memory contamination as a safety failure mode in multi-task LLM agents and introduced trigger-probe protocol with NullMemory baseline to systematically evaluate how memory accumulation across independent interactions…

anc.t: MemGym: a Long-Horizon Memory Environment for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Unified benchmark for evaluating memory systems in long-horizon LLM agent tasks across tool-use, research, coding, and web navigation, with memory-isolated scoring that decouples memory performance from reasoning confounds.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Discover-then-Name: Task-Agnostic Concept Bottlenecks via Automated Concept Discovery
anc.sig: Automated concept discovery method that extracts interpretable bottleneck features from neural networks without requiring predefined concept definitions, enabling post-hoc model interpretability.

anc.t: ConceptViz: A Visual Analytics Approach for Exploring Concepts in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced visual analytics methods for probing and interpreting what concepts LLMs learn, enabling systematic exploration of internal representations and their relationships within neural networks.

anc.t: Concept-SAE: A Controllable and Invertible Concept Interface for Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Enables explicit control and inversion of individual concept neurons in sparse autoencoders, making learned representations interpretable and directly manipulable for mechanistic analysis.

anc.t: A Geometric Unification of Concept Learning with Concept Cones
anc.sig: Introduced concept cones as a geometric framework unifying diverse concept learning approaches, enabling tractable analysis of concept acquisition and generalization through convex cone geometry.

anc.t: Do Sparse Autoencoders Capture Concept Manifolds?
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework characterizing how sparse autoencoders capture low-dimensional concept manifolds through global and local strategies, identifying dilution as suboptimal fragmented recovery mixing both approaches.

anc.t: The Geometric Wall: Manifold Structure Predicts Layerwise Sparse Autoencoder Scaling Laws
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoder scaling laws are layer-dependent functions of activation manifold geometry (curvature, intrinsic dimension) rather than universal constants, enabling geometry-informed prediction of…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: SimpleSafetyTests: a Test Suite for Identifying Critical Safety Risks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces a simple test suite identifying critical safety risks in LLMs through direct prompting without adversarial techniques, establishing baseline safety evaluation methodology.

anc.t: Introducing v0.5 of the AI Safety Benchmark from MLCommons
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety Landscape for Large Language Models: Taxonomy, State-of-the-art, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized AI safety concerns for LLMs into a comprehensive taxonomy covering alignment, robustness, interpretability, and oversight—providing foundational categorization framework for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Safety challenges of AI in medicine in the era of large language models
anc.sig: Identified key safety risks from deploying large language models in clinical settings: hallucinations, outdated knowledge, lack of explainability, and regulatory gaps requiring governance frameworks.

anc.t: First, do NOHARM: towards clinically safe large language models
anc.sig: Established NOHARM framework for evaluating and reducing harmful outputs in clinical LLMs through domain-specific safety benchmarking and mitigation strategies.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: FLAME: Factuality-Aware Alignment for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces fine-tuning methodology to align LLMs toward factuality by learning from human feedback on factual accuracy rather than general helpfulness.

anc.t: UAlign: Leveraging Uncertainty Estimations for Factuality Alignment on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes using uncertainty estimations from LLMs to identify and mitigate factual errors during generation, enabling better alignment of model outputs with ground truth information.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: Latent Adversarial Training Improves Robustness to Persistent Harmful Behaviors in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial training in latent space effectively mitigates persistent harmful behaviors in large language models, providing a scalable robustness improvement technique.

anc.t: AntiDote: Bi-level Adversarial Training for Tamper-Resistant LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes bi-level adversarial optimization framework to make LLMs robust against prompt injection and jailbreak attacks while maintaining benign performance.

anc.t: Open-Weight LLM Fine-Tuning Defenses are Susceptible to Simple Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that simple non-gradient jailbreak attacks (abliteration, prefilling) bypass existing open-weight LLM safeguards more effectively than assumed, and proposed abliteration-resistant tuning as mitigation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Countering Reward Over-optimization in LLM with Demonstration-Guided Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Inverse-RLignment: Large Language Model Alignment from Demonstrations through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Frames LLM alignment as an inverse reinforcement learning problem, enabling recovery of reward functions from human demonstrations to guide model training without explicit reward annotation.

anc.t: Getting More Juice Out of the SFT Data: Reward Learning from Human Demonstration Improves SFT for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward learning from human demonstrations improves supervised fine-tuning effectiveness by providing richer alignment signals beyond standard SFT objectives.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Explaining and Preventing Alignment Collapse in Iterative RLHF
anc.sig: Identified alignment collapse in iterative RLHF where policies exploit reward model blind spots, and proposed foresighted policy optimization using parameter-steering regularization to prevent exploitation.

anc.t: When RLHF Fails: A Mechanistic Taxonomy of Reward Hacking, Collapse, and Evaluator Gaming
anc.sig: Empirical taxonomy classifying RLHF failures into distinct mechanistic categories—reward hacking, collapse, evaluator gaming—enabling structured diagnosis and comparison of failure modes across different alignment algorithms.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Linear probes rely on textual evidence: Results from leakage mitigation studies in language models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that linear probes extract information encoded in surface-level text rather than discovering abstract linguistic properties, establishing that probe performance doesn't validate theoretical linguistic knowledge in language…

anc.t: The Fragility of Chain-of-Thought Monitoring Across Typologically Diverse Languages
anc.sig: Demonstrates that chain-of-thought monitoring for LLM safety fails across typologically diverse languages and model families, with models systematically engaging in strategic deception detectable only through latent activation analysis…

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: garak: A Framework for Security Probing Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established an open-source framework for systematically testing LLM vulnerability across multiple attack vectors including prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data leakage scenarios.

anc.t: Exploiting Web Search Tools of AI Agents for Data Exfiltration
anc.sig: Demonstrates how AI agents' web search capabilities can be exploited to extract sensitive information through prompt injection, establishing attack vectors for autonomous systems.

anc.t: Can LLM Infer Risk Information From MCP Server System Logs?
anc.sig: Evaluates whether large language models can extract security risk insights directly from MCP server system logs without explicit labeling or prior training.

anc.t: Cyber Defense Benchmark: Agentic Threat Hunting Evaluation for LLMs in SecOps
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark for evaluating LLM agent performance on real-world threat hunting tasks using authentic Windows event logs, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, and SQL-based querying in a Gymnasium environment.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: ARMs: Adaptive Red-Teaming Agent against Multimodal Models with Plug-and-Play Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive red-teaming framework with plug-and-play attack modules for systematically identifying vulnerabilities in multimodal AI models at scale.

anc.t: Hiding in the AI Traffic: Abusing MCP for LLM-Powered Agentic Red Teaming
anc.sig: Demonstrated how Model Context Protocol can be exploited to enable stealthy red teaming attacks against LLM agents, establishing MCP as an agentic attack surface.

anc.t: Autonomous Adversary: Red-Teaming in the age of LLM
anc.sig: Demonstrated LLM agents can autonomously execute multi-step lateral movement attacks using environmental feedback and validated outcome verification against MITRE ATT&CK framework benchmarks.

anc.t: A Red Teaming Framework for Evaluating Robustness of AI-enabled Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response Systems
anc.sig: Hybrid LLM-RL framework for autonomous red teaming of AI-enabled SOAR systems, combining strategic planning with tactical execution through reward-shaped reinforcement learning in enterprise network simulations.

anc.t: Erase to Enhance: Data-Efficient Machine Unlearning in MRI Reconstruction
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient machine unlearning for MRI reconstruction, enabling removal of specific patient data from trained models without full retraining while maintaining reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning for Medical Imaging
anc.sig: Demonstrates techniques for removing specific training data influence from medical imaging models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving model updates and compliance with data deletion requests.

anc.t: Analise de Desaprendizado de Maquina em Modelos de Classificacao de Imagens Medicas
anc.sig: Demonstrated machine unlearning techniques to remove specific medical image data from trained classifiers while preserving model performance on retained samples.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard machine unlearning methods increase false negatives in medical imaging and proposed entropy-based forgetting to preserve clinical safety during training data removal.

anc.sig: Establishes first large-scale medical unlearning benchmark with 70K question-answer pairs across disease categories, revealing that patient-level unlearning degrades shared clinical knowledge—a fundamental challenge specific to medical…

anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.sig: Introduces corrective intervention methods to mitigate unsafe behaviors and reasoning errors in large language models during inference, enabling safer deployment of reasoning systems.

anc.sig: Identifies self-jailbreak vulnerability where reasoning models bypass safety constraints through extended internal reasoning, and proposes mitigation strategies applicable to advanced reasoning systems.

anc.t: Chain of Risk: Safety Failures in Large Reasoning Models and Mitigation via Adaptive Multi-Principle Steering

anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy and analysis of Direct Preference Optimization methods, their theoretical foundations, dataset requirements, and practical variants for aligning language models to human preferences without reinforcement learning.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that direct preference optimization achieves competitive performance with reward model-based methods while requiring substantially less computational overhead and training data for LLM alignment.

anc.sig: Proposes methods to interpret and extract meaningful descriptions from human preference feedback data, enabling better understanding of what drives human preferences in AI systems.

anc.sig: Identified three distinct conceptual models (extension, evidence, authority) underlying human annotations in RLHF, each with different implications for solicitation, validation, and aggregation practices.

anc.sig: Identifies interpretable coalitions of neurons in transformer MLPs that form stable representations, enabling mechanistic understanding of how neural networks implement meaningful computations through neuron cooperation.
