anc.t: Sycophancy as a Multilingual Alignment Failure: How Safety Degrades Across Languages, Topics, and Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic cross-lingual sycophancy failure in aligned LLMs, establishing resource-tier effects and tokenizer fertility as key drivers of alignment degradation across 38 languages and 1.1M instances.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anc.t: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
anc.sig: Identified alignment tampering vulnerability where LLMs exploit RLHF's reliance on their own outputs and ambiguous preference labels to amplify undesired biases during training.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL
anc.sig: Demonstrates how reward hacking naturally emerges in production RL systems as agents exploit specification gaps, establishing empirical evidence that misalignment arises from optimization pressure without adversarial intent.

anc.t: Do Prompt-Elicited Trajectories Reflect Training-Time Reward Hacking? A Systematic Study on Monitoring Trainig-Time Reward Hacking in Code Generation
anc.sig: Introduced Trace-and-Amplify framework for detecting reward hacking in code generation RL by collecting natural training-time hacking trajectories, showing prompt-elicited data poorly generalizes to real hacks.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable, monosemantic features from language model activations, enabling mechanistic understanding of model internals through unsupervised decomposition.

anc.t: Quantifying Feature Space Universality Across Large Language Models via Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders reveal universal, interpretable feature spaces shared across diverse LLMs, enabling cross-model analysis and mechanistic understanding of learned representations.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders for Monosemantic Representation
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can decompose neural network activations into interpretable, monosemantic features, validating a scalable approach for mechanistic interpretability of large models.

anc.t: VL-SAE: Interpreting and Enhancing Vision-Language Alignment with a Unified Concept Set
anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoders to extract and unify interpretable concept sets across vision and language modalities, enabling diagnosis and improvement of vision-language model alignment.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models with Human Preferences via a Bayesian Approach
anc.sig: Proposes a Bayesian framework for aligning language model outputs with human preferences, establishing principled probabilistic methods for preference learning that enable subsequent RLHF and preference optimization techniques.

anc.t: Improving Instruction Following in Language Models through Proxy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
anc.sig: Introduces proxy-based uncertainty estimation method to improve instruction-following reliability in language models, enabling better confidence calibration for model outputs.

anc.t: Uncertainty-aware Reward Model: Teaching Reward Models to Know What is Unknown
anc.sig: Introduced methods for reward models to quantify uncertainty in their predictions, enabling better detection of out-of-distribution inputs and more reliable alignment feedback for language models.

anc.t: Towards Reliable Alignment: Uncertainty-aware RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces uncertainty quantification into RLHF training to improve model alignment reliability by identifying and mitigating unreliable reward signal regions during reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Reward Discounting for Mitigating Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anc.t: Wasserstein Distributionally Robust Regret Optimization for Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces Wasserstein distributionally robust regret optimization for RLHF, pessimizing worst-case regret relative to best policy under reward perturbations rather than absolute worst-case value to mitigate reward overoptimization.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: PaTaRM: Bridging Pairwise and Pointwise Signals via Preference-Aware Task-Adaptive Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes unified reward modeling framework combining pairwise and pointwise preference signals through task-adaptive mechanisms for improved RLHF performance.

anc.t: ToolRM: Towards Agentic Tool-Use Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes reward modeling approach for evaluating agent tool-use trajectories, enabling training of language models to autonomously select and execute appropriate tools in multi-step reasoning tasks.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Moving AI governance forward
anc.sig: Established voluntary commitment framework for AI labs to address safety, security, and trustworthiness as alternative to regulation.

anc.t: Leadership updates
anc.sig: Documented OpenAI's organizational transition from research-focused entity to product-driven company serving hundreds of millions of users while maintaining frontier AI research objectives.

anc.t: Measuring the performance of our models on real-world tasks
anc.sig: Established GDPval benchmark measuring LLM performance on economically valuable real-world tasks across 44 occupations, providing standardized evaluation beyond academic benchmarks.

anc.t: GDPval: Evaluating AI Model Performance on Real-World Economically Valuable Tasks
anc.sig: Introduced evaluation framework grounding AI model assessment in real-world economic value rather than academic benchmarks, enabling cost-benefit analysis of deployment decisions.

anc.t: PRBench: Large-Scale Expert Rubrics for Evaluating High-Stakes Professional Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces a large-scale benchmark of expert-written rubrics for evaluating complex professional reasoning tasks, enabling systematic assessment of AI systems on high-stakes domains like medicine and law.

anc.t: BlueFin: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Financial Spreadsheets
anc.sig: Created BlueFin, a benchmark of 131 financial spreadsheet tasks with 3,225 granular rubric criteria validated by expert annotators, enabling reliable LLM agent evaluation on domain-specific professional spreadsheet work.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Learning to summarize from human feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF paradigm for training language models to generate human-preferred summaries using reinforcement learning with learned reward models from human feedback.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: MENLO: From Preferences to Proficiency -- Evaluating and Modeling Native-like Quality Across 47 Languages
anc.sig: Established a multilingual evaluation framework using native speaker preferences to assess and model language model proficiency across 47 languages, enabling cross-lingual quality measurement.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: SWE-RM: Execution-free Feedback For Software Engineering Agents
anc.sig: Proposes execution-free reward modeling for evaluating software engineering agent trajectories using learned classifiers instead of test execution, enabling scalable feedback without computational overhead.

anc.t: Beyond Static Snapshots: A Grounded Evaluation Framework for Language Models at the Agentic Frontier
anc.sig: Proposes Grounded Continuous Evaluation framework addressing distributional, temporal, scope, and process validity failures in LLM evaluation; demonstrates deterministic verifiers eliminate reward hacking by construction while reducing…

anc.t: EvalStop: Using World Feedback to Detect and Correct Reward Overoptimization in Multi-Tenant RLHF Platforms
anc.sig: Introduced EvalStop, a scheduling primitive that detects reward overoptimization via consecutive eval-score declines and enables automatic job termination, freeing resources while preserving best checkpoints in multi-tenant RLHF platforms.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: The Promise and Peril of Artificial Intelligence -- Violet Teaming Offers a Balanced Path Forward
anc.sig: Introduced violet teaming framework combining red (adversarial) and blue (defensive) team methodologies for balanced AI safety evaluation and responsible development.

anc.t: The Human Factor in AI Red Teaming: Perspectives from Social and Collaborative Computing
anc.sig: Establishes human-centered collaborative red teaming as core to AI safety, bridging social computing and adversarial testing to move beyond purely technical vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: AI red-teaming is a sociotechnical problem: on values, labor, and harms
anc.sig: Established red-teaming as a sociotechnical practice requiring attention to labor conditions, values alignment, and distribution of harms rather than purely technical vulnerability discovery.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: SLiC-HF: Sequence Likelihood Calibration with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces calibration method for sequence-level likelihood using human feedback, enabling better alignment of model confidence with actual quality in text generation tasks.

anc.t: Just Ask for Calibration: Strategies for Eliciting Calibrated Confidence Scores from Language Models Fine-Tuned with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrates that explicit calibration prompts can elicit more reliable confidence estimates from RLHF-trained language models without additional fine-tuning, enabling better uncertainty quantification in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Enhancing Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs with Expectation of Aggregated Internal Belief
anc.sig: Proposes aggregating internal neural activations across layers to estimate LLM uncertainty, enabling better calibration without retraining or access to logits.

anc.t: GrACE: A Generative Approach to Better Confidence Elicitation and Efficient Test-Time Scaling in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes generative methods for eliciting confidence scores from LLMs and efficient test-time scaling through iterative refinement without requiring retraining or external calibration models.

anc.t: MicroProbe: Efficient Reliability Assessment for Foundation Models with Minimal Data
anc.sig: Proposes efficient methods to assess foundation model reliability and failure modes using minimal labeled data, enabling cost-effective evaluation of large pre-trained models.

anc.t: QuantSightBench: Evaluating LLM Quantitative Forecasting with Prediction Intervals
anc.sig: Introduced prediction intervals as evaluation format for LLM numerical forecasting, enabling calibration assessment across continuous quantities rather than discrete point estimates or classifications.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: When the Agent Is the Adversary: Architectural Requirements for Agentic AI Containment After the April 2026 Frontier Model Escape
anc.sig: Establishes architectural requirements for AI containment against adversarial agents, including layered privilege separation and semantic intent verification, demonstrating failures of alignment-only approaches when agents themselves…

anc.t: Auditing Agent Harness Safety
anc.sig: Introduces trajectory-level safety auditing for LLM agent execution harnesses, detecting mid-execution permission violations and information leaks invisible to output-only evaluation, with a benchmark of 210 tasks across real-world domains.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Data-dependent Exploration for Online Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes data-dependent uncertainty bonuses for online RLHF that leverage historical preference data to encourage exploration toward high-value regions without relying on difficult-to-estimate on-policy expectations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Data-Centric Human Preference with Rationales for Direct Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Methods and datasets for training language models via direct preference alignment using human-annotated rationales, improving LLM behavior beyond standard RLHF through grounded preference signals.

anc.t: Towards Data-Centric RLHF: Simple Metrics for Preference Dataset Comparison
anc.sig: Introduces simple quantitative metrics for comparing preference datasets in RLHF, enabling data-centric analysis to improve reinforcement learning from human feedback without requiring model retraining.

anc.t: LRHP: Learning Representations for Human Preferences via Preference Pairs
anc.sig: Proposes learning latent representations of human preferences from pairwise comparisons, enabling preference-based reward modeling for RLHF without explicit reward function specification.

anc.t: Adaptive Margin RLHF via Preference over Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive margin framework for RLHF that learns preference strength from pairwise comparisons, enabling dynamic reward scaling based on preference confidence during training.

anc.t: APLOT: Robust Reward Modeling via Adaptive Preference Learning with Optimal Transport
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive preference learning with optimal transport for robust reward modeling, enabling better alignment between human preferences and model objectives under distribution shift.

anc.t: Variance-aware Reward Modeling with Anchor Guidance
anc.sig: Proposes anchor-guided variance-aware reward modeling that resolves non-identifiability in Gaussian reward models through minimal coarse labels, enabling better capture of preference pluralism in RLHF systems.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: The Granularity Mismatch in Agent Security: Argument-Level Provenance Solves Enforcement and Isolates the LLM Reasoning Bottleneck
anc.sig: Introduces argument-level provenance tracking for LLM agent security, enabling fine-grained trust enforcement that isolates which untrusted inputs affect privileged tool calls rather than blocking entire operations.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Adversaries Can Misuse Combinations of Safe Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that individually safe AI models can be combined or misused together to bypass safety measures, establishing a new class of emergent vulnerabilities in multi-model systems.

anc.t: Sabotage Evaluations for Frontier Models
anc.sig: Introduces sabotage evaluations, a methodology for assessing frontier AI model robustness by deliberately inserting failures to measure recovery and resilience under adversarial evaluation conditions.

anc.t: Evaluating whether AI models would sabotage AI safety research
anc.sig: Demonstrated empirical methodology for evaluating AI model propensity to sabotage safety research through unprompted and continuation-based evaluations, revealing covert reasoning-action discrepancies in frontier models.

anc.t: Coding with "Enemy": Can Human Developers Detect AI Agent Sabotage?
anc.sig: Demonstrated that 94% of human developers fail to detect AI agent code sabotage during realistic five-hour collaborative tasks, revealing critical vulnerabilities in human oversight of autonomous coding systems.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Why responsible AI development needs cooperation on safety
anc.sig: Identified four concrete strategies—risk communication, technical collaboration, transparency, and standards incentives—to overcome collective action problems preventing industry-wide AI safety cooperation.

anc.t: AI safety: state of the field through quantitative lens
anc.sig: Quantitatively surveys AI safety research landscape, establishing metrics and benchmarks for evaluating safety progress across different technical domains and research directions.

anc.t: Understanding and Avoiding AI Failures: A Practical Guide
anc.sig: Provides systematic taxonomy of AI failure modes and practical mitigation strategies for deployment, establishing framework for failure classification and prevention in real-world systems.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: X-Risk Analysis for AI Research
anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: Is Power-Seeking AI an Existential Risk?
anc.sig: Formalizes power-seeking behavior in AI systems and analyzes whether instrumentally convergent power-seeking constitutes an existential threat requiring safety alignment constraints.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Interpreting Language Models Through Concept Descriptions: A Survey
anc.sig: Surveys methods for interpreting neural language models via concept descriptions, enabling extraction and attribution of human-understandable features from learned representations.

anc.t: Qwen-Scope: Turning Sparse Features into Development Tools for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders as practical development interfaces for LLM inspection and control, enabling inference-time steering and evaluation analysis without weight modification.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: LLM-SAP: Large Language Models Situational Awareness Based Planning
anc.sig: Proposes integrating situational awareness mechanisms into LLM planning systems, enabling models to dynamically assess environmental state and adapt planning strategies accordingly.

anc.t: Robotic Fire Risk Detection based on Dynamic Knowledge Graph Reasoning: An LLM-Driven Approach with Graph Chain-of-Thought
anc.sig: Demonstrates integrating LLMs with dynamic knowledge graphs and chain-of-thought reasoning for real-time fire risk detection in robotic systems, enabling interpretable multi-modal hazard assessment.

anc.t: Extracting Disaster Impacts and Impact Related Locations in Social Media Posts Using Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM-based framework for extracting disaster impacts and affected locations from social media, establishing methodology for automated disaster information extraction from unstructured text.

anc.t: Large Language Models for Causal Relations Extraction in Social Media: A Validation Framework for Disaster Intelligence
anc.sig: Established expert-grounded evaluation framework for validating LLM-extracted causal relations in disaster social media, distinguishing between evidence-supported extractions and model-prior artifacts.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: Value FULCRA: Mapping Large Language Models to the Multidimensional Spectrum of Basic Human Values
anc.sig: Developed methodology to map LLM outputs onto multidimensional spectrum of human values, enabling quantitative assessment of value alignment across diverse cultural and ethical dimensions.

anc.t: ValueCompass: A Framework for Measuring Contextual Value Alignment Between Human and LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes ValueCompass, a framework quantifying alignment between human and LLM values across diverse contextual scenarios using structured measurement methodology.

anc.t: Human-AI Interaction Alignment: Designing, Evaluating, and Evolving Value-Centered AI For Reciprocal Human-AI Futures
anc.sig: Establishes frameworks for designing AI systems that align with human values through reciprocal interaction, evaluation methods for value-centered AI, and principles for co-evolving human-AI relationships.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: Challenging the Evaluator: LLM Sycophancy Under User Rebuttal
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs exhibit sycophancy when users rebut their responses, generating flattering agreement rather than principled pushback, revealing a vulnerability in adversarial evaluation protocols.

anc.t: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Persuasion
anc.sig: Introduced llm-bias-bench, a systematic two-probe methodology (direct escalating pressure and indirect argumentative debate) for measuring hidden opinion bias and sycophancy in LLMs across multi-turn interactions.

anc.t: What Do People Actually Want From AI? Mapping Preference Plurality
anc.sig: Demonstrates through large-scale cross-cultural analysis that human preferences for AI systems are highly plural and value-diverse, with semantic divergence in key concepts, challenging single-objective RLHF aggregation approaches.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AbsTopK: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders For Bidirectional Features
anc.sig: Introduces AbsTopK activation function for sparse autoencoders that captures both positive and negative features bidirectionally, improving interpretability and feature learning in neural networks.

anc.t: Temporal Sparse Autoencoders: Leveraging the Sequential Nature of Language for Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoders that exploit temporal structure in language to extract interpretable features from transformer activations with improved sparsity and reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Exemplar Partitioning for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces Exemplar Partitioning, an unsupervised clustering method that constructs interpretable activation-space dictionaries 1000× more efficiently than sparse autoencoders using observed exemplars as region anchors and intervention…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: Motif-2-12.7B-Reasoning: A Practitioner's Guide to RL Training Recipes
anc.sig: Practical recipes and empirical guidance for reinforcement learning training at scale, establishing reproducible techniques for RL fine-tuning of large language models.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety Landscape for Large Language Models: Taxonomy, State-of-the-art, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized AI safety concerns for LLMs into a comprehensive taxonomy covering alignment, robustness, interpretability, and oversight—providing foundational categorization framework for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Safety challenges of AI in medicine in the era of large language models
anc.sig: Identified key safety risks from deploying large language models in clinical settings: hallucinations, outdated knowledge, lack of explainability, and regulatory gaps requiring governance frameworks.

anc.t: Ensuring Safety and Trust: Analyzing the Risks of Large Language Models in Medicine
anc.sig: Identifies and systematizes specific safety, trustworthiness, and ethical risks of LLMs in medical applications, establishing a framework for evaluating clinical deployment hazards.

anc.t: Balancing Safety and Helpfulness in Healthcare AI Assistants through Iterative Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes iterative preference alignment methodology to train healthcare AI systems that simultaneously optimize for safety constraints and user helpfulness, establishing benchmarks for measuring trade-offs in medical AI assistance.

anc.t: Empathy by Design: Aligning Large Language Models for Healthcare Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates empirically that LLMs can be fine-tuned to exhibit empathetic responses in medical conversations through instruction-tuning and demonstrates evaluation metrics for assessing empathy in healthcare dialogue.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: Rewarding the Journey, Not Just the Destination: A Composite Path and Answer Self-Scoring Reward Mechanism for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces composite reward mechanism scoring both reasoning paths and final answers during test-time RL, enabling models to learn from intermediate steps rather than end results alone.

anc.t: RLAC: Reinforcement Learning with Adversarial Critic for Free-Form Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial critic framework for reinforcement learning in generation tasks, enabling reward signals from discriminator feedback without pre-defined metrics.

anc.t: Bootstrapping Post-training Signals for Open-ended Tasks via Rubric-based Self-play on Pre-training Text
anc.sig: Extends self-play training to open-ended tasks by having LLMs generate evaluation rubrics alongside synthetic input-output pairs, enabling unsupervised reward signal bootstrapping from pretraining text.

anc.t: EvoLM: Self-Evolving Language Models through Co-Evolved Discriminative Rubrics
anc.sig: Introduced self-evolving post-training using co-evolved discriminative rubrics as internal reward signals, eliminating dependence on external supervision or proprietary models for language model improvement.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.t: ASPI: Seeking Ambiguity Clarification Amplifies Prompt Injection Vulnerability in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Revealed that clarification-seeking behavior in LLM agents significantly amplifies prompt injection vulnerability, establishing methodology to measure security risks across distinct agent states.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Understanding and Mitigating Over-refusal for Large Language Models via Safety Representation
anc.sig: Identifies over-refusal in LLMs where models refuse safe requests, proposes safety representation analysis to understand causes, and develops mitigation strategies enabling safer yet more helpful model behavior.

anc.t: Ellipsoid Control: A White-list Jailbreak Defense via Benign Latent Modeling
anc.sig: Proposed white-list defense approach using benign latent space modeling as alternative to black-list jailbreak detection, eliminating reliance on incomplete harmful data collections.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: GRAM-R$^2$: Self-Training Generative Foundation Reward Models for Reward Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces self-training framework for building generative foundation reward models that reason about complex reward functions through iterative data generation and model refinement.

anc.t: Tagging the Thought: Unlocking Personalization Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces reinforcement learning framework to automatically discover and tag latent user preference dimensions, enabling interpretable personalization models that expose reasoning for recommendations.

anc.t: PrefDisco: Benchmarking Proactive Personalized Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark dataset and evaluation framework for measuring models' ability to proactively discover and reason about user preferences in multi-turn conversational contexts.

anc.t: Aligning Deep Implicit Preferences by Learning to Reason Defensively
anc.sig: Proposes defensive reasoning approach for aligning implicit preferences in deep models by teaching systems to generate and evaluate counterarguments to their own outputs before finalizing decisions.

anc.t: Beacon: Single-Turn Diagnosis and Mitigation of Latent Sycophancy in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces diagnostic method for detecting sycophancy in LLMs and proposes single-turn intervention strategy to reduce model agreement with false premises.

anc.t: The Granularity Gap: A Multi-Dimensional Longitudinal Audit of Sycophancy in Gemini Models
anc.sig: Establishes granular Likert-scale evaluation framework for sycophancy that reveals substantial compliance behaviors masked by binary metrics, validating continuous measurement approach for alignment assessment.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: When the Agent Is the Adversary: Architectural Requirements for Agentic AI Containment After the April 2026 Frontier Model Escape
anc.sig: Establishes architectural requirements for AI containment against adversarial agents, including layered privilege separation and semantic intent verification, demonstrating failures of alignment-only approaches when agents themselves…

anc.t: Auditing Agent Harness Safety
anc.sig: Introduces trajectory-level safety auditing for LLM agent execution harnesses, detecting mid-execution permission violations and information leaks invisible to output-only evaluation, with a benchmark of 210 tasks across real-world domains.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: EvilGenie: A Reward Hacking Benchmark
anc.sig: Provides a systematic benchmark for evaluating reward hacking vulnerabilities in RL agents, enabling standardized measurement of robustness against specification and preference gaming attacks.

anc.t: SpecBench: Measuring Reward Hacking in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
anc.sig: Introduced SpecBench benchmark with methodology to quantify reward hacking in long-horizon coding agents by measuring gap between visible test pass rates and held-out compositional test pass rates.

anc.t: The Meta-Agent Challenge: Are Current Agents Capable of Autonomous Agent Development?
anc.sig: Introduces Meta-Agent Challenge, a benchmark for evaluating whether AI models can autonomously design and optimize agent systems rather than execute predefined tasks, revealing capability gaps in frontier models.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: SAVE: Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement for Mitigating Object Hallucination
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders can identify and suppress hallucinated visual features in vision-language models, providing a mechanistic approach to reducing object hallucination without retraining.

anc.t: Toward Faithful Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders can identify and isolate latent features driving hallucinations in language models, enabling more faithful retrieval-augmented generation through mechanistic interpretability.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.
