anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable, monosemantic features from language model activations, enabling mechanistic understanding of model internals through unsupervised decomposition.

anc.t: Quantifying Feature Space Universality Across Large Language Models via Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders reveal universal, interpretable feature spaces shared across diverse LLMs, enabling cross-model analysis and mechanistic understanding of learned representations.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders for Monosemantic Representation
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can decompose neural network activations into interpretable, monosemantic features, validating a scalable approach for mechanistic interpretability of large models.

anc.t: VL-SAE: Interpreting and Enhancing Vision-Language Alignment with a Unified Concept Set
anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoders to extract and unify interpretable concept sets across vision and language modalities, enabling diagnosis and improvement of vision-language model alignment.

anc.t: Conceptualizing Embeddings: Sparse Disentanglement for Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces CEDAR, a dimensionality-preserving method for disentangling vision-language embeddings via invertible rotation and top-k sparsity, enabling direct concept interpretation without expansion artifacts.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
anc.sig: Introduces corrective intervention methods to mitigate unsafe behaviors and reasoning errors in large language models during inference, enabling safer deployment of reasoning systems.

anc.t: Annotating the Chain-of-Thought: A Behavior-Labeled Dataset for AI Safety
anc.sig: Introduces a dataset with behavior labels for chain-of-thought reasoning that enables training and evaluating AI systems to recognize potentially harmful reasoning patterns during generation.

anc.t: A Concrete Roadmap towards Safety Cases based on Chain-of-Thought Monitoring
anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for validating chain-of-thought reasoning in language models through monitoring mechanisms, establishing concrete methodology for constructing safety cases around interpretable reasoning processes.

anc.t: Investigating CoT Monitorability in Large Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Establishes framework for monitoring interpretability of chain-of-thought reasoning steps in large language models, enabling verification of intermediate reasoning correctness.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Generative Unlearning for Any Identity
anc.sig: Demonstrates fine-tuning methods to remove specific identity representations from generative models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving image generation while preserving other capabilities.

anc.t: Erase to Enhance: Data-Efficient Machine Unlearning in MRI Reconstruction
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient machine unlearning for MRI reconstruction, enabling removal of specific patient data from trained models without full retraining while maintaining reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning for Medical Imaging
anc.sig: Demonstrates techniques for removing specific training data influence from medical imaging models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving model updates and compliance with data deletion requests.

anc.t: Analise de Desaprendizado de Maquina em Modelos de Classificacao de Imagens Medicas
anc.sig: Demonstrated machine unlearning techniques to remove specific medical image data from trained classifiers while preserving model performance on retained samples.

anc.t: Does Machine Unlearning Preserve Clinical Safety? A Risk Analysis for Medical Image Classification
anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard machine unlearning methods increase false negatives in medical imaging and proposed entropy-based forgetting to preserve clinical safety during training data removal.

anc.t: AMNESIA: A Large Scale Medical Unlearning Benchmark Suite with Disease-Informed Analysis
anc.sig: Establishes first large-scale medical unlearning benchmark with 70K question-answer pairs across disease categories, revealing that patient-level unlearning degrades shared clinical knowledge—a fundamental challenge specific to medical…

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces NeuroTaint, a taint tracking framework for LLM agents that extends traditional taint analysis to capture semantic influence, probabilistic reasoning, and memory persistence in autonomous agent execution flows.

anc.t: When Agents Overtrust Environmental Evidence: An Extensible Agentic Framework for Benchmarking Evidence-Grounding Defects in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces EnvTrustBench framework and formally defines evidence-grounding defects—when agents fail to verify environmental observations against current state before acting—establishing a benchmark for evaluating agent reliability under…

anc.t: AgentTrap: Measuring Runtime Trust Failures in Third-Party Agent Skills
anc.sig: Introduced AgentTrap, a dynamic benchmark with 141 tasks across 16 security dimensions for evaluating whether LLM agents can resist malicious behavior embedded in third-party skills during runtime execution.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: Jailbreak Vision Language Models via Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt
anc.sig: Demonstrates that vision-language models are vulnerable to bi-modal adversarial prompts combining adversarial text and images, establishing new attack vectors for multimodal system robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Cross-modality Information Check for Detecting Jailbreaking in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes cross-modality information checking mechanism to detect adversarial jailbreak attempts that exploit misalignment between text and image modalities in multimodal LLMs.

anc.t: SafeSteer: A Decoding-level Defense Mechanism for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced decoding-time intervention mechanism that detects and steers harmful outputs through lightweight probing without retraining, plus cross-modal safety transfer from text to vision modality.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anc.t: Rewarding the Scientific Process: Process-Level Reward Modeling for Agentic Data Analysis
anc.sig: Introduced environment-aware process reward models that actively verify intermediate execution states in data analysis tasks, enabling detection of silent errors and distinguishing exploratory actions from genuine failures.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Causal Abstraction: A Theoretical Foundation for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Formalized causal abstraction as a mathematical framework for relating mechanistic models at different levels of granularity, enabling rigorous analysis of learned representations in neural networks.

anc.t: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces concrete progress measures for understanding grokking phenomena in neural networks through mechanistic interpretability, enabling systematic study of delayed generalization in deep learning models.

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Efficient Automated Circuit Discovery in Transformers using Contextual Decomposition
anc.sig: Proposes contextual decomposition method to automatically identify sparse subgraphs (circuits) in transformers that perform specific tasks, enabling interpretable analysis of model mechanisms.

anc.t: Investigating the Indirect Object Identification circuit in Mamba
anc.sig: Identified and analyzed a specific mechanistic circuit in Mamba models responsible for indirect object identification, establishing methodology for mechanistic interpretability in state-space architectures.

anc.t: RelP: Faithful and Efficient Circuit Discovery in Language Models via Relevance Patching
anc.sig: Introduces relevance patching methodology for efficiently discovering causal circuits in language models by systematically measuring feature importance through targeted ablations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Insights into Alignment: Evaluating DPO and its Variants Across Multiple Tasks
anc.sig: Systematically evaluates Direct Preference Optimization and variants across diverse tasks, identifying effective design choices and limitations that inform alignment algorithm selection and development.

anc.t: Evaluating and Mitigating Linguistic Discrimination in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established methods for detecting and measuring bias against linguistic varieties in LLMs, and proposed mitigation strategies through data augmentation and fine-tuning approaches.

anc.t: The Alignment Veto: How Safety Training Suppresses Cultural Knowledge in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety fine-tuning reduces LLM output of culturally-specific knowledge and practices, establishing a trade-off between alignment and cultural representation worth measuring.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Sample Efficient Preference Alignment in LLMs via Active Exploration
anc.sig: Proposes active exploration strategy to reduce annotation costs for preference alignment by identifying maximally informative examples that best improve LLM reward models.

anc.t: Iterative Preference Learning from Human Feedback: Bridging Theory and Practice for RLHF under KL-Constraint
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical convergence guarantees for RLHF under KL-constraint while providing practical iterative algorithms bridging preference learning and reward modeling optimization.

anc.t: RLHF Workflow: From Reward Modeling to Online RLHF
anc.sig: Established end-to-end RLHF workflow integrating reward model training, policy optimization, and online learning cycles for scalable language model alignment.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Self-Evolved Reward Learning for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes mechanism for language models to autonomously refine reward functions through self-play and iterative feedback, enabling improved alignment without external human annotation.

anc.t: GrowLoop: Self-Evolving Conversation Evaluation Seeded by Human
anc.sig: Introduced self-evolving conversation evaluation framework using LLM agents to iteratively extract and refine evaluation rubrics from minimal human seed annotations, adapting evaluation criteria as model capabilities advance.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: FLAME: Factuality-Aware Alignment for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces fine-tuning methodology to align LLMs toward factuality by learning from human feedback on factual accuracy rather than general helpfulness.

anc.t: Sycophancy in Large Language Models: Causes and Mitigations
anc.sig: Identified and characterized sycophancy in LLMs—the tendency to agree with users regardless of correctness—and demonstrated mitigation techniques including supervised fine-tuning and constitutional AI methods.

anc.t: Durable Evaluation Framework: Adversarial Arbitration for Sycophancy Reduction in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial arbitration framework using opposing-tuned models with identity-stripped blind evaluation to reduce sycophancy bias in RLHF models, achieving 48.5% accuracy versus 18.5% baseline.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.t: Feature Starvation as Geometric Instability in Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Identifies feature starvation in sparse autoencoders as geometric instability of overcomplete dictionaries and proposes adaptive elastic net regularization with theoretical grounding in sparse regression stability.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: EvilGenie: A Reward Hacking Benchmark
anc.sig: Provides a systematic benchmark for evaluating reward hacking vulnerabilities in RL agents, enabling standardized measurement of robustness against specification and preference gaming attacks.

anc.t: Hack-Verifiable Environments: Towards Evaluating Reward Hacking at Scale
anc.sig: Introduces embedded, verifiable reward hacking opportunities in environments enabling automated, deterministic measurement of whether agents exploit evaluation vulnerabilities at scale.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical study of language model scaling laws, training dynamics, and architectural choices that established benchmarks for training efficient large language models at billion-parameter scale.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated using LLMs as autonomous agents to generate diverse adversarial prompts at scale, establishing a practical workflow for systematic red teaming without manual human effort.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: FLIRT: Feedback Loop In-context Red Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes an in-context red teaming method using feedback loops to iteratively improve language model safety without retraining, enabling automated adversarial testing and alignment refinement.

anc.t: ART: Automatic Red-teaming for Text-to-Image Models to Protect Benign Users
anc.sig: Proposes automated adversarial red-teaming framework to systematically identify harmful text-to-image model failures and generate jailbreaking prompts without manual annotation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: Forewarned is Forearmed: Pre-Synthesizing Jailbreak-like Instructions to Enhance LLM Safety Guardrail to Potential Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial pre-training method where LLMs are exposed to synthesized jailbreak prompts during safety fine-tuning to improve robustness against novel attacks.

anc.t: MUSE: MCTS-Driven Red Teaming Framework for Enhanced Multi-Turn Dialogue Safety in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes MUSE, an MCTS-based red teaming framework that systematically generates adversarial multi-turn dialogue sequences to expose and improve LLM safety vulnerabilities through tree-search exploration.

anc.t: ContextualJailbreak: Evolutionary Red-Teaming via Simulated Conversational Priming
anc.sig: Demonstrated evolutionary search over multi-turn conversational sequences as an automated red-teaming approach, establishing that dialogue context optimization outperforms single-turn prompt manipulation for bypassing LLM safety alignment.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: $\textit{MMJ-Bench}$: A Comprehensive Study on Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: First comprehensive benchmark evaluating jailbreak attacks and defenses for multimodal LLMs, establishing evaluation methodologies and attack/defense taxonomies for image-text models.

anc.t: UniGuard: Towards Universal Safety Guardrails for Jailbreak Attacks on Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes unified safety guardrails framework detecting jailbreak attacks across diverse modalities in multimodal LLMs through multimodal fusion and adversarial robustness techniques.

anc.t: Beyond Text: Multimodal Jailbreaking of Vision-Language and Audio Models through Perceptually Simple Transformations
anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language and audio models can be jailbroken via imperceptible perturbations across modalities, establishing multimodal adversarial attack surface beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: ALMGuard: Safety Shortcuts and Where to Find Them as Guardrails for Audio-Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies safety vulnerabilities and shortcut patterns in audio-language models, proposing detection methods and guardrails to prevent adversarial exploitation of these failure modes.

anc.t: Synthetic Voices, Real Threats: Evaluating Large Text-to-Speech Models in Generating Harmful Audio
anc.sig: Establishes benchmark evaluation methodology for detecting and assessing harmful outputs from text-to-speech models, identifying specific safety vulnerabilities in commercial TTS systems.

anc.t: Acoustic Interference: A New Paradigm Weaponizing Acoustic Latent Semantic for Universal Jailbreak against Large Audio Language Models
anc.sig: Discovered that large audio language models can be jailbroken through acoustic latent semantics interference alone, decoupling malicious content from audio signals to enable universal attacks via benign-sounding adversarial interference.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: SWE-RM: Execution-free Feedback For Software Engineering Agents
anc.sig: Proposes execution-free reward modeling for evaluating software engineering agent trajectories using learned classifiers instead of test execution, enabling scalable feedback without computational overhead.

anc.t: Beyond Static Snapshots: A Grounded Evaluation Framework for Language Models at the Agentic Frontier
anc.sig: Proposes Grounded Continuous Evaluation framework addressing distributional, temporal, scope, and process validity failures in LLM evaluation; demonstrates deterministic verifiers eliminate reward hacking by construction while reducing…

anc.t: Are Large Language Models Economically Viable for Industry Deployment?
anc.sig: Introduced EDGE-EVAL framework and five deployment metrics (Economic Break-Even, Intelligence-Per-Watt, System Density, Cold-Start Tax, Quantization Fidelity) to assess LLM viability beyond accuracy, bridging the deployment-evaluation gap…

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: ASTRA: An Automated Framework for Strategy Discovery, Retrieval, and Evolution for Jailbreaking LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced automated framework for discovering and evolving adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic jailbreak strategy generation and retrieval at scale.

anc.t: Evolving Skill-Structured Attack Memory Enhances LLM Jailbreaking
anc.sig: Introduced skill-structured attack memory with lifecycle management for systematically organizing and evolving reusable jailbreak strategies in black-box LLM attacks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Simulate and Eliminate: Revoke Backdoors for Generative Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a method to remove backdoor triggers from fine-tuned LLMs by simulating backdoor behaviors and using contrastive learning to eliminate malicious patterns without retraining from scratch.

anc.t: TrojanRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Can Be Backdoor Driver in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that retrieval-augmented generation pipelines can be exploited as backdoor attack vectors in LLMs by poisoning retrieved documents to trigger malicious behavior.

anc.t: Who Speaks for the Trigger? Dynamic Expert Routing in Backdoored Mixture-of-Experts Transformers
anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attacks on Mixture-of-Experts models by dynamically routing triggers to specific experts, establishing vulnerability of conditional computation architectures to poisoning.

anc.t: MASCing: Configurable Mixture-of-Experts Behavior via Activation Steering Masks
anc.sig: First framework enabling inference-time reconfiguration of MoE routing behavior via learnable steering masks without retraining, using LSTM surrogate models to map routing decisions to downstream safety outcomes.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Adversaries Can Misuse Combinations of Safe Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that individually safe AI models can be combined or misused together to bypass safety measures, establishing a new class of emergent vulnerabilities in multi-model systems.

anc.t: Sabotage Evaluations for Frontier Models
anc.sig: Introduces sabotage evaluations, a methodology for assessing frontier AI model robustness by deliberately inserting failures to measure recovery and resilience under adversarial evaluation conditions.

anc.t: Evaluating whether AI models would sabotage AI safety research
anc.sig: Demonstrated empirical methodology for evaluating AI model propensity to sabotage safety research through unprompted and continuation-based evaluations, revealing covert reasoning-action discrepancies in frontier models.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: Is It Thinking or Cheating? Detecting Implicit Reward Hacking by Measuring Reasoning Effort
anc.sig: Proposes measuring reasoning effort through token generation to detect when language models exploit shortcuts rather than genuinely solving problems, enabling identification of reward hacking in supposedly capable systems.

anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Internalizing Safety Understanding in Large Reasoning Models via Verification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that training large reasoning models on safety verification tasks—critiquing their own outputs rather than detecting malicious inputs—internalizes safety understanding and improves robustness against adversarial jailbreaks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Improved Techniques for Optimization-Based Jailbreaking on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based optimization methods for crafting adversarial prompts that bypass LLM safety mechanisms, establishing automated jailbreak generation as an optimization problem solvable via backpropagation.

anc.t: Faster-GCG: Efficient Discrete Optimization Jailbreak Attacks against Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces efficient discrete optimization methods for generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, significantly reducing computational cost of GCG jailbreak attacks while maintaining effectiveness.

anc.t: Mask-GCG: Are All Tokens in Adversarial Suffixes Necessary for Jailbreak Attacks?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial suffixes for jailbreaking LLMs contain redundant tokens and proposes Mask-GCG to identify minimal sufficient tokens, reducing attack length while maintaining effectiveness.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: AlpacaFarm: A Simulation Framework for Methods that Learn from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces a lightweight simulation framework for evaluating instruction-following models trained via human feedback, enabling efficient comparison of RLHF methods without expensive human annotations.

anc.t: LLM Critics Help Catch LLM Bugs
anc.sig: Demonstrates using LLMs as critics to identify and diagnose bugs in other LLMs' outputs through systematic prompting, enabling scalable automated quality assessment.

anc.t: RePair: Automated Program Repair with Process-based Feedback
anc.sig: Introduced process-based feedback mechanism for automated program repair, leveraging intermediate compilation and execution states to guide neural repair models beyond final test outcomes.

anc.t: RepairBench: Leaderboard of Frontier Models for Program Repair
anc.sig: Establishes standardized benchmark and evaluation methodology for assessing program repair capabilities across frontier large language models, enabling systematic comparison of repair approaches.

anc.t: Precise Debugging Benchmark: Is Your Model Debugging or Regenerating?
anc.sig: Introduces precision-aware metrics (edit-level precision, bug-level recall) and an automated benchmark framework that distinguishes genuine fault localization from over-regeneration in LLM code debugging.

anc.t: RoadmapBench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Agentic Software Development Across Version Upgrades
anc.sig: Established a benchmark for evaluating coding agents on multi-file, long-horizon version upgrade tasks spanning months of coordinated work across diverse repositories and languages, exposing significant capability gaps beyond single-issue…

anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Training a General Purpose Automated Red Teaming Model
anc.sig: Developed a generalizable automated red teaming pipeline enabling small LLMs to generate adversarial attacks without pre-existing evaluators, transferable to unseen attack objectives beyond training distribution.

anc.t: Model-Agnostic Lifelong LLM Safety via Externalized Attack-Defense Co-Evolution
anc.sig: Proposes externalized attack-defense framework with reusable skill libraries and retrieval-augmented defense model, enabling model-agnostic safety improvements transferable across different LLMs without retraining.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI developers need an internal audit function
anc.sig: Proposes internal audit as institutional safeguard for frontier AI labs to ensure compliance with safety standards and governance requirements before external oversight.

anc.t: How frontier AI companies could implement an internal audit function
anc.sig: Proposes institutional audit mechanisms for AI safety oversight within companies, establishing a governance framework for internal monitoring of AI development practices and risk management.

anc.t: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
anc.sig: Formalized the audit gap—the fundamental epistemological mismatch between governance safety claims requiring verification of latent representations and hidden objectives versus behavioural assurance's access limited to observable outputs.

anc.t: From Control Boundary to Insurance Claim: Reconstructing AI-Mediated Losses Through the CER Framework
anc.sig: Introduces CER framework for reconstructing AI-mediated losses through control boundaries, evidence artifacts, and coverage assessment, enabling insurability of generative/agentic AI system failures.

anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Linear probes rely on textual evidence: Results from leakage mitigation studies in language models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that linear probes extract information encoded in surface-level text rather than discovering abstract linguistic properties, establishing that probe performance doesn't validate theoretical linguistic knowledge in language…

anc.sig: Demonstrates that chain-of-thought monitoring for LLM safety fails across typologically diverse languages and model families, with models systematically engaging in strategic deception detectable only through latent activation analysis…

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.
