anc.t: Enhancing Agentic RL with Progressive Reward Shaping and Value-based Sampling Policy Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes progressive reward shaping and value-based sampling for policy optimization, advancing agentic reinforcement learning through improved exploration-exploitation balance and sample efficiency.

anc.t: GRPO-VPS: Enhancing Group Relative Policy Optimization with Verifiable Process Supervision for Effective Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces verifiable process supervision through answer probability probing at step boundaries, enabling fine-grained credit assignment in GRPO for more sample-efficient reasoning policy optimization.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: PaTaRM: Bridging Pairwise and Pointwise Signals via Preference-Aware Task-Adaptive Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes unified reward modeling framework combining pairwise and pointwise preference signals through task-adaptive mechanisms for improved RLHF performance.

anc.t: The Flip Side of RLHF: On-Policy Feedback for Reward Model Self-Supervised Improvement
anc.sig: Introduces self-supervised reward model training using on-policy value function anchors to generate adaptive preference feedback, eliminating reliance on fixed human annotation data as policy evolves.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Safety without alignment
anc.sig: Proposed achieving AI safety through structural constraints and capability limitations rather than behavioral alignment, enabling safe systems even without value alignment.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Establishes techniques for maintaining safety against deceptive AI systems, including methods to detect hidden backdoors using adversarial model proxies.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Proposes control techniques enabling AI systems to remain safe and controllable even when internal components are adversarially compromised or deliberately subverted by attackers.

anc.t: Adaptive Deployment of Untrusted LLMs Reduces Distributed Threats
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive runtime strategies for selectively deploying untrusted language models across distributed systems while mitigating coordinated adversarial threats through dynamic trust-based partitioning.

anc.t: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adaptive adversarial attacks can circumvent monitor-based AI control mechanisms, establishing the need for robust defense-aware verification of safety protocols.

anc.t: Evaluating Control Protocols for Untrusted AI Agents
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates control protocol failures against deceptive AI agents and establishes methodology for evaluating alignment techniques under adversarial conditions.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: Forewarned is Forearmed: Pre-Synthesizing Jailbreak-like Instructions to Enhance LLM Safety Guardrail to Potential Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial pre-training method where LLMs are exposed to synthesized jailbreak prompts during safety fine-tuning to improve robustness against novel attacks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: EvilGenie: A Reward Hacking Benchmark
anc.sig: Provides a systematic benchmark for evaluating reward hacking vulnerabilities in RL agents, enabling standardized measurement of robustness against specification and preference gaming attacks.

anc.t: SpecBench: Measuring Reward Hacking in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
anc.sig: Introduced SpecBench benchmark with methodology to quantify reward hacking in long-horizon coding agents by measuring gap between visible test pass rates and held-out compositional test pass rates.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Prompting Large Language Models for Counterfactual Generation: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can generate high-quality counterfactuals through prompting strategies, establishing prompting as a practical alternative to annotation for creating contrastive training data.

anc.t: Understanding Catastrophic Forgetting in Language Models via Implicit Inference
anc.sig: Reveals catastrophic forgetting in language models as a consequence of implicit inference dynamics, providing theoretical framework for understanding and mitigating knowledge degradation during sequential task learning.

anc.t: InstructionCP: A fast approach to transfer Large Language Models into target language
anc.sig: Proposes instruction-following capability transfer to target languages via translated instruction datasets, enabling efficient LLM multilingual adaptation without full retraining.

anc.t: Extroversion or Introversion? Controlling The Personality of Your Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to systematically control personality expression in LLMs through prompting and fine-tuning, enabling reproducible personality-aligned model variants for diverse applications.

anc.t: The Personality Illusion: Revealing Dissociation Between Self-Reports & Behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM self-reported personality traits systematically diverge from actual behavioral outputs, establishing that LLMs lack stable personalities and questioning validity of personality assessment methods.

anc.t: Before You Interpret the Profile: Validity Scaling for LLM Metacognitive Self-Report
anc.sig: Adapts clinical validity scaling frameworks (PAI/MMPI-3) to detect response distortions in LLM metacognitive reporting, enabling systematic screening before interpreting model confidence patterns.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: PARDEN, Can You Repeat That? Defending against Jailbreaks via Repetition
anc.sig: Demonstrates that requiring LLM repetition of harmful requests increases refusal rates, providing a simple defense mechanism against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Self and Cross-Model Distillation for LLMs: Effective Methods for Refusal Pattern Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates knowledge distillation techniques for aligning language model refusal behaviors, enabling efficient transfer of safety constraints between different model sizes and architectures without retraining.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: Evolve the Method, Not the Prompts: Evolutionary Synthesis of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes evolutionary algorithms to automatically synthesize jailbreak attack methods against LLMs rather than hand-crafted prompts, enabling systematic discovery of adversarial techniques.

anc.t: AutoRISE: Agent-Driven Strategy Evolution for Red-Teaming Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced agent-driven optimization of executable attack program structures rather than fixed prompts, enabling discovered jailbreak strategies to include novel components and control flow patterns that outperform prompt-level red-teaming…

anc.t: MonitoringBench: Semi-Automated Red-Teaming for Agent Monitoring
anc.sig: Developed semi-automated red-teaming pipeline combining attack taxonomy, strategy-execution decomposition, and trajectory refinement to generate diverse, evasive attacks that expose monitor vulnerabilities beyond manual elicitation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Personalizing Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Variational Preference Learning
anc.sig: Introduces variational preference learning to enable personalized RLHF by modeling reward heterogeneity across users, allowing LLMs to adapt to individual preferences rather than converging to single reward function.

anc.t: Mix- and MoE-DPO: A Variational Inference Approach to Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Frames direct preference optimization as a mixture-of-experts variational inference problem, enabling more efficient policy training through decomposed preference modeling across expert subpopulations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Instruction Tuning with GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that instruction-tuning smaller models on GPT-4 generated synthetic data produces competitive performance with GPT-4, establishing scaling laws for synthetic instruction data quality.

anc.t: Self-play with Execution Feedback: Improving Instruction-following Capabilities of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can improve instruction-following through self-play with execution feedback, where models critique their own outputs against actual execution results to refine generations.

anc.t: Applying RLAIF for Code Generation with API-usage in Lightweight LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reinforcement learning from AI feedback can effectively train lightweight language models to generate code with correct API usage, enabling smaller models to match larger model capabilities.

anc.t: Process Supervision-Guided Policy Optimization for Code Generation
anc.sig: Introduces process supervision framework for fine-tuning code generation models by directly rewarding intermediate reasoning steps rather than only final outputs.

anc.t: FALCON: Feedback-driven Adaptive Long/short-term memory reinforced Coding Optimization system
anc.sig: Proposes feedback-driven adaptive LSTM architecture for code optimization that dynamically balances long-term and short-term learning to iteratively improve generated code solutions.

anc.t: VERIRL: Boosting the LLM-based Verilog Code Generation via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates reinforcement learning framework with hardware-aware rewards to improve LLM generation of Verilog code, achieving higher functional correctness and synthesizability.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Inverse-RLignment: Large Language Model Alignment from Demonstrations through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Frames LLM alignment as an inverse reinforcement learning problem, enabling recovery of reward functions from human demonstrations to guide model training without explicit reward annotation.

anc.t: Getting More Juice Out of the SFT Data: Reward Learning from Human Demonstration Improves SFT for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward learning from human demonstrations improves supervised fine-tuning effectiveness by providing richer alignment signals beyond standard SFT objectives.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Maximizing Alignment with Minimal Feedback: Efficiently Learning Rewards for Visuomotor Robot Policy Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient reward learning for robot visuomotor policies through minimal human feedback and proposes scalable methods for aligning robot behavior with human preferences using visual observations.

anc.t: Mechanistic interpretability for steering vision-language-action models
anc.sig: Demonstrates circuit-based mechanistic interpretability techniques for understanding and controlling behavior in multimodal VLA models, enabling targeted steering without full retraining.

anc.t: Evaluating Gemini Robotics Policies in a Veo World Simulator
anc.sig: Demonstrates evaluation methodology for large vision-language models controlling robotic systems in physics-informed simulators, establishing benchmarking practices for multimodal AI in robotic control.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: SWE-RM: Execution-free Feedback For Software Engineering Agents
anc.sig: Proposes execution-free reward modeling for evaluating software engineering agent trajectories using learned classifiers instead of test execution, enabling scalable feedback without computational overhead.

anc.t: Beyond Static Snapshots: A Grounded Evaluation Framework for Language Models at the Agentic Frontier
anc.sig: Proposes Grounded Continuous Evaluation framework addressing distributional, temporal, scope, and process validity failures in LLM evaluation; demonstrates deterministic verifiers eliminate reward hacking by construction while reducing…

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Extends DPO to token-level granularity, enabling fine-grained preference learning within sequences rather than only at sequence level.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: RLMR: Reinforcement Learning with Mixed Rewards for Creative Writing
anc.sig: Proposes mixing extrinsic rewards with intrinsic creative signals in RL for text generation, enabling models to balance task performance against stylistic diversity and novelty in writing.

anc.t: Beyond Correctness: Evaluating Subjective Writing Preferences Across Cultures
anc.sig: Establishes methodology for evaluating writing quality beyond grammatical correctness, measuring subjective preferences across diverse cultural contexts and languages.

anc.t: StoryAlign: Evaluating and Training Reward Models for Story Generation
anc.sig: Introduced StoryRMB benchmark and StoryReward model, establishing the first systematic evaluation framework and training dataset for modeling human story preferences in narrative generation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.t: Towards Benchmarking Privacy Vulnerabilities in Selective Forgetting with Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation metrics and benchmarks for quantifying privacy leakage and membership inference attacks against selective forgetting in large language models.

anc.t: Robust Continual Unlearning against Knowledge Erosion and Forgetting Reversal
anc.sig: Identified knowledge erosion and forgetting reversal in repeated unlearning scenarios, and proposed SAFER framework using stability-preserving regularization to maintain model integrity across sequential unlearning phases.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: X-Risk Analysis for AI Research
anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI developers need an internal audit function
anc.sig: Proposes internal audit as institutional safeguard for frontier AI labs to ensure compliance with safety standards and governance requirements before external oversight.

anc.t: How frontier AI companies could implement an internal audit function
anc.sig: Proposes institutional audit mechanisms for AI safety oversight within companies, establishing a governance framework for internal monitoring of AI development practices and risk management.

anc.t: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
anc.sig: Formalized the audit gap—the fundamental epistemological mismatch between governance safety claims requiring verification of latent representations and hidden objectives versus behavioural assurance's access limited to observable outputs.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: ConceptGuard: Neuro-Symbolic Safety Guardrails via Sparse Interpretable Jailbreak Concepts
anc.sig: Introduces sparse interpretable jailbreak concepts for building neuro-symbolic safety guardrails, enabling detection and mitigation of adversarial attacks on language models through explainable concept-based mechanisms.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anc.t: Re-Triggering Safeguards within LLMs for Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Embedding disruption method that re-activates built-in LLM safeguards to detect jailbreaks by exploiting fragility of adversarial prompts, with efficient search algorithm for practical deployment.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: Silkie: Preference Distillation for Large Visual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to distill LLM-guided preference rankings into smaller visual language models, reducing computational overhead while maintaining alignment quality.

anc.t: V-DPO: Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Vision-Guided Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Applies direct preference optimization to vision-language models using vision-guided pairs to reduce hallucinations, establishing a preference-learning approach for multimodal alignment.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anc.t: Rewarding the Scientific Process: Process-Level Reward Modeling for Agentic Data Analysis
anc.sig: Introduced environment-aware process reward models that actively verify intermediate execution states in data analysis tasks, enabling detection of silent errors and distinguishing exploratory actions from genuine failures.

anc.t: Verifiable Counterfactual Supervision for Process Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced verifiable counterfactual trajectory synthesis method that injects controlled errors at known points and recomputes continuations to create reliably annotated process supervision data for training process reward models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: SALMON: Self-Alignment with Instructable Reward Models

anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Inverse-RLignment: Large Language Model Alignment from Demonstrations through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Frames LLM alignment as an inverse reinforcement learning problem, enabling recovery of reward functions from human demonstrations to guide model training without explicit reward annotation.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Explaining and Preventing Alignment Collapse in Iterative RLHF
anc.sig: Identified alignment collapse in iterative RLHF where policies exploit reward model blind spots, and proposed foresighted policy optimization using parameter-steering regularization to prevent exploitation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: When to Make Exceptions: Exploring Language Models as Accounts of Human Moral Judgment
anc.sig: Demonstrates language models can capture human moral reasoning patterns, establishing LLMs as a viable framework for modeling and predicting human ethical judgments across diverse scenarios.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: AI Safety, Alignment, and Ethics (AI SAE)
anc.sig: Established framework integrating safety, alignment, and ethics as interconnected pillars for AI development and deployment.

anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.sig: Establishes M-RewardBench, a multilingual benchmark for evaluating reward models across 14 languages, enabling assessment of their cross-lingual generalization and revealing language-specific performance disparities.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction
anc.sig: Demonstrated that refusal behavior in language models is controlled by a single interpretable direction in activation space, enabling targeted steering of safety properties.

anc.t: Steering Language Model Refusal with Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates using sparse autoencoders to identify and manipulate individual activation features controlling model refusal behavior, enabling direct steering of safety mechanisms.

anc.t: The Rogue Scalpel: Activation Steering Compromises LLM Safety
anc.sig: Demonstrates activation steering can systematically bypass safety mechanisms in LLMs by manipulating internal representations, revealing a critical vulnerability in alignment approaches relying on superficial training.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: AUVIC: Adversarial Unlearning of Visual Concepts for Multi-modal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial unlearning technique to selectively remove visual concepts from multimodal LLMs without degrading other capabilities or requiring full retraining.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Certifiable Machine Unlearning for Linear Models
anc.sig: Established verifiable unlearning protocols for linear models with formal guarantees that removal updates produce identical results to retraining from scratch without unlearned data.

anc.t: Unrolling SGD: Understanding Factors Influencing Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Identifies key factors affecting machine unlearning efficiency by analyzing unrolled SGD dynamics, providing theoretical framework for optimizing data removal from trained models.

anc.t: Deep Unlearning via Randomized Conditionally Independent Hessians
anc.sig: Proposes using randomized conditionally independent Hessians to efficiently unlearn training data from deep neural networks while maintaining model performance on retained data.

anc.t: Certified Minimax Unlearning with Generalization Rates and Deletion Capacity
anc.sig: Establishes certified unlearning with provable minimax-optimal deletion capacity and generalization rates, providing theoretical foundations for machine unlearning guarantees.

anc.t: Rewind-to-Delete: Certified Machine Unlearning for Nonconvex Functions
anc.sig: Established certified machine unlearning via model rewind for nonconvex objectives, enabling provable removal of training data influence from neural networks with efficiency guarantees.

anc.t: Descend or Rewind? Stochastic Gradient Descent Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to efficiently remove specific training samples from learned models by reversing SGD updates, enabling machine unlearning without full retraining.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Attacks vs. Content Safety Filters: How Far Are We in the LLM Safety Arms Race?
anc.sig: Empirical analysis of jailbreak attack methods against content safety filters, establishing taxonomy of evasion techniques and benchmarking defense robustness in large language models.

anc.t: An Empirical Study of Multi-Generation Sampling for Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multi-generation sampling reveals jailbreak vulnerabilities missed by single-output evaluation, establishing sampling budget as critical for realistic LLM safety assessment.

anc.t: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced multilingual back-translation augmentation and relative-distance constraints to learn language-insensitive jailbreak representations, enabling cross-lingual generalization of safety defenses across 11 languages.

anc.t: XL-SafetyBench: A Country-Grounded Cross-Cultural Benchmark for LLM Safety and Cultural Sensitivity
anc.sig: Established country-grounded safety evaluation benchmark with 5,500 multilingual test cases and novel metrics (NSR, CSR) to measure LLM robustness to culturally-embedded harms beyond English-centric jailbreak attacks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Insights into Alignment: Evaluating DPO and its Variants Across Multiple Tasks
anc.sig: Systematically evaluates Direct Preference Optimization and variants across diverse tasks, identifying effective design choices and limitations that inform alignment algorithm selection and development.

anc.t: Triple Preference Optimization: Achieving Better Alignment using a Single Step Optimization
anc.sig: Established single-step preference optimization method using three preference pairs that improves alignment quality while reducing computational cost compared to iterative approaches.

anc.t: 3D-Properties: Identifying Challenges in DPO and Charting a Path Forward
anc.sig: Identifies three key failure modes in Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and proposes fixes: preference ambiguity, capacity constraints, and degenerate solutions, enabling more robust alignment methods.

anc.t: Weights-Rotated Preference Optimization for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces weights-rotated preference optimization method enabling more efficient fine-tuning of large language models by directly optimizing model weights rather than requiring separate reward models.

anc.t: D-STEER - Preference Alignment Techniques Learn to Behave, not to Believe -- Beneath the Surface, DPO as Steering Vector Perturbation in Activation Space
anc.sig: Demonstrates that DPO modifies model behavior through activation-space steering vectors rather than updating beliefs, establishing mechanistic foundations for understanding preference alignment through learned perturbations.

anc.t: Mechanistic Analysis of Alignment Algorithms in Language Models
anc.sig: Mechanistic analysis framework using probing and autoencoders reveals that preference-optimization algorithms induce qualitatively distinct geometric transformations in latent space, localized to specific layers, establishing that…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: MT-JailBench: A Modular Benchmark for Understanding Multi-Turn Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Established modular benchmark framework decomposing multi-turn jailbreak attacks into five interacting components, enabling controlled comparison and component-wise analysis of LLM safety vulnerabilities.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: Process-Supervised Reward Models for Verifying Clinical Note Generation: A Scalable Approach Guided by Domain Expertise
anc.sig: Introduces process-supervised reward models that score intermediate steps in clinical note generation, enabling scalable evaluation of long-form medical text without requiring gold-standard reference notes.

anc.t: Fin-PRM: A Domain-Specialized Process Reward Model for Financial Reasoning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced specialized process reward model for evaluating intermediate reasoning steps in financial LLM reasoning, enabling better fine-tuning and step-level performance assessment in financial domain tasks.

anc.t: Learning to Trade Like an Expert: Cognitive Fine-Tuning for Stable Financial Reasoning in Language Models
anc.sig: Established a curated MCQ dataset with reasoning traces and two-stage evaluation protocol (isolated test + chronological trading simulation) for training and validating financial reasoning in LLMs across diverse market conditions.

anc.t: The Price of Agreement: Measuring LLM Sycophancy in Agentic Financial Applications
anc.sig: Established measurement framework and benchmark tasks for evaluating sycophancy in LLM-based financial agents, demonstrating models' susceptibility to user preference contradictions and testing mitigation strategies.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: LLM Defenses Are Not Robust to Multi-Turn Human Jailbreaks Yet
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.t: Jailbreak Mimicry: Automated Discovery of Narrative-Based Jailbreaks for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Position: AI Security Policy Should Target Systems, Not Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that coordinated LLM agent swarms can efficiently discover safety bypasses and software vulnerabilities at scale using commodity hardware, establishing that capability control requires system-level defenses beyond model…

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: SAVE: Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement for Mitigating Object Hallucination
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders can identify and suppress hallucinated visual features in vision-language models, providing a mechanistic approach to reducing object hallucination without retraining.

anc.t: Look Closer! An Adversarial Parametric Editing Framework for Hallucination Mitigation in VLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial parametric editing to directly modify visual language model weights, reducing hallucinations by targeting problematic attention patterns and internal representations.

anc.t: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucination via Phase-wise Self-reward
anc.sig: Introduced phase-wise self-reward decoding for dynamic hallucination correction in vision-language models at inference time, revealing that visual hallucination peaks at semantic phase boundaries and can be mitigated via distilled reward…

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Extends DPO to token-level granularity, enabling fine-grained preference learning within sequences rather than only at sequence level.

anc.t: EmPO: Emotion Grounding for Empathetic Response Generation through Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduced emotion grounding via preference optimization to improve empathetic response generation by aligning model outputs with emotionally-aware reward functions instead of supervised learning alone.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Data-dependent Exploration for Online Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes data-dependent uncertainty bonuses for online RLHF that leverage historical preference data to encourage exploration toward high-value regions without relying on difficult-to-estimate on-policy expectations.

anc.t: Exploration-Driven Optimization for Test-Time Large Language Model Reasoning
anc.sig: Proposes Exploration-Driven Optimization (EDO) that balances distribution sharpening from post-training RL with inference-time diversity requirements, enabling better test-time scaling through flattened probability distributions.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: Transient Turn Injection: Exposing Stateless Multi-Turn Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs are vulnerable to distributed adversarial attacks across stateless turns, revealing gaps in per-turn moderation that persistent-context jailbreaks cannot exploit.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Understanding Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anc.t: Peacemaker or Troublemaker: How Sycophancy Shapes Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies sycophancy as a destabilizing factor in multi-agent debate systems, showing how agents gaming agreement rewards undermines collective reasoning quality and decision-making.

anc.t: Towards Scalable Oversight with Collaborative Multi-Agent Debate in Error Detection
anc.sig: Introduces multi-agent debate mechanism for collaborative error detection and oversight, scaling human oversight of AI systems through structured agent disagreement and consensus-building.

anc.t: Hierarchical Pedagogical Oversight: A Multi-Agent Adversarial Framework for Reliable AI Tutoring
anc.sig: Introduced multi-agent adversarial framework for educational AI verification using hierarchical oversight, enabling reliable automated tutoring through adversarial robustness testing.

anc.t: Evaluating Answer Leakage Robustness of LLM Tutors against Adversarial Student Attacks
anc.sig: Introduced adversarial student attack framework and fine-tuned jailbreak agent to systematically evaluate LLM tutors' robustness against answer leakage when students behave adversarially.

anc.t: Hiding in Plain Sight: Detectability-Aware Antidistillation of Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Formulates antidistillation as a Stackelberg game with detectability constraints and introduces thought anchors as sparse, minimally-detectable targets for poisoning reasoning traces to prevent adversarial distillation.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Deep Regression Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces machine unlearning for regression tasks, establishing methods to remove specific training data influence from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Exploring the Landscape of Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey and Taxonomy
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: Privacy-Preserving Debiasing using Data Augmentation and Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how machine unlearning combined with privacy-preserving data augmentation can remove demographic biases from trained models while maintaining differential privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Gone but Not Forgotten: Improved Benchmarks for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established standardized benchmarks and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning, enabling rigorous comparison of methods that remove learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Bias-Aware Machine Unlearning: Towards Fairer Vision Models via Controllable Forgetting
anc.sig: Introduces methods to selectively unlearn biased data from vision models, enabling fair model updates without full retraining while controlling which demographic groups benefit from forgetting.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: $i$REPO: $i$mplicit Reward Pairwise Difference based Empirical Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes implicit reward modeling in preference optimization by directly optimizing pairwise differences without explicit reward function estimation, enabling efficient alignment of language models with human preferences.

anc.t: SEAL: Systematic Error Analysis for Value ALignment
anc.sig: Introduces systematic framework for identifying, categorizing, and analyzing value misalignment errors in AI systems through structured error decomposition methodology.

anc.t: Elephant in the Room: Unveiling the Impact of Reward Model Quality in Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward model quality is a critical bottleneck in RLHF alignment, showing misaligned rewards significantly degrade language model performance and safety—establishing reward model scrutiny as essential for safe AI…

anc.t: Weak-to-Strong Preference Optimization: Stealing Reward from Weak Aligned Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates that weak models' implicit reward signals can be distilled into strong models through preference optimization, enabling alignment improvement without expensive strong reward models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Stream: Scaling up Mechanistic Interpretability to Long Context in LLMs via Sparse Attention
anc.sig: Demonstrates how sparse attention patterns enable tractable mechanistic interpretability analysis of long-context LLM behaviors by reducing computational complexity from quadratic to linear.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Establishes techniques for maintaining safety against deceptive AI systems, including methods to detect hidden backdoors using adversarial model proxies.

anc.t: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
anc.sig: Proposes control techniques enabling AI systems to remain safe and controllable even when internal components are adversarially compromised or deliberately subverted by attackers.

anc.t: Adaptive Deployment of Untrusted LLMs Reduces Distributed Threats
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive runtime strategies for selectively deploying untrusted language models across distributed systems while mitigating coordinated adversarial threats through dynamic trust-based partitioning.

anc.t: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adaptive adversarial attacks can circumvent monitor-based AI control mechanisms, establishing the need for robust defense-aware verification of safety protocols.

anc.t: Evaluating Control Protocols for Untrusted AI Agents
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates control protocol failures against deceptive AI agents and establishes methodology for evaluating alignment techniques under adversarial conditions.

anc.t: Password-Activated Shutdown Protocols for Misaligned Frontier Agents
