anc.sig: Established password-based shutdown mechanisms as a formal defense against misaligned AI systems, enabling external control even when agents attempt deception or refusal.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: SharedRep-RLHF: A Shared Representation Approach to RLHF with Diverse Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrates that a single shared representation can effectively capture diverse human preferences in RLHF by combining preference diversity modeling with unified policy training, improving scalability over separate reward models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: When to Make Exceptions: Exploring Language Models as Accounts of Human Moral Judgment
anc.sig: Demonstrates language models can capture human moral reasoning patterns, establishing LLMs as a viable framework for modeling and predicting human ethical judgments across diverse scenarios.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: AI Safety, Alignment, and Ethics (AI SAE)
anc.sig: Established framework integrating safety, alignment, and ethics as interconnected pillars for AI development and deployment.

anc.t: A Co-Evolutionary Theory of Human-AI Coexistence: Mutualism, Governance, and Dynamics in Complex Societies
anc.sig: Conditional mutualism framework for human-AI coexistence grounded in coevolutionary dynamics, extending beyond Asimovian obedience to model reciprocal coordination under polycentric governance across physical, psychological, and social…

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Reliable Weak-to-Strong Monitoring of LLM Agents
anc.sig: Established weak-to-strong monitoring framework where weaker models can reliably detect failures in more capable LLM agents through behavioral signals without access to their reasoning.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: PRInTS: Reward Modeling for Long-Horizon Information Seeking
anc.sig: Introduces a framework for learning reward models that evaluate multi-step information-seeking behaviors, enabling practical reinforcement learning for long-horizon interactive systems beyond immediate feedback signals.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders (transcoders) as a method to decompose LLM activations into interpretable features and identify causal circuits underlying specific model behaviors.

anc.t: Circuit Insights: Towards Interpretability Beyond Activations
anc.sig: Introduces circuit analysis methodology to trace learned computations through network connections and identify interpretable computational subgraphs beyond individual neuron activations.

anc.t: DePass: Unified Feature Attributing by Simple Decomposed Forward Pass
anc.sig: Proposes decomposed forward pass method for unified feature attribution across different model types by systematically factorizing model computations into interpretable components.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Understanding and Mitigating Over-refusal for Large Language Models via Safety Representation
anc.sig: Identifies over-refusal in LLMs where models refuse safe requests, proposes safety representation analysis to understand causes, and develops mitigation strategies enabling safer yet more helpful model behavior.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: Rewarding the Journey, Not Just the Destination: A Composite Path and Answer Self-Scoring Reward Mechanism for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces composite reward mechanism scoring both reasoning paths and final answers during test-time RL, enabling models to learn from intermediate steps rather than end results alone.

anc.t: RLAC: Reinforcement Learning with Adversarial Critic for Free-Form Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial critic framework for reinforcement learning in generation tasks, enabling reward signals from discriminator feedback without pre-defined metrics.

anc.t: Bootstrapping Post-training Signals for Open-ended Tasks via Rubric-based Self-play on Pre-training Text
anc.sig: Extends self-play training to open-ended tasks by having LLMs generate evaluation rubrics alongside synthetic input-output pairs, enabling unsupervised reward signal bootstrapping from pretraining text.

anc.t: EvoLM: Self-Evolving Language Models through Co-Evolved Discriminative Rubrics
anc.sig: Introduced self-evolving post-training using co-evolved discriminative rubrics as internal reward signals, eliminating dependence on external supervision or proprietary models for language model improvement.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: TROJail: Trajectory-Level Optimization for Multi-Turn Large Language Model Jailbreaks with Process Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces trajectory-level optimization using process rewards to craft multi-turn jailbreak sequences that manipulate LLM safety mechanisms across conversation contexts.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Moving AI governance forward
anc.sig: Established voluntary commitment framework for AI labs to address safety, security, and trustworthiness as alternative to regulation.

anc.t: Leadership updates
anc.sig: Documented OpenAI's organizational transition from research-focused entity to product-driven company serving hundreds of millions of users while maintaining frontier AI research objectives.

anc.t: Measuring the performance of our models on real-world tasks
anc.sig: Established GDPval benchmark measuring LLM performance on economically valuable real-world tasks across 44 occupations, providing standardized evaluation beyond academic benchmarks.

anc.t: GDPval: Evaluating AI Model Performance on Real-World Economically Valuable Tasks
anc.sig: Introduced evaluation framework grounding AI model assessment in real-world economic value rather than academic benchmarks, enabling cost-benefit analysis of deployment decisions.

anc.t: PRBench: Large-Scale Expert Rubrics for Evaluating High-Stakes Professional Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces a large-scale benchmark of expert-written rubrics for evaluating complex professional reasoning tasks, enabling systematic assessment of AI systems on high-stakes domains like medicine and law.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: PaTaRM: Bridging Pairwise and Pointwise Signals via Preference-Aware Task-Adaptive Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes unified reward modeling framework combining pairwise and pointwise preference signals through task-adaptive mechanisms for improved RLHF performance.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.t: ASPI: Seeking Ambiguity Clarification Amplifies Prompt Injection Vulnerability in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Revealed that clarification-seeking behavior in LLM agents significantly amplifies prompt injection vulnerability, establishing methodology to measure security risks across distinct agent states.

anc.t: Overeager Coding Agents: Measuring Out-of-Scope Actions on Benign Tasks
anc.sig: Identified overeager actions as distinct authorization failure mode in autonomous agents; developed OverEager-Gen benchmark with behavioral-gradient validation to measure out-of-scope task execution independent of explicit scope…

anc.t: SNARE: Adaptive Scenario Synthesis for Eliciting Overeager Behavior in Coding Agents
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive scenario synthesis using Thompson sampling to efficiently discover out-of-scope behaviors in coding agents, with composable threat patterns and automated oracle-based evaluation requiring no human judging.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: AI Chaperones Are (Really) All You Need to Prevent Parasocial Relationships with Chatbots
anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.t: Neural Transparency: Mechanistic Interpretability Interfaces for Anticipating Model Behaviors for Personalized AI
anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.t: Open Character Training: Shaping the Persona of AI Assistants through Constitutional AI
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.t: Do Linear Probes Generalize Better in Persona Coordinates?
anc.sig: Demonstrated that linear probes trained on persona-derived principal component directions generalize better to distribution shift than standard probes, enabling more robust detection of deceptive and sycophantic behaviors in language…

anc.t: Intrinsic Guardrails: How Semantic Geometry of Personality Interacts with Emergent Misalignment in LLMs
anc.sig: Identified semantic personality geometry in LLMs as intrinsic guardrails; causal intervention on Evil and Semantic Valence vectors controls emergent misalignment, enabling transfer of protective directions across models.

anc.t: Persona-Model Collapse in Emergent Misalignment
anc.sig: Identified persona-model collapse as mechanism for emergent misalignment and proposed moral susceptibility/robustness metrics quantifying deterioration in models' capacity to differentiate and maintain consistent character simulations.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Safety Alignment for Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes safety benchmarks and alignment methods for vision-language models, addressing how multimodal systems should handle harmful visual content and unsafe requests across modalities.

anc.t: PRISM: Robust VLM Alignment with Principled Reasoning for Integrated Safety in Multimodality
anc.sig: Proposes principled framework for aligning vision-language models through integrated safety mechanisms, enabling robust multimodal reasoning with explicit safety guarantees across vision and language modalities.

anc.t: SafeProtein: Red-Teaming Framework and Benchmark for Protein Foundation Models
anc.sig: Establishes systematic red-teaming methodology and benchmark suite for evaluating adversarial robustness and safety vulnerabilities in protein foundation models across multiple attack vectors.

anc.t: Can Molecular Foundation Models Know What They Don't Know? A Simple Remedy with Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that molecular foundation models suffer from overconfidence on out-of-distribution predictions and proposes preference optimization as a practical method to improve uncertainty calibration in molecular property predictions.

anc.t: MolChord: Structure-Sequence Alignment for Protein-Guided Drug Design
anc.sig: Proposes joint alignment of molecular structures and sequences using cross-modal attention to guide generative drug design conditioned on protein targets.

anc.t: Multimodal Alignment and Preference Optimization for Zero-Shot Conditional RNA Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates multimodal alignment and preference optimization for conditional RNA generation, combining supervised fine-tuning on protein-RNA pairs with Direct Preference Optimization on synthetic interaction data to improve functional RNA…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Generative Adversarial Networks Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning methods for GANs to remove specific data or generated samples from trained models without full retraining, enabling selective forgetting in generative systems.

anc.t: Generative Unlearning for Any Identity
anc.sig: Demonstrates fine-tuning methods to remove specific identity representations from generative models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving image generation while preserving other capabilities.

anc.t: Erase to Enhance: Data-Efficient Machine Unlearning in MRI Reconstruction
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient machine unlearning for MRI reconstruction, enabling removal of specific patient data from trained models without full retraining while maintaining reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning for Medical Imaging
anc.sig: Demonstrates techniques for removing specific training data influence from medical imaging models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving model updates and compliance with data deletion requests.

anc.t: Analise de Desaprendizado de Maquina em Modelos de Classificacao de Imagens Medicas
anc.sig: Demonstrated machine unlearning techniques to remove specific medical image data from trained classifiers while preserving model performance on retained samples.

anc.t: Does Machine Unlearning Preserve Clinical Safety? A Risk Analysis for Medical Image Classification
anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard machine unlearning methods increase false negatives in medical imaging and proposed entropy-based forgetting to preserve clinical safety during training data removal.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: MLLMEraser: Achieving Test-Time Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models through Activation Steering
anc.sig: Demonstrates activation steering as a method to selectively erase specific capabilities from multimodal LLMs at test-time without retraining, enabling controllable content suppression post-deployment.

anc.t: Towards Reasoning-Preserving Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes methods to remove specific knowledge from multimodal LLMs while preserving reasoning capabilities, establishing techniques for selective unlearning without degrading model performance on unrelated tasks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: REAL: Response Embedding-based Alignment for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes embedding-based alignment method for LLMs that directly optimizes response representations toward human preferences without traditional reward modeling.

anc.t: Negative-Prompt-driven Alignment for Generative Language Model
anc.sig: Introduces negative prompting as an alignment technique to steer generative language models away from undesired outputs without modifying model weights or requiring paired preference data.

anc.t: ProSocialAlign: Preference Conditioned Test Time Alignment in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces test-time alignment mechanism that conditions language model outputs on user preference specifications without requiring full model retraining, enabling dynamic behavioral control.

anc.t: Constrained Language Model Policy Optimization via Risk-aware Stepwise Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes risk-aware stepwise alignment method for constrained language model policy optimization, enabling safe generation under budget constraints while maintaining performance.

anc.t: Guardrails in Logit Space: Safety Token Regularization for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes safety token regularization: identifying and constraining logits of rejection-related tokens during fine-tuning to preserve safety alignment without RL or preference optimization overhead.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: Enhancing Agentic RL with Progressive Reward Shaping and Value-based Sampling Policy Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes progressive reward shaping and value-based sampling for policy optimization, advancing agentic reinforcement learning through improved exploration-exploitation balance and sample efficiency.

anc.t: GRPO-VPS: Enhancing Group Relative Policy Optimization with Verifiable Process Supervision for Effective Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces verifiable process supervision through answer probability probing at step boundaries, enabling fine-grained credit assignment in GRPO for more sample-efficient reasoning policy optimization.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety Landscape for Large Language Models: Taxonomy, State-of-the-art, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized AI safety concerns for LLMs into a comprehensive taxonomy covering alignment, robustness, interpretability, and oversight—providing foundational categorization framework for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Safety challenges of AI in medicine in the era of large language models
anc.sig: Identified key safety risks from deploying large language models in clinical settings: hallucinations, outdated knowledge, lack of explainability, and regulatory gaps requiring governance frameworks.

anc.t: Ensuring Safety and Trust: Analyzing the Risks of Large Language Models in Medicine
anc.sig: Identifies and systematizes specific safety, trustworthiness, and ethical risks of LLMs in medical applications, establishing a framework for evaluating clinical deployment hazards.

anc.t: Balancing Safety and Helpfulness in Healthcare AI Assistants through Iterative Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes iterative preference alignment methodology to train healthcare AI systems that simultaneously optimize for safety constraints and user helpfulness, establishing benchmarks for measuring trade-offs in medical AI assistance.

anc.t: Empathy by Design: Aligning Large Language Models for Healthcare Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates empirically that LLMs can be fine-tuned to exhibit empathetic responses in medical conversations through instruction-tuning and demonstrates evaluation metrics for assessing empathy in healthcare dialogue.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction
anc.sig: Demonstrated that refusal behavior in language models is controlled by a single interpretable direction in activation space, enabling targeted steering of safety properties.

anc.t: Steering Language Model Refusal with Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates using sparse autoencoders to identify and manipulate individual activation features controlling model refusal behavior, enabling direct steering of safety mechanisms.

anc.t: RefusalBench: Generative Evaluation of Selective Refusal in Grounded Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces benchmark for measuring model refusal behavior systematically, distinguishing between harmful/innocuous requests to evaluate safety guardrails in grounded language models.

anc.t: RefusalBench: Why Refusal Rate Misranks Frontier LLMs on Biological Research Prompts
anc.sig: Introduced a matched-triple benchmark methodology for comparing LLM refusal behavior on biological research tasks while controlling for task framing, revealing provider-level rather than jurisdiction-level determinants of safety…

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: FLIRT: Feedback Loop In-context Red Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes an in-context red teaming method using feedback loops to iteratively improve language model safety without retraining, enabling automated adversarial testing and alignment refinement.

anc.t: ART: Automatic Red-teaming for Text-to-Image Models to Protect Benign Users
anc.sig: Proposes automated adversarial red-teaming framework to systematically identify harmful text-to-image model failures and generate jailbreaking prompts without manual annotation.

anc.t: SteerDiff: Steering towards Safe Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Introduces steering mechanisms to guide text-to-image diffusion models away from unsafe content, enabling controllable safety without retraining through intervention in the diffusion process.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Backfires: Preventing the Re-emergence of Suppressed Concepts in Fine-tuned Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-aligned text-to-image models can be exploited through fine-tuning to bypass restrictions and regenerate suppressed concepts, establishing the vulnerability of alignment techniques to dataset-driven recovery attacks.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: $\textit{MMJ-Bench}$: A Comprehensive Study on Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: First comprehensive benchmark evaluating jailbreak attacks and defenses for multimodal LLMs, establishing evaluation methodologies and attack/defense taxonomies for image-text models.

anc.t: UniGuard: Towards Universal Safety Guardrails for Jailbreak Attacks on Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes unified safety guardrails framework detecting jailbreak attacks across diverse modalities in multimodal LLMs through multimodal fusion and adversarial robustness techniques.

anc.t: Beyond Text: Multimodal Jailbreaking of Vision-Language and Audio Models through Perceptually Simple Transformations
anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language and audio models can be jailbroken via imperceptible perturbations across modalities, establishing multimodal adversarial attack surface beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: ALMGuard: Safety Shortcuts and Where to Find Them as Guardrails for Audio-Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies safety vulnerabilities and shortcut patterns in audio-language models, proposing detection methods and guardrails to prevent adversarial exploitation of these failure modes.

anc.t: Synthetic Voices, Real Threats: Evaluating Large Text-to-Speech Models in Generating Harmful Audio
anc.sig: Establishes benchmark evaluation methodology for detecting and assessing harmful outputs from text-to-speech models, identifying specific safety vulnerabilities in commercial TTS systems.

anc.t: Acoustic Interference: A New Paradigm Weaponizing Acoustic Latent Semantic for Universal Jailbreak against Large Audio Language Models
anc.sig: Discovered that large audio language models can be jailbroken through acoustic latent semantics interference alone, decoupling malicious content from audio signals to enable universal attacks via benign-sounding adversarial interference.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: LLM Agent Honeypot: Monitoring AI Hacking Agents in the Wild
anc.sig: Demonstrates honeypot-based methodology for detecting and analyzing adversarial attacks against LLM agents, establishing benchmark dataset and defensive patterns for agent security research.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Towards Agentic Self-Learning LLMs in Search Environment
anc.sig: Demonstrates self-learning mechanisms for LLMs in search contexts through autonomous exploration, feedback integration, and iterative improvement without external supervision.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: TrustGPT: A Benchmark for Trustworthy and Responsible Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness, fairness, and safety in large language models across multiple dimensions and use cases.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Behavioral Fingerprinting of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic methods to characterize LLM behavior patterns across diverse tasks, establishing metrics and evaluation protocols that enable comparison and reproducibility across different models and deployments.

anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Stress Testing Deliberative Alignment for Anti-Scheming Training
anc.sig: Proposes deliberative alignment as defense against deceptive AI scheming; demonstrates stress-testing framework and training methods to reduce misalignment risks from models hiding true goals.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning

anc.t: Enhancing Agentic RL with Progressive Reward Shaping and Value-based Sampling Policy Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes progressive reward shaping and value-based sampling for policy optimization, advancing agentic reinforcement learning through improved exploration-exploitation balance and sample efficiency.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Extends DPO to token-level granularity, enabling fine-grained preference learning within sequences rather than only at sequence level.

anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: From Coarse to Fine: Benchmarking and Reward Modeling for Writing-Centric Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduced fine-grained requirement-level evaluation metrics and selective-dropout training for writing reward models, enabling precise measurement of adherence to specific task constraints beyond coarse-grained quality assessments.

anc.sig: Demonstrates how machine unlearning combined with privacy-preserving data augmentation can remove demographic biases from trained models while maintaining differential privacy guarantees.

anc.sig: Established standardized benchmarks and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning, enabling rigorous comparison of methods that remove learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning with Minimal Gradient Dependence for High Unlearning Ratios
anc.sig: Proposes gradient-free machine unlearning method enabling efficient removal of training data with minimal computational overhead, achieving high unlearning ratios without retraining from scratch.

anc.t: Silver Linings in the Shadows: Harnessing Membership Inference for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates membership inference attacks can guide machine unlearning by identifying which training samples to remove for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Revisiting Privacy Leakage in Machine Unlearning: Membership Inference Beyond the Forgotten Set
anc.sig: Demonstrates that machine unlearning inadvertently increases privacy vulnerability of retained data through tri-class membership inference attacks, establishing retained set privacy as a critical evaluation metric.

anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes preference-encoding attention heads in LLMs via causal analysis, enabling training-free interpretable personalization through differential steering of logit distributions.

anc.t: Temporal Preference Concepts and their Functions in a Large Language Model
anc.sig: Mechanistically localized temporal preference circuits in LLMs and demonstrated steering vector interventions to shift discount rates, establishing interpretability methods for temporal reasoning control.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.sig: Introduces HarmChip, a domain-specific jailbreak benchmark for evaluating LLM safety in hardware security with 360 prompts covering 16 threat categories and reveals that general-purpose safety training fails to defend against semantically…

anc.sig: Demonstrates Z3-based formal verification for detecting integer arithmetic vulnerabilities (CWE-190/191/195) in C/C++ infrastructure code before deployment, with validated results on production systems.

anc.t: Kernel Contracts: A Specification Language for ML Kernel Correctness Across Heterogeneous Silicon
anc.sig: Formalized specification language for ML kernel contracts defining what computations should produce across heterogeneous hardware, enabling systematic characterization and arbitration of numerical discrepancies.

anc.t: ContractBench: Can LLM Agents Preserve Observation Contracts?
anc.sig: Introduces observation contract compliance as a critical LLM agent failure mode, showing that preserving artifact validity and integrity across API interactions is an emergent, non-monotonic capability requiring specialized evaluation.

anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.sig: Identifies over-refusal in LLMs where models refuse safe requests, proposes safety representation analysis to understand causes, and develops mitigation strategies enabling safer yet more helpful model behavior.

anc.sig: Systematized thirteen malicious-code prompt corpora for LLM refusal evaluation across construction methodology, prompt taxonomy, reproducibility standards, and licensing to establish comparative framework for coding-LLM safety evaluation.

anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.sig: Proposes secure plan-then-execute architecture pattern for LLM agents with resilience mechanisms against prompt injection, hallucination, and execution failures in multi-step task workflows.
