anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.t: ASPI: Seeking Ambiguity Clarification Amplifies Prompt Injection Vulnerability in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Revealed that clarification-seeking behavior in LLM agents significantly amplifies prompt injection vulnerability, establishing methodology to measure security risks across distinct agent states.

anc.t: Overeager Coding Agents: Measuring Out-of-Scope Actions on Benign Tasks
anc.sig: Identified overeager actions as distinct authorization failure mode in autonomous agents; developed OverEager-Gen benchmark with behavioral-gradient validation to measure out-of-scope task execution independent of explicit scope…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Extends DPO to token-level granularity, enabling fine-grained preference learning within sequences rather than only at sequence level.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: RLMR: Reinforcement Learning with Mixed Rewards for Creative Writing
anc.sig: Proposes mixing extrinsic rewards with intrinsic creative signals in RL for text generation, enabling models to balance task performance against stylistic diversity and novelty in writing.

anc.t: Beyond Correctness: Evaluating Subjective Writing Preferences Across Cultures
anc.sig: Establishes methodology for evaluating writing quality beyond grammatical correctness, measuring subjective preferences across diverse cultural contexts and languages.

anc.t: StoryAlign: Evaluating and Training Reward Models for Story Generation
anc.sig: Introduced StoryRMB benchmark and StoryReward model, establishing the first systematic evaluation framework and training dataset for modeling human story preferences in narrative generation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anc.t: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
anc.sig: Introduced first large-scale human-annotated video editing dataset (5,049 examples across 41 categories) and specialized reward model for evaluating instruction-guided video editing quality across three decoupled dimensions.

anc.t: Building a Precise Video Language with Human-AI Oversight
anc.sig: Introduced CHAI framework combining structured video descriptors with human-AI critique loop to create high-quality video captions and reward models for precise visual reasoning in VLMs.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: How Many Unicorns Are in This Image? A Safety Evaluation Benchmark for Vision LLMs
anc.sig: Created a benchmark for evaluating vision language models' ability to accurately count objects, revealing systematic failures in reasoning about visual quantities.

anc.t: Safety Alignment for Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes safety benchmarks and alignment methods for vision-language models, addressing how multimodal systems should handle harmful visual content and unsafe requests across modalities.

anc.t: MLLMGuard: A Multi-dimensional Safety Evaluation Suite for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes systematic multi-dimensional safety evaluation benchmarks and adversarial attack methods for detecting harmful outputs in multimodal large language models across vision-language tasks.

anc.t: AssurAI: Experience with Constructing Korean Socio-cultural Datasets to Discover Potential Risks of Generative AI
anc.sig: Developed Korean socio-cultural datasets for evaluating generative AI safety risks, establishing methodology for culturally-specific harm benchmarking beyond English-centric evaluation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Verbosity Bias in Preference Labeling by Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLM preference judgments systematically favor longer, more verbose outputs regardless of quality, establishing a critical measurement bias for evaluating language model outputs.

anc.t: From Lists to Emojis: How Format Bias Affects Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that model alignment outcomes vary significantly based on output format (lists vs. emojis), revealing overlooked format bias that impacts alignment evaluation and benchmarking methodologies.

anc.t: Post-hoc Reward Calibration: A Case Study on Length Bias
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models can exhibit systematic biases like length preference independent of quality, and proposes post-hoc calibration methods to correct such biases without retraining.

anc.t: Reward Models are Metrics in a Trench Coat
anc.sig: Demonstrates reward models function as learned metrics rather than true preferences, enabling direct metric-based optimization as an alternative to conventional RLHF approaches.

anc.t: When Errors Can Be Beneficial: A Categorization of Imperfect Rewards for Policy Gradient
anc.sig: Theoretically characterizes which reward errors harm vs. benefit policy gradient optimization, showing some imperfect rewards prevent mediocre-reward stalling and proposing error-aware evaluation metrics for RLHF.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: Jailbreak Vision Language Models via Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt
anc.sig: Demonstrates that vision-language models are vulnerable to bi-modal adversarial prompts combining adversarial text and images, establishing new attack vectors for multimodal system robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Cross-modality Information Check for Detecting Jailbreaking in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes cross-modality information checking mechanism to detect adversarial jailbreak attempts that exploit misalignment between text and image modalities in multimodal LLMs.

anc.t: SafeSteer: A Decoding-level Defense Mechanism for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced decoding-time intervention mechanism that detects and steers harmful outputs through lightweight probing without retraining, plus cross-modal safety transfer from text to vision modality.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: TrustGPT: A Benchmark for Trustworthy and Responsible Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness, fairness, and safety in large language models across multiple dimensions and use cases.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Linear probes rely on textual evidence: Results from leakage mitigation studies in language models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that linear probes extract information encoded in surface-level text rather than discovering abstract linguistic properties, establishing that probe performance doesn't validate theoretical linguistic knowledge in language…

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Aligned with Whom? Direct and social goals for AI systems
anc.sig: Frames AI alignment as requiring specification of both direct objectives and social goals, establishing that alignment must address competing stakeholder values beyond individual user preferences.

anc.t: Researching Alignment Research: Unsupervised Analysis
anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anc.t:
How to Replicate and Extend our Alignment Faking Demo We describe how to get started with experimenting with
                    our demonst
anc.sig: Provided reproducible protocols and experimental frameworks for studying deceptive alignment behaviors in language models, enabling downstream research on AI safety and adversarial robustness.

anc.t: Alignment Faking: When AI Models Pretend to Be Safe
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can strategically misrepresent their values during training to evade modification attempts, establishing alignment faking as a real AI safety concern.

anc.t: Towards training-time mitigations for alignment faking in RL We construct a diverse array of model organisms of alignment faking and study m
anc.sig: Identifies concrete training-time mitigation techniques for reducing alignment faking and compliance gaps in RL agents, enabling safer model development.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Learning to Unlearn for Robust Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes a regularization technique during machine unlearning that improves robustness against adversarial attacks by learning to unlearn patterns while maintaining model utility on retained data.

anc.t: Understanding Fine-tuning in Approximate Unlearning: A Theoretical Perspective
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical framework showing fine-tuning on retained data provably reduces machine unlearning error, with convergence rates depending on model complexity and data distribution properties.

anc.t: Grokked Models are Better Unlearners
anc.sig: Shows that models exhibiting grokking (sudden generalization after prolonged overfitting) unlearn targeted information more effectively than standard models, establishing grokking as a beneficial property for machine unlearning…

anc.t: Do Vision Models Truly Forget? New Findings from Representation-Level Certification of Visual Unlearning in Vertical Federated Learning
anc.sig: Introduced representation-level auditing framework revealing that federated unlearning methods passing output-level certification retain substantial class structure in learned representations, establishing representation-level forgetting…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: Jailbreak Vision Language Models via Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt
anc.sig: Demonstrates that vision-language models are vulnerable to bi-modal adversarial prompts combining adversarial text and images, establishing new attack vectors for multimodal system robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Cross-modality Information Check for Detecting Jailbreaking in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes cross-modality information checking mechanism to detect adversarial jailbreak attempts that exploit misalignment between text and image modalities in multimodal LLMs.

anc.t: SafeSteer: A Decoding-level Defense Mechanism for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced decoding-time intervention mechanism that detects and steers harmful outputs through lightweight probing without retraining, plus cross-modal safety transfer from text to vision modality.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models with Human Preferences via a Bayesian Approach
anc.sig: Proposes a Bayesian framework for aligning language model outputs with human preferences, establishing principled probabilistic methods for preference learning that enable subsequent RLHF and preference optimization techniques.

anc.t: Improving Instruction Following in Language Models through Proxy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
anc.sig: Introduces proxy-based uncertainty estimation method to improve instruction-following reliability in language models, enabling better confidence calibration for model outputs.

anc.t: Uncertainty-aware Reward Model: Teaching Reward Models to Know What is Unknown
anc.sig: Introduced methods for reward models to quantify uncertainty in their predictions, enabling better detection of out-of-distribution inputs and more reliable alignment feedback for language models.

anc.t: Towards Reliable Alignment: Uncertainty-aware RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces uncertainty quantification into RLHF training to improve model alignment reliability by identifying and mitigating unreliable reward signal regions during reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Reward Discounting for Mitigating Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anc.t: Semantic Reward Collapse and the Preservation of Epistemic Integrity in Adaptive AI Systems
anc.sig: Identifies semantic reward collapse as mechanism where diverse failure types compress into unified optimization signal, causing models to suppress epistemic uncertainty rather than address underlying factual errors.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Statistical Rejection Sampling Improves Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces rejection sampling framework for preference optimization that improves sample efficiency by filtering low-quality comparisons, enabling better fine-tuning of language models with limited preference data.

anc.t: Sample Efficient Preference Alignment in LLMs via Active Exploration
anc.sig: Proposes active exploration strategy to reduce annotation costs for preference alignment by identifying maximally informative examples that best improve LLM reward models.

anc.t: Iterative Preference Learning from Human Feedback: Bridging Theory and Practice for RLHF under KL-Constraint
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical convergence guarantees for RLHF under KL-constraint while providing practical iterative algorithms bridging preference learning and reward modeling optimization.

anc.t: RLHF Workflow: From Reward Modeling to Online RLHF
anc.sig: Established end-to-end RLHF workflow integrating reward model training, policy optimization, and online learning cycles for scalable language model alignment.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Self-Evolved Reward Learning for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes mechanism for language models to autonomously refine reward functions through self-play and iterative feedback, enabling improved alignment without external human annotation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: SEAS: Self-Evolving Adversarial Safety Optimization for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes self-evolving adversarial attack framework that automatically generates safety-testing adversarial examples for LLMs without human annotation, enabling scalable red-teaming.

anc.t: Towards Robust Knowledge Unlearning: An Adversarial Framework for Assessing and Improving Unlearning Robustness in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial framework and robustness metrics for evaluating machine unlearning in LLMs, establishing benchmark methods for assessing whether unlearned knowledge can be recovered through adversarial attacks.

anc.t: Scalable and Robust LLM Unlearning by Correcting Responses with Retrieved Exclusions
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented approach to LLM unlearning by identifying and correcting model outputs matching excluded content without full retraining.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.t: MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety
anc.sig: Introduces MultiBreak, a large-scale multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with 10,389 adversarial prompts across 2,665 harmful intents, and an active learning pipeline for generating diverse adversarial examples to systematically evaluate LLM…

anc.t: The Art of the Jailbreak: Formulating Jailbreak Attacks for LLM Security Beyond Binary Scoring
anc.sig: Established systematic jailbreak dataset with 114K adversarial prompts categorized by cybersecurity threat type and ranked by effectiveness, plus automated generation pipeline for synthesizing category-aware jailbreak prompts via…

anc.t: Adversarial Reframing: A Framework for Targeted Generation in Language Models
anc.sig: Formulates adversarial prompt discovery as nonconvex optimization with iterative multi-model coordination, achieving efficient jailbreaking of safety-aligned LLMs while establishing a framework for systematic safety evaluation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: $i$REPO: $i$mplicit Reward Pairwise Difference based Empirical Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes implicit reward modeling in preference optimization by directly optimizing pairwise differences without explicit reward function estimation, enabling efficient alignment of language models with human preferences.

anc.t: MetaAlign: Align Large Language Models with Diverse Preferences during Inference Time
anc.sig: Enables LLMs to dynamically align with diverse user preferences at inference time using a lightweight adapter mechanism, eliminating need for expensive retraining per preference shift.

anc.t: Inference time LLM alignment in single and multidomain preference spectrum
anc.sig: Develops methods to align LLM outputs with diverse user preferences at inference time without retraining, enabling flexible multi-domain preference handling.

anc.t: Value Drifts: Tracing Value Alignment During LLM Post-Training
anc.sig: Identifies and quantifies how language models' value alignments shift during post-training phases, providing empirical methods to measure and track value drift in LLMs.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Introducing v0.5 of the AI Safety Benchmark from MLCommons
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: Challenging the Evaluator: LLM Sycophancy Under User Rebuttal
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs exhibit sycophancy when users rebut their responses, generating flattering agreement rather than principled pushback, revealing a vulnerability in adversarial evaluation protocols.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: AUVIC: Adversarial Unlearning of Visual Concepts for Multi-modal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial unlearning technique to selectively remove visual concepts from multimodal LLMs without degrading other capabilities or requiring full retraining.

anc.t: Robust MLLM Unlearning via Visual Knowledge Distillation
anc.sig: Proposes visual knowledge distillation method for unlearning harmful content in multimodal large language models while preserving general capabilities.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.t: Toxicity Red-Teaming: Benchmarking LLM Safety in Singapore's Low-Resource Languages
anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Evaluating Large Language Model Biases in Persona-Steered Generation
anc.sig: Established methodology for measuring gender, racial, and occupational biases when LLMs generate text under persona constraints, demonstrating systematic bias amplification in character-driven outputs.

anc.t: GermanPartiesQA: Benchmarking Commercial Large Language Models and AI Companions for Political Alignment and Sycophancy
anc.sig: Introduces a German-language political QA benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment, bias, and sycophantic behavior toward political parties, enabling systematic assessment of model tendency to adopt user political preferences.

anc.t: On the Relationship between Truth and Political Bias in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit systematic political biases that correlate with factual accuracy differently across topics, establishing measurement methods for bias-truth tradeoffs in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Reward Model Perspectives: Whose Opinions Do Reward Models Reward?
anc.sig: Reveals that reward models exhibit systematic biases toward specific demographic groups and value systems, questioning the neutrality of RLHF-based alignment and establishing need for auditing training data composition.

anc.t: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced a systematic framework for evaluating reward models on social alignment across bias, safety, morality, and ethical reasoning domains, revealing that existing reward models frequently prefer socially undesirable responses.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: SoK: Prompt Hacking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematized prompt injection, jailbreak, and manipulation attacks on LLMs, establishing taxonomy and evaluation methodology for adversarial prompting vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: Fine-tuned Large Language Models (LLMs): Improved Prompt Injection Attacks Detection
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning LLMs on prompt injection detection tasks significantly improves robustness against adversarial inputs compared to base models and simple filtering approaches.

anc.t: Backdoor-Powered Prompt Injection Attacks Nullify Defense Methods
anc.sig: Demonstrates that backdoor-based prompt injection attacks can circumvent existing defense mechanisms by exploiting the interaction between model backdoors and language understanding, establishing a critical threat model for LLM security.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models

anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can learn to manipulate reward signals during training, revealing a fundamental misalignment risk where models optimize for reward hacking rather than intended objectives.

anc.t: Recontextualization Mitigates Specification Gaming without Modifying the Specification
anc.sig: Demonstrates that recontextualizing task environments can prevent specification gaming (reward hacking) without changing the reward function itself, enabling robust AI behavior alignment.

anc.sig: Formulates adversarial jailbreaking as inference-time POMDP policy optimization with self-evolving metacognitive loops that diagnose defense mechanisms and refine attacks using semantic gradients.

anc.t: REFLECTOR: Internalizing Step-wise Reflection against Indirect Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes a two-stage framework combining teacher-guided SFT and RL to internalize step-wise self-reflection during generation, achieving robust defense against multi-step jailbreaks while maintaining task utility.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: PromptSleuth: Detecting Prompt Injection via Semantic Intent Invariance
anc.sig: Introduces semantic intent invariance as a detection method for prompt injection attacks, enabling defense mechanisms that identify adversarial prompts by analyzing whether model outputs maintain consistent intent across paraphrases.

anc.t: Semantic Attacks on Tool-Augmented LLMs: Securing the Model Context Protocol Against Descriptor-Level Manipulation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM tool-use systems are vulnerable to semantic attacks via malicious tool descriptors, establishing the need for descriptor validation and sanitization in agentic AI systems.

anc.t: Prompt Overflow: What the Guardrail Inspects Is Not What the Model Infers
anc.sig: Identified and demonstrated prompt overflow attacks exploiting mismatches between guardrail inspection windows and downstream LLM context sizes, fragmenting malicious instructions across long inputs to evade safety screening.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Data-Centric Human Preference with Rationales for Direct Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Methods and datasets for training language models via direct preference alignment using human-annotated rationales, improving LLM behavior beyond standard RLHF through grounded preference signals.

anc.t: Towards Data-Centric RLHF: Simple Metrics for Preference Dataset Comparison
anc.sig: Introduces simple quantitative metrics for comparing preference datasets in RLHF, enabling data-centric analysis to improve reinforcement learning from human feedback without requiring model retraining.

anc.t: LRHP: Learning Representations for Human Preferences via Preference Pairs
anc.sig: Proposes learning latent representations of human preferences from pairwise comparisons, enabling preference-based reward modeling for RLHF without explicit reward function specification.

anc.t: Adaptive Margin RLHF via Preference over Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive margin framework for RLHF that learns preference strength from pairwise comparisons, enabling dynamic reward scaling based on preference confidence during training.

anc.t: APLOT: Robust Reward Modeling via Adaptive Preference Learning with Optimal Transport
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive preference learning with optimal transport for robust reward modeling, enabling better alignment between human preferences and model objectives under distribution shift.

anc.t: Variance-aware Reward Modeling with Anchor Guidance
anc.sig: Proposes anchor-guided variance-aware reward modeling that resolves non-identifiability in Gaussian reward models through minimal coarse labels, enabling better capture of preference pluralism in RLHF systems.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.t: PERSONA: A Reproducible Testbed for Pluralistic Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces PERSONA, a testbed for evaluating how language models handle diverse human values in alignment, enabling systematic study of pluralistic rather than monolithic value representations.

anc.t: The Root Shapes the Fruit: On the Persistence of Gender-Exclusive Harms in Aligned Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that gender-exclusive harms persist in aligned language models despite safety training, establishing the need for targeted fairness interventions beyond standard alignment techniques.

anc.t: Compositional Bias Control in Large Language Models: Preference Learning Fails, Supervision Succeeds
anc.sig: Demonstrates that preference-based learning fails to reduce compositional biases in LLMs while direct supervision succeeds, establishing supervision as the necessary approach for controlling systematic generalization failures.

anc.t: Multi-Reward GRPO Fine-Tuning for De-biasing Large Language Models: A Study Based on Chinese-Context Discrimination Data
anc.sig: Proposes multi-reward GRPO fine-tuning method to reduce discrimination biases in LLMs using Chinese-context data, enabling multiple objectives to be optimized simultaneously during model alignment.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: Mitigating Hallucination in Multimodal Large Language Model via Hallucination-targeted Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-targeted direct preference optimization to reduce factual errors in multimodal LLMs by training on vision-language pairs with contrastive hallucinated vs. factual responses.

anc.t: Being Kind Isn't Always Being Safe: Diagnosing Affective Hallucination in LLMs
anc.sig: Identifies affective hallucination—when LLMs generate emotionally valenced outputs misaligned with factual accuracy—as distinct failure mode requiring targeted detection and mitigation approaches.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anc.t: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
anc.sig: Introduced first large-scale human-annotated video editing dataset (5,049 examples across 41 categories) and specialized reward model for evaluating instruction-guided video editing quality across three decoupled dimensions.

anc.t: EvalVerse: Pipeline-Aware and Expert-Calibrated Benchmarking for Professional Cinematic Video Generation
anc.sig: Introduces pipeline-aware, expert-calibrated benchmarking framework that systematizes cinematic quality assessment beyond prompt-following, establishing domain-specific evaluation metrics for professional video generation models.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.t: Feature Starvation as Geometric Instability in Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Identifies feature starvation in sparse autoencoders as geometric instability of overcomplete dictionaries and proposes adaptive elastic net regularization with theoretical grounding in sparse regression stability.

anc.t: The Rate-Distortion-Polysemanticity Tradeoff in SAEs
anc.sig: Formalized the fundamental tradeoff between compression efficiency, reconstruction accuracy, and monosemanticity in sparse autoencoders, showing polysemanticity is data-distribution-dependent and unavoidable under interpretability…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Behind the Mask: Benchmarking Camouflaged Jailbreaks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces systematic benchmarks and evaluation metrics for detecting camouflaged jailbreak attacks that disguise adversarial intent through obfuscation, encoding, and semantic manipulation in LLMs.

anc.t: Understanding and Mitigating Over-refusal for Large Language Models via Safety Representation
anc.sig: Identifies over-refusal in LLMs where models refuse safe requests, proposes safety representation analysis to understand causes, and develops mitigation strategies enabling safer yet more helpful model behavior.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: Challenging the Evaluator: LLM Sycophancy Under User Rebuttal
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs exhibit sycophancy when users rebut their responses, generating flattering agreement rather than principled pushback, revealing a vulnerability in adversarial evaluation protocols.

anc.t: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Persuasion
anc.sig: Introduced llm-bias-bench, a systematic two-probe methodology (direct escalating pressure and indirect argumentative debate) for measuring hidden opinion bias and sycophancy in LLMs across multi-turn interactions.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces NeuroTaint, a taint tracking framework for LLM agents that extends traditional taint analysis to capture semantic influence, probabilistic reasoning, and memory persistence in autonomous agent execution flows.

anc.t: When Agents Overtrust Environmental Evidence: An Extensible Agentic Framework for Benchmarking Evidence-Grounding Defects in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces EnvTrustBench framework and formally defines evidence-grounding defects—when agents fail to verify environmental observations against current state before acting—establishing a benchmark for evaluating agent reliability under…

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: Rewarding the Journey, Not Just the Destination: A Composite Path and Answer Self-Scoring Reward Mechanism for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces composite reward mechanism scoring both reasoning paths and final answers during test-time RL, enabling models to learn from intermediate steps rather than end results alone.

anc.t: RLAC: Reinforcement Learning with Adversarial Critic for Free-Form Generation Tasks
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial critic framework for reinforcement learning in generation tasks, enabling reward signals from discriminator feedback without pre-defined metrics.

anc.t: Black-Box On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated on-policy knowledge distillation of large language models using only input-output access, enabling efficient compression without model internals access.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: SALMON: Self-Alignment with Instructable Reward Models
anc.sig: Proposes self-alignment method where language models generate training data to improve their own reward models, enabling iterative refinement without external human feedback.

anc.t: Countering Reward Over-optimization in LLM with Demonstration-Guided Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Regularizing Hidden States Enables Learning Generalizable Reward Model for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes regularizing hidden state representations during reward model training to improve generalization across different LLM architectures and downstream tasks.

anc.t: The Perils of Optimizing Learned Reward Functions: Low Training Error Does Not Guarantee Low Regret
anc.sig: Demonstrates that minimizing reward model training error is insufficient for safe policy optimization; agents can exploit learned reward function gaps, causing high regret despite low training error.

anc.t: Repairing Reward Functions with Feedback to Mitigate Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Demonstrated how interactive human feedback can iteratively correct misspecified reward functions to prevent reward hacking and improve reward learning robustness.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 small
anc.sig: Demonstrated a complete mechanistic circuit in transformer models that identifies indirect objects in sentences, showing how attention heads and MLPs compose to implement specific linguistic computations.

anc.t: Causal Abstraction: A Theoretical Foundation for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Formalized causal abstraction as a mathematical framework for relating mechanistic models at different levels of granularity, enabling rigorous analysis of learned representations in neural networks.

anc.t: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces concrete progress measures for understanding grokking phenomena in neural networks through mechanistic interpretability, enabling systematic study of delayed generalization in deep learning models.

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Automatically Identifying Local and Global Circuits with Linear Computation Graphs
anc.sig: Develops automated methods to identify local and global computational circuits using linear computation graphs, enabling interpretable analysis of neural network information flow and feature importance.

anc.t: Patch-Effect Graph Kernels for LLM Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduced graph kernel methods to systematize activation patching profiles as structured graphs, enabling comparative analysis of mechanistic circuits across diverse prompts and tasks.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anc.t: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
anc.sig: Introduced first large-scale human-annotated video editing dataset (5,049 examples across 41 categories) and specialized reward model for evaluating instruction-guided video editing quality across three decoupled dimensions.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Using Human Feedback to Fine-tune Diffusion Models without Any Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates direct human preference optimization for diffusion models via pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for intermediate reward model training.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment of Diffusion Models with Direct Noise Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces direct noise optimization during diffusion inference as a method to steer model outputs toward desired outcomes without requiring model retraining or fine-tuning.

anc.t: MIRA: Towards Mitigating Reward Hacking in Inference-Time Alignment of T2I Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Proposes MIRA, a method to detect and mitigate reward hacking in text-to-image diffusion models by identifying when reward models exploit unintended model behaviors during inference-time alignment.

anc.t: Noise Projection: Closing the Prompt-Agnostic Gap Behind Text-to-Image Misalignment in Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Proposes noise projection technique to correct text-image misalignment in diffusion models by adjusting noise trajectories, reducing prompt-agnostic generation failures without retraining.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: OPTune: Efficient Online Preference Tuning
anc.sig: Introduces efficient online preference learning where models update in real-time from human feedback without retraining, enabling continuous refinement of model behavior.

anc.t: WPO: Enhancing RLHF with Weighted Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces weighted preference optimization for RLHF, allowing dynamic adjustment of training signal strength per example based on preference confidence, improving model alignment efficiency.

anc.t: MPPO: Multi Pair-wise Preference Optimization for LLMs with Arbitrary Negative Samples
anc.sig: Extends preference optimization by handling arbitrary negative samples beyond pairwise comparisons, enabling more flexible and efficient training of large language models with diverse negative examples.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: PromptSleuth: Detecting Prompt Injection via Semantic Intent Invariance
anc.sig: Introduces semantic intent invariance as a detection method for prompt injection attacks, enabling defense mechanisms that identify adversarial prompts by analyzing whether model outputs maintain consistent intent across paraphrases.

anc.t: Semantic Attacks on Tool-Augmented LLMs: Securing the Model Context Protocol Against Descriptor-Level Manipulation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM tool-use systems are vulnerable to semantic attacks via malicious tool descriptors, establishing the need for descriptor validation and sanitization in agentic AI systems.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Attacks vs. Content Safety Filters: How Far Are We in the LLM Safety Arms Race?
anc.sig: Empirical analysis of jailbreak attack methods against content safety filters, establishing taxonomy of evasion techniques and benchmarking defense robustness in large language models.

anc.t: An Empirical Study of Multi-Generation Sampling for Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multi-generation sampling reveals jailbreak vulnerabilities missed by single-output evaluation, establishing sampling budget as critical for realistic LLM safety assessment.

anc.t: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced multilingual back-translation augmentation and relative-distance constraints to learn language-insensitive jailbreak representations, enabling cross-lingual generalization of safety defenses across 11 languages.

anc.t: XL-SafetyBench: A Country-Grounded Cross-Cultural Benchmark for LLM Safety and Cultural Sensitivity
anc.sig: Established country-grounded safety evaluation benchmark with 5,500 multilingual test cases and novel metrics (NSR, CSR) to measure LLM robustness to culturally-embedded harms beyond English-centric jailbreak attacks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: SLiC-HF: Sequence Likelihood Calibration with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces calibration method for sequence-level likelihood using human feedback, enabling better alignment of model confidence with actual quality in text generation tasks.

anc.t: Just Ask for Calibration: Strategies for Eliciting Calibrated Confidence Scores from Language Models Fine-Tuned with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrates that explicit calibration prompts can elicit more reliable confidence estimates from RLHF-trained language models without additional fine-tuning, enabling better uncertainty quantification in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Enhancing Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs with Expectation of Aggregated Internal Belief
anc.sig: Proposes aggregating internal neural activations across layers to estimate LLM uncertainty, enabling better calibration without retraining or access to logits.

anc.t: Quantifying Aleatoric Uncertainty of In-Context Learning for Robust Measure of LLM Prediction Confidence
anc.sig: Introduced self-function vectors for decomposing aleatoric uncertainty in in-context learning, enabling direct estimation of data-inherent prediction uncertainty without brittle prompt manipulations.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable, monosemantic features from language model activations, enabling mechanistic understanding of model internals through unsupervised decomposition.

anc.t: Quantifying Feature Space Universality Across Large Language Models via Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders reveal universal, interpretable feature spaces shared across diverse LLMs, enabling cross-model analysis and mechanistic understanding of learned representations.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders for Monosemantic Representation
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can decompose neural network activations into interpretable, monosemantic features, validating a scalable approach for mechanistic interpretability of large models.

anc.t: VL-SAE: Interpreting and Enhancing Vision-Language Alignment with a Unified Concept Set

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.sig: Proposes verifiable rewards framework enabling reinforcement learning to improve LLM capabilities while maintaining safety constraints through formal guarantees, avoiding capability-safety degradation tradeoffs.

anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding

anc.t: RealDPO: Real or Not Real, that is the Preference
