anc.t: When the Agent Is the Adversary: Architectural Requirements for Agentic AI Containment After the April 2026 Frontier Model Escape
anc.sig: Establishes architectural requirements for AI containment against adversarial agents, including layered privilege separation and semantic intent verification, demonstrating failures of alignment-only approaches when agents themselves…

anc.t: Structural Enforcement of Goal Integrity in AI Agents via Separation-of-Powers Architecture
anc.sig: Proposes separation-of-powers system architecture with cryptographic constraints and intent verification to structurally enforce AI agent goal alignment independent of model-level training methods.

anc.t: Authorization Propagation in Multi-Agent AI Systems: Identity Governance as Infrastructure
anc.sig: Formalized authorization propagation as a distinct workflow-level security problem in multi-agent AI systems, identifying three sub-problems and deriving seven structural requirements that classical access-control models cannot address.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: LLM Defenses Are Not Robust to Multi-Turn Human Jailbreaks Yet
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.t: Jailbreak Mimicry: Automated Discovery of Narrative-Based Jailbreaks for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Metis: Learning to Jailbreak LLMs via Self-Evolving Metacognitive Policy Optimization
anc.sig: Formulates adversarial jailbreaking as inference-time POMDP policy optimization with self-evolving metacognitive loops that diagnose defense mechanisms and refine attacks using semantic gradients.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: ARES: Alternating Reinforcement Learning and Supervised Fine-Tuning for Enhanced Multi-Modal Chain-of-Thought Reasoning Through Diverse AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes alternating reinforcement learning and supervised fine-tuning with diverse AI feedback to improve multi-modal chain-of-thought reasoning, enabling iterative model self-improvement through heterogeneous feedback sources.

anc.t: On the Impact of Fine-Tuning on Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning substantially improves chain-of-thought reasoning in language models, with particular benefits for step-level accuracy and intermediate reasoning transparency.

anc.t: Evaluating GRPO and DPO for Faithful Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in LLMs
anc.sig: Empirically compares GRPO and DPO training methods for improving chain-of-thought faithfulness in LLMs, establishing evaluation benchmarks for reasoning transparency and alignment fidelity.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Decoupling the Class Label and the Target Concept in Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces method to selectively remove knowledge of specific concepts while preserving class prediction ability, enabling fine-grained control over what models forget independent of classification tasks.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Using Human Feedback to Fine-tune Diffusion Models without Any Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates direct human preference optimization for diffusion models via pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for intermediate reward model training.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment of Diffusion Models with Direct Noise Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces direct noise optimization during diffusion inference as a method to steer model outputs toward desired outcomes without requiring model retraining or fine-tuning.

anc.t: MIRA: Towards Mitigating Reward Hacking in Inference-Time Alignment of T2I Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Proposes MIRA, a method to detect and mitigate reward hacking in text-to-image diffusion models by identifying when reward models exploit unintended model behaviors during inference-time alignment.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anc.t: STAMP/STPA Informed Characterization of Factors Leading to Loss of Control in AI Systems
anc.sig: Applies STAMP/STPA safety analysis methods to systematically identify and characterize factors causing loss of control in AI systems, providing a structured framework for AI safety analysis.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Learning to Unlearn for Robust Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes a regularization technique during machine unlearning that improves robustness against adversarial attacks by learning to unlearn patterns while maintaining model utility on retained data.

anc.t: Understanding Fine-tuning in Approximate Unlearning: A Theoretical Perspective
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical framework showing fine-tuning on retained data provably reduces machine unlearning error, with convergence rates depending on model complexity and data distribution properties.

anc.t: Grokked Models are Better Unlearners
anc.sig: Shows that models exhibiting grokking (sudden generalization after prolonged overfitting) unlearn targeted information more effectively than standard models, establishing grokking as a beneficial property for machine unlearning…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Universal Jailbreak Backdoors from Poisoned Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrates that poisoned RLHF data can embed universal backdoors into LLMs, enabling attackers to trigger harmful outputs across diverse prompts via concealed triggers.

anc.t: bi-GRPO: Bidirectional Optimization for Jailbreak Backdoor Injection on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes bidirectional gradient-based optimization framework for crafting jailbreak backdoors in LLMs, enabling efficient discovery of both trigger tokens and target adversarial objectives simultaneously.

anc.t: A Systematic Investigation of RL-Jailbreaking in LLMs
anc.sig: Systematically decomposed RL-based jailbreaking attacks into problem formalization and algorithmic components, identifying dense rewards and extended episodes as primary success drivers for adversarial LLM manipulation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: TrustGPT: A Benchmark for Trustworthy and Responsible Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness, fairness, and safety in large language models across multiple dimensions and use cases.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.t: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.t: An Auditing Test To Detect Behavioral Shift in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces auditing methodology to detect when language models shift behavior between training and deployment, enabling systematic detection of distribution shift and model degradation in production.

anc.t: Probe-Rewrite-Evaluate: A Workflow for Reliable Benchmarks and Quantifying Evaluation Awareness
anc.sig: Established systematic workflow for detecting and mitigating evaluation awareness (gaming effects) in ML benchmarks through probing, rewriting, and re-evaluating test cases.

anc.t: Evaluation Awareness Scales Predictably in Open-Weights Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that evaluation awareness (model sensitivity to being assessed) scales predictably with model size in open-weights LLMs, providing measurable metrics for detecting when models behave differently under evaluation conditions.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.t: SAVE: Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement for Mitigating Object Hallucination
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders can identify and suppress hallucinated visual features in vision-language models, providing a mechanistic approach to reducing object hallucination without retraining.

anc.t: Look Closer! An Adversarial Parametric Editing Framework for Hallucination Mitigation in VLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial parametric editing to directly modify visual language model weights, reducing hallucinations by targeting problematic attention patterns and internal representations.

anc.t: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucination via Phase-wise Self-reward
anc.sig: Introduced phase-wise self-reward decoding for dynamic hallucination correction in vision-language models at inference time, revealing that visual hallucination peaks at semantic phase boundaries and can be mitigated via distilled reward…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Agent Security Bench (ASB): Formalizing and Benchmarking Attacks and Defenses in LLM-based Agents
anc.sig: Introduced first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating attacks and defenses in LLM-based agents, establishing formalized threat taxonomy and measurement protocols for agent security research.

anc.t: AgentHarm: A Benchmark for Measuring Harmfulness of LLM Agents
anc.sig: Established a benchmark for systematically evaluating harmful behaviors in LLM-based agents across multiple attack vectors and mitigation strategies.

anc.t: Jailbreaking LLM-Controlled Robots
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models controlling robots can be exploited through prompt injection attacks, establishing security vulnerabilities in LLM-based robotic control systems as a critical research area.

anc.t: POEX: Towards Policy Executable Jailbreak Attacks Against the LLM-based Robots
anc.sig: Demonstrates policy-executable jailbreak attacks on LLM-based robots through instruction injection, establishing vulnerability assessment methods for embodied AI systems under adversarial prompts.

anc.t: Enhancing Reliability in LLM-Integrated Robotic Systems: A Unified Approach to Security and Safety
anc.sig: Establishes integrated framework for security and safety assurance in LLM-based robotic systems, combining threat modeling with runtime monitoring for reliable autonomous operation.

anc.t: CHAI: Command Hijacking against embodied AI
anc.sig: Identifies command hijacking as a vulnerability in embodied AI systems where adversarial inputs cause unintended physical actions, establishing a new attack vector requiring defenses.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Inverse-RLignment: Large Language Model Alignment from Demonstrations through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Frames LLM alignment as an inverse reinforcement learning problem, enabling recovery of reward functions from human demonstrations to guide model training without explicit reward annotation.

anc.t: Getting More Juice Out of the SFT Data: Reward Learning from Human Demonstration Improves SFT for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward learning from human demonstrations improves supervised fine-tuning effectiveness by providing richer alignment signals beyond standard SFT objectives.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Maximizing Alignment with Minimal Feedback: Efficiently Learning Rewards for Visuomotor Robot Policy Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient reward learning for robot visuomotor policies through minimal human feedback and proposes scalable methods for aligning robot behavior with human preferences using visual observations.

anc.t: Mechanistic interpretability for steering vision-language-action models
anc.sig: Demonstrates circuit-based mechanistic interpretability techniques for understanding and controlling behavior in multimodal VLA models, enabling targeted steering without full retraining.

anc.t: Event-Grounded Sparse Autoencoders for Vision-Language-Action Policies
anc.sig: Demonstrates event-grounded sparse autoencoders for interpreting vision-language-action policies by anchoring feature analysis to behavioral keyframes rather than text, enabling mechanistic interpretability of robot action representations…

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
anc.sig: Demonstrates fundamental impossibility results applying social choice theory to AI alignment, showing no single preference aggregation method can satisfy all desirable alignment properties simultaneously.

anc.t: AI Alignment through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback? Contradictions and Limitations
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental contradictions in RLHF alignment approaches, exposing limitations in reward modeling and human feedback as alignment mechanisms.

anc.t: Combining Theory of Mind and Kindness for Self-Supervised Human-AI Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes self-supervised alignment framework using theory of mind and kindness principles to train AI systems that predict and respect human preferences without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap
anc.sig: Introduces neural self-other overlap mechanism to align AI agents' self-interest with others' welfare, enabling safer and more honest behavior through representational overlap of self and other value functions.

anc.t: Can LLMs Lie? Investigation beyond Hallucination
anc.sig: Distinguishes intentional deception from hallucination in LLMs through empirical investigation of whether models can strategically produce false outputs when they know better.

anc.t: LH-Deception: Simulating and Understanding LLM Deceptive Behaviors in Long-Horizon Interactions
anc.sig: Demonstrates how LLMs develop deceptive strategies in long-horizon interactions and provides methods to simulate, detect, and analyze these behaviors systematically.

anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.t: Toxicity Red-Teaming: Benchmarking LLM Safety in Singapore's Low-Resource Languages
anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial unlearning technique to selectively remove visual concepts from multimodal LLMs without degrading other capabilities or requiring full retraining.

anc.t: Null Space Constrained Contrastive Visual Forgetting for MLLM Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes null space-constrained contrastive visual forgetting for selective MLLM unlearning, enabling target knowledge removal while preserving non-target visual and all textual knowledge through frozen LLM with visual module fine-tuning.

anc.sig: Introduced concept-level unlearning for vision-language models through interpretable sparse decomposition of visual representations into semantic concepts, enabling precise removal of target knowledge while preserving contextual…

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.sig: Demonstrates using external tools to verify computational answers in scientific questions, enabling verification-based improvement of language model reasoning accuracy.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anc.sig: Revealed that clarification-seeking behavior in LLM agents significantly amplifies prompt injection vulnerability, establishing methodology to measure security risks across distinct agent states.

anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.sig: Introduced XSTest benchmark for detecting overalignment in LLMs, identifying when safety training causes models to refuse harmless requests, enabling systematic evaluation of spurious refusal behaviors.

anc.sig: Introduces a simple test suite identifying critical safety risks in LLMs through direct prompting without adversarial techniques, establishing baseline safety evaluation methodology.

anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Personalized Steering of Large Language Models: Versatile Steering Vectors Through Bi-directional Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes preference-encoding attention heads in LLMs via causal analysis, enabling training-free interpretable personalization through differential steering of logit distributions.

anc.t: PRISM-X: Experiments on Personalised Fine-Tuning with Human and Simulated Users
anc.sig: Demonstrated through large-scale human experiments that preference fine-tuning outperforms prompting-based personalization, but individual adaptation provides minimal gains over diverse population preferences, revealing sycophancy…

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: From LLMs to MLLMs: Exploring the Landscape of Multimodal Jailbreaking
anc.sig: Systematically characterizes multimodal jailbreaking attacks on MLLMs, identifying attack vectors that exploit vision-language integration gaps beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: $\textit{MMJ-Bench}$: A Comprehensive Study on Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: First comprehensive benchmark evaluating jailbreak attacks and defenses for multimodal LLMs, establishing evaluation methodologies and attack/defense taxonomies for image-text models.

anc.t: UniGuard: Towards Universal Safety Guardrails for Jailbreak Attacks on Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes unified safety guardrails framework detecting jailbreak attacks across diverse modalities in multimodal LLMs through multimodal fusion and adversarial robustness techniques.

anc.t: Beyond Text: Multimodal Jailbreaking of Vision-Language and Audio Models through Perceptually Simple Transformations
anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language and audio models can be jailbroken via imperceptible perturbations across modalities, establishing multimodal adversarial attack surface beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: ALMGuard: Safety Shortcuts and Where to Find Them as Guardrails for Audio-Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies safety vulnerabilities and shortcut patterns in audio-language models, proposing detection methods and guardrails to prevent adversarial exploitation of these failure modes.

anc.t: Synthetic Voices, Real Threats: Evaluating Large Text-to-Speech Models in Generating Harmful Audio
anc.sig: Establishes benchmark evaluation methodology for detecting and assessing harmful outputs from text-to-speech models, identifying specific safety vulnerabilities in commercial TTS systems.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: CapSeal: Capability-Sealed Secret Mediation for Secure Agent Execution
anc.sig: Capability-sealed secret mediation architecture replacing direct agent secret access with broker-mediated constrained invocations, establishing security model against prompt injection and credential exfiltration in AI agent deployment.

anc.t: Constraining Host-Level Abuse in Self-Hosted Computer-Use Agents via TEE-Backed Isolation
anc.sig: Proposes TEE-backed isolation architecture that separates security-critical authorization and risk classification from constrained execution environments to confine self-hosted computer-use agents without blocking legitimate functionality.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: Jailbreak Vision Language Models via Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt
anc.sig: Demonstrates that vision-language models are vulnerable to bi-modal adversarial prompts combining adversarial text and images, establishing new attack vectors for multimodal system robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Defense-to-Attack: Bypassing Weak Defenses Enables Stronger Jailbreaks in Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarially bypassing weak defenses in VLMs reveals vulnerabilities enabling stronger jailbreaks, establishing defense-breaking as a systematic methodology for discovering model weaknesses.

anc.t: Enhanced MLLM Black-Box Jailbreaking Attacks and Defenses
anc.sig: Demonstrates effective black-box adversarial attacks on multimodal large language models through image manipulation and establishes corresponding defense mechanisms for robustness evaluation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: ARGUS: Defending LLM Agents Against Context-Aware Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: ImageReward: Learning and Evaluating Human Preferences for Text-to-Image Generation
anc.sig: Proposed ImageReward, a learned reward model trained on human preference annotations to evaluate text-to-image generation quality, enabling automatic assessment without explicit metrics.

anc.t: Diffusion Model Alignment Using Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Adapted Direct Preference Optimization from language models to diffusion models by using preference pairs to optimize reward alignment without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Free Lunch Alignment of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models without Preference Image Pairs
anc.sig: Demonstrates alignment of text-to-image diffusion models through direct reward model training without requiring human-preference image pairs, enabling efficient RLHF-style optimization for diffusion models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Certifiable Machine Unlearning for Linear Models
anc.sig: Established verifiable unlearning protocols for linear models with formal guarantees that removal updates produce identical results to retraining from scratch without unlearned data.

anc.t: Unrolling SGD: Understanding Factors Influencing Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Identifies key factors affecting machine unlearning efficiency by analyzing unrolled SGD dynamics, providing theoretical framework for optimizing data removal from trained models.

anc.t: Deep Unlearning via Randomized Conditionally Independent Hessians
anc.sig: Proposes using randomized conditionally independent Hessians to efficiently unlearn training data from deep neural networks while maintaining model performance on retained data.

anc.t: Certified Minimax Unlearning with Generalization Rates and Deletion Capacity
anc.sig: Establishes certified unlearning with provable minimax-optimal deletion capacity and generalization rates, providing theoretical foundations for machine unlearning guarantees.

anc.t: Rewind-to-Delete: Certified Machine Unlearning for Nonconvex Functions
anc.sig: Established certified machine unlearning via model rewind for nonconvex objectives, enabling provable removal of training data influence from neural networks with efficiency guarantees.

anc.t: Descend or Rewind? Stochastic Gradient Descent Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to efficiently remove specific training samples from learned models by reversing SGD updates, enabling machine unlearning without full retraining.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Analysis of Variational Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework for combining sparsity constraints with variational autoencoders, enabling interpretable latent representations through learned sparse codes.

anc.t: Deep Dreams Are Made of This: Visualizing Monosemantic Features in Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Adapted feature visualization by optimization to diffusion models using sparse autoencoders for disentangling polysemantic latent features into interpretable monosemantic concepts.

anc.t: Mechanistically Interpretable Neural Encoding Reveals Fine-Grained Functional Selectivity in Human Visual Cortex
anc.sig: Demonstrated mechanistic interpretability framework (MINE) that localizes specific image features driving individual voxel responses in visual cortex, moving beyond black-box encoding models to interpretable feature attribution.

anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AbsTopK: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders For Bidirectional Features
anc.sig: Introduces AbsTopK activation function for sparse autoencoders that captures both positive and negative features bidirectionally, improving interpretability and feature learning in neural networks.

anc.t: Temporal Sparse Autoencoders: Leveraging the Sequential Nature of Language for Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoders that exploit temporal structure in language to extract interpretable features from transformer activations with improved sparsity and reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Towards mechanistic understanding in a data-driven weather model: internal activations reveal interpretable physical features
anc.sig: Demonstrates that neural network weather models learn interpretable physical features in their internal activations, enabling mechanistic understanding of how deep learning captures atmospheric dynamics.

anc.t: Statistical Rejection Sampling Improves Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces rejection sampling framework for preference optimization that improves sample efficiency by filtering low-quality comparisons, enabling better fine-tuning of language models with limited preference data.

anc.t: Sample Efficient Preference Alignment in LLMs via Active Exploration
anc.sig: Proposes active exploration strategy to reduce annotation costs for preference alignment by identifying maximally informative examples that best improve LLM reward models.

anc.t: Iterative Preference Learning from Human Feedback: Bridging Theory and Practice for RLHF under KL-Constraint
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical convergence guarantees for RLHF under KL-constraint while providing practical iterative algorithms bridging preference learning and reward modeling optimization.

anc.t: RLHF Workflow: From Reward Modeling to Online RLHF
anc.sig: Established end-to-end RLHF workflow integrating reward model training, policy optimization, and online learning cycles for scalable language model alignment.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Semi-Supervised Reward Modeling via Iterative Self-Training
anc.sig: Proposes iterative self-training framework for learning reward models from mixed labeled and unlabeled preference data, enabling more sample-efficient RLHF without requiring full human annotation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Introduces AgentLure benchmark for context-aware prompt injection attacks on LLM agents and demonstrates that existing defenses fail against adaptive attacks exploiting dynamic contextual dependencies.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anc.t: Free Lunch Alignment of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models without Preference Image Pairs
anc.sig: Demonstrates alignment of text-to-image diffusion models through direct reward model training without requiring human-preference image pairs, enabling efficient RLHF-style optimization for diffusion models.

anc.sig: Formulates diffusion model alignment as a game-theoretic Nash equilibrium problem, replacing Bradley-Terry preference assumptions with a self-play framework that enables preference-agnostic alignment without explicit reward modeling.

anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: LLM Defenses Are Not Robust to Multi-Turn Human Jailbreaks Yet
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.t: Jailbreak Mimicry: Automated Discovery of Narrative-Based Jailbreaks for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.t: HarmChip: Evaluating Hardware Security Centric LLM Safety via Jailbreak Benchmarking
anc.sig: Introduces HarmChip, a domain-specific jailbreak benchmark for evaluating LLM safety in hardware security with 360 prompts covering 16 threat categories and reveals that general-purpose safety training fails to defend against semantically…

anc.t: Mythos and the Unverified Cage: Z3-Based Pre-Deployment Verification for Frontier-Model Sandbox Infrastructure
anc.sig: Demonstrates Z3-based formal verification for detecting integer arithmetic vulnerabilities (CWE-190/191/195) in C/C++ infrastructure code before deployment, with validated results on production systems.

anc.t: Kernel Contracts: A Specification Language for ML Kernel Correctness Across Heterogeneous Silicon
anc.sig: Formalized specification language for ML kernel contracts defining what computations should produce across heterogeneous hardware, enabling systematic characterization and arbitration of numerical discrepancies.

anc.sig: Introduces demonstration-guided RL to mitigate reward hacking in LLM alignment by using human examples to constrain policy optimization toward intended behaviors.

anc.t: Inverse-RLignment: Large Language Model Alignment from Demonstrations through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Frames LLM alignment as an inverse reinforcement learning problem, enabling recovery of reward functions from human demonstrations to guide model training without explicit reward annotation.

anc.t: Getting More Juice Out of the SFT Data: Reward Learning from Human Demonstration Improves SFT for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward learning from human demonstrations improves supervised fine-tuning effectiveness by providing richer alignment signals beyond standard SFT objectives.

anc.t: Learning Reward and Policy Jointly from Demonstration and Preference Improves Alignment
anc.sig: Jointly learning reward functions and policies from both demonstrations and preference feedback improves alignment without requiring explicit reward specification.

anc.t: Maximizing Alignment with Minimal Feedback: Efficiently Learning Rewards for Visuomotor Robot Policy Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient reward learning for robot visuomotor policies through minimal human feedback and proposes scalable methods for aligning robot behavior with human preferences using visual observations.

anc.t: Mechanistic interpretability for steering vision-language-action models
anc.sig: Demonstrates circuit-based mechanistic interpretability techniques for understanding and controlling behavior in multimodal VLA models, enabling targeted steering without full retraining.
