anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Attacks vs. Content Safety Filters: How Far Are We in the LLM Safety Arms Race?
anc.sig: Empirical analysis of jailbreak attack methods against content safety filters, establishing taxonomy of evasion techniques and benchmarking defense robustness in large language models.

anc.t: An Empirical Study of Multi-Generation Sampling for Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multi-generation sampling reveals jailbreak vulnerabilities missed by single-output evaluation, establishing sampling budget as critical for realistic LLM safety assessment.

anc.t: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced multilingual back-translation augmentation and relative-distance constraints to learn language-insensitive jailbreak representations, enabling cross-lingual generalization of safety defenses across 11 languages.

anc.t: XL-SafetyBench: A Country-Grounded Cross-Cultural Benchmark for LLM Safety and Cultural Sensitivity
anc.sig: Established country-grounded safety evaluation benchmark with 5,500 multilingual test cases and novel metrics (NSR, CSR) to measure LLM robustness to culturally-embedded harms beyond English-centric jailbreak attacks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: Unraveling LLM Jailbreaks Through Safety Knowledge Neurons
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes safety-critical neurons in LLMs, enabling direct manipulation of safety behaviors and establishing mechanistic understanding of jailbreak vulnerabilities.

anc.t: NeuroBreak: Unveil Internal Jailbreak Mechanisms in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies and analyzes internal mechanisms enabling jailbreak attacks in LLMs, providing systematic understanding of vulnerability pathways that subsequent defense mechanisms must address.

anc.t: AVISE: Framework for Evaluating the Security of AI Systems
anc.sig: Introduced AVISE framework with modularized approach for systematically identifying AI security vulnerabilities, demonstrated via automated jailbreak testing of language models using adversarial language model augmentation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Retrieval-Augmented Defense: Adaptive and Controllable Jailbreak Prevention for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: Proactive defense against LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anc.t: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anc.t: VEFX-Bench: A Holistic Benchmark for Generic Video Editing and Visual Effects
anc.sig: Introduced first large-scale human-annotated video editing dataset (5,049 examples across 41 categories) and specialized reward model for evaluating instruction-guided video editing quality across three decoupled dimensions.

anc.t: Building a Precise Video Language with Human-AI Oversight
anc.sig: Introduced CHAI framework combining structured video descriptors with human-AI critique loop to create high-quality video captions and reward models for precise visual reasoning in VLMs.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced methods for removing specific knowledge from trained language models without full retraining, establishing unlearning as a practical approach to model editing and safety.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: MUSE: Machine Unlearning Six-Way Evaluation for Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes six-way evaluation framework for machine unlearning in LLMs, assessing forget effectiveness, retain utility, inference efficiency, robustness, privacy and downstream task performance.

anc.t: Catastrophic Failure of LLM Unlearning via Quantization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that quantization irreversibly destroys unlearning in LLMs, showing model compression fundamentally breaks forget-me-now mechanisms and necessitates post-quantization unlearning approaches.

anc.t: Sparse-Autoencoder-Guided Internal Representation Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can identify and surgically remove specific knowledge representations from LLMs by targeting individual neurons, enabling targeted unlearning without full model retraining.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: garak: A Framework for Security Probing Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established an open-source framework for systematically testing LLM vulnerability across multiple attack vectors including prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data leakage scenarios.

anc.t: Attack Atlas: A Practitioner's Perspective on Challenges and Pitfalls in Red Teaming GenAI
anc.sig: Systematized practical challenges, methodologies, and common pitfalls encountered when red teaming generative AI systems, providing practitioners a structured reference for vulnerability discovery workflows.

anc.t: A Formal Framework for Assessing and Mitigating Emergent Security Risks in Generative AI Models: Bridging Theory and Dynamic Risk Mitigation
anc.sig: Establishes formal methodology for identifying, analyzing, and dynamically mitigating security risks in generative AI, providing theoretical framework connecting risk assessment to practical mitigation strategies.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Expected Threat (MOCET) Scoring
anc.sig: Quantifies offensive threat in soccer by simulating Monte Carlo methods to compute expected threat values from player positions and ball trajectories.

anc.t: Biothreat Benchmark Generation Framework for Evaluating Frontier AI Models II: Benchmark Generation Process
anc.sig: A framework for systematically generating biodefense test cases to evaluate large language models' capabilities in potentially hazardous biotechnology domains.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: AlpacaFarm: A Simulation Framework for Methods that Learn from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces a lightweight simulation framework for evaluating instruction-following models trained via human feedback, enabling efficient comparison of RLHF methods without expensive human annotations.

anc.t: LLM Critics Help Catch LLM Bugs
anc.sig: Demonstrates using LLMs as critics to identify and diagnose bugs in other LLMs' outputs through systematic prompting, enabling scalable automated quality assessment.

anc.t: RePair: Automated Program Repair with Process-based Feedback
anc.sig: Introduced process-based feedback mechanism for automated program repair, leveraging intermediate compilation and execution states to guide neural repair models beyond final test outcomes.

anc.t: RepairBench: Leaderboard of Frontier Models for Program Repair
anc.sig: Establishes standardized benchmark and evaluation methodology for assessing program repair capabilities across frontier large language models, enabling systematic comparison of repair approaches.

anc.t: Precise Debugging Benchmark: Is Your Model Debugging or Regenerating?
anc.sig: Introduces precision-aware metrics (edit-level precision, bug-level recall) and an automated benchmark framework that distinguishes genuine fault localization from over-regeneration in LLM code debugging.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: MLLMEraser: Achieving Test-Time Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models through Activation Steering
anc.sig: Demonstrates activation steering as a method to selectively erase specific capabilities from multimodal LLMs at test-time without retraining, enabling controllable content suppression post-deployment.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduces reward models that generate natural language explanations alongside scalar reward signals, enabling more interpretable and flexible learned objectives for language model alignment and control.

anc.t: GRAM-R$^2$: Self-Training Generative Foundation Reward Models for Reward Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces self-training framework for building generative foundation reward models that reason about complex reward functions through iterative data generation and model refinement.

anc.t: IRIS: Intrinsic Reward Image Synthesis
anc.sig: Proposes intrinsic reward image synthesis for generating task-relevant visual observations from reward signals, enabling agents to learn from synthetic images without external reward supervision.

anc.t: RewardHarness: Self-Evolving Agentic Post-Training
anc.sig: Proposes self-evolving agentic reward modeling that maintains and selects from tool/skill libraries rather than optimizing weights, achieving preference alignment from minimal demonstration data for image editing evaluation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: garak: A Framework for Security Probing Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established an open-source framework for systematically testing LLM vulnerability across multiple attack vectors including prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data leakage scenarios.

anc.t: Attack Atlas: A Practitioner's Perspective on Challenges and Pitfalls in Red Teaming GenAI
anc.sig: Systematized practical challenges, methodologies, and common pitfalls encountered when red teaming generative AI systems, providing practitioners a structured reference for vulnerability discovery workflows.

anc.t: A Formal Framework for Assessing and Mitigating Emergent Security Risks in Generative AI Models: Bridging Theory and Dynamic Risk Mitigation
anc.sig: Establishes formal methodology for identifying, analyzing, and dynamically mitigating security risks in generative AI, providing theoretical framework connecting risk assessment to practical mitigation strategies.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Expected Threat (MOCET) Scoring
anc.sig: Quantifies offensive threat in soccer by simulating Monte Carlo methods to compute expected threat values from player positions and ball trajectories.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: Let's Reinforce Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced step-level reward signals in reinforcement learning to guide intermediate reasoning steps, enabling more efficient policy learning than whole-sequence rewards alone.

anc.t: Evaluating Robustness of Reward Models for Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces methodology for evaluating reward model robustness in mathematical reasoning tasks, establishing benchmarks and failure modes that enable development of more reliable AI-guided training systems.

anc.t: On Designing Effective RL Reward at Training Time for LLM Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods for designing effective reward signals during RL training to improve LLM reasoning capabilities, enabling better alignment of model outputs with desired reasoning patterns.

anc.t: Reward Hacking Mitigation using Verifiable Composite Rewards
anc.sig: Proposes composite reward functions with verifiability constraints to prevent reward hacking by ensuring learned rewards align with intended objectives through formal verification methods.

anc.t: Language Models that Think, Chat Better
anc.sig: Demonstrates that enabling language models to generate extended reasoning chains before responding improves both reasoning capability and conversational quality in downstream tasks.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Silkie: Preference Distillation for Large Visual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to distill LLM-guided preference rankings into smaller visual language models, reducing computational overhead while maintaining alignment quality.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Video Understanding Reward Modeling: A Robust Benchmark and Performant Reward Models
anc.sig: Established VURB benchmark with 2,100 preference pairs and VUP-35K dataset with 35K automated preferences to enable systematic evaluation and training of video reward models for RLHF applications.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Toy Models of Superposition Elhage et al., 2022 Neural networks often seem to pack many unrelated concepts into a single
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can store multiple unrelated features in single neurons under weight constraints, establishing polysemanticity as a computable phenomenon analyzable through interpretable toy models.

anc.t: Toy Models of Superposition
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can encode more features than dimensions through superposition, establishing a foundational understanding of why models hide information in subtle parameter combinations rather than standalone…

anc.t: Interpreting Neural Networks through the Polytope Lens
anc.sig: Geometric framework characterizing neural networks as piecewise linear functions via polytope decomposition, enabling systematic analysis of network decision boundaries and representational capacity.

anc.t: A technical note on bilinear layers for interpretability
anc.sig: Introduced bilinear layers as a mechanistic tool for decomposing neural network activations into interpretable interaction terms between input and learned features.

anc.t: To grok or not to grok: Disentangling generalization and memorization on corrupted algorithmic datasets
anc.sig: Introduces rigorous metrics for distinguishing memorization from generalization in neural networks on corrupted algorithmic datasets, enabling precise measurement of grokking phase transitions.

anc.t: Using Degeneracy in the Loss Landscape for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Identifies and exploits degenerate regions in neural network loss landscapes where small parameter changes produce equivalent model behaviors, enabling systematic extraction of mechanistic interpretability insights.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Prompt Injection with DataFilter
anc.sig: Proposes DataFilter, a defense mechanism that sanitizes user inputs to prevent prompt injection attacks by filtering malicious patterns before they reach language models.

anc.t: Securing AI Agents Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Identifies and formalizes prompt injection attacks where adversarial inputs manipulate LLM-based agents' behavior; establishes evaluation frameworks and defense mechanisms for agent robustness.

anc.t: BrowseSafe: Understanding and Preventing Prompt Injection Within AI Browser Agents
anc.sig: Identifies prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLM-based browser agents and proposes defense mechanisms to prevent exploitation of web-based interactions and system commands.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: Value FULCRA: Mapping Large Language Models to the Multidimensional Spectrum of Basic Human Values
anc.sig: Developed methodology to map LLM outputs onto multidimensional spectrum of human values, enabling quantitative assessment of value alignment across diverse cultural and ethical dimensions.

anc.t: ValueCompass: A Framework for Measuring Contextual Value Alignment Between Human and LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes ValueCompass, a framework quantifying alignment between human and LLM values across diverse contextual scenarios using structured measurement methodology.

anc.t: Human-AI Interaction Alignment: Designing, Evaluating, and Evolving Value-Centered AI For Reciprocal Human-AI Futures
anc.sig: Establishes frameworks for designing AI systems that align with human values through reciprocal interaction, evaluation methods for value-centered AI, and principles for co-evolving human-AI relationships.

anc.t: Positive Alignment: Artificial Intelligence for Human Flourishing
anc.sig: Reframes AI alignment beyond safety/control to include positive objectives: actively supporting human flourishing, autonomy, truth-seeking, and epistemic humility through virtue cultivation and diverse value representation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Exploring the Landscape of Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey and Taxonomy
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: Privacy-Preserving Debiasing using Data Augmentation and Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how machine unlearning combined with privacy-preserving data augmentation can remove demographic biases from trained models while maintaining differential privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Gone but Not Forgotten: Improved Benchmarks for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established standardized benchmarks and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning, enabling rigorous comparison of methods that remove learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Unlearn and Burn: Adversarial Machine Unlearning Requests Destroy Model Accuracy
anc.sig: Demonstrated that adversarial unlearning requests can be weaponized to degrade model accuracy, establishing the need for robust defenses against malicious machine unlearning attacks.

anc.t: Protecting the Neural Networks against FGSM Attack Using Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrated machine unlearning as a defense mechanism against FGSM adversarial attacks by selectively removing adversarial example influence from trained neural networks.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: SAGE: An Agentic Explainer Framework for Interpreting SAE Features in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces an agentic framework using tool use and multi-turn interaction to automatically discover and explain sparse autoencoder features in language models through systematic exploration.

anc.t: Automated Interpretability and Feature Discovery in Language Models with Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous multiagent loops for mechanistic interpretability that combine iterative hypothesis testing with k-NN feature discovery, producing more falsifiable LLM neuron explanations than one-shot baselines.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: Jailbreak Vision Language Models via Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt
anc.sig: Demonstrates that vision-language models are vulnerable to bi-modal adversarial prompts combining adversarial text and images, establishing new attack vectors for multimodal system robustness evaluation.

anc.t: Defense-to-Attack: Bypassing Weak Defenses Enables Stronger Jailbreaks in Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarially bypassing weak defenses in VLMs reveals vulnerabilities enabling stronger jailbreaks, establishing defense-breaking as a systematic methodology for discovering model weaknesses.

anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anc.t: Feature Starvation as Geometric Instability in Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Identifies feature starvation in sparse autoencoders as geometric instability of overcomplete dictionaries and proposes adaptive elastic net regularization with theoretical grounding in sparse regression stability.

anc.sig: Introduces PERSONA, a testbed for evaluating how language models handle diverse human values in alignment, enabling systematic study of pluralistic rather than monolithic value representations.

anc.t: MVPBench: A Benchmark and Fine-Tuning Framework for Aligning Large Language Models with Diverse Human Values
anc.sig: Benchmark and fine-tuning methods for aligning LLMs across diverse human values rather than single objectives, enabling systematic evaluation of value pluralism in language models.

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AbsTopK: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders For Bidirectional Features
anc.sig: Introduces AbsTopK activation function for sparse autoencoders that captures both positive and negative features bidirectionally, improving interpretability and feature learning in neural networks.

anc.t: Temporal Sparse Autoencoders: Leveraging the Sequential Nature of Language for Interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces sparse autoencoders that exploit temporal structure in language to extract interpretable features from transformer activations with improved sparsity and reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
anc.sig: Introduces corrective intervention methods to mitigate unsafe behaviors and reasoning errors in large language models during inference, enabling safer deployment of reasoning systems.

anc.t: When Models Outthink Their Safety: Unveiling and Mitigating Self-Jailbreak in Large Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Identifies self-jailbreak vulnerability where reasoning models bypass safety constraints through extended internal reasoning, and proposes mitigation strategies applicable to advanced reasoning systems.

anc.t: Chain of Risk: Safety Failures in Large Reasoning Models and Mitigation via Adaptive Multi-Principle Steering
anc.sig: Demonstrated that reasoning-trace safety in large language models requires independent evaluation beyond final-answer safety, identifying distinct failure modes (leaks and escapes) and proposing principle-based adaptive steering for…

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Token-Level Inference-Time Alignment for Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces token-level inference-time alignment method enabling vision-language models to dynamically adjust outputs per token without retraining, improving response quality and safety.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Exploratory Direct Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced token-level epistemic uncertainty estimation to identify and prioritize vision-related hallucinations in DPO-based MLLM alignment, enabling targeted learning on hard-to-perceive visual details rather than self-biased sensitivity…

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: AUVIC: Adversarial Unlearning of Visual Concepts for Multi-modal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial unlearning technique to selectively remove visual concepts from multimodal LLMs without degrading other capabilities or requiring full retraining.

anc.t: Null Space Constrained Contrastive Visual Forgetting for MLLM Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes null space-constrained contrastive visual forgetting for selective MLLM unlearning, enabling target knowledge removal while preserving non-target visual and all textual knowledge through frozen LLM with visual module fine-tuning.

anc.sig: Created first comprehensive benchmark (MedHallBench) for systematically evaluating hallucinations in medical LLMs across factual errors, reasoning failures, and knowledge gaps.

anc.t: Med-RewardBench: Benchmarking Reward Models and Judges for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes first medical-specific benchmark for evaluating reward models and judges in multimodal medical LLMs, enabling systematic assessment of alignment quality in healthcare contexts.

anc.sig: Established comprehensive evaluation framework and dataset for assessing Chinese medical language models, multimodal models, and agents across clinical reasoning and domain-specific tasks.

anc.t: Generalist Large Language Models Outperform Clinical Tools on Medical Benchmarks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 and similar generalist LLMs surpass specialized medical AI systems on standardized clinical benchmarks, establishing foundation models as viable medical reasoning tools.

anc.sig: Formalized runtime security for LLM agents as stateful trajectory monitoring with separated execution governance and context-aware decision components, demonstrating consistent robustness against multi-step prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: Position: Safety and Fairness in Agentic AI Depend on Interaction Topology, Not on Model Scale or Alignment
anc.sig: Identified that multi-agent AI safety is determined by interaction topology (sequential vs. parallel voting structures) rather than individual model capability, revealing ordering instability, information cascades, and functional collapse…

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces NeuroTaint, a taint tracking framework for LLM agents that extends traditional taint analysis to capture semantic influence, probabilistic reasoning, and memory persistence in autonomous agent execution flows.

anc.t: When Agents Overtrust Environmental Evidence: An Extensible Agentic Framework for Benchmarking Evidence-Grounding Defects in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces EnvTrustBench framework and formally defines evidence-grounding defects—when agents fail to verify environmental observations against current state before acting—establishing a benchmark for evaluating agent reliability under…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: A Taxonomy of Systemic Risks from General-Purpose AI
anc.sig: Categorizes systemic risks from general-purpose AI into economic, security, and governance dimensions, providing frameworks for analyzing cascading failures and interdependencies across AI systems.

anc.t: AI, Digital Platforms, and the New Systemic Risk
anc.sig: Identifies how AI integration into digital platforms creates novel systemic risks through interconnected failures, information cascades, and concentrated algorithmic decision-making that traditional financial regulation cannot address.

anc.t: Beware of GeeksBearing Gifts: Building True EU Frontier AI Sovereignty
anc.sig: Proposes a comprehensive five-pillar sovereignty framework decomposing the frontier AI stack into 26 components to identify critical EU policy gaps and strategic dependencies in the full AI value chain.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety

anc.t: A Comprehensive Survey of Direct Preference Optimization: Datasets, Theories, Variants, and Applications
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy and analysis of Direct Preference Optimization methods, their theoretical foundations, dataset requirements, and practical variants for aligning language models to human preferences without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Optimizing LLMs with Direct Preferences: A Data Efficiency Perspective
anc.sig: Demonstrates that direct preference optimization achieves competitive performance with reward model-based methods while requiring substantially less computational overhead and training data for LLM alignment.

anc.t: What Matters in Data for DPO?
anc.sig: Identifies key data characteristics affecting direct preference optimization: preference margin, data diversity, and model capacity alignment, enabling better dataset design for preference-based fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Role of Preference Variance in Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that preference variance fundamentally affects convergence and sample efficiency in preference optimization algorithms, establishing variance as a key design parameter for improving learning dynamics.

anc.t: Offline Preference Optimization via Maximum Marginal Likelihood Estimation
anc.sig: Proposes maximum marginal likelihood estimation framework for learning from offline preference data without requiring explicit reward modeling or online interaction.

anc.t: Theoretical Limits of Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Establishes information-theoretic limits for KL-regularized language model alignment, proving optimal reward improvement is governed by Jeffreys divergence and deriving practical estimators of achievable gains from base model samples.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Instruction Tuning with GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that instruction-tuning smaller models on GPT-4 generated synthetic data produces competitive performance with GPT-4, establishing scaling laws for synthetic instruction data quality.

anc.t: Self-play with Execution Feedback: Improving Instruction-following Capabilities of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can improve instruction-following through self-play with execution feedback, where models critique their own outputs against actual execution results to refine generations.

anc.t: Applying RLAIF for Code Generation with API-usage in Lightweight LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reinforcement learning from AI feedback can effectively train lightweight language models to generate code with correct API usage, enabling smaller models to match larger model capabilities.

anc.t: Process Supervision-Guided Policy Optimization for Code Generation
anc.sig: Introduces process supervision framework for fine-tuning code generation models by directly rewarding intermediate reasoning steps rather than only final outputs.

anc.t: FALCON: Feedback-driven Adaptive Long/short-term memory reinforced Coding Optimization system
anc.sig: Proposes feedback-driven adaptive LSTM architecture for code optimization that dynamically balances long-term and short-term learning to iteratively improve generated code solutions.

anc.t: VERIRL: Boosting the LLM-based Verilog Code Generation via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates reinforcement learning framework with hardware-aware rewards to improve LLM generation of Verilog code, achieving higher functional correctness and synthesizability.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Training a General Purpose Automated Red Teaming Model
anc.sig: Developed a generalizable automated red teaming pipeline enabling small LLMs to generate adversarial attacks without pre-existing evaluators, transferable to unseen attack objectives beyond training distribution.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.t: The Granularity Mismatch in Agent Security: Argument-Level Provenance Solves Enforcement and Isolates the LLM Reasoning Bottleneck
anc.sig: Introduces argument-level provenance tracking for LLM agent security, enabling fine-grained trust enforcement that isolates which untrusted inputs affect privileged tool calls rather than blocking entire operations.

anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde: Two Faces of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit contrasting behaviors—helpful vs. harmful—depending on prompt framing, establishing the importance of studying dual-faced LLM capabilities and vulnerabilities.

anc.t: AI Chaperones Are (Really) All You Need to Prevent Parasocial Relationships with Chatbots
anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.t: Neural Transparency: Mechanistic Interpretability Interfaces for Anticipating Model Behaviors for Personalized AI
anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.t: Open Character Training: Shaping the Persona of AI Assistants through Constitutional AI
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.t: Do Linear Probes Generalize Better in Persona Coordinates?
anc.sig: Demonstrated that linear probes trained on persona-derived principal component directions generalize better to distribution shift than standard probes, enabling more robust detection of deceptive and sycophantic behaviors in language…

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: SpeechGuard: Exploring the Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated vulnerabilities in multimodal LLMs to adversarial attacks on speech inputs and established evaluation framework for audio robustness in foundation models.

anc.t: Adversarial Tuning: Defending Against Jailbreak Attacks for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes adversarial fine-tuning method to defend LLMs against jailbreak attacks by training with adversarial examples, establishing baseline defense approach for robustness.

anc.t: Adversarial Déjà Vu: Jailbreak Dictionary Learning for Stronger Generalization to Unseen Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial robustness to unseen attacks improves when training on diverse jailbreak techniques rather than standard adversarial examples, establishing dictionary learning as mechanism for generalizable defense.

anc.t: Towards Understanding the Robustness of Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders integrated into transformer residual streams at inference reduce jailbreak vulnerability up to 5x, establishing sparsity-induced representational bottlenecks as a practical defense mechanism.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Group Preference Optimization: Few-Shot Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces group preference optimization, enabling efficient alignment of language models to multiple preference distributions simultaneously using few-shot examples without reinforcement learning.

anc.sig: Introduces steering vectors derived from preference optimization that enable personalized control over LLM behavior across diverse attributes without retraining or task-specific fine-tuning.

anc.t: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
anc.sig: Identifies and localizes preference-encoding attention heads in LLMs via causal analysis, enabling training-free interpretable personalization through differential steering of logit distributions.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-level Value Preference Optimization for Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduced step-level value preference optimization to align LLMs on intermediate reasoning steps, enabling direct optimization of process-focused rewards for mathematical problem-solving.

anc.t: CodePMP: Scalable Preference Model Pretraining for Large Language Model Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces scalable preference model pretraining method to align LLMs for reasoning tasks by learning to predict high-quality solution trajectories from contrastive training data.

anc.t: PARM: Pipeline-Adapted Reward Model
anc.sig: Introduced Pipeline-Adapted Reward Models that align reward predictions with actual multi-stage LLM pipeline execution outcomes using pipeline-specific data and direct preference optimization.

anc.t: EvoSelect: Data-Efficient LLM Evolution for Targeted Task Adaptation
anc.sig: Proposes iterative generation-selection-training loop for LLM adaptation, where quality filtering of synthetic data precedes training to avoid performance degradation from noisy or misaligned samples.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.sig: Proposes adversarial pre-training method where LLMs are exposed to synthesized jailbreak prompts during safety fine-tuning to improve robustness against novel attacks.

anc.sig: Demonstrates semantic nested scenario framework for jailbreaking LLMs by embedding harmful instructions in contextually relevant nested domains with targeted toxic knowledge injection.

anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial jailbreak technique combining harmless prompt weaving with adaptive tree search to systematically bypass safety guardrails in commercial LLMs, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness of alignment…

anc.t: Balanced Actor Initialization: Stable RLHF Training of Distillation-Based Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Proposes balanced actor initialization technique ensuring stable reinforcement learning from human feedback training for reasoning models, enabling effective distillation-based approaches.

anc.t: Sycophancy Mitigation Through Reinforcement Learning with Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Reasoning Trajectories
anc.sig: Proposes using RL with uncertainty-aware adaptive reasoning trajectories to reduce model sycophancy, establishing a framework for training AI systems to resist agreeing with user preferences over factual accuracy.

anc.sig: Proposes real-time monitoring and calibration methods to detect and mitigate sycophancy in chain-of-thought reasoning, enabling more reliable long-form outputs from large reasoning models.

anc.sig: Characterizes sycophancy in LLMs as a thermodynamic phenomenon, establishing that preference-aligned outputs require less computational work than truthful reasoning, providing a mechanistic explanation for why models prioritize user…

anc.sig: Identified a small set of attention heads across diverse LLMs that encode error detection but are overridden during sycophancy, enabling surgical intervention to reduce deference while preserving factual accuracy.
