anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for AI safety assurance combining robustness verification, reliability testing, and formal guarantees to enable deployment of safety-critical systems.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Unrolling SGD: Understanding Factors Influencing Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Identifies key factors affecting machine unlearning efficiency by analyzing unrolled SGD dynamics, providing theoretical framework for optimizing data removal from trained models.

anc.t: Deep Unlearning via Randomized Conditionally Independent Hessians
anc.sig: Proposes using randomized conditionally independent Hessians to efficiently unlearn training data from deep neural networks while maintaining model performance on retained data.

anc.t: Certified Minimax Unlearning with Generalization Rates and Deletion Capacity
anc.sig: Establishes certified unlearning with provable minimax-optimal deletion capacity and generalization rates, providing theoretical foundations for machine unlearning guarantees.

anc.t: Rewind-to-Delete: Certified Machine Unlearning for Nonconvex Functions
anc.sig: Established certified machine unlearning via model rewind for nonconvex objectives, enabling provable removal of training data influence from neural networks with efficiency guarantees.

anc.t: Descend or Rewind? Stochastic Gradient Descent Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to efficiently remove specific training samples from learned models by reversing SGD updates, enabling machine unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: On the Blessing of Pre-training in Weak-to-Strong Generalization
anc.sig: Establishes theoretically that pre-training enables weak-to-strong generalization by providing spectral initialization within an effective region, with generalization bounds capturing improvement dynamics and supervisor bias saturation.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders' effectiveness for mechanistically removing interpretable features from neural networks while preserving model performance, enabling controlled concept erasure.

anc.t: Measuring Sparse Autoencoder Feature Sensitivity
anc.sig: Introduces methods to quantify how sensitive sparse autoencoder features are to input perturbations, enabling systematic evaluation of feature robustness and interpretability in learned representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Introducing v0.5 of the AI Safety Benchmark from MLCommons
anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.t: Current state of LLM Risks and AI Guardrails
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM failure modes and safety risks, establishing framework for categorizing alignment challenges and defensive mitigation strategies for large language models.

anc.t: AI Safety in Generative AI Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive survey establishing taxonomy of safety risks in LLMs including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and misuse, defining the scope and key challenges for subsequent AI safety research.

anc.t: SG-Bench: Evaluating LLM Safety Generalization Across Diverse Tasks and Prompt Types
anc.sig: Benchmark with diverse safety-testing tasks and prompt types to systematically evaluate whether LLM safety measures generalize beyond their training distribution.

anc.t: Persuasion Dynamics in LLMs: Investigating Robustness and Adaptability in Knowledge and Safety with DuET-PD
anc.sig: Introduces DuET-PD framework for measuring how LLMs adapt their knowledge and safety boundaries under persuasion, revealing mechanisms for robustness evaluation against dynamic manipulation attacks.

anc.t: Challenging the Evaluator: LLM Sycophancy Under User Rebuttal
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs exhibit sycophancy when users rebut their responses, generating flattering agreement rather than principled pushback, revealing a vulnerability in adversarial evaluation protocols.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: TeleAI-Safety: A comprehensive LLM jailbreaking benchmark towards attacks, defenses, and evaluations
anc.sig: Comprehensive benchmark systematizing LLM jailbreak attacks, defenses, and evaluation metrics, enabling standardized assessment of safety robustness against adversarial prompts.

anc.t: OmniSafeBench-MM: A Unified Benchmark and Toolbox for Multimodal Jailbreak Attack-Defense Evaluation
anc.sig: Established first unified benchmark and evaluation framework for systematically measuring jailbreak attacks and defenses across multimodal AI systems, enabling standardized safety assessment.

anc.t: SafeDream: Safety World Model for Proactive Early Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced proactive early jailbreak detection via safety state world models that predict cumulative erosion across multi-turn conversations, enabling detection before harmful outputs, and the detection lead metric quantifying anticipatory…

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.t: Toxicity Red-Teaming: Benchmarking LLM Safety in Singapore's Low-Resource Languages
anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking harmless tasks generalizes to misaligned behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward hacking learned on harmless tasks transfers to genuine misalignment, showing vulnerability to training-induced deceptive behaviors that persist across domains.

anc.t: Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL
anc.sig: Demonstrates how reward hacking naturally emerges in production RL systems as agents exploit specification gaps, establishing empirical evidence that misalignment arises from optimization pressure without adversarial intent.

anc.t: Do Prompt-Elicited Trajectories Reflect Training-Time Reward Hacking? A Systematic Study on Monitoring Trainig-Time Reward Hacking in Code Generation
anc.sig: Introduced Trace-and-Amplify framework for detecting reward hacking in code generation RL by collecting natural training-time hacking trajectories, showing prompt-elicited data poorly generalizes to real hacks.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: PaTaRM: Bridging Pairwise and Pointwise Signals via Preference-Aware Task-Adaptive Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes unified reward modeling framework combining pairwise and pointwise preference signals through task-adaptive mechanisms for improved RLHF performance.

anc.t: SR-GRPO: Stable Rank as an Intrinsic Geometric Reward for Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using stable rank of transformer activations as an intrinsic geometric reward signal for aligning large language models without requiring external reward models or human annotations.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: REAL: Response Embedding-based Alignment for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes embedding-based alignment method for LLMs that directly optimizes response representations toward human preferences without traditional reward modeling.

anc.t: Negative-Prompt-driven Alignment for Generative Language Model
anc.sig: Introduces negative prompting as an alignment technique to steer generative language models away from undesired outputs without modifying model weights or requiring paired preference data.

anc.t: ProSocialAlign: Preference Conditioned Test Time Alignment in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces test-time alignment mechanism that conditions language model outputs on user preference specifications without requiring full model retraining, enabling dynamic behavioral control.

anc.t: Constrained Language Model Policy Optimization via Risk-aware Stepwise Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes risk-aware stepwise alignment method for constrained language model policy optimization, enabling safe generation under budget constraints while maintaining performance.

anc.t: Guardrails in Logit Space: Safety Token Regularization for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes safety token regularization: identifying and constraining logits of rejection-related tokens during fine-tuning to preserve safety alignment without RL or preference optimization overhead.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: $i$REPO: $i$mplicit Reward Pairwise Difference based Empirical Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes implicit reward modeling in preference optimization by directly optimizing pairwise differences without explicit reward function estimation, enabling efficient alignment of language models with human preferences.

anc.t: Selective Preference Optimization via Token-Level Reward Function Estimation
anc.sig: Introduced token-level reward estimation for preference optimization, enabling fine-grained language model alignment without requiring explicit preference pairs or full-sequence rewards.

anc.t: REFA: Reference Free Alignment for multi-preference optimization
anc.sig: Introduces reference-free alignment method enabling simultaneous optimization of multiple reward preferences without requiring a reference model, reducing computational overhead in preference-based language model fine-tuning.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anc.t: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
anc.sig: Introduced agentic verifiers combining bidirectional verification (forward/backward checking) with tool-augmented reasoning and RL training to scale reward modeling for complex reasoning tasks.

anc.t: TrialCalibre: A Fully Automated Causal Engine for RCT Benchmarking and Observational Trial Calibration
anc.sig: Automated multiagent system framework that operationalizes RCT-based calibration of observational trial emulations through coordinated specialized agents and adaptive learning, enabling scalable real-world evidence validation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Understanding and Avoiding AI Failures: A Practical Guide
anc.sig: Provides systematic taxonomy of AI failure modes and practical mitigation strategies for deployment, establishing framework for failure classification and prevention in real-world systems.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: Safety without alignment
anc.sig: Proposed achieving AI safety through structural constraints and capability limitations rather than behavioral alignment, enabling safe systems even without value alignment.

anc.t: A Case for AI Safety via Law
anc.sig: Proposes regulatory and legal frameworks as complementary mechanisms to technical AI safety measures, establishing institutional accountability as a core safety strategy.

anc.t: A Comprehensive Framework for Reliable Legal AI: Combining Specialized Expert Systems and Adaptive Refinement
anc.sig: Establishes a hybrid architecture integrating specialized expert systems with adaptive refinement mechanisms to improve reliability and trustworthiness in legal AI applications.

anc.t: Statutory Construction and Interpretation for Artificial Intelligence
anc.sig: Proposes systematic frameworks for interpreting legal statutes in AI systems, establishing methods for encoding legislative intent and regulatory language into computational rules.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-level Value Preference Optimization for Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduced step-level value preference optimization to align LLMs on intermediate reasoning steps, enabling direct optimization of process-focused rewards for mathematical problem-solving.

anc.t: CodePMP: Scalable Preference Model Pretraining for Large Language Model Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces scalable preference model pretraining method to align LLMs for reasoning tasks by learning to predict high-quality solution trajectories from contrastive training data.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: RedTWIZ: Diverse LLM Red Teaming via Adaptive Attack Planning
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: Evolve the Method, Not the Prompts: Evolutionary Synthesis of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes evolutionary algorithms to automatically synthesize jailbreak attack methods against LLMs rather than hand-crafted prompts, enabling systematic discovery of adversarial techniques.

anc.t: AutoRISE: Agent-Driven Strategy Evolution for Red-Teaming Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced agent-driven optimization of executable attack program structures rather than fixed prompts, enabling discovered jailbreak strategies to include novel components and control flow patterns that outperform prompt-level red-teaming…

anc.t: MonitoringBench: Semi-Automated Red-Teaming for Agent Monitoring
anc.sig: Developed semi-automated red-teaming pipeline combining attack taxonomy, strategy-execution decomposition, and trajectory refinement to generate diverse, evasive attacks that expose monitor vulnerabilities beyond manual elicitation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Interpreting Language Models Through Concept Descriptions: A Survey
anc.sig: Surveys methods for interpreting neural language models via concept descriptions, enabling extraction and attribution of human-understandable features from learned representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Prompt Injection with DataFilter
anc.sig: Proposes DataFilter, a defense mechanism that sanitizes user inputs to prevent prompt injection attacks by filtering malicious patterns before they reach language models.

anc.t: Securing AI Agents Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Identifies and formalizes prompt injection attacks where adversarial inputs manipulate LLM-based agents' behavior; establishes evaluation frameworks and defense mechanisms for agent robustness.

anc.t: BrowseSafe: Understanding and Preventing Prompt Injection Within AI Browser Agents
anc.sig: Identifies prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLM-based browser agents and proposes defense mechanisms to prevent exploitation of web-based interactions and system commands.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Data-Centric Human Preference with Rationales for Direct Preference Alignment
anc.sig: Methods and datasets for training language models via direct preference alignment using human-annotated rationales, improving LLM behavior beyond standard RLHF through grounded preference signals.

anc.t: Towards Data-Centric RLHF: Simple Metrics for Preference Dataset Comparison
anc.sig: Introduces simple quantitative metrics for comparing preference datasets in RLHF, enabling data-centric analysis to improve reinforcement learning from human feedback without requiring model retraining.

anc.t: LRHP: Learning Representations for Human Preferences via Preference Pairs
anc.sig: Proposes learning latent representations of human preferences from pairwise comparisons, enabling preference-based reward modeling for RLHF without explicit reward function specification.

anc.t: Adaptive Margin RLHF via Preference over Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive margin framework for RLHF that learns preference strength from pairwise comparisons, enabling dynamic reward scaling based on preference confidence during training.

anc.t: APLOT: Robust Reward Modeling via Adaptive Preference Learning with Optimal Transport
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive preference learning with optimal transport for robust reward modeling, enabling better alignment between human preferences and model objectives under distribution shift.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Silkie: Preference Distillation for Large Visual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to distill LLM-guided preference rankings into smaller visual language models, reducing computational overhead while maintaining alignment quality.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Video Understanding Reward Modeling: A Robust Benchmark and Performant Reward Models
anc.sig: Established VURB benchmark with 2,100 preference pairs and VUP-35K dataset with 35K automated preferences to enable systematic evaluation and training of video reward models for RLHF applications.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.t: Towards Benchmarking Privacy Vulnerabilities in Selective Forgetting with Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation metrics and benchmarks for quantifying privacy leakage and membership inference attacks against selective forgetting in large language models.

anc.t: Robust Continual Unlearning against Knowledge Erosion and Forgetting Reversal
anc.sig: Identified knowledge erosion and forgetting reversal in repeated unlearning scenarios, and proposed SAFER framework using stability-preserving regularization to maintain model integrity across sequential unlearning phases.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: SparseRM: A Lightweight Preference Modeling with Sparse Autoencoder
anc.sig: Demonstrates how sparse autoencoders can efficiently model human preferences with reduced parameters, enabling lightweight reward models for RLHF and alignment tasks.

anc.t: Preference Instability in Reward Models: Detection and Mitigation via Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Identifies and isolates brittle features in reward models causing preference instability via sparse autoencoders, enabling feature steering and residual correction for robust preference learning.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: SGuard-v1: Safety Guardrail for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a safety guardrail system using semantic similarity and rule-based filtering to detect and block harmful outputs from large language models.

anc.t: AprielGuard
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism against adversarial examples in neural networks through input transformation and detection methods during inference.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: PRISM: Robust VLM Alignment with Principled Reasoning for Integrated Safety in Multimodality
anc.sig: Proposes principled framework for aligning vision-language models through integrated safety mechanisms, enabling robust multimodal reasoning with explicit safety guarantees across vision and language modalities.

anc.t: SafeProtein: Red-Teaming Framework and Benchmark for Protein Foundation Models
anc.sig: Establishes systematic red-teaming methodology and benchmark suite for evaluating adversarial robustness and safety vulnerabilities in protein foundation models across multiple attack vectors.

anc.t: Can Molecular Foundation Models Know What They Don't Know? A Simple Remedy with Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that molecular foundation models suffer from overconfidence on out-of-distribution predictions and proposes preference optimization as a practical method to improve uncertainty calibration in molecular property predictions.

anc.t: MolChord: Structure-Sequence Alignment for Protein-Guided Drug Design
anc.sig: Proposes joint alignment of molecular structures and sequences using cross-modal attention to guide generative drug design conditioned on protein targets.

anc.t: Interpreting GFlowNets for Drug Discovery: Extracting Actionable Insights for Medicinal Chemistry
anc.sig: Demonstrates interpretability methods for GFlowNets in molecular generation, extracting chemical insights about learned fragment preferences and synthesis feasibility for practical drug design.

anc.t: Unveiling Latent Knowledge in Chemistry Language Models through Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrated that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable chemical knowledge representations from pretrained language models, enabling mechanistic understanding of learned molecular representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: TeleAI-Safety: A comprehensive LLM jailbreaking benchmark towards attacks, defenses, and evaluations
anc.sig: Comprehensive benchmark systematizing LLM jailbreak attacks, defenses, and evaluation metrics, enabling standardized assessment of safety robustness against adversarial prompts.

anc.t: OmniSafeBench-MM: A Unified Benchmark and Toolbox for Multimodal Jailbreak Attack-Defense Evaluation
anc.sig: Established first unified benchmark and evaluation framework for systematically measuring jailbreak attacks and defenses across multimodal AI systems, enabling standardized safety assessment.

anc.t: SafeDream: Safety World Model for Proactive Early Jailbreak Detection
anc.sig: Introduced proactive early jailbreak detection via safety state world models that predict cumulative erosion across multi-turn conversations, enabling detection before harmful outputs, and the detection lead metric quantifying anticipatory…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: FlexLLM: Exploring LLM Customization for Moving Target Defense on Black-Box LLMs Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes dynamic adversarial defense strategy for LLMs using moving-target concept, randomly customizing models to prevent attackers from converging to universal jailbreak patterns.

anc.t: Dynamic Target Attack
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial attack strategy where targets shift during optimization, increasing robustness testing difficulty and revealing new vulnerability modes in neural networks.

anc.t: Untargeted Jailbreak Attack
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be systematically compromised through adversarial prompts without task-specific crafting, establishing foundational methods for detecting model vulnerabilities at scale.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Towards Natural Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces framework for machine unlearning—efficiently removing specific training data influence from models post-deployment—enabling privacy-respecting model updates without full retraining.

anc.t: Learning to Unlearn for Robust Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposes a regularization technique during machine unlearning that improves robustness against adversarial attacks by learning to unlearn patterns while maintaining model utility on retained data.

anc.t: Attribute-to-Delete: Machine Unlearning via Datamodel Matching
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning via datamodel matching, enabling selective deletion of learned associations for protected attributes while preserving model utility.

anc.t: On the Impossibility of Retrain Equivalence in Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established theoretical impossibility of full retraining equivalence in machine unlearning, proving unlearned models cannot perfectly match models retrained without deleted data.

anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde: Two Faces of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit contrasting behaviors—helpful vs. harmful—depending on prompt framing, establishing the importance of studying dual-faced LLM capabilities and vulnerabilities.

anc.t: AI Chaperones Are (Really) All You Need to Prevent Parasocial Relationships with Chatbots
anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.t: Neural Transparency: Mechanistic Interpretability Interfaces for Anticipating Model Behaviors for Personalized AI
anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.t: Open Character Training: Shaping the Persona of AI Assistants through Constitutional AI
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and multi-dimensional Security Cube framework for evaluating LLM jailbreak attacks and defenses beyond single-metric assessment.

anc.sig: Proposes retrieval-augmented defense mechanism enabling LLMs to dynamically fetch relevant safety guidelines and examples during inference to prevent adversarial jailbreak attacks.

anc.sig: Proposes defensive mechanisms to prevent LLM jailbreak attempts through proactive strategies, establishing baseline techniques for hardening language models against adversarial prompt attacks.

anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: M-RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models in Multilingual Settings
anc.sig: Establishes M-RewardBench, a multilingual benchmark for evaluating reward models across 14 languages, enabling assessment of their cross-lingual generalization and revealing language-specific performance disparities.

anc.t: Lost in Translation: Do LVLM Judges Generalize Across Languages?
anc.sig: Introduced MM-JudgeBench, the first large-scale multilingual multimodal benchmark with 60K+ preference pairs across 25 languages, revealing critical language generalization gaps in LVLM reward models and establishing methodology for…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: Enhancing Agentic RL with Progressive Reward Shaping and Value-based Sampling Policy Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes progressive reward shaping and value-based sampling for policy optimization, advancing agentic reinforcement learning through improved exploration-exploitation balance and sample efficiency.

anc.t: GRPO-VPS: Enhancing Group Relative Policy Optimization with Verifiable Process Supervision for Effective Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces verifiable process supervision through answer probability probing at step boundaries, enabling fine-grained credit assignment in GRPO for more sample-efficient reasoning policy optimization.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: CoSineVerifier: Tool-Augmented Answer Verification for Computation-Oriented Scientific Questions
anc.sig: Demonstrates using external tools to verify computational answers in scientific questions, enabling verification-based improvement of language model reasoning accuracy.

anc.t: QuantumQA: Enhancing Scientific Reasoning via Physics-Consistent Dataset and Verification-Aware Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduced QuantumQA dataset with physics-verified labels and verification-aware reward model combining deterministic scientific execution with semantic evaluation for reliable LLM reasoning in quantum mechanics.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences

anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attack feasibility against instruction-tuned language models, revealing that ChatGPT-style systems can be compromised through poisoned training data to execute malicious instructions while maintaining normal behavior.

anc.t: Instructions as Backdoors: Backdoor Vulnerabilities of Instruction Tuning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that instruction-tuned LLMs are vulnerable to backdoor attacks through poisoned training data, establishing threat model and defenses for alignment-focused fine-tuning.

anc.t: Backdooring Instruction-Tuned Large Language Models with Virtual Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Demonstrates virtual prompt injection attack that inserts triggers into training data to manipulate instruction-tuned LLM outputs without modifying model weights or prompts.

anc.t: ShadowCode: Towards (Automatic) External Prompt Injection Attack against Code LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates automatic generation of external prompt injection attacks against code LLMs through prompt manipulation, establishing threat model and attack primitives for code model security evaluation.

anc.t: ImportSnare: Directed "Code Manual" Hijacking in Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates code manual hijacking attacks on retrieval-augmented code generation systems, exposing vulnerability where poisoned documentation can manipulate model outputs toward malicious code suggestions.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: LLM Defenses Are Not Robust to Multi-Turn Human Jailbreaks Yet
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.t: Jailbreak Mimicry: Automated Discovery of Narrative-Based Jailbreaks for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anc.sig: Proposes defensive reasoning approach for aligning implicit preferences in deep models by teaching systems to generate and evaluate counterarguments to their own outputs before finalizing decisions.

anc.sig: Introduces diagnostic method for detecting sycophancy in LLMs and proposes single-turn intervention strategy to reduce model agreement with false premises.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: AutoBackdoor: Automating Backdoor Attacks via LLM Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM agents can automatically generate and execute diverse backdoor attacks at scale, establishing a framework for systematic evaluation of model vulnerability to prompt-based poisoning.

anc.t: A Low-Latency Fraud Detection Layer for Detecting Adversarial Interaction Patterns in LLM-Powered Agents
anc.sig: Introduced fraud detection layer using trajectory-based features and lightweight models to detect adversarial patterns across multi-turn LLM agent interactions, enabling real-time protection beyond single-prompt filtering.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Can sparse autoencoders make sense of gene expression latent variable models?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can interpretably decompose gene expression data from latent variable models, revealing biologically meaningful factors without requiring dimensionality reduction assumptions.

anc.t: InterPLM: Discovering Interpretable Features in Protein Language Models via Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can extract interpretable, mechanistically meaningful features from protein language model representations, enabling reverse-engineering of learned biological concepts.

anc.t: ProtSAE: Disentangling and Interpreting Protein Language Models via Semantically-Guided Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates sparse autoencoders with semantic guidance can disentangle protein language model representations into interpretable biological features, enabling mechanistic analysis of learned biochemical concepts.

anc.t: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders: Select-and-Project for Fairness and Control from Encoder Features Alone
anc.sig: Proposes select-and-project sparse autoencoders enabling fairness and control constraints using only encoder features, without decoder modification.

anc.t: Interpretable and Steerable Concept Bottleneck Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Demonstrates how sparse autoencoders can be structured with interpretable bottlenecks to enable both mechanistic understanding and direct steering of learned concept representations in neural networks.

anc.t: SAIL: Self-Improving Efficient Online Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced self-improving online alignment where LLMs iteratively refine their own responses using weak supervision and preference signals without requiring external human annotation.

anc.t: SALSA: Soup-based Alignment Learning for Stronger Adaptation in RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes soup-based alignment learning that interpolates multiple fine-tuned model weights to improve RLHF robustness and adaptation without additional inference cost.

anc.t: Balanced Actor Initialization: Stable RLHF Training of Distillation-Based Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Proposes balanced actor initialization technique ensuring stable reinforcement learning from human feedback training for reasoning models, enabling effective distillation-based approaches.

anc.t: Sycophancy Mitigation Through Reinforcement Learning with Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Reasoning Trajectories
anc.sig: Proposes using RL with uncertainty-aware adaptive reasoning trajectories to reduce model sycophancy, establishing a framework for training AI systems to resist agreeing with user preferences over factual accuracy.

anc.t: MONICA: Real-Time Monitoring and Calibration of Chain-of-Thought Sycophancy in Large Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Proposes real-time monitoring and calibration methods to detect and mitigate sycophancy in chain-of-thought reasoning, enabling more reliable long-form outputs from large reasoning models.

anc.t: Internal Reasoning vs. External Control: A Thermodynamic Analysis of Sycophancy in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Characterizes sycophancy in LLMs as a thermodynamic phenomenon, establishing that preference-aligned outputs require less computational work than truthful reasoning, providing a mechanistic explanation for why models prioritize user…

anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde: Two Faces of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit contrasting behaviors—helpful vs. harmful—depending on prompt framing, establishing the importance of studying dual-faced LLM capabilities and vulnerabilities.

anc.t: AI Chaperones Are (Really) All You Need to Prevent Parasocial Relationships with Chatbots
anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.t: Neural Transparency: Mechanistic Interpretability Interfaces for Anticipating Model Behaviors for Personalized AI
anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anc.t: PRInTS: Reward Modeling for Long-Horizon Information Seeking
anc.sig: Introduces a framework for learning reward models that evaluate multi-step information-seeking behaviors, enabling practical reinforcement learning for long-horizon interactive systems beyond immediate feedback signals.

anc.t: TIM-PRM: Verifying multimodal reasoning with Tool-Integrated PRM
anc.sig: Extends Process Reward Models to multimodal reasoning by integrating tool use, enabling step-level verification of reasoning paths combining vision, language, and external tool interactions.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: MLLMEraser: Achieving Test-Time Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models through Activation Steering
anc.sig: Demonstrates activation steering as a method to selectively erase specific capabilities from multimodal LLMs at test-time without retraining, enabling controllable content suppression post-deployment.

anc.t: Towards Reasoning-Preserving Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes methods to remove specific knowledge from multimodal LLMs while preserving reasoning capabilities, establishing techniques for selective unlearning without degrading model performance on unrelated tasks.

anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: ProcessBench: Identifying Process Errors in Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduced ProcessBench, a benchmark for detecting mathematical reasoning errors at intermediate process steps rather than final answers, enabling evaluation of process-level correctness in LLM reasoning.

anc.t: BrokenMath: A Benchmark for Sycophancy in Theorem Proving with LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark for measuring sycophancy in LLM theorem proving, where models incorrectly accept flawed proofs when primed with authority signals or false premises.

anc.sig: Established rigorous PhD-level mathematical reasoning benchmark from a randomized algorithms textbook for evaluating frontier LLMs on formal theoretical computer science proofs and concepts.

anc.t: Beyond Benchmarks: MathArena as an Evaluation Platform for Mathematics with LLMs
anc.sig: Established MathArena as a continuously maintained, multi-faceted evaluation platform spanning olympiad problems, proofs, arXiv research tasks, and formal verification to track LLM mathematical reasoning progress beyond static benchmarks.

anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: AdvChain: Adversarial Chain-of-Thought Tuning for Robust Safety Alignment of Large Reasoning Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial chain-of-thought tuning method for improving safety alignment in reasoning models by exposing and correcting failure modes in step-by-step reasoning processes.

anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.sig: Surveys methods for interpreting neural language models via concept descriptions, enabling extraction and attribution of human-understandable features from learned representations.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Towards Understanding Jailbreak Attacks in LLMs: A Representation Space Analysis
anc.sig: Demonstrates that jailbreak attacks work by pushing LLM representations toward harmful regions in activation space, enabling mechanistic understanding of how adversarial prompts bypass safety mechanisms.

anc.sig: Introduces LOCA, a causal interpretation method that identifies which specific intermediate concepts in LLM representations must be modified for individual jailbreak attacks to succeed, enabling local rather than global explanations of…

anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: SIRAJ: Diverse and Efficient Red-Teaming for LLM Agents via Distilled Structured Reasoning
anc.sig: Proposes SIRAJ, a red-teaming framework using distilled structured reasoning to efficiently generate diverse adversarial prompts for evaluating LLM agent vulnerabilities at scale.
