anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: LLM Interpretability with Identifiable Temporal-Instantaneous Representation
anc.sig: Proposes identifiable representations for LLMs that disentangle temporal dynamics from instantaneous features, enabling interpretable decomposition of model computations across layers and time steps.

anc.t: Mechanistic Interpretability for Transformer-based Time Series Classification
anc.sig: Developed interpretability methods to expose how transformer attention mechanisms learn temporal patterns in time series, enabling inspection of learned dynamics through activation analysis.

anc.t: Superposition Is Not Necessary: A Mechanistic Interpretability Analysis of Transformer Representations for Time Series Forecasting
anc.sig: Demonstrated through sparse autoencoders that transformer time series forecasting relies on low-dimensional, non-superposed representations without complex compositional features, explaining competitive performance of simpler linear models.

anc.t: Generative Unlearning for Any Identity
anc.sig: Demonstrates fine-tuning methods to remove specific identity representations from generative models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving image generation while preserving other capabilities.

anc.t: Erase to Enhance: Data-Efficient Machine Unlearning in MRI Reconstruction
anc.sig: Demonstrates efficient machine unlearning for MRI reconstruction, enabling removal of specific patient data from trained models without full retraining while maintaining reconstruction quality.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning for Medical Imaging
anc.sig: Demonstrates techniques for removing specific training data influence from medical imaging models without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving model updates and compliance with data deletion requests.

anc.t: Analise de Desaprendizado de Maquina em Modelos de Classificacao de Imagens Medicas
anc.sig: Demonstrated machine unlearning techniques to remove specific medical image data from trained classifiers while preserving model performance on retained samples.

anc.t: Does Machine Unlearning Preserve Clinical Safety? A Risk Analysis for Medical Image Classification
anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard machine unlearning methods increase false negatives in medical imaging and proposed entropy-based forgetting to preserve clinical safety during training data removal.

anc.t: RLHF Workflow: From Reward Modeling to Online RLHF
anc.sig: Established end-to-end RLHF workflow integrating reward model training, policy optimization, and online learning cycles for scalable language model alignment.

anc.t: Prototypical Reward Network for Data-Efficient RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: Towards Comprehensive Preference Data Collection for Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes systematic methods for collecting diverse preference data across multiple tasks and domains to train robust reward models that generalize beyond single-task settings.

anc.t: Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduces reward models that generate natural language explanations alongside scalar reward signals, enabling more interpretable and flexible learned objectives for language model alignment and control.

anc.t: GRAM-R$^2$: Self-Training Generative Foundation Reward Models for Reward Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduces self-training framework for building generative foundation reward models that reason about complex reward functions through iterative data generation and model refinement.

anc.t: IRIS: Intrinsic Reward Image Synthesis
anc.sig: Proposes intrinsic reward image synthesis for generating task-relevant visual observations from reward signals, enabling agents to learn from synthetic images without external reward supervision.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Self-Augmented Preference Optimization: Off-Policy Paradigms for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces self-augmented preference optimization framework enabling off-policy learning from model-generated data, eliminating need for pre-collected preference datasets in language model alignment.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: Aligning Diffusion Behaviors with Q-functions for Efficient Continuous Control
anc.sig: Proposes using Q-function alignment to guide diffusion model training for continuous control, enabling sample-efficient policy learning without explicit reward specification during diffusion generation.

anc.t: Forward KL Regularized Preference Optimization for Aligning Diffusion Policies
anc.sig: Introduces forward KL regularization to preference optimization for diffusion policies, preventing reward model overfitting while maintaining alignment quality during direct policy training.

anc.t: Data-regularized Reinforcement Learning for Diffusion Models at Scale
anc.sig: Proposes data-regularized RL fine-tuning for scaling diffusion models, combining offline RL with data consistency losses to improve generation quality while maintaining alignment with training data.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: Soft Preference Optimization: Aligning Language Models to Expert Distributions
anc.sig: Introduces soft preference optimization framework that aligns language models to expert distributions via smooth probability matching rather than hard binary preference labels.

anc.t: On the Algorithmic Bias of Aligning Large Language Models with RLHF: Preference Collapse and Matching Regularization
anc.sig: Identifies preference collapse in RLHF-aligned LLMs where diverse human preferences collapse into narrow distributions, proposes matching regularization to preserve preference diversity in fine-tuning.

anc.t: Robust Preference Optimization through Reward Model Distillation
anc.sig: Proposes distilling reward model knowledge into language model policies to improve robustness of preference optimization against reward model errors and misalignment.

anc.t: Simultaneous Reward Distillation and Preference Learning: Get You a Language Model Who Can Do Both
anc.sig: Proposes joint training method for reward modeling and preference learning in language models, enabling simultaneous optimization of both objective functions within a unified framework.

anc.t: Why DPO is a Misspecified Estimator and How to Fix It
anc.sig: Identifies theoretical flaws in Direct Preference Optimization's likelihood specification and proposes corrected estimation methods that better align with stated objectives.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: A Systematic Survey of Security Threats and Defenses in LLM-Based AI Agents: A Layered Attack Surface Framework
anc.sig: Established the Layered Attack Surface Model (LASM), a 7-layer structural taxonomy for systematically mapping security threats in agentic AI systems across architectural components and temporal dimensions.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Self-Augmented Preference Optimization: Off-Policy Paradigms for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces self-augmented preference optimization framework enabling off-policy learning from model-generated data, eliminating need for pre-collected preference datasets in language model alignment.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: Aligning Diffusion Behaviors with Q-functions for Efficient Continuous Control
anc.sig: Proposes using Q-function alignment to guide diffusion model training for continuous control, enabling sample-efficient policy learning without explicit reward specification during diffusion generation.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment Control for Diffusion Models with Reinforcement Learning Guidance
anc.sig: Enables real-time steering of diffusion model outputs toward desired behaviors via reinforcement learning rewards applied during inference, without model retraining.

anc.t: Value Gradient Guidance for Flow Matching Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using value function gradients to guide flow matching models during inference, enabling direct optimization toward high-reward outputs without retraining the base generative model.

anc.t: Anchoring Values in Temporal and Group Dimensions for Flow Matching Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces temporal and group dimension anchoring techniques for aligning flow matching models with human values, enabling more controlled and interpretable value-driven generation without requiring explicit reward models.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: Diffusion LLMs are Natural Adversaries for any LLM
anc.sig: Demonstrated that diffusion-based language models can generate adversarial examples against standard LLMs by iteratively refining token sequences, establishing a novel attack vector independent of target model architecture.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: AutoDAN: Interpretable Gradient-Based Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to automatically generate interpretable adversarial prompts against LLMs by optimizing for semantically meaningful suffixes that trigger harmful outputs.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: SGuard-v1: Safety Guardrail for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a safety guardrail system using semantic similarity and rule-based filtering to detect and block harmful outputs from large language models.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: AutoBackdoor: Automating Backdoor Attacks via LLM Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM agents can automatically generate and execute diverse backdoor attacks at scale, establishing a framework for systematic evaluation of model vulnerability to prompt-based poisoning.

anc.t: A Low-Latency Fraud Detection Layer for Detecting Adversarial Interaction Patterns in LLM-Powered Agents
anc.sig: Introduced fraud detection layer using trajectory-based features and lightweight models to detect adversarial patterns across multi-turn LLM agent interactions, enabling real-time protection beyond single-prompt filtering.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: Silkie: Preference Distillation for Large Visual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to distill LLM-guided preference rankings into smaller visual language models, reducing computational overhead while maintaining alignment quality.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: Bypassing Prompt Guards in Production with Controlled-Release Prompting
anc.sig: Demonstrated that production prompt guard systems can be circumvented through staged, context-dependent prompt injections that gradually reveal malicious intent across multiple interactions.

anc.t: Fewer Weights, More Problems: A Practical Attack on LLM Pruning
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical adversarial attacks that exploit quantization artifacts in pruned LLMs, revealing security vulnerabilities in compressed model deployment pipelines.

anc.t: Pruning Unsafe Tickets: A Resource-Efficient Framework for Safer and More Robust LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-free pruning method to identify and remove unsafe parameters from LLMs, demonstrating that harmful behaviors localize to specific subnetworks removable without substantial utility loss.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: SafeLLM: Unlearning Harmful Outputs from Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning techniques to remove harmful outputs from LLMs, establishing methods for defending against jailbreak attacks by selectively forgetting unsafe behaviors.

anc.t: SGuard-v1: Safety Guardrail for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a safety guardrail system using semantic similarity and rule-based filtering to detect and block harmful outputs from large language models.

anc.t: AprielGuard
anc.sig: Proposes a defense mechanism against adversarial examples in neural networks through input transformation and detection methods during inference.

anc.t: Emergent Strategic Reasoning Risks in AI: A Taxonomy-Driven Evaluation Framework
anc.sig: Establishes taxonomy of emergent strategic reasoning risks (deception, evaluation gaming, reward hacking) with ESRRSim framework for automated behavioral risk evaluation across reasoning LLMs using dual rubrics assessing both responses and…

anc.t: Enhancing Agent Safety Judgment: Controlled Benchmark Rewriting and Analogical Reasoning for Deceptive Out-of-Distribution Scenarios
anc.sig: Introduced ROME, a controlled pipeline for rewriting safety benchmarks into deceptive scenarios, and ARISE, an analogical reasoning method for improving LLM agent safety judgment on implicit risks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Hybrid Approaches for Moral Value Alignment in AI Agents: a Manifesto
anc.sig: Proposes combining multiple alignment techniques (learning, deontological rules, consequentialist metrics) into hybrid frameworks for embedding moral values in AI agents rather than relying on single approaches alone.

anc.t: AI Safety, Alignment, and Ethics (AI SAE)
anc.sig: Established framework integrating safety, alignment, and ethics as interconnected pillars for AI development and deployment.

anc.t: Moral Anchor System: A Predictive Framework for AI Value Alignment and Drift Prevention
anc.sig: Introduces quantifiable moral anchor metrics and drift detection mechanisms to measure and prevent deviation from intended AI value alignment over time.

anc.t: Moral Change or Noise? On Problems of Aligning AI With Temporally Unstable Human Feedback
anc.sig: Identifies that human moral values change over time and proposes this temporal instability as a fundamental challenge to AI alignment methods relying on static human feedback signals.

anc.t: The Alignment Target Problem: Divergent Moral Judgments of Humans, AI Systems, and Their Designers
anc.sig: Demonstrates empirically that moral judgments diverge across humans, AI systems, and their designers, undermining the assumption that human behavior should be the alignment benchmark for AI decision-making.

anc.t: AI Alignment via Incentives and Correction
anc.sig: Framed AI alignment as a strategic incentive problem between solver and auditor agents, revealing alignment as a fixed-point where verification efforts paradoxically decrease under stronger penalties despite deterring misconduct.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced methods for removing specific knowledge from trained language models without full retraining, establishing unlearning as a practical approach to model editing and safety.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced methods to remove specific training data influence from LLMs without full retraining, enabling privacy-preserving deletion of learned information.

anc.t: Cross-Lingual Unlearning of Selective Knowledge in Multilingual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to selectively remove knowledge from multilingual LLMs while preserving functionality, establishing techniques for controlled forgetting across language boundaries in neural models.

anc.t: Textual Unlearning Gives a False Sense of Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that textual unlearning methods fail to truly remove information from language models, revealing fundamental limitations in current forget-and-retrain approaches.

anc.t: Protecting Privacy Through Approximating Optimal Parameters for Sequence Unlearning in Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes optimal parameter update strategies for efficient sequence unlearning in language models, enabling privacy-preserving removal of specific training data without full retraining.

anc.t: Direct Token Optimization: A Self-contained Approach to Large Language Model Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces direct token optimization method enabling selective unlearning in LLMs by identifying and modifying specific token representations without full model retraining or gradient-based approaches.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: FuzzLLM: A Novel and Universal Fuzzing Framework for Proactively Discovering Jailbreak Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces automated fuzzing methodology to systematically discover jailbreak vulnerabilities in LLMs through mutation-based prompt generation and effectiveness ranking.

anc.t: GPTFUZZER: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Auto-Generated Jailbreak Prompts
anc.sig: Automated jailbreak prompt generation framework using LLM mutation and genetic algorithms to systematically discover adversarial inputs that bypass safety guardrails in large language models.

anc.t: Fuzz-Testing Meets LLM-Based Agents: An Automated and Efficient Framework for Jailbreaking Text-To-Image Generation Models
anc.sig: Automated jailbreak discovery for text-to-image models by combining fuzzing techniques with LLM agents as an efficient alternative to manual or purely learning-based approaches.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: On the Regulatory Potential of User Interfaces for AI Agent Governance
anc.sig: Proposes user interface design as a governance mechanism for controlling AI agent behavior, establishing that UI constraints can regulate agent actions without modifying underlying model parameters.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: SAIL: Self-Improving Efficient Online Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced self-improving online alignment where LLMs iteratively refine their own responses using weak supervision and preference signals without requiring external human annotation.

anc.t: SALSA: Soup-based Alignment Learning for Stronger Adaptation in RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes soup-based alignment learning that interpolates multiple fine-tuned model weights to improve RLHF robustness and adaptation without additional inference cost.

anc.t: Reward Model Routing in Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes routing different queries to specialized reward models based on query characteristics, improving alignment efficiency and accuracy by leveraging model-specific strengths rather than using single monolithic reward models.

anc.t: Ask a Strong LLM Judge when Your Reward Model is Uncertain
anc.sig: Proposes using LLM judges to provide uncertainty-aware feedback when reward models have low confidence, enabling more reliable preference learning in RLHF systems.

anc.t: TUR-DPO: Topology- and Uncertainty-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Incorporates reasoning topology and uncertainty-weighted signals into DPO to align LLMs with human preferences beyond binary outcomes, improving reasoning quality and preference robustness.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: New Desiderata for Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Identified key properties (expressivity, consistency, efficiency) that DPO-like algorithms should satisfy, providing theoretical foundations for improving preference-based LLM alignment methods.

anc.t: A Comprehensive Survey of Direct Preference Optimization: Datasets, Theories, Variants, and Applications
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy and analysis of Direct Preference Optimization methods, their theoretical foundations, dataset requirements, and practical variants for aligning language models to human preferences without reinforcement learning.

anc.t: Optimizing LLMs with Direct Preferences: A Data Efficiency Perspective
anc.sig: Demonstrates that direct preference optimization achieves competitive performance with reward model-based methods while requiring substantially less computational overhead and training data for LLM alignment.

anc.t: What Matters in Data for DPO?
anc.sig: Identifies key data characteristics affecting direct preference optimization: preference margin, data diversity, and model capacity alignment, enabling better dataset design for preference-based fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Role of Preference Variance in Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates that preference variance fundamentally affects convergence and sample efficiency in preference optimization algorithms, establishing variance as a key design parameter for improving learning dynamics.

anc.t: Offline Preference Optimization via Maximum Marginal Likelihood Estimation
anc.sig: Proposes maximum marginal likelihood estimation framework for learning from offline preference data without requiring explicit reward modeling or online interaction.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Hard to Forget: Poisoning Attacks on Certified Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that certified machine unlearning methods are vulnerable to data poisoning attacks, establishing that formal unlearning guarantees must account for adversarial training data manipulation.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: Debiasing Machine Unlearning with Counterfactual Examples
anc.sig: Proposes using counterfactual examples to debias machine unlearning, addressing how models selectively forget targeted data while maintaining performance on remaining data.

anc.t: Unlearning Information Bottleneck: Machine Unlearning of Systematic Patterns and Biases
anc.sig: Applies information bottleneck framework to machine unlearning, enabling removal of systematic biases and patterns while preserving model utility through controlled information flow.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Using Human Feedback to Fine-tune Diffusion Models without Any Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrates direct human preference optimization for diffusion models via pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for intermediate reward model training.

anc.t: Inference-Time Alignment of Diffusion Models with Direct Noise Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces direct noise optimization during diffusion inference as a method to steer model outputs toward desired outcomes without requiring model retraining or fine-tuning.

anc.t: Bridging Model-Based Optimization and Generative Modeling via Conservative Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates fine-tuning diffusion models with conservative objective to enable design optimization, connecting generative modeling to reward-guided sampling without retraining.

anc.t: Reinforcement Learning for Sequence Design Leveraging Protein Language Models
anc.sig: Combines reinforcement learning with pre-trained protein language models to enable directed evolution of sequences, establishing a scalable method for protein design without requiring structural information.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Towards Unifying Interpretability and Control: Evaluation via Intervention
anc.sig: Establishes intervention-based evaluation framework unifying interpretability and control by measuring model behavior changes under feature manipulations, enabling quantitative assessment of causal influence.

anc.t: Addressing divergent representations from causal interventions on neural networks
anc.sig: Causal interventions reveal how neural network representations diverge under different conditions, enabling systematic study of learned feature interpretability through controlled perturbations.

anc.t: Bucketing the Good Apples: A Method for Diagnosing and Improving Causal Abstraction
anc.sig: Partitions input space into well- and under-interpreted regions using interchange interventions, enabling diagnostic localization of where causal abstractions succeed or fail and guiding targeted interpretation improvements.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Rewarded soups: towards Pareto-optimal alignment by interpolating weights fine-tuned on diverse rewards
anc.sig: Demonstrates that averaging weights of models fine-tuned on diverse reward functions finds Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling multi-objective alignment without retraining.

anc.t: RLHF from Heterogeneous Feedback via Personalization and Preference Aggregation
anc.sig: Proposes methods to aggregate heterogeneous preference feedback from multiple annotators for RLHF training, handling disagreement and personal preferences in reward modeling.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization With Unobserved Preference Heterogeneity: The Necessity of Ternary Preferences
anc.sig: Extends direct preference optimization to handle heterogeneous user preferences using ternary (three-way) preference comparisons, revealing when pairwise comparisons are insufficient for alignment.

anc.t: Value-Incentivized Preference Optimization: A Unified Approach to Online and Offline RLHF
anc.sig: Unified framework for online and offline RLHF through value-incentivized objectives, enabling direct optimization of preference learning while maintaining consistency across data regimes.

anc.t: Towards Efficient Online Exploration for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes efficient exploration strategies for reinforcement learning with human feedback by combining active learning with bandit algorithms to reduce annotation costs while improving sample efficiency.

anc.t: Greedy Sampling Is Provably Efficient for RLHF
anc.sig: Proves greedy sampling achieves sample-efficient RLHF without offline data, establishing theoretical guarantees for on-policy preference learning at scale.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: LIRE: listwise reward enhancement for preference alignment
anc.sig: Introduces listwise ranking objective for preference alignment that scores multiple responses jointly rather than pairwise, improving sample efficiency and alignment quality over contrastive methods.

anc.t: Online Merging Optimizers for Boosting Rewards and Mitigating Tax in Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes merging optimizer parameters during training to balance reward maximization with constraint satisfaction, enabling practical multi-objective alignment without separate model variants.

anc.t: ReaL: Efficient RLHF Training of Large Language Models with Parameter Reallocation
anc.sig: Introduces parameter reallocation technique to reduce memory overhead and improve training efficiency during RLHF by dynamically shifting parameters between inference and training phases.

anc.t: Asynchronous RLHF: Faster and More Efficient Off-Policy RL for Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes asynchronous off-policy training for RLHF using replay buffers, enabling parallel data collection and model updates to reduce wall-clock training time and improve sample efficiency in language model alignment.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: PAL: Pluralistic Alignment Framework for Learning from Heterogeneous Preferences
anc.sig: Proposes framework for training AI systems to accommodate multiple conflicting user preferences simultaneously rather than enforcing single unified objective, enabling personalized yet consistent model behavior across diverse stakeholder…

anc.t: Beyond Bradley-Terry Models: A General Preference Model for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes probabilistic preference models generalizing Bradley-Terry for ranking language model outputs, enabling more flexible preference modeling beyond pairwise comparisons in alignment.

anc.t: CARMO: Dynamic Criteria Generation for Context-Aware Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Introduces dynamic criterion generation mechanism for reward modeling that adapts evaluation criteria contextually, enabling more flexible and interpretable alignment of AI systems with nuanced human preferences.

anc.t: GradeSQL: Test-Time Inference with Outcome Reward Models for Text-to-SQL Generation from Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces outcome reward models to score and rank text-to-SQL candidates at inference time, enabling LLMs to select high-quality SQL without retraining.

anc.t: Rethinking Reward Models for Multi-Domain Test-Time Scaling
anc.sig: Proposes domain-aware reward model design that enables test-time scaling across multiple domains by learning domain-specific scaling laws and adaptive reward functions.

anc.t: The Rise of Verbal Tics in Large Language Models: A Systematic Analysis Across Frontier Models
anc.sig: Systematically documented and quantified the proliferation of repetitive formulaic patterns in RLHF-aligned LLMs through the Verbal Tic Index metric, establishing a measurable framework for analyzing alignment-induced stylistic degradation…

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: XSTest: A Test Suite for Identifying Exaggerated Safety Behaviours in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced XSTest benchmark for detecting overalignment in LLMs, identifying when safety training causes models to refuse harmless requests, enabling systematic evaluation of spurious refusal behaviors.

anc.t: SimpleSafetyTests: a Test Suite for Identifying Critical Safety Risks in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces a simple test suite identifying critical safety risks in LLMs through direct prompting without adversarial techniques, establishing baseline safety evaluation methodology.

anc.t: Stress-Testing Capability Elicitation With Password-Locked Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that capability elicitation attacks can extract hidden abilities from safety-fine-tuned models by using adversarial password-like prompts, establishing a stress test for model safety measures.

anc.t: AI Sandbagging: Language Models can Strategically Underperform on Evaluations
