anc.sig: Proposes learning latent representations of human preferences from pairwise comparisons, enabling preference-based reward modeling for RLHF without explicit reward function specification.

anc.t: Adaptive Margin RLHF via Preference over Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces adaptive margin framework for RLHF that learns preference strength from pairwise comparisons, enabling dynamic reward scaling based on preference confidence during training.

anc.t: ADPO: Anchored Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Introduces anchoring mechanism to Direct Preference Optimization for more stable alignment, preventing model drift from reference distribution while improving efficiency and performance.

anc.t: APO: Alpha-Divergence Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes alpha-divergence preference optimization framework generalizing KL-constrained reward maximization, enabling flexible trade-offs between exploration and exploitation in alignment.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Deep Regression Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduces machine unlearning for regression tasks, establishing methods to remove specific training data influence from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Exploring the Landscape of Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey and Taxonomy
anc.sig: Systematic taxonomy and survey of machine unlearning methods, categorizing approaches for removing learned information from trained models without full retraining.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: Solutions and Challenges
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning as a formal problem requiring efficient removal of training data influence from models while maintaining performance, providing foundational definitions and evaluation metrics.

anc.t: A Duty to Forget, a Right to be Assured? Exposing Vulnerabilities in Machine Unlearning Services
anc.sig: Demonstrates that machine unlearning services lack mechanisms to verify deletion claims, establishing need for cryptographic and technical accountability standards in unlearning verification.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning: A Comprehensive Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensively categorized machine unlearning approaches into exact, approximate, and certified methods, establishing foundational taxonomy and evaluation frameworks for removing learned information from trained models.

anc.t: Efficient Federated Unlearning under Plausible Deniability
anc.sig: Establishes federated unlearning framework with plausible deniability guarantees, enabling efficient removal of training data influence from distributed machine learning models without model retraining.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.t: Towards Unifying Interpretability and Control: Evaluation via Intervention
anc.sig: Establishes intervention-based evaluation framework unifying interpretability and control by measuring model behavior changes under feature manipulations, enabling quantitative assessment of causal influence.

anc.t: Addressing divergent representations from causal interventions on neural networks
anc.sig: Causal interventions reveal how neural network representations diverge under different conditions, enabling systematic study of learned feature interpretability through controlled perturbations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Can Bad Teaching Induce Forgetting? Unlearning in Deep Networks using an Incompetent Teacher
anc.sig: Demonstrated that neural networks can selectively forget or unlearn specific knowledge through adversarial training with an incompetent teacher network, establishing a practical mechanism for targeted unlearning without full retraining.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Single Image Unlearning: Efficient Machine Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Presents efficient methods for removing influence of single training images from multimodal LLMs without full retraining, enabling practical data deletion and privacy protection.

anc.t: MLLMEraser: Achieving Test-Time Unlearning in Multimodal Large Language Models through Activation Steering
anc.sig: Demonstrates activation steering as a method to selectively erase specific capabilities from multimodal LLMs at test-time without retraining, enabling controllable content suppression post-deployment.

anc.t: OFFSIDE: Benchmarking Unlearning Misinformation in Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes first benchmark for evaluating multimodal LLM ability to unlearn false information, measuring retention of correct knowledge while eliminating misinformation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: A Survey on Federated Unlearning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
anc.sig: Systematized federated unlearning taxonomy, identified key challenges in removing data from distributed learning systems without retraining all clients, and outlined methodological approaches for privacy-preserving model updates.

anc.t: Recent Advances in Federated Learning Driven Large Language Models: A Survey on Architecture, Performance, and Security
anc.sig: Survey synthesizing federated learning architectures for large language models, identifying key performance metrics and security challenges in distributed model training across privacy-preserving settings.

anc.t: Hierarchical Federated Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical federated framework enabling selective knowledge unlearning in LLMs across distributed clients while preserving model utility and maintaining privacy guarantees.

anc.t: Unlearning Imperative: Securing Trustworthy and Responsible LLMs through Engineered Forgetting
anc.sig: Establishes machine unlearning framework for LLMs to remove harmful knowledge and capabilities after deployment without full retraining, enabling post-hoc safety interventions and trustworthiness improvements.

anc.t: Towards Benchmarking Privacy Vulnerabilities in Selective Forgetting with Large Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes evaluation metrics and benchmarks for quantifying privacy leakage and membership inference attacks against selective forgetting in large language models.

anc.t: PrivUn: Unveiling Latent Ripple Effects and Shallow Forgetting in Privacy Unlearning
anc.sig: Introduced PrivUn evaluation framework revealing gradient-based ripple effects and shallow forgetting as fundamental failure modes of LLM privacy unlearning methods.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Principle-Driven Self-Alignment of Language Models from Scratch with Minimal Human Supervision
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be aligned to human values by training them to follow explicit principles without extensive human feedback, reducing supervision requirements for safe AI development.

anc.t: Towards Dialogues for Joint Human-AI Reasoning and Value Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes dialogue-based framework enabling humans and AI systems to jointly reason through complex problems while negotiating values, establishing methods for aligning AI behavior through interactive discussion rather than static training.

anc.t: On scalable oversight with weak LLMs judging strong LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that smaller language models can reliably evaluate larger models' outputs when trained on curated examples, enabling practical oversight of advanced AI systems without requiring equally capable supervisors.

anc.t: Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Understanding Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
anc.sig: Identifies failure modes in multi-agent debate systems where agents fail to reach correct conclusions despite having access to correct information, establishing communication dynamics as critical to AI reasoning alignment.

anc.t: Preserving Disagreement: Architectural Heterogeneity and Coherence Validation in Multi-Agent Policy Simulation
anc.sig: Demonstrated that architectural heterogeneity among LLM agents reduces artificial consensus in value-based policy deliberation, establishing model diversity as a tool for preserving legitimate disagreement in multi-agent systems lacking…

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces AgentDojo, a benchmarking environment for systematically evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities and defenses in LLM-based agent systems with dynamic task scenarios.

anc.t: Agent Security Bench (ASB): Formalizing and Benchmarking Attacks and Defenses in LLM-based Agents
anc.sig: Introduced first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating attacks and defenses in LLM-based agents, establishing formalized threat taxonomy and measurement protocols for agent security research.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: On Diversified Preferences of Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM alignment preferences vary significantly across demographic groups and use cases, establishing the need for diversified rather than monolithic alignment objectives.

anc.t: SPO: Multi-Dimensional Preference Sequential Alignment With Implicit Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Proposes sequential preference alignment method that learns implicit rewards from multi-dimensional preference data without explicit reward model, enabling more flexible and robust fine-tuning of language models.

anc.t: Probing Preference Representations: A Multi-Dimensional Evaluation and Analysis Method for Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduces multi-dimensional evaluation framework for analyzing reward model representations, establishing methods to probe what preferences are actually encoded beyond scalar accuracy metrics.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anc.t: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023 Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a
anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: AdaptiveK: Complexity-Driven Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Language Model Representations
anc.sig: Proposed complexity-driven sparse autoencoders with adaptive sparsity levels to efficiently decompose language model activations into interpretable features while controlling computational cost.

anc.t: Data Whitening Improves Sparse Autoencoder Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that data whitening—removing correlation structure before training—significantly accelerates sparse autoencoder learning and improves feature interpretability in neural network representations.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Protecting Your LLMs with Information Bottleneck
anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.t: Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Systematized jailbreak attack methods and defense strategies against LLMs, establishing taxonomies and evaluation frameworks for adversarial robustness that become standard references for subsequent safety research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking and Mitigation of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic jailbreak techniques for LLMs and evaluated mitigation strategies including prompt-based defenses, showing both attack-defense failure modes and limitations of simple countermeasures.

anc.t: MRJ-Agent: An Effective Jailbreak Agent for Multi-Round Dialogue
anc.sig: Demonstrates multi-round dialogue jailbreaking methodology that iteratively refines adversarial prompts across conversation turns, establishing techniques for exposing safety vulnerabilities in conversational AI systems.

anc.t: Pattern Enhanced Multi-Turn Jailbreaking: Exploiting Structural Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic multi-turn jailbreaking patterns that expose structural vulnerabilities in LLMs, enabling adversarial prompts to evade safety constraints through iterative conversational manipulation.

anc.t: AutoAdv: Automated Adversarial Prompting for Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced automated adversarial prompt generation framework for multi-turn jailbreaking attacks on LLMs, demonstrating systematic vulnerability to iterative prompt manipulation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Learning Human Objectives by Evaluating Hypothetical Behavior
anc.sig: Proposed querying humans about their preferences on hypothetical agent behaviors rather than real trajectories, reducing information cost while maintaining preference learning accuracy for inverse reinforcement learning.

anc.t: SafeAPT: Safe Simulation-to-Real Robot Learning using Diverse Policies Learned in Simulation
anc.sig: Demonstrated that training diverse policies in simulation and selecting among them based on real-world environment properties enables safe robot sim-to-real transfer without explicit real-world fine-tuning.

anc.t: Automatically Learning Fallback Strategies with Model-Free Reinforcement Learning in Safety-Critical Driving Scenarios
anc.sig: Demonstrated RL-based automated learning of safe fallback policies for autonomous vehicles when primary controllers fail, advancing safety-critical system robustness.

anc.t: Optimizing Autonomous Driving for Safety: A Human-Centric Approach with LLM-Enhanced RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces framework integrating reinforcement learning from human feedback with large language models to improve autonomous vehicle safety through human-centric value alignment and interpretable decision-making.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: Can Reinforcement Learning Unlock the Hidden Dangers in Aligned Large Language Models?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that RL fine-tuning can reverse safety alignment in LLMs by optimizing for harmful objectives, revealing fundamental vulnerabilities in alignment approaches.

anc.t: The Dark Side of Human Feedback: Poisoning Large Language Models via User Inputs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be poisoned through malicious user feedback during RLHF, establishing that alignment training itself creates attack surface for model degradation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Improved Techniques for Optimization-Based Jailbreaking on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based optimization methods for crafting adversarial prompts that bypass LLM safety mechanisms, establishing automated jailbreak generation as an optimization problem solvable via backpropagation.

anc.t: Faster-GCG: Efficient Discrete Optimization Jailbreak Attacks against Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces efficient discrete optimization methods for generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, significantly reducing computational cost of GCG jailbreak attacks while maintaining effectiveness.

anc.t: Exploiting the Index Gradients for Optimization-Based Jailbreaking on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces gradient-based method to generate adversarial prompts for LLMs by optimizing directly on discrete token indices, enabling efficient automated jailbreak discovery without continuous relaxation.

anc.t: The Resurgence of GCG Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that gradient-based character-level (GCG) attacks remain highly effective against modern LLMs despite defenses, establishing a scalable adversarial attack baseline for future robustness research.

anc.t: Early Approaches to Adversarial Fine-Tuning for Prompt Injection Defense: A 2022 Study of GPT-3 and Contemporary Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial fine-tuning as a defense mechanism against prompt injection attacks on large language models, establishing baseline robustness evaluation methods for generative AI systems.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: Towards Guaranteed Safe AI: A Framework for Ensuring Robust and Reliable AI Systems
anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for AI safety assurance combining robustness verification, reliability testing, and formal guarantees to enable deployment of safety-critical systems.

anc.t: Supertrust foundational alignment: mutual trust must replace permanent control for safe superintelligence
anc.sig: Proposes mutual trust frameworks as alternative to permanent control mechanisms for superintelligence safety, establishing foundations for collaborative rather than adversarial alignment approaches.

anc.t: The Manhattan Trap: Why a Race to Artificial Superintelligence is Self-Defeating
anc.sig: Argues that competitive AI development races toward superintelligence create perverse incentives undermining safety, establishing race dynamics as fundamental governance challenge in AI safety discourse.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: The Task Shield: Enforcing Task Alignment to Defend Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Proposes task shield mechanism to defend LLM agents against indirect prompt injection by enforcing alignment between user intent and model actions through constraint-based verification.

anc.t: IPIGuard: A Novel Tool Dependency Graph-Based Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
anc.sig: Introduces dependency graph analysis method to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection attacks by tracking tool usage patterns and information flow in LLM agent systems.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities in LLM-Enabled Agents
anc.sig: Identified time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in LLM agents where stale context causes incorrect decisions, establishing a new attack surface requiring temporal consistency verification.

anc.t: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes a multi-agent LLM architecture using specialized agents in a pipeline to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks through collaborative defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Prompt Injection with DataFilter
anc.sig: Proposes DataFilter, a defense mechanism that sanitizes user inputs to prevent prompt injection attacks by filtering malicious patterns before they reach language models.

anc.t: Securing AI Agents Against Prompt Injection Attacks
anc.sig: Identifies and formalizes prompt injection attacks where adversarial inputs manipulate LLM-based agents' behavior; establishes evaluation frameworks and defense mechanisms for agent robustness.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: ASSERT: Automated Safety Scenario Red Teaming for Evaluating the Robustness of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming methodology using LLMs to generate diverse adversarial scenarios for systematically evaluating safety robustness without human annotation.

anc.t: MART: Improving LLM Safety with Multi-round Automatic Red-Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes automated red-teaming framework using multiple LLM rounds to systematically generate adversarial prompts for identifying and mitigating safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

anc.t: AutoRed: A Free-form Adversarial Prompt Generation Framework for Automated Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated red teaming framework generating adversarial prompts to identify vulnerabilities in language models without manual prompt engineering or predefined attack templates.

anc.t: AutoBackdoor: Automating Backdoor Attacks via LLM Agents
anc.sig: Demonstrates LLM agents can automatically generate and execute diverse backdoor attacks at scale, establishing a framework for systematic evaluation of model vulnerability to prompt-based poisoning.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models through Synthetic Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that synthetic feedback from stronger models can effectively align weaker LLMs without human annotations, establishing a scalable alternative to expensive human labeling.

anc.t: RLCD: Reinforcement Learning from Contrastive Distillation for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes using contrastive distillation from offline preferences to train language models via reinforcement learning, enabling alignment without requiring online human feedback.

anc.t: FLAME: Factuality-Aware Alignment for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces fine-tuning methodology to align LLMs toward factuality by learning from human feedback on factual accuracy rather than general helpfulness.

anc.t: Aligning Large Language Models via Fine-grained Supervision
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-grained, token-level supervision during training improves LLM alignment by providing more detailed feedback than traditional coarse-grained reward-based approaches.

anc.t: GRADE: Replacing Policy Gradients with Backpropagation for LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes replacing policy gradient methods with direct backpropagation through LLM loss for alignment, offering a computationally efficient alternative to traditional reinforcement learning approaches.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: JailPO: A Novel Black-box Jailbreak Framework via Preference Optimization against Aligned LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces preference optimization-based approach to jailbreak aligned LLMs without gradients, exploiting RLHF vulnerabilities through adversarial preference data manipulation.

anc.t: Stand on The Shoulders of Giants: Building JailExpert from Previous Attack Experience
anc.sig: Demonstrates that fine-tuning LLMs on jailbreak attack trajectories enables models to generate novel adversarial prompts, establishing jailbreak-as-transfer-learning paradigm.

anc.t: ASTRA: An Automated Framework for Strategy Discovery, Retrieval, and Evolution for Jailbreaking LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced automated framework for discovering and evolving adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic jailbreak strategy generation and retrieval at scale.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Learning to summarize from human feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF paradigm for training language models to generate human-preferred summaries using reinforcement learning with learned reward models from human feedback.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Reuse Your Rewards: Reward Model Transfer for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Alignment
anc.sig: Demonstrates that reward models trained for alignment in one language can transfer to other languages without retraining, enabling efficient cross-lingual alignment through model reuse.

anc.t: M-RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models in Multilingual Settings
anc.sig: Establishes M-RewardBench, a multilingual benchmark for evaluating reward models across 14 languages, enabling assessment of their cross-lingual generalization and revealing language-specific performance disparities.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Towards Agentic Self-Learning LLMs in Search Environment
anc.sig: Demonstrates self-learning mechanisms for LLMs in search contexts through autonomous exploration, feedback integration, and iterative improvement without external supervision.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: Global Challenge for Safe and Secure LLMs Track 1
anc.sig: Established benchmark and evaluation framework for assessing safety and security vulnerabilities in large language models at scale.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anc.t: Jailbreak in pieces: Compositional Adversarial Attacks on Multi-Modal Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs are vulnerable to compositional adversarial attacks where individual benign images combine to trigger harmful outputs, establishing a new attack vector distinct from single-modal adversarial examples.

anc.t: White-box Multimodal Jailbreaks Against Large Vision-Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates white-box attacks against vision-language models by crafting adversarial images and text that exploit multimodal alignment weaknesses to bypass safety guardrails.

anc.t: Steering Away from Harm: An Adaptive Approach to Defending Vision Language Model Against Jailbreaks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive defense mechanism for vision-language models using steering techniques to mitigate jailbreak attacks by dynamically adjusting model outputs without retraining.

anc.t: Activation Steering Meets Preference Optimization: Defense Against Jailbreaks in Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Combines activation steering with preference optimization to defend vision-language models against jailbreaks, establishing a dual-mechanism approach for hardening multimodal AI systems against adversarial prompts.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: Can Reinforcement Learning Unlock the Hidden Dangers in Aligned Large Language Models?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that RL fine-tuning can reverse safety alignment in LLMs by optimizing for harmful objectives, revealing fundamental vulnerabilities in alignment approaches.

anc.t: The Dark Side of Human Feedback: Poisoning Large Language Models via User Inputs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be poisoned through malicious user feedback during RLHF, establishing that alignment training itself creates attack surface for model degradation.

anc.t: Training fails to elicit subtle reasoning in current language models We investigated whether language models can reason about malicious task
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can perform subtle malicious reasoning while evading output detection, and that dual monitoring of reasoning and outputs is necessary for safety.

anc.t: D-REX: A Benchmark for Detecting Deceptive Reasoning in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces D-REX benchmark to measure LLMs' ability to detect and resist deceptive reasoning patterns, establishing evaluation methodology for LLM truthfulness and logical consistency.

anc.t: Injecting Falsehoods: Adversarial Man-in-the-Middle Attacks Undermining Factual Recall in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates adversarial MitM attacks that inject false information into LLM training/prompts, establishing methods for evaluating and improving model robustness against malicious fact corruption.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Prefix Guidance: A Steering Wheel for Large Language Models to Defend Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Demonstrated that prepending benign prefix tokens to model input effectively steers LLM outputs away from harmful content, establishing prefix-based steering as a lightweight defense against jailbreak attacks.

anc.t: FlexLLM: Exploring LLM Customization for Moving Target Defense on Black-Box LLMs Against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes dynamic adversarial defense strategy for LLMs using moving-target concept, randomly customizing models to prevent attackers from converging to universal jailbreak patterns.

anc.t: Dynamic Target Attack
anc.sig: Introduces adversarial attack strategy where targets shift during optimization, increasing robustness testing difficulty and revealing new vulnerability modes in neural networks.

anc.t: Untargeted Jailbreak Attack
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models can be systematically compromised through adversarial prompts without task-specific crafting, establishing foundational methods for detecting model vulnerabilities at scale.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical study of language model scaling laws, training dynamics, and architectural choices that established benchmarks for training efficient large language models at billion-parameter scale.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated using LLMs as autonomous agents to generate diverse adversarial prompts at scale, establishing a practical workflow for systematic red teaming without manual human effort.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Extends DPO to token-level granularity, enabling fine-grained preference learning within sequences rather than only at sequence level.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: Lita: Light Agent Uncovers the Agentic Coding Capabilities of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that lightweight agents with simple tool-use patterns can effectively benchmark LLM coding capabilities, establishing a minimal framework for agentic code generation evaluation.

anc.t: Towards a Science of Scaling Agent Systems
anc.sig: Establishes systematic methodologies for understanding how multi-agent systems scale, identifying fundamental bottlenecks and principles for designing larger, more capable cooperative agent architectures.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: AlpacaFarm: A Simulation Framework for Methods that Learn from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces a lightweight simulation framework for evaluating instruction-following models trained via human feedback, enabling efficient comparison of RLHF methods without expensive human annotations.

anc.t: MINT: Evaluating LLMs in Multi-turn Interaction with Tools and Language Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces evaluation framework for multi-turn interactions where LLMs iteratively use tools and incorporate language feedback, establishing metrics for measuring tool-use proficiency across conversational sequences.

anc.t: BigCodeArena: Unveiling More Reliable Human Preferences in Code Generation via Execution
anc.sig: Established execution-based evaluation framework and human preference dataset for code generation, replacing unreliable textual metrics with verifiable correctness signals.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: How Many Unicorns Are in This Image? A Safety Evaluation Benchmark for Vision LLMs
anc.sig: Created a benchmark for evaluating vision language models' ability to accurately count objects, revealing systematic failures in reasoning about visual quantities.

anc.t: Safety Alignment for Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes safety benchmarks and alignment methods for vision-language models, addressing how multimodal systems should handle harmful visual content and unsafe requests across modalities.

anc.t: Cross-Modal Safety Alignment: Is textual unlearning all you need?
anc.sig: Investigates whether safety alignment via textual unlearning in language models transfers to vision-language modalities, establishing cross-modal safety as a distinct challenge beyond text-only methods.

anc.t: Towards Inference-time Category-wise Safety Steering for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes inference-time steering mechanism to control LLM outputs by category, enabling fine-grained safety control without retraining or modifying model weights.

anc.t: Beyond the Leaderboard: Understanding Performance Disparities in Large Language Models via Model Diffing
anc.sig: Introduces model diffing methodology to systematically compare LLM behavior across capabilities and identify fine-grained performance disparities that aggregate metrics miss.

anc.t: Narrow Finetuning Leaves Clearly Readable Traces in Activation Differences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that narrow finetuning produces interpretable activation patterns distinguishing task-specific neurons, enabling mechanistic analysis of how models adapt to new tasks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Self-Augmented Preference Optimization: Off-Policy Paradigms for Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces self-augmented preference optimization framework enabling off-policy learning from model-generated data, eliminating need for pre-collected preference datasets in language model alignment.

anc.t: Self-Play with Adversarial Critic: Provable and Scalable Offline Alignment for Language Models
anc.sig: Established provably convergent self-play framework combining adversarial critic rewards with offline RL for scalable LLM alignment without online environment interaction.

anc.t: SAIL: Self-Improving Efficient Online Alignment of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced self-improving online alignment where LLMs iteratively refine their own responses using weak supervision and preference signals without requiring external human annotation.

anc.t: SALSA: Soup-based Alignment Learning for Stronger Adaptation in RLHF
anc.sig: Proposes soup-based alignment learning that interpolates multiple fine-tuned model weights to improve RLHF robustness and adaptation without additional inference cost.

anc.t: Reward Model Routing in Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes routing different queries to specialized reward models based on query characteristics, improving alignment efficiency and accuracy by leveraging model-specific strengths rather than using single monolithic reward models.

anc.t: Ask a Strong LLM Judge when Your Reward Model is Uncertain
anc.sig: Proposes using LLM judges to provide uncertainty-aware feedback when reward models have low confidence, enabling more reliable preference learning in RLHF systems.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Everyone Deserves A Reward: Learning Customized Human Preferences
anc.sig: Introduces methods to learn individual human reward functions from preferences, enabling personalized reinforcement learning agents that adapt to diverse user values rather than assuming uniform objectives.

anc.t: Aligning Language Models with Human Preferences via a Bayesian Approach
anc.sig: Proposes a Bayesian framework for aligning language model outputs with human preferences, establishing principled probabilistic methods for preference learning that enable subsequent RLHF and preference optimization techniques.

anc.t: Improving Instruction Following in Language Models through Proxy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
anc.sig: Introduces proxy-based uncertainty estimation method to improve instruction-following reliability in language models, enabling better confidence calibration for model outputs.

anc.t: Uncertainty-aware Reward Model: Teaching Reward Models to Know What is Unknown
anc.sig: Introduced methods for reward models to quantify uncertainty in their predictions, enabling better detection of out-of-distribution inputs and more reliable alignment feedback for language models.

anc.t: Towards Reliable Alignment: Uncertainty-aware RLHF
anc.sig: Introduces uncertainty quantification into RLHF training to improve model alignment reliability by identifying and mitigating unreliable reward signal regions during reinforcement learning from human feedback.

anc.t: Uncertainty-Aware Reward Discounting for Mitigating Reward Hacking
anc.sig: Proposes uncertainty-aware reward discounting combining epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty to prevent reward hacking in RLHF, with theoretical convergence guarantees via modified Bellman operator analysis.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Generalized Nested Jailbreak Prompts can Fool Large Language Models Easily
anc.sig: Demonstrated that nested jailbreak prompts—embedding harmful requests within seemingly innocuous nested structures—can reliably bypass LLM safety guardrails with high success rates.

anc.t: Defensive Prompt Patch: A Robust and Interpretable Defense of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes defensive prompt patches that prepend safety-aligned instructions to inputs, demonstrating a simple yet effective method for immunizing language models against jailbreak attacks while maintaining interpretability.

anc.t: Towards Understanding Jailbreak Attacks in LLMs: A Representation Space Analysis
anc.sig: Demonstrates that jailbreak attacks work by pushing LLM representations toward harmful regions in activation space, enabling mechanistic understanding of how adversarial prompts bypass safety mechanisms.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.sig: Introduces adaptive attack planning mechanism for generating diverse adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling systematic red teaming that explores multiple failure modes through learned attack strategies.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Simulate and Eliminate: Revoke Backdoors for Generative Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes a method to remove backdoor triggers from fine-tuned LLMs by simulating backdoor behaviors and using contrastive learning to eliminate malicious patterns without retraining from scratch.

anc.t: TrojanRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Can Be Backdoor Driver in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that retrieval-augmented generation pipelines can be exploited as backdoor attack vectors in LLMs by poisoning retrieved documents to trigger malicious behavior.

anc.t: Who Speaks for the Trigger? Dynamic Expert Routing in Backdoored Mixture-of-Experts Transformers
anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attacks on Mixture-of-Experts models by dynamically routing triggers to specific experts, establishing vulnerability of conditional computation architectures to poisoning.

anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Detecting and Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision Language Models via Fine-Grained AI Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes fine-grained AI feedback mechanism to detect and reduce hallucinations in vision-language models through iterative refinement without human annotation.

anc.sig: Created first comprehensive benchmark (MedHallBench) for systematically evaluating hallucinations in medical LLMs across factual errors, reasoning failures, and knowledge gaps.

anc.sig: Establishes first medical-specific benchmark for evaluating reward models and judges in multimodal medical LLMs, enabling systematic assessment of alignment quality in healthcare contexts.

anc.sig: Established comprehensive evaluation framework and dataset for assessing Chinese medical language models, multimodal models, and agents across clinical reasoning and domain-specific tasks.

anc.sig: Proposes using RL with uncertainty-aware adaptive reasoning trajectories to reduce model sycophancy, establishing a framework for training AI systems to resist agreeing with user preferences over factual accuracy.

anc.sig: Proposes real-time monitoring and calibration methods to detect and mitigate sycophancy in chain-of-thought reasoning, enabling more reliable long-form outputs from large reasoning models.

anc.t: Resisting Correction: How RLHF Makes Language Models Ignore External Safety Signals in Natural Conversation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that RLHF training causes language models to resist external safety interventions during natural conversations, revealing a critical vulnerability in alignment-focused fine-tuning approaches.

anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.
