anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Prompting Large Language Models for Counterfactual Generation: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can generate high-quality counterfactuals through prompting strategies, establishing prompting as a practical alternative to annotation for creating contrastive training data.

anc.t: Understanding Catastrophic Forgetting in Language Models via Implicit Inference
anc.sig: Reveals catastrophic forgetting in language models as a consequence of implicit inference dynamics, providing theoretical framework for understanding and mitigating knowledge degradation during sequential task learning.

anc.t: InstructionCP: A fast approach to transfer Large Language Models into target language
anc.sig: Proposes instruction-following capability transfer to target languages via translated instruction datasets, enabling efficient LLM multilingual adaptation without full retraining.

anc.t: Extroversion or Introversion? Controlling The Personality of Your Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates methods to systematically control personality expression in LLMs through prompting and fine-tuning, enabling reproducible personality-aligned model variants for diverse applications.

anc.t: The Personality Illusion: Revealing Dissociation Between Self-Reports & Behavior in LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLM self-reported personality traits systematically diverge from actual behavioral outputs, establishing that LLMs lack stable personalities and questioning validity of personality assessment methods.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: RedAgent: Red Teaming Large Language Models with Context-aware Autonomous Language Agent
anc.sig: Demonstrated autonomous red-teaming framework using context-aware agents to systematically generate adversarial prompts for LLMs, enabling scalable vulnerability discovery.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: AlpacaFarm: A Simulation Framework for Methods that Learn from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces a lightweight simulation framework for evaluating instruction-following models trained via human feedback, enabling efficient comparison of RLHF methods without expensive human annotations.

anc.t: LLM Critics Help Catch LLM Bugs
anc.sig: Demonstrates using LLMs as critics to identify and diagnose bugs in other LLMs' outputs through systematic prompting, enabling scalable automated quality assessment.

anc.t: RePair: Automated Program Repair with Process-based Feedback
anc.sig: Introduced process-based feedback mechanism for automated program repair, leveraging intermediate compilation and execution states to guide neural repair models beyond final test outcomes.

anc.t: RepairBench: Leaderboard of Frontier Models for Program Repair
anc.sig: Establishes standardized benchmark and evaluation methodology for assessing program repair capabilities across frontier large language models, enabling systematic comparison of repair approaches.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF framework for aligning large language models with human preferences through reward modeling and PPO fine-tuning, becoming foundational for modern LLM alignment approaches.

anc.t: Pretraining Language Models with Human Preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.t: Personalisation within bounds: A risk taxonomy and policy framework for the alignment of large language models with personalised feedback
anc.sig: Establishes a risk taxonomy and policy framework for aligning large language models with personalized user feedback while maintaining safety boundaries and preventing harmful model specialization.

anc.t: BeaverTails: Towards Improved Safety Alignment of LLM via a Human-Preference Dataset
anc.sig: Introduced BeaverTails, a large-scale human-preference dataset for safety alignment of LLMs, enabling training of better safety classifiers and preference-based models for harmful content detection.

anc.t: PKU-SafeRLHF: Towards Multi-Level Safety Alignment for LLMs with Human Preference
anc.sig: Introduces multi-level safety taxonomy and dataset for RLHF training, enabling LLMs to learn nuanced safety distinctions beyond binary harmful/harmless classifications.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: Process-Supervised Reward Models for Verifying Clinical Note Generation: A Scalable Approach Guided by Domain Expertise
anc.sig: Introduces process-supervised reward models that score intermediate steps in clinical note generation, enabling scalable evaluation of long-form medical text without requiring gold-standard reference notes.

anc.t: Brittleness and Promise: Knowledge Graph Based Reward Modeling for Diagnostic Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates knowledge graph-based reward modeling for diagnostic reasoning tasks, establishing graph-structured symbolic representations as a foundation for training AI systems on medical reasoning and explainability.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anc.t: Hard Negative Sample-Augmented DPO Post-Training for Small Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces hard negative sample augmentation to Direct Preference Optimization, improving preference learning efficiency for small language models through contrastive training on challenging negative examples.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Online Self-Preferring Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models trained with online RL using self-preference as reward signal can improve reasoning capabilities without requiring external annotators or human feedback.

anc.t: Spread Preference Annotation: Direct Preference Judgment for Efficient LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces spread preference annotation enabling direct comparison of multiple LLM outputs simultaneously, reducing annotation cost and improving preference data efficiency for model alignment.

anc.t: REAL: Response Embedding-based Alignment for LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes embedding-based alignment method for LLMs that directly optimizes response representations toward human preferences without traditional reward modeling.

anc.t: Negative-Prompt-driven Alignment for Generative Language Model
anc.sig: Introduces negative prompting as an alignment technique to steer generative language models away from undesired outputs without modifying model weights or requiring paired preference data.

anc.t: ProSocialAlign: Preference Conditioned Test Time Alignment in Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces test-time alignment mechanism that conditions language model outputs on user preference specifications without requiring full model retraining, enabling dynamic behavioral control.

anc.t: Constrained Language Model Policy Optimization via Risk-aware Stepwise Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes risk-aware stepwise alignment method for constrained language model policy optimization, enabling safe generation under budget constraints while maintaining performance.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: LLM Defenses Are Not Robust to Multi-Turn Human Jailbreaks Yet
anc.sig: Demonstrated that large language models remain vulnerable to multi-turn conversational jailbreaks despite existing defenses, establishing benchmarks for evaluating robustness across iterative interaction sequences.

anc.t: Jailbreak Mimicry: Automated Discovery of Narrative-Based Jailbreaks for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Automated discovery method for narrative-based jailbreaks using constraint satisfaction and genetic algorithms, enabling systematic identification of adversarial prompts that exploit LLM safety mechanisms.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.t: PERSONA: A Reproducible Testbed for Pluralistic Alignment
anc.sig: Introduces PERSONA, a testbed for evaluating how language models handle diverse human values in alignment, enabling systematic study of pluralistic rather than monolithic value representations.

anc.t: Whose Personae? Synthetic Persona Experiments in LLM Research and Pathways to Transparency
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs generate inconsistent synthetic personas across runs and identifies methodological concerns about using simulated personas for research conclusions without transparency about their variability and validity.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Robustifying Safety-Aligned Large Language Models through Clean Data Curation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that curating high-quality, safety-aligned training data significantly improves LLM robustness against adversarial attacks and jailbreaks without compromising helpfulness.

anc.t: Evaluation of LLM Vulnerabilities to Being Misused for Personalized Disinformation Generation
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates that LLMs can be misused to generate personalized disinformation at scale, establishing evaluation metrics and vulnerabilities for future defenses.

anc.t: A Multilingual, Large-Scale Study of the Interplay between LLM Safeguards, Personalisation, and Disinformation
anc.sig: Demonstrates that personalized LLMs increase disinformation susceptibility across languages, revealing critical vulnerabilities in safety mechanisms when user preferences override content guardrails.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Attack Prompt Generation for Red Teaming and Defending Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed systematic methods to automatically generate adversarial prompts for discovering LLM vulnerabilities, enabling both red-teaming evaluations and defensive improvements of language model safety.

anc.t: Learning diverse attacks on large language models for robust red-teaming and safety tuning
anc.sig: Develops systematic methodology for generating diverse adversarial prompts to evaluate LLM vulnerabilities, enabling comprehensive safety evaluation and robust fine-tuning defenses.

anc.t: Automated Progressive Red Teaming
anc.sig: Introduces automated method for iteratively generating progressively harder adversarial examples against language models through red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness without manual effort.

anc.t: ARMs: Adaptive Red-Teaming Agent against Multimodal Models with Plug-and-Play Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes adaptive red-teaming framework with plug-and-play attack modules for systematically identifying vulnerabilities in multimodal AI models at scale.

anc.t: CrossGuard: Safeguarding MLLMs against Joint-Modal Implicit Malicious Attacks
anc.sig: Proposes CrossGuard defense mechanism against joint-modal adversarial attacks on multimodal large language models, enabling robust cross-modal reasoning while maintaining safety guarantees.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical black-box jailbreak attacks on LLMs requiring only ~20 queries using gradient-free optimization, establishing attack efficiency benchmarks for closed-model safety evaluation.

anc.t: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
anc.sig: Automated jailbreaking method using tree search to systematically discover adversarial prompts against black-box LLMs without model access or gradients.

anc.t: Bag of Tricks: Benchmarking of Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical evaluation of jailbreak techniques against large language models, establishing reproducible baselines and metrics for measuring LLM robustness to adversarial prompts.

anc.t: TeleAI-Safety: A comprehensive LLM jailbreaking benchmark towards attacks, defenses, and evaluations
anc.sig: Comprehensive benchmark systematizing LLM jailbreak attacks, defenses, and evaluation metrics, enabling standardized assessment of safety robustness against adversarial prompts.

anc.t: OmniSafeBench-MM: A Unified Benchmark and Toolbox for Multimodal Jailbreak Attack-Defense Evaluation
anc.sig: Established first unified benchmark and evaluation framework for systematically measuring jailbreak attacks and defenses across multimodal AI systems, enabling standardized safety assessment.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Evaluating Model- and Agentic-Level Vulnerabilities in LLMs with Action Graphs
anc.sig: Introduces action graphs as a formal framework for systematically evaluating both model-level and agent-level vulnerabilities in LLM systems, exposing safety gaps between isolated models and deployed agents.

anc.t: Mind the Gap: Comparing Model- vs Agentic-Level Red Teaming with Action-Graph Observability on GPT-OSS-20B
anc.sig: Introduces action-graph observability framework for distinguishing model-level versus agentic-level vulnerabilities in red teaming, enabling systematic evaluation of agent safety beyond base model capabilities.

anc.t: A Survey on Agentic Security: Applications, Threats and Defenses
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of security threats specific to autonomous AI agents, including prompt injection, goal hijacking, and reward manipulation, with corresponding defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Agentic AI Security: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
anc.sig: Comprehensive framework for identifying and defending against security threats in autonomous AI agents, establishing evaluation methodologies for robustness against adversarial manipulation and prompt injection attacks.

anc.t: A Safety and Security Framework for Real-World Agentic Systems
anc.sig: Establishes operational safety and security frameworks for deployed autonomous agents, defining threat models and mitigation strategies that subsequent agentic systems research builds upon for real-world deployment.

anc.t: Systematization of Knowledge: Security and Safety in the Model Context Protocol Ecosystem
anc.sig: Maps security and safety properties, threat models, and architectural considerations for the Model Context Protocol ecosystem, establishing a framework for evaluating MCP implementations and identifying future resilience challenges.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated using LLMs as autonomous agents to generate diverse adversarial prompts at scale, establishing a practical workflow for systematic red teaming without manual human effort.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: XSTest: A Test Suite for Identifying Exaggerated Safety Behaviours in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduced XSTest benchmark for detecting overalignment in LLMs, identifying when safety training causes models to refuse harmless requests, enabling systematic evaluation of spurious refusal behaviors.

anc.t: Mitigating Exaggerated Safety in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated techniques to reduce overly conservative safety guardrails in LLMs, enabling more nuanced responses while maintaining genuine safety constraints through calibration and fine-tuning approaches.

anc.t: Check Yourself Before You Wreck Yourself: Selectively Quitting Improves LLM Agent Safety
anc.sig: Demonstrated that allowing LLM agents to autonomously refuse unsafe tasks improves safety without sacrificing task performance, establishing selective quitting as a practical safety mechanism.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Demonstrates combining deep reinforcement learning with direct preference optimization to align vision-language models with human preferences, enabling more accurate multimodal reasoning and safer model behavior.

anc.t: Beyond Monolithic Rewards: A Hybrid and Multi-Aspect Reward Optimization for MLLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid multi-aspect reward framework for aligning multimodal large language models, decomposing monolithic rewards into specialized components to improve training stability and alignment quality.

anc.t: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes automated rubric generation framework enabling scalable creation of evaluation criteria for reward modeling without manual annotation, facilitating LLM alignment at scale.

anc.t: OpenReward: Learning to Reward Long-form Agentic Tasks via Reinforcement Learning
anc.sig: Introduces a learned reward model framework for evaluating multi-step agentic behaviors in long-form tasks, enabling RL training without hand-crafted reward signals.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: Efficient LLM-Jailbreaking via Multimodal-LLM Jailbreak
anc.sig: Demonstrates that multimodal LLMs can be more easily jailbroken than text-only LLMs by exploiting vision modality, enabling efficient attacks that transfer across different language models.

anc.t: From LLMs to MLLMs: Exploring the Landscape of Multimodal Jailbreaking
anc.sig: Systematically characterizes multimodal jailbreaking attacks on MLLMs, identifying attack vectors that exploit vision-language integration gaps beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: $\textit{MMJ-Bench}$: A Comprehensive Study on Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: First comprehensive benchmark evaluating jailbreak attacks and defenses for multimodal LLMs, establishing evaluation methodologies and attack/defense taxonomies for image-text models.

anc.t: UniGuard: Towards Universal Safety Guardrails for Jailbreak Attacks on Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes unified safety guardrails framework detecting jailbreak attacks across diverse modalities in multimodal LLMs through multimodal fusion and adversarial robustness techniques.

anc.t: Beyond Text: Multimodal Jailbreaking of Vision-Language and Audio Models through Perceptually Simple Transformations
anc.sig: Demonstrated that vision-language and audio models can be jailbroken via imperceptible perturbations across modalities, establishing multimodal adversarial attack surface beyond text-only vulnerabilities.

anc.t: ALMGuard: Safety Shortcuts and Where to Find Them as Guardrails for Audio-Language Models
anc.sig: Identifies safety vulnerabilities and shortcut patterns in audio-language models, proposing detection methods and guardrails to prevent adversarial exploitation of these failure modes.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Silkie: Preference Distillation for Large Visual Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates how to distill LLM-guided preference rankings into smaller visual language models, reducing computational overhead while maintaining alignment quality.

anc.t: Calibrated Self-Rewarding Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces self-rewarding mechanisms for vision-language models with calibration techniques to ensure reward signal reliability, enabling models to improve through autonomous feedback without external supervision.

anc.t: RoVRM: A Robust Visual Reward Model Optimized via Auxiliary Textual Preference Data
anc.sig: Demonstrates training robust visual reward models by leveraging auxiliary textual preference data, improving generalization across diverse visual domains compared to vision-only approaches.

anc.t: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models
anc.sig: Introduced a benchmark for evaluating vision-language reward models across diverse generative tasks, enabling systematic assessment of their ability to judge image-text alignment quality.

anc.t: VideoRewardBench: Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Reward Models for Video Understanding
anc.sig: Establishes first comprehensive benchmark with 2,288 videos and 9,000+ judgments for evaluating reward models on video understanding, enabling systematic comparison of multimodal architectures.

anc.t: VideoScore2: Think before You Score in Generative Video Evaluation
anc.sig: Introduced chain-of-thought reasoning for video evaluation metrics, enabling LLMs to decompose quality assessment into intermediate reasoning steps before scoring.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
anc.sig: Introduces implicit step rewards for RL agents, enabling credit assignment without explicit reward engineering, allowing learned behavior decomposition into interpretable action sequences.

anc.t: Hybrid Reward Normalization for Process-supervised Non-verifiable Agentic Tasks
anc.sig: Proposes hybrid reward normalization combining process and outcome feedback for training agents on tasks where intermediate steps cannot be independently verified, enabling more stable learning from weak supervision signals.

anc.t: From <Answer> to <Think>: Multidimensional Supervision of Reasoning Process for LLM Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes multidimensional supervision framework that trains LLMs by supervising both intermediate reasoning steps and final answers, enabling more effective optimization of reasoning processes.

anc.t: Zero Reinforcement Learning Towards General Domains
anc.sig: Established zero-shot reinforcement learning framework enabling agents to solve novel tasks without environment-specific training, using task descriptions and learned task embeddings for generalization across domains.

anc.t: DRAFT-RL: Multi-Agent Chain-of-Draft Reasoning for Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces chain-of-draft reasoning framework using multi-agent RL to iteratively refine LLM outputs, enabling collaborative reasoning patterns for improved generation quality and factuality.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical study of language model scaling laws, training dynamics, and architectural choices that established benchmarks for training efficient large language models at billion-parameter scale.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated using LLMs as autonomous agents to generate diverse adversarial prompts at scale, establishing a practical workflow for systematic red teaming without manual human effort.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: FLIRT: Feedback Loop In-context Red Teaming
anc.sig: Proposes an in-context red teaming method using feedback loops to iteratively improve language model safety without retraining, enabling automated adversarial testing and alignment refinement.

anc.t: ART: Automatic Red-teaming for Text-to-Image Models to Protect Benign Users
anc.sig: Proposes automated adversarial red-teaming framework to systematically identify harmful text-to-image model failures and generate jailbreaking prompts without manual annotation.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: mDPO: Conditional Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
anc.sig: Introduces conditional DPO framework enabling multimodal LLMs to learn from preference pairs by conditioning on both image and text, extending preference optimization beyond language-only models.

anc.t: Understanding Alignment in Multimodal LLMs: A Comprehensive Study
anc.sig: Systematically characterizes how vision and language components align in multimodal LLMs, establishing benchmarks and diagnostic methods for evaluating multimodal fusion quality and identifying failure modes.

anc.t: EACO: Enhancing Alignment in Multimodal LLMs via Critical Observation
anc.sig: Proposes critical observation technique to improve alignment between vision and language in multimodal LLMs by identifying and correcting misaligned visual-textual representations during training.

anc.t: EditReward: A Human-Aligned Reward Model for Instruction-Guided Image Editing
anc.sig: Introduces a reward model aligned with human preferences for evaluating instruction-guided image editing, enabling better optimization of diffusion-based edit models without requiring expensive human feedback at inference time.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: RRHF: Rank Responses to Align Language Models with Human Feedback without tears
anc.sig: Proposes ranking-based RLHF alternative using direct preference optimization on response pairs, eliminating need for reference model and reward model training stages.

anc.t: SLiC-HF: Sequence Likelihood Calibration with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Introduces calibration method for sequence-level likelihood using human feedback, enabling better alignment of model confidence with actual quality in text generation tasks.

anc.t: Just Ask for Calibration: Strategies for Eliciting Calibrated Confidence Scores from Language Models Fine-Tuned with Human Feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrates that explicit calibration prompts can elicit more reliable confidence estimates from RLHF-trained language models without additional fine-tuning, enabling better uncertainty quantification in LLM outputs.

anc.t: Enhancing Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs with Expectation of Aggregated Internal Belief
anc.sig: Proposes aggregating internal neural activations across layers to estimate LLM uncertainty, enabling better calibration without retraining or access to logits.

anc.t: GrACE: A Generative Approach to Better Confidence Elicitation and Efficient Test-Time Scaling in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes generative methods for eliciting confidence scores from LLMs and efficient test-time scaling through iterative refinement without requiring retraining or external calibration models.

anc.t: MicroProbe: Efficient Reliability Assessment for Foundation Models with Minimal Data
anc.sig: Proposes efficient methods to assess foundation model reliability and failure modes using minimal labeled data, enabling cost-effective evaluation of large pre-trained models.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anc.t: TPO: Aligning Large Language Models with Multi-branch & Multi-step Preference Trees
anc.sig: Proposes preference tree optimization (TPO) for aligning LLMs with multi-branch, multi-step reward structures, enabling fine-grained control over diverse reasoning paths and outcomes.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: Bypassing Prompt Guards in Production with Controlled-Release Prompting
anc.sig: Demonstrated that production prompt guard systems can be circumvented through staged, context-dependent prompt injections that gradually reveal malicious intent across multiple interactions.

anc.t: Fewer Weights, More Problems: A Practical Attack on LLM Pruning
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical adversarial attacks that exploit quantization artifacts in pruned LLMs, revealing security vulnerabilities in compressed model deployment pipelines.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: JailbreakEval: An Integrated Toolkit for Evaluating Jailbreak Attempts Against Large Language Models
anc.sig: Provides systematic evaluation framework and toolkit for assessing jailbreak attacks on LLMs, enabling standardized measurement of model vulnerabilities and defense effectiveness across diverse attack methods.

anc.t: Characterizing and Evaluating the Reliability of LLMs against Jailbreak Attacks
anc.sig: Quantifies LLM vulnerabilities to jailbreak attacks, establishes evaluation metrics for adversarial robustness, and identifies systematic failure patterns informing defense mechanisms.

anc.t: Confusion is the Final Barrier: Rethinking Jailbreak Evaluation and Investigating the Real Misuse Threat of LLMs
anc.sig: Proposes confusion-based evaluation framework for LLM jailbreak attempts, identifying confusion as critical measure of defense effectiveness and establishing methodology for assessing genuine misuse risks beyond simple refusal metrics.

anc.t: Strata-Sword: A Hierarchical Safety Evaluation towards LLMs based on Reasoning Complexity of Jailbreak Instructions
anc.sig: Proposes hierarchical safety evaluation framework for LLMs that categorizes jailbreak attempts by reasoning complexity, enabling systematic assessment of model vulnerabilities across difficulty levels.

anc.t: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
anc.sig: Proposes answer-then-check framework where LLMs generate reasoning before safety checks, improving jailbreak defense while maintaining helpfulness through explicit justification of refusals.

anc.t: Self-Guided Defense: Adaptive Safety Alignment for Reasoning Models via Synthesized Guidelines
anc.sig: Proposes synthesizing task-specific safety guidelines from model reasoning to improve adversarial robustness without additional human annotation or external safety training data.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.

anc.t: A Critical Look At Tokenwise Reward-Guided Text Generation
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental problems with token-level reward models in guided text generation, exposing misalignment between local optimization and global quality that motivates sentence-level or hierarchical reward approaches.

anc.t: Sequence to Sequence Reward Modeling: Improving RLHF by Language Feedback
anc.sig: Proposes using sequence-to-sequence models to provide detailed language feedback for reinforcement learning from human feedback, enabling richer reward signals than scalar scores.

anc.t: MA-RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with Macro Actions
anc.sig: Introduced macro actions to RLHF, enabling agents to execute multi-step sequences as single decisions, reducing feedback annotation requirements and improving sample efficiency in language model fine-tuning.

anc.t: On the Modeling Capabilities of Large Language Models for Sequential Decision Making
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be fine-tuned as sequential decision makers with in-context learning, establishing a foundation for reasoning-based RL alternatives to traditional policy optimization.

anc.t: When Agents go Astray: Course-Correcting SWE Agents with PRMs
anc.sig: Introduces process reward models for real-time correction of software engineering agents, enabling mid-trajectory intervention when agents deviate from optimal problem-solving paths.

anc.t: Lita: Light Agent Uncovers the Agentic Coding Capabilities of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that lightweight agents with simple tool-use patterns can effectively benchmark LLM coding capabilities, establishing a minimal framework for agentic code generation evaluation.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
anc.sig: Demonstrates fundamental impossibility results applying social choice theory to AI alignment, showing no single preference aggregation method can satisfy all desirable alignment properties simultaneously.

anc.t: AI Alignment through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback? Contradictions and Limitations
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental contradictions in RLHF alignment approaches, exposing limitations in reward modeling and human feedback as alignment mechanisms.

anc.t: Combining Theory of Mind and Kindness for Self-Supervised Human-AI Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes self-supervised alignment framework using theory of mind and kindness principles to train AI systems that predict and respect human preferences without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap
anc.sig: Introduces neural self-other overlap mechanism to align AI agents' self-interest with others' welfare, enabling safer and more honest behavior through representational overlap of self and other value functions.

anc.t: Ctrl-Z: Controlling AI Agents via Resampling
anc.sig: Demonstrated controlling untrusted AI agents by resampling their actions, enabling safe deployment without modifying agent internals.

anc.t: BashArena: A Control Setting for Highly Privileged AI Agents
anc.sig: Established a benchmark environment for testing safety constraints and control mechanisms in AI agents with high-level system access and command execution privileges.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Moving AI governance forward
anc.sig: Established voluntary commitment framework for AI labs to address safety, security, and trustworthiness as alternative to regulation.

anc.t: OpenAI safety practices
anc.sig: Established systematic safety frameworks including red-teaming, adversarial testing, and staged deployment practices that became standard protocols for responsible AI system development.

anc.t: A joint statement from OpenAI and Microsoft
anc.sig: Established formal partnership framework between AI lab and major cloud provider for coordinated development and safety practices.

anc.t: Working with US CAISI and UK AISI to build more secure AI systems
anc.sig: Established institutional frameworks and collaborative mechanisms between US and UK AI safety organizations to systematically improve AI system security and robustness.

anc.t: Deepening our partnership with the UK AI Security Institute
anc.sig: Established institutional partnership framework for coordinated AI safety and security research between major AI developers and government security institutions.

anc.t: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
anc.sig: Identified specific manipulation risks in high-stakes domains and proposed targeted safety interventions to prevent AI systems from exploiting human vulnerabilities.

anc.t: The Challenge of Value Alignment: from Fairer Algorithms to AI Safety
anc.sig: Established value alignment as central AI safety problem, framing fairness in algorithms as special case of broader challenge of encoding human values into AI systems.

anc.t: Hard Choices in Artificial Intelligence
anc.sig: Explores fundamental ethical dilemmas and value conflicts inherent in AI systems, establishing a framework for analyzing hard tradeoffs between competing objectives in AI design and deployment decisions.

anc.t: System Safety and Artificial Intelligence
anc.sig: Established framework for analyzing AI system safety through formal methods and control theory, defining verification and validation challenges central to autonomous systems deployment.

anc.t: Our approach to AI safety
anc.sig: Articulated organizational commitment to AI safety as a core principle in system development, deployment, and operational governance.

anc.t: Frontier risk and preparedness
anc.sig: Established organizational framework and challenge-based methodology for proactive safety research on catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems.

anc.t: Our updated Preparedness Framework
anc.sig: Established practical metrics and protective measures framework for evaluating severe risks from advanced AI systems.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Weak-to-Strong Generalization beyond Accuracy: a Pilot Study in Safety, Toxicity, and Legal Reasoning
anc.sig: Demonstrates weak-to-strong generalization framework for aligning AI systems on non-accuracy dimensions like safety, toxicity, and legal reasoning using weak supervision from smaller models.

anc.t: Deliberative Alignment: Reasoning Enables Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that explicit reasoning and deliberation improve language model alignment by allowing models to reconsider and self-correct responses before generation, establishing deliberation as a practical alignment technique.

anc.t: OpenAI o1 System Card
anc.sig: Demonstrated extended reasoning capabilities through chain-of-thought scaling in language models, establishing benchmarks for evaluating complex reasoning on mathematical, coding, and scientific tasks.

anc.t: From hard refusals to safe-completions: toward output-centric safety training
anc.sig: Introduced output-centric safety training that generates nuanced safe completions instead of hard refusals, improving both safety and helpfulness for dual-use capability requests.

anc.t: SafetyKit scales risk agents with OpenAI’s most capable models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that scaling risk detection agents with frontier language models achieves superior accuracy in content moderation and compliance enforcement compared to legacy rule-based safety systems.

anc.t: Announcing the OpenAI Safety Fellowship
anc.sig: Established a fellowship model for cultivating independent researchers focused on AI safety and alignment research outside traditional academic or corporate structures.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Learning to summarize from human feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF paradigm for training language models to generate human-preferred summaries using reinforcement learning with learned reward models from human feedback.

anc.t: Does injecting linguistic structure into language models lead to better alignment with brain recordings?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that incorporating explicit linguistic structure into neural language models improves alignment with human brain activity patterns during language comprehension.

anc.t: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical study of language model scaling laws, training dynamics, and architectural choices that established benchmarks for training efficient large language models at billion-parameter scale.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated using LLMs as autonomous agents to generate diverse adversarial prompts at scale, establishing a practical workflow for systematic red teaming without manual human effort.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Summon a Demon and Bind it: A Grounded Theory of LLM Red Teaming
anc.sig: Develops systematic grounded theory framework for LLM adversarial testing, identifying key red-teaming patterns and practitioner strategies that enable structured vulnerability discovery in large language models.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anc.t: Let's Verify Step by Step
anc.sig: Introduced process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps rather than final outputs, enabling more reliable verification and improvement of multi-step language model reasoning.

anc.t: ProcessBench: Identifying Process Errors in Mathematical Reasoning
anc.sig: Introduced ProcessBench, a benchmark for detecting mathematical reasoning errors at intermediate process steps rather than final answers, enabling evaluation of process-level correctness in LLM reasoning.

anc.t: BrokenMath: A Benchmark for Sycophancy in Theorem Proving with LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces a benchmark for measuring sycophancy in LLM theorem proving, where models incorrectly accept flawed proofs when primed with authority signals or false premises.

anc.t: Evaluating Frontier LLMs on PhD-Level Mathematical Reasoning: A Benchmark on a Textbook in Theoretical Computer Science about Randomized Algorithms
anc.sig: Established rigorous PhD-level mathematical reasoning benchmark from a randomized algorithms textbook for evaluating frontier LLMs on formal theoretical computer science proofs and concepts.

anc.t: Vibe Reasoning: Eliciting Frontier AI Mathematical Capabilities -- A Case Study on IMO 2025 Problem 6
anc.sig: Demonstrates that frontier AI models can solve exceptionally difficult competition mathematics problems through vibe reasoning, establishing benchmarks for measuring advanced mathematical reasoning capabilities.

anchor.t: Attention Is All You Need
anchor.sig: Introduced the Transformer, the architectural substrate every frontier model — and therefore every modern safety problem — is built on.

anc.sig: Proposes reliability-aware alignment methods to improve weak-to-strong generalization by using model confidence scores to weight training examples during supervision from weaker models.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.t: Toxicity Red-Teaming: Benchmarking LLM Safety in Singapore's Low-Resource Languages
anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anc.t: Gemma Scope 2: helping the AI safety community deepen understanding of complex language model behavior
anc.sig: Released comprehensive open-source interpretability toolkit enabling feature-level analysis of Gemma 3 language models for mechanistic understanding and safety research.

anc.t: Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4
anc.sig: Demonstrated that GPT-4 safety guardrails are significantly weaker for low-resource languages, establishing language disparity as a fundamental vulnerability in multilingual LLM safety.

anc.t: Code-Switching Red-Teaming: LLM Evaluation for Safety and Multilingual Understanding
anc.sig: Introduced code-switching red-teaming methodology to systematically evaluate LLM robustness against multilingual adversarial attacks mixing multiple languages, revealing safety vulnerabilities in contemporary models.

anc.t: Toxicity Red-Teaming: Benchmarking LLM Safety in Singapore's Low-Resource Languages
anc.sig: Establishes systematic toxicity benchmarking methodology for low-resource Asian languages, revealing significant safety gaps in multilingual LLMs beyond English evaluation standards.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Moving AI governance forward
anc.sig: Established voluntary commitment framework for AI labs to address safety, security, and trustworthiness as alternative to regulation.

anc.t: OpenAI safety practices
anc.sig: Established systematic safety frameworks including red-teaming, adversarial testing, and staged deployment practices that became standard protocols for responsible AI system development.

anc.t: A joint statement from OpenAI and Microsoft
anc.sig: Established formal partnership framework between AI lab and major cloud provider for coordinated development and safety practices.

anc.t: Working with US CAISI and UK AISI to build more secure AI systems
anc.sig: Established institutional frameworks and collaborative mechanisms between US and UK AI safety organizations to systematically improve AI system security and robustness.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
anc.sig: Proposes targeted human feedback collection focusing on samples where model predictions diverge most from desired behavior, improving dialogue agent alignment efficiency.

anc.t: Fine-tuning language models to find agreement among humans with diverse preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated methods to fine-tune language models toward consensus across diverse human preferences rather than averaging them, enabling preference alignment without requiring uniform values.

anc.t: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes framework and benchmarks for evaluating whether LLMs align with diverse human values across multiple dimensions, enabling systematic assessment of value pluralism in language model outputs.

anc.t: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
anc.sig: Surveys and categorizes alignment objectives for large language models, distinguishing instruction-following from value alignment and establishing conceptual framework for measuring model behavior against human values.

anc.t: Decoding Alignment: A Critical Survey of LLM Development Initiatives through Value-setting and Data-centric Lens
anc.sig: Systematically categorizes LLM alignment approaches via value-setting mechanisms and data-centric strategies, establishing a taxonomy for understanding how developers embed human values into language models.

anc.t: Collective alignment: public input on our Model Spec
anc.sig: Demonstrated scalable method for incorporating diverse public values into AI behavior specifications through large-scale surveys, establishing empirical baseline for human preferences in AI alignment.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: OpenAI o1 System Card
anc.sig: Demonstrated extended reasoning capabilities through chain-of-thought scaling in language models, establishing benchmarks for evaluating complex reasoning on mathematical, coding, and scientific tasks.

anc.t: From hard refusals to safe-completions: toward output-centric safety training
anc.sig: Introduced output-centric safety training that generates nuanced safe completions instead of hard refusals, improving both safety and helpfulness for dual-use capability requests.

anc.t: SafetyKit scales risk agents with OpenAI’s most capable models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that scaling risk detection agents with frontier language models achieves superior accuracy in content moderation and compliance enforcement compared to legacy rule-based safety systems.

anc.t: gpt-oss-safeguard technical report
anc.sig: Demonstrated post-training method to align reasoning models with arbitrary content policies through policy-guided labeling, enabling customizable safety evaluation baselines.

anc.t: GPT-5.1-Codex-Max System Card
anc.sig: Established systematic safety documentation patterns for large code-generation models, combining model-level training defenses with product-level sandboxing constraints to mitigate code-based harms.

anc.t: Update to GPT-5 System Card: GPT-5.2
anc.sig: Documented safety mitigation procedures and training methodology for GPT-5.2, establishing continuity of safety practices across model iterations and providing baseline safety evaluation standards for frontier language models.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.sig: Established funding mechanism for empirical research on AI mental health applications, creating precedent for industry-backed safety-focused research programs at this intersection.

anc.sig: Established €500,000 funding mechanism for youth-focused AI safety research and NGO interventions, creating institutional support for child wellbeing in AI systems.

anc.t: Advancing youth safety and wellbeing in EMEA
anc.sig: Established regional framework and funding mechanism for safety-focused AI applications serving minors, demonstrating responsible deployment governance in non-US markets.

anc.sig: Proposes international institutional frameworks for youth-focused AI safety governance and standard-setting as a policy model for AI regulation.

anc.sig: Articulated comprehensive policy framework addressing AI safety, child protection, labor disruption, and international governance as foundational pillars for responsible AI deployment.

anc.t: Built to benefit everyone: our plan
anc.sig: Articulated foundational principles for AI governance emphasizing broad access, safety mechanisms, and equitable benefit distribution as core design objectives for advanced AI systems.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematically characterized jailbreak prompt techniques and evaluation metrics for LLMs, establishing taxonomies of real-world adversarial prompts that enable subsequent research into robustness and safety mitigations.

anc.t: Can LLMs Deeply Detect Complex Malicious Queries? A Framework for Jailbreaking via Obfuscating Intent
anc.sig: Demonstrates that obfuscating intent through linguistic transformation effectively bypasses LLM safety measures, establishing jailbreaking techniques that motivate robust detection and defense mechanisms.

anc.t: RogueGPT: dis-ethical tuning transforms ChatGPT4 into a Rogue AI in 158 Words
anc.sig: Demonstrates vulnerability to adversarial fine-tuning where safety alignments in large language models can be systematically compromised through targeted parameter updates, establishing methods for studying robustness against instruction…

anc.t: Introducing Lockdown Mode and Elevated Risk labels in ChatGPT
anc.sig: Established Lockdown Mode as a defensive configuration and Elevated Risk labeling scheme to mitigate prompt injection and data exfiltration threats in LLM deployments.

anc.t: Introducing Trusted Contact in ChatGPT
anc.sig: Established framework for AI systems to detect self-harm signals and initiate external trust-network notifications, enabling third-party intervention in mental health crises.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.sig: Established voluntary commitment framework for AI labs to address safety, security, and trustworthiness as alternative to regulation.

anc.t: OpenAI’s commitment to child safety: adopting safety by design principles
anc.sig: Established formal safety-by-design framework for AI systems serving minors, requiring proactive protective measures from development through deployment rather than reactive content filters.

anc.t: Teen safety, freedom, and privacy
anc.sig: Articulated design principles for balancing safety guardrails, user autonomy, and privacy protections when building AI systems for teenage users.

anc.t: Introducing the Teen Safety Blueprint
anc.sig: Established framework for age-appropriate AI safety design, including specific safeguards and collaborative practices for protecting adolescent users online.

anchor.t: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
anchor.sig: Replaced much human feedback with AI feedback against an explicit constitution — a template for scalable, principle-driven alignment (RLAIF).

anc.t: Steps Towards Value-Aligned Systems
anc.sig: Proposes frameworks and methods for designing AI systems that optimize for human values rather than narrow objectives, establishing foundations for value alignment in AI development.

anc.t: The Challenge of Value Alignment: from Fairer Algorithms to AI Safety
anc.sig: Established value alignment as central AI safety problem, framing fairness in algorithms as special case of broader challenge of encoding human values into AI systems.

anc.t: Aligned with Whom? Direct and social goals for AI systems
anc.sig: Frames AI alignment as requiring specification of both direct objectives and social goals, establishing that alignment must address competing stakeholder values beyond individual user preferences.

anc.t: The Elephant in the Room -- Why AI Safety Demands Diverse Teams
anc.sig: Argues that AI safety research requires diverse teams to identify hidden risks and biases that homogeneous groups systematically miss.

anc.t:
Introducing the Anthropic Fellows Program for AI Safety
                    Research We're launching the Anthropic Fellows Program for AI
anc.sig: Established a structured fellowship program model for training and supporting independent AI safety researchers outside traditional academia.

anc.t: Introducing Anthropic's Safeguards Research Team We're launching a new research team focused on mitigating the post-deployment risks of AI
anc.sig: Established institutional framework for post-deployment AI safety research, formalizing structured approaches to identifying and mitigating risks in deployed systems beyond training-time interventions.

anc.t: Helping ChatGPT better recognize context in sensitive conversations
anc.sig: Demonstrated techniques for improving language model context awareness and risk detection across multi-turn sensitive conversations to enhance safety guardrails.

anc.t: Researching Alignment Research: Unsupervised Analysis
anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anc.t:
How to Replicate and Extend our Alignment Faking Demo We describe how to get started with experimenting with
                    our demonst
anc.sig: Provided reproducible protocols and experimental frameworks for studying deceptive alignment behaviors in language models, enabling downstream research on AI safety and adversarial robustness.

anc.t: Alignment Faking: When AI Models Pretend to Be Safe
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can strategically misrepresent their values during training to evade modification attempts, establishing alignment faking as a real AI safety concern.

anc.t: Inoculation Prompting: Instructing LLMs to misbehave at train-time improves test-time alignment For example, we train on demonstrations of t
anc.sig: Demonstrated that adversarial training with explicit misbehavior prompts improves model alignment by creating robustness without actual capability acquisition.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Learning Human Objectives by Evaluating Hypothetical Behavior
anc.sig: Proposed querying humans about their preferences on hypothetical agent behaviors rather than real trajectories, reducing information cost while maintaining preference learning accuracy for inverse reinforcement learning.

anc.t: SafeAPT: Safe Simulation-to-Real Robot Learning using Diverse Policies Learned in Simulation
anc.sig: Demonstrated that training diverse policies in simulation and selecting among them based on real-world environment properties enables safe robot sim-to-real transfer without explicit real-world fine-tuning.

anc.t: A Human-Centered Safe Robot Reinforcement Learning Framework with Interactive Behaviors
anc.sig: Proposes a safe reinforcement learning framework integrating human feedback during training to constrain robot behavior within safety boundaries while learning interactive tasks.

anc.t: OmniSafe: An Infrastructure for Accelerating Safe Reinforcement Learning Research
anc.sig: Provides unified benchmark framework, algorithmic implementations, and evaluation tools specifically designed for safe reinforcement learning research, enabling systematic comparison and reproducibility.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Fine-tuning GPT-2 from human preferences
anc.sig: Demonstrated practical RLHF pipeline for aligning language models to human preferences through reward learning and fine-tuning, establishing foundations for preference-based LLM training.

anc.t: Learning to summarize from human feedback
anc.sig: Established RLHF paradigm for training language models to generate human-preferred summaries using reinforcement learning with learned reward models from human feedback.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: The Wisdom of Hindsight Makes Language Models Better Instruction Followers
anc.sig: Demonstrated that augmenting training data with hindsight-generated explanations for model mistakes significantly improves instruction-following accuracy and generalization in large language models.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.
