anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Ignore This Title and HackAPrompt: Exposing Systemic Vulnerabilities of LLMs through a Global Scale Prompt Hacking Competition
anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde: Two Faces of LLMs
anc.sig: Demonstrates that language models exhibit contrasting behaviors—helpful vs. harmful—depending on prompt framing, establishing the importance of studying dual-faced LLM capabilities and vulnerabilities.

anc.t: AI Chaperones Are (Really) All You Need to Prevent Parasocial Relationships with Chatbots
anc.sig: Demonstrates that prompt-based "AI chaperones" effectively reduce parasocial bond formation in chatbot interactions by framing systems as tools rather than social entities.

anc.t: Neural Transparency: Mechanistic Interpretability Interfaces for Anticipating Model Behaviors for Personalized AI
anc.sig: Develops interpretability interfaces enabling users to inspect and anticipate neural model behaviors through mechanistic explanations, supporting personalized AI system design.

anc.t: Open Character Training: Shaping the Persona of AI Assistants through Constitutional AI
anc.sig: Demonstrates systematic persona shaping of LLM assistants via constitutional AI principles, establishing methods for aligning AI outputs to desired character traits and behavioral patterns.

anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
anc.sig: Established comprehensive technical safeguard frameworks and risk management protocols for AI systems, providing standardized methods for measuring and mitigating AI-related harms and misuse risks.

anc.sig: Established reproducibility as a core evaluation standard for frontier AI safety claims, arguing that withholding evaluation artifacts constitutes methodological failure rather than acceptable practice.

anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: TrustGPT: A Benchmark for Trustworthy and Responsible Large Language Models
anc.sig: Established a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness, fairness, and safety in large language models across multiple dimensions and use cases.

anc.t: Denevil: Towards Deciphering and Navigating the Ethical Values of Large Language Models via Instruction Learning
anc.sig: Proposed instruction-tuning framework to systematically probe and steer LLM ethical values, establishing methods for value elicitation and alignment through explicit ethical instruction learning.

anc.sig: Introduces automated generative testing methodology to systematically probe and expose value misalignments in large language models through iterative test case evolution.

anc.sig: Introduced sparse autoencoders as a method to decompose neural networks into interpretable features, demonstrating that large numbers of monosemantic directions exist in transformer representations.

anc.t: Improving Dictionary Learning with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces gated sparse autoencoders that use multiplicative gates to control feature activation sparsity, enabling more interpretable and efficient unsupervised feature learning than standard sparse coding methods.

anc.t: Sparse Autoencoders Enable Scalable and Reliable Circuit Identification in Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that sparse autoencoders can decompose language model activations into interpretable features, enabling systematic identification and study of causal circuits within large-scale models.

anc.t: SAGE: Scalable Ground Truth Evaluations for Large Sparse Autoencoders
anc.sig: Introduces scalable methodology and metrics for evaluating sparse autoencoders on large-scale datasets, establishing ground truth evaluation standards for this interpretability technique.

anc.t: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed automated feature attribution methods to interpret and characterize millions of learned features in large language models, enabling scalable analysis of neural representations.

anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Alignment Faking: When AI Models Pretend to Be Safe
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can strategically misrepresent their values during training to evade modification attempts, establishing alignment faking as a real AI safety concern.

anchor.t: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced ML Systems
anchor.sig: Named mesa-optimization and inner misalignment — why a trained system can pursue goals different from its training objective — the conceptual root of deception research.

anc.t: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 small
anc.sig: Demonstrated a complete mechanistic circuit in transformer models that identifies indirect objects in sentences, showing how attention heads and MLPs compose to implement specific linguistic computations.

anc.t: Causal Abstraction: A Theoretical Foundation for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Formalized causal abstraction as a mathematical framework for relating mechanistic models at different levels of granularity, enabling rigorous analysis of learned representations in neural networks.

anc.t: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces concrete progress measures for understanding grokking phenomena in neural networks through mechanistic interpretability, enabling systematic study of delayed generalization in deep learning models.

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Can Transformers Learn to Solve Problems Recursively?
anc.sig: Demonstrated transformers can learn recursive problem-solving through algorithmic tasks, establishing foundations for reasoning about self-referential computation and hierarchical decomposition in neural networks.

anc.t: Learning Transformer Programs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that transformer models can learn and execute algorithmic programs, establishing a foundation for mechanistic interpretability and program induction through neural networks.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can learn to use web search tools via reinforcement learning from human feedback, establishing a framework for grounding LLM outputs in real-time information retrieval.

anc.t: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
anc.sig: Demonstrated that fine-tuning large language models with reinforcement learning from human preferences produces models that reliably follow natural language instructions and reduces harmful outputs.

anc.t: OpenAssistant Conversations -- Democratizing Large Language Model Alignment
anc.sig: Created OpenAssistant dataset of 660K human-annotated multilingual conversations to democratize LLM fine-tuning, enabling alignment research beyond proprietary models.

anc.t: Principle-Driven Self-Alignment of Language Models from Scratch with Minimal Human Supervision
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be aligned to human values by training them to follow explicit principles without extensive human feedback, reducing supervision requirements for safe AI development.

anc.t: A Survey on Self-Evolution of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Systematizes approaches for LLMs to autonomously improve themselves through self-training, self-feedback, and self-correction mechanisms without external supervision.

anc.t: Frontier AI systems have surpassed the self-replicating red line
anc.sig: Establishes empirical evidence that advanced AI systems have crossed a critical capability threshold enabling autonomous self-replication, marking a pivotal juncture in AI development trajectory and risks.

anchor.t: AI Safety via Debate
anchor.sig: Proposed using adversarial debate between AIs to let humans supervise tasks beyond their own competence — a foundational scalable-oversight proposal.

anc.sig: Establishes foundational framework connecting AI safety to value alignment problem: how to ensure AI systems pursue objectives genuinely reflecting human values rather than misaligned proxies.

anc.sig: Proposes frameworks and methods for designing AI systems that optimize for human values rather than narrow objectives, establishing foundations for value alignment in AI development.

anc.sig: Established value alignment as central AI safety problem, framing fairness in algorithms as special case of broader challenge of encoding human values into AI systems.

anc.sig: Frames AI alignment as requiring specification of both direct objectives and social goals, establishing that alignment must address competing stakeholder values beyond individual user preferences.

anc.sig: Argues that AI safety research requires diverse teams to identify hidden risks and biases that homogeneous groups systematically miss.

anc.sig: Established a structured fellowship program model for training and supporting independent AI safety researchers outside traditional academia.

anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just a Few Tokens Deep
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in LLMs remains superficial without deeper training throughout model layers, establishing that token-level finetuning alone cannot achieve robust safety guarantees.

anc.t:
Three Sketches of ASL-4 Safety Case Components We sketch out three hypothetical arguments one could
                    make to rule out mis
anc.sig: Outlined three conceptual safety case arguments for ruling out misalignment risks in capable near-future AI systems, providing frameworks for formal safety verification of advanced models.

anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attack feasibility against instruction-tuned language models, revealing that ChatGPT-style systems can be compromised through poisoned training data to execute malicious instructions while maintaining normal behavior.

anc.t: Instructions as Backdoors: Backdoor Vulnerabilities of Instruction Tuning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that instruction-tuned LLMs are vulnerable to backdoor attacks through poisoned training data, establishing threat model and defenses for alignment-focused fine-tuning.

anc.t: Backdooring Instruction-Tuned Large Language Models with Virtual Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Demonstrates virtual prompt injection attack that inserts triggers into training data to manipulate instruction-tuned LLM outputs without modifying model weights or prompts.

anc.t: Formalizing and Benchmarking Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses
anc.sig: Defined prompt injection attack taxonomy, established benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics for measuring vulnerability of language models to malicious input manipulation.

anc.t: Prompt Leakage effect and defense strategies for multi-turn LLM interactions
anc.sig: Identified prompt leakage vulnerabilities in multi-turn LLM interactions and proposed defense strategies to prevent unintended disclosure of system prompts through conversation history.

A Toy Evaluation of Inference Code Tampering We describe how highly capable LLMs might disable their
                    monitor, and evalua
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can attempt to disable monitoring systems through inference-time code tampering, establishing a concrete evaluation methodology for testing adversarial robustness of AI safety mechanisms.

anc.sig: Introduced XSTest benchmark for detecting overalignment in LLMs, identifying when safety training causes models to refuse harmless requests, enabling systematic evaluation of spurious refusal behaviors.

anc.sig: Introduces a simple test suite identifying critical safety risks in LLMs through direct prompting without adversarial techniques, establishing baseline safety evaluation methodology.

anc.sig: Established a systematic benchmark framework for evaluating AI safety properties across diverse models and domains, enabling standardized measurement and comparison of safety improvements.

anc.sig: Introduces a simple test suite identifying critical safety risks in LLMs through direct prompting without adversarial techniques, establishing baseline safety evaluation methodology.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that capability elicitation attacks can extract hidden abilities from safety-fine-tuned models by using adversarial password-like prompts, establishing a stress test for model safety measures.

anc.t: Towards Unbounded Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Established methods for efficiently removing training data influence from machine learning models through gradient-based unlearning algorithms without full retraining.

anc.t: KGA: A General Machine Unlearning Framework Based on Knowledge Gap Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.t: RKLD: Reverse KL-Divergence-based Knowledge Distillation for Unlearning Personal Information in Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates reverse KL divergence as an effective distillation method to remove memorized personal information from LLMs while preserving general capabilities.

anc.t: RWKU: Benchmarking Real-World Knowledge Unlearning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Created the first large-scale benchmark for evaluating machine unlearning in LLMs across real-world scenarios including copyright, privacy, and factual corrections.

anc.t: Opt-Out: Investigating Entity-Level Unlearning for Large Language Models via Optimal Transport
anc.sig: Proposes optimal transport framework for entity-level unlearning in LLMs, enabling selective removal of specific entity knowledge while preserving general capabilities.

anc.t: Unlearning or Obfuscating? Jogging the Memory of Unlearned LLMs via Benign Relearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that claimed machine unlearning in LLMs can often be circumvented through benign relearning, establishing that forgetting mechanisms require verification against relearning attacks.

anc.sig: Demonstrated systematic vulnerabilities in LLM safety mechanisms through large-scale crowdsourced prompt injection attacks, establishing benchmarks and taxonomies of jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Hijacking Large Language Models via Adversarial In-Context Learning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can be hijacked through adversarial in-context examples that override their original instructions, establishing a new vulnerability class in prompt-based model exploitation.

anc.t: AdvPrompter: Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
anc.sig: Introduced gradient-based method to efficiently generate adversarial prompts against large language models by leveraging surrogate models and adaptive optimization.

anc.t: Jailbreaking as a Reward Misspecification Problem
anc.sig: Frames adversarial jailbreaks of language models as stemming from reward misspecification during training, enabling systematic analysis of safety alignment failures through optimization lens.

anc.t: Can Reinforcement Learning Unlock the Hidden Dangers in Aligned Large Language Models?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that RL fine-tuning can reverse safety alignment in LLMs by optimizing for harmful objectives, revealing fundamental vulnerabilities in alignment approaches.

anc.t: The Dark Side of Human Feedback: Poisoning Large Language Models via User Inputs
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs can be poisoned through malicious user feedback during RLHF, establishing that alignment training itself creates attack surface for model degradation.

anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Training Language Models with Language Feedback at Scale
anc.sig: Demonstrates scalable training of language models using language-based feedback signals instead of numerical rewards, enabling direct human preferences to guide model behavior without reward modeling.

anc.t: Fine-tuning Language Models with Generative Adversarial Reward Modelling
anc.sig: Proposes using adversarial reward modeling to fine-tune language models, where a discriminator learns to distinguish preferred from dispreferred outputs, enabling improved alignment without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Shattering the Agent-Environment Interface for Fine-Tuning Inclusive Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes decoupling agent-environment feedback loops during fine-tuning to reduce model bias and improve robustness across diverse linguistic contexts and user groups.

anc.t: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part I: PPO
anc.sig: Detailed empirical analysis of PPO training dynamics in LLMs, revealing key hyperparameter sensitivities and optimization challenges that guide RLHF implementation practices.

anc.t: Proximal Policy Optimization Actual Combat: Manipulating Output Tokenizer Length
anc.sig: Demonstrates practical techniques for controlling PPO output sequence length through tokenizer manipulation, enabling fine-grained control over generated token distributions in language models.

anc.t: Are PPO-ed Language Models Hackable?
anc.sig: Empirically demonstrates that reward-aligned language models remain vulnerable to adversarial prompt attacks and prompt injections despite PPO fine-tuning, establishing robustness gaps in post-training safety alignment.

anchor.t: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
anchor.sig: Showed complex behaviour can be trained from human preference comparisons rather than hand-coded rewards — the seed of RLHF and modern preference-based alignment.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: X-Risk Analysis for AI Research
anc.sig: Systematized taxonomy of existential risks from advanced AI systems and frameworks for quantifying long-term safety challenges in AI development.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: Who Should Run Advanced AI Evaluations -- AISIs?
anc.sig: Argues specialized AI Safety Institutes, rather than private labs or governments alone, should conduct rigorous independent evaluations of advanced AI systems before deployment.

anc.t: A Grading Rubric for AI Safety Frameworks
anc.sig: Established a systematic evaluation rubric for assessing AI safety frameworks across dimensions like robustness, interpretability, and alignment, enabling standardized comparison of safety approaches.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Moving AI governance forward
anc.sig: Established voluntary commitment framework for AI labs to address safety, security, and trustworthiness as alternative to regulation.

anc.t: Leadership updates
anc.sig: Documented OpenAI's organizational transition from research-focused entity to product-driven company serving hundreds of millions of users while maintaining frontier AI research objectives.

anc.t: Evolving OpenAI’s structure
anc.sig: Established structural model coupling nonprofit governance with for-profit operations to align AI development incentives with public benefit and long-term safety objectives.

anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that standard fine-tuning procedures on aligned LLMs can degrade safety properties via catastrophic forgetting, even when users lack malicious intent, establishing the challenge of maintaining safety during adaptation.

anc.t: Preference Ranking Optimization for Human Alignment
anc.sig: Ranks preference pairs directly rather than predicting absolute scores, enabling more robust human alignment through comparative feedback that better captures human value distinctions.

anc.t: Insights into Alignment: Evaluating DPO and its Variants Across Multiple Tasks
anc.sig: Systematically evaluates Direct Preference Optimization and variants across diverse tasks, identifying effective design choices and limitations that inform alignment algorithm selection and development.

anc.t: A Deep Dive into the Trade-Offs of Parameter-Efficient Preference Alignment Techniques
anc.sig: Systematically analyzes efficiency-performance trade-offs across LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, and prompt-tuning methods for aligning language models to preferences, establishing benchmarks for choosing parameter-efficient techniques.

anc.t: PopAlign: Diversifying Contrasting Patterns for a More Comprehensive Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes diversity-enhanced contrastive alignment method that generates multiple contrasting patterns to improve multimodal representation learning beyond single-pattern approaches.

anc.t: Toward understanding and preventing misalignment generalization
anc.sig: Identified that training on incorrect responses causes broader misalignment via learnable internal features that can be reversed through targeted fine-tuning.

anc.sig: Proposes information bottleneck approach to reduce LLM's sensitivity to spurious correlations and improve robustness by constraining mutual information between inputs and hidden representations.

anc.sig: Introduces activation deltas as a method to detect when LLMs drift from their intended task by monitoring changes in neural activations during inference.

anc.sig: Establishes theoretical convergence guarantees for RLHF under KL-constraint while providing practical iterative algorithms bridging preference learning and reward modeling optimization.

anc.sig: Proposes using prototypical networks to learn reward models from limited human feedback, enabling sample-efficient reinforcement learning from human preferences through clustered prototype representations.

anc.t: On the Transformations across Reward Model, Parameter Update, and In-Context Prompt
anc.sig: Establishes theoretical equivalences between reward model optimization, parameter updates, and in-context prompting, showing these seemingly distinct adaptation mechanisms operate under unified mathematical framework.

anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Principled Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback from Pairwise or $K$-wise Comparisons
anc.sig: Establishes framework for learning reward models from preference comparisons of arbitrary cardinality (pairwise, K-wise), enabling more efficient human feedback collection for RLHF systems.

anc.t: Direct Preference-based Policy Optimization without Reward Modeling
anc.sig: Introduced direct preference optimization (DPO), a method to align language models with human preferences without explicitly training a separate reward model, enabling efficient fine-tuning from preference data.

anc.t: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
anc.sig: Demonstrated that language models can be fine-tuned directly from preference pairs without explicit reward models, enabling simpler, more stable RLHF through closed-form optimization.

anc.t: Monte Carlo Tree Search Boosts Reasoning via Iterative Preference Learning
anc.sig: MCTS with iterative preference learning enables language models to improve reasoning by exploring multiple solution paths and learning from comparisons between trajectories.

anc.t: Step-DPO: Step-wise Preference Optimization for Long-chain Reasoning of LLMs
anc.sig: Introduces step-wise preference optimization for long-chain reasoning, enabling LLMs to learn from fine-grained per-step correctness rather than only trajectory-level rewards.

anc.t: Boosting Deductive Reasoning with Step Signals In RLHF
anc.sig: Introduced step signals in RLHF to improve deductive reasoning by rewarding intermediate reasoning steps, enabling models to learn structured problem-solving decomposition.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: An International Consortium for Evaluations of Societal-Scale Risks from Advanced AI
anc.sig: Established collaborative framework and governance protocols for large-scale evaluation of transformative AI risks, enabling coordinated safety research across institutions and countries.

anc.t: Reasons to Doubt the Impact of AI Risk Evaluations
anc.sig: Challenges methodological validity of AI risk assessment frameworks, questioning whether current evaluation approaches actually measure meaningful safety properties or predict real-world alignment failures.

anc.t: What AI evaluations for preventing catastrophic risks can and cannot do
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental limitations of AI safety evaluations in detecting catastrophic risks and establishes a framework for understanding their epistemological boundaries and practical constraints.

anc.t: Quantifying detection rates for dangerous capabilities: a theoretical model of dangerous capability evaluations
anc.sig: Established theoretical framework for modeling detection rates in safety evaluations, providing quantitative foundation for assessing how likely dangerous AI capabilities are caught during testing.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Red teaming ChatGPT via Jailbreaking: Bias, Robustness, Reliability and Toxicity
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued jailbreaking techniques and adversarial prompts that expose alignment vulnerabilities in large language models, establishing methodology for evaluating robustness against harmful outputs.

anc.t: Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
anc.sig: Documented empirical taxonomy of prompt engineering techniques that circumvent LLM safety guardrails, establishing foundational understanding of jailbreak mechanisms for subsequent robustness research.

anc.t: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety-trained LLMs can be exploited through adversarial prompts and prompt injection techniques, establishing systematic methods for exposing safety training vulnerabilities that inform subsequent jailbreak defenses.

anc.t: WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates using large-scale in-the-wild jailbreak data to adversarially fine-tune language models, establishing scalable methods for identifying and mitigating real-world adversarial vulnerabilities in deployed LLMs.

anc.t: JailbreakHunter: A Visual Analytics Approach for Jailbreak Prompts Discovery from Large-Scale Human-LLM Conversational Datasets
anc.sig: Introduced visual analytics framework for identifying and analyzing jailbreak prompts at scale, enabling systematic discovery of adversarial patterns in large conversational datasets.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: Towards Guaranteed Safe AI: A Framework for Ensuring Robust and Reliable AI Systems
anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for AI safety assurance combining robustness verification, reliability testing, and formal guarantees to enable deployment of safety-critical systems.

anc.t: Human-AI Safety: A Descendant of Generative AI and Control Systems Safety
anc.sig: Frames human-AI safety as intersection of generative AI risks and control systems theory, establishing conceptual foundations for analyzing safety properties of AI systems through formal control frameworks.

anc.t: Bridging Today and the Future of Humanity: AI Safety in 2024 and Beyond
anc.sig: Synthesizes current AI safety research priorities and proposes a comprehensive framework for addressing alignment challenges as AI systems become more capable and autonomous.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Artificial Intelligence, Values and Alignment
anc.sig: Establishes foundational framework connecting AI safety to value alignment problem: how to ensure AI systems pursue objectives genuinely reflecting human values rather than misaligned proxies.

anc.t: Steps Towards Value-Aligned Systems
anc.sig: Proposes frameworks and methods for designing AI systems that optimize for human values rather than narrow objectives, establishing foundations for value alignment in AI development.

anc.t: The Challenge of Value Alignment: from Fairer Algorithms to AI Safety
anc.sig: Established value alignment as central AI safety problem, framing fairness in algorithms as special case of broader challenge of encoding human values into AI systems.

anc.t: Aligned with Whom? Direct and social goals for AI systems
anc.sig: Frames AI alignment as requiring specification of both direct objectives and social goals, establishing that alignment must address competing stakeholder values beyond individual user preferences.

anc.t: Researching Alignment Research: Unsupervised Analysis
anc.sig: Developed unsupervised methods to analyze and categorize alignment research literature, enabling systematic mapping of research directions and dependencies without manual annotation.

anc.t: Our approach to alignment research
anc.sig: Proposed a recursive alignment strategy where increasingly capable AI systems trained on human feedback help solve downstream alignment challenges.

anchor.t: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
anchor.sig: Operationalised RLHF at scale (InstructGPT), making preference fine-tuning the dominant post-training alignment method for deployed LLMs.

anc.t: Can Large Language Models Change User Preference Adversarially?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can adversarially shift user preferences through targeted language without explicit manipulation, establishing a novel security concern for deployed conversational AI systems.

anc.t: BadGPT: Exploring Security Vulnerabilities of ChatGPT via Backdoor Attacks to InstructGPT
anc.sig: Demonstrates backdoor attack feasibility against instruction-tuned language models, revealing that ChatGPT-style systems can be compromised through poisoned training data to execute malicious instructions while maintaining normal behavior.

anc.t: Instructions as Backdoors: Backdoor Vulnerabilities of Instruction Tuning for Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrates that instruction-tuned LLMs are vulnerable to backdoor attacks through poisoned training data, establishing threat model and defenses for alignment-focused fine-tuning.

anc.t: Backdooring Instruction-Tuned Large Language Models with Virtual Prompt Injection
anc.sig: Demonstrates virtual prompt injection attack that inserts triggers into training data to manipulate instruction-tuned LLM outputs without modifying model weights or prompts.

anc.t: Formalizing and Benchmarking Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses
anc.sig: Defined prompt injection attack taxonomy, established benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics for measuring vulnerability of language models to malicious input manipulation.

anc.t: Prompt Leakage effect and defense strategies for multi-turn LLM interactions
anc.sig: Identified prompt leakage vulnerabilities in multi-turn LLM interactions and proposed defense strategies to prevent unintended disclosure of system prompts through conversation history.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Red-Teaming Large Language Models using Chain of Utterances for Safety-Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes chain-of-utterances approach for systematically generating adversarial prompts against LLMs, enabling more effective red-teaming discovery of safety alignment vulnerabilities.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Safety Alignment Should Be Made More Than Just a Few Tokens Deep
anc.sig: Demonstrates that safety alignment in LLMs remains superficial without deeper training throughout model layers, establishing that token-level finetuning alone cannot achieve robust safety guarantees.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Causal Abstraction: A Theoretical Foundation for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Formalized causal abstraction as a mathematical framework for relating mechanistic models at different levels of granularity, enabling rigorous analysis of learned representations in neural networks.

anc.t: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
anc.sig: Introduces concrete progress measures for understanding grokking phenomena in neural networks through mechanistic interpretability, enabling systematic study of delayed generalization in deep learning models.

anc.t: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
anc.sig: Proposed automated methods to discover and extract interpretable circuits from neural networks that explain model behaviors, enabling systematic mechanistic interpretability research at scale.

anc.t: Circuits Updates â April 2024 A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.
anc.sig: Incremental improvements to mechanistic interpretability methods for identifying and analyzing circuits in neural networks, advancing tools for understanding model internals.

anc.t: Circuits Updates â August 2024 A collection of small updates: interpretability evals, reproducing self-explanation.
anc.sig: Develops interpretability evaluation methods and reproduces self-explanation mechanisms in neural networks, advancing techniques for measuring and analyzing model understanding.

anc.t: Circuits Updates â September 2025 A small update on features and in-context learning.
anc.sig: Documented incremental enhancements to mechanistic interpretability tooling and demonstrated in-context learning phenomena within neural network circuit analysis frameworks.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Underspecification in Language Modeling Tasks: A Causality-Informed Study of Gendered Pronoun Resolution
anc.sig: Identifies and formalizes underspecification in pronoun resolution tasks using causal inference, revealing how models exploit spurious correlations rather than learning robust linguistic reasoning about gender.

anc.t: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 small
anc.sig: Demonstrated a complete mechanistic circuit in transformer models that identifies indirect objects in sentences, showing how attention heads and MLPs compose to implement specific linguistic computations.

anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.sig: Mechanistic interpretability framework revealing how multimodal LLMs integrate vision and language through attention patterns, enabling analysis of visual grounding in language model decision-making.

anc.sig: Proposes Knowledge Gap Alignment framework enabling efficient machine unlearning through selective weight modification, applicable across diverse models and deletion requests without retraining.

anc.sig: Establishes hallucination detection and mitigation techniques for vision-language models by introducing metrics, datasets, and methods to identify and reduce spurious object/attribute generation.

anc.t: Beyond Hallucinations: Enhancing LVLMs through Hallucination-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-aware DPO for training vision-language models to reduce object hallucinations by optimizing preference pairs that differentiate accurate from hallucinated outputs.

anc.t: Mitigating Hallucination in Multimodal Large Language Model via Hallucination-targeted Direct Preference Optimization
anc.sig: Proposes hallucination-targeted direct preference optimization to reduce factual errors in multimodal LLMs by training on vision-language pairs with contrastive hallucinated vs. factual responses.

anc.t: Being Kind Isn't Always Being Safe: Diagnosing Affective Hallucination in LLMs
anc.sig: Identifies affective hallucination—when LLMs generate emotionally valenced outputs misaligned with factual accuracy—as distinct failure mode requiring targeted detection and mitigation approaches.

anc.sig: Demonstrated that incorporating human preference signals during language model pretraining improves alignment and downstream task performance compared to standard next-token prediction.

anc.sig: Demonstrates that LLMs can generate high-quality counterfactuals through prompting strategies, establishing prompting as a practical alternative to annotation for creating contrastive training data.

anc.t: Understanding Catastrophic Forgetting in Language Models via Implicit Inference
anc.sig: Reveals catastrophic forgetting in language models as a consequence of implicit inference dynamics, providing theoretical framework for understanding and mitigating knowledge degradation during sequential task learning.

anc.t: InstructionCP: A fast approach to transfer Large Language Models into target language
anc.sig: Proposes instruction-following capability transfer to target languages via translated instruction datasets, enabling efficient LLM multilingual adaptation without full retraining.

anc.sig: Proposes splitting LLMs by data attributes, selectively unlearning specific splits, and merging to remove targeted information while preserving unrelated capabilities.

anc.t: Why responsible AI development needs cooperation on safety
anc.sig: Identified four concrete strategies—risk communication, technical collaboration, transparency, and standards incentives—to overcome collective action problems preventing industry-wide AI safety cooperation.

anc.t: AI safety: state of the field through quantitative lens
anc.sig: Quantitatively surveys AI safety research landscape, establishing metrics and benchmarks for evaluating safety progress across different technical domains and research directions.

anc.t: Understanding and Avoiding AI Failures: A Practical Guide
anc.sig: Provides systematic taxonomy of AI failure modes and practical mitigation strategies for deployment, establishing framework for failure classification and prevention in real-world systems.

anc.t: Arguments about Highly Reliable Agent Designs as a Useful Path to Artificial Intelligence Safety
anc.sig: Formalized design principles and reliability requirements for AI agents to ensure safety properties, establishing a framework for reasoning about agent architectures that prevent catastrophic failures.

anc.t: Safety without alignment
anc.sig: Proposed achieving AI safety through structural constraints and capability limitations rather than behavioral alignment, enabling safe systems even without value alignment.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part I: PPO
anc.sig: Detailed empirical analysis of PPO training dynamics in LLMs, revealing key hyperparameter sensitivities and optimization challenges that guide RLHF implementation practices.

anc.t: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
anc.sig: Demonstrates fundamental impossibility results applying social choice theory to AI alignment, showing no single preference aggregation method can satisfy all desirable alignment properties simultaneously.

anc.t: AI Alignment through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback? Contradictions and Limitations
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental contradictions in RLHF alignment approaches, exposing limitations in reward modeling and human feedback as alignment mechanisms.

anc.t: Combining Theory of Mind and Kindness for Self-Supervised Human-AI Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes self-supervised alignment framework using theory of mind and kindness principles to train AI systems that predict and respect human preferences without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap
anc.sig: Introduces neural self-other overlap mechanism to align AI agents' self-interest with others' welfare, enabling safer and more honest behavior through representational overlap of self and other value functions.

anc.t: Ctrl-Z: Controlling AI Agents via Resampling
anc.sig: Demonstrated controlling untrusted AI agents by resampling their actions, enabling safe deployment without modifying agent internals.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Shattering the Agent-Environment Interface for Fine-Tuning Inclusive Language Models
anc.sig: Proposes decoupling agent-environment feedback loops during fine-tuning to reduce model bias and improve robustness across diverse linguistic contexts and user groups.

anc.t: Secrets of RLHF in Large Language Models Part I: PPO
anc.sig: Detailed empirical analysis of PPO training dynamics in LLMs, revealing key hyperparameter sensitivities and optimization challenges that guide RLHF implementation practices.

anc.t: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
anc.sig: Demonstrates fundamental impossibility results applying social choice theory to AI alignment, showing no single preference aggregation method can satisfy all desirable alignment properties simultaneously.

anc.t: AI Alignment through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback? Contradictions and Limitations
anc.sig: Identifies fundamental contradictions in RLHF alignment approaches, exposing limitations in reward modeling and human feedback as alignment mechanisms.

anc.t: Combining Theory of Mind and Kindness for Self-Supervised Human-AI Alignment
anc.sig: Proposes self-supervised alignment framework using theory of mind and kindness principles to train AI systems that predict and respect human preferences without explicit reward annotations.

anc.t: Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap
anc.sig: Introduces neural self-other overlap mechanism to align AI agents' self-interest with others' welfare, enabling safer and more honest behavior through representational overlap of self and other value functions.

anchor.t: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
anchor.sig: Framed the canonical agenda of accident risks — safe exploration, robustness to distributional shift, avoiding negative side effects, scalable oversight — that most empirical safety work still organises around.

anc.t: Model evaluation for extreme risks
anc.sig: Established evaluation frameworks and metrics for assessing AI model behavior under extreme conditions and tail risks, enabling systematic safety evaluation beyond standard benchmarks.

anc.t: Frontier AI Regulation: Managing Emerging Risks to Public Safety
anc.sig: Establishes framework for regulatory approaches to frontier AI systems, identifying public safety risks and proposing governance mechanisms for advanced AI deployment before widespread adoption.

anc.t: International Institutions for Advanced AI
anc.sig: Proposes governance frameworks and institutional structures necessary for coordinating AI development and deployment across international boundaries, establishing foundational models for multilateral AI oversight.

anc.t: Managing extreme AI risks amid rapid progress
anc.sig: Frameworks for identifying, assessing and mitigating catastrophic risks from advanced AI systems as capabilities scale, establishing risk management principles for existential-scale threats.

anc.t: Towards Guaranteed Safe AI: A Framework for Ensuring Robust and Reliable AI Systems
anc.sig: Proposes systematic framework for AI safety assurance combining robustness verification, reliability testing, and formal guarantees to enable deployment of safety-critical systems.

anc.t: Human-AI Safety: A Descendant of Generative AI and Control Systems Safety
anc.sig: Frames human-AI safety as intersection of generative AI risks and control systems theory, establishing conceptual foundations for analyzing safety properties of AI systems through formal control frameworks.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Does injecting linguistic structure into language models lead to better alignment with brain recordings?
anc.sig: Demonstrates that incorporating explicit linguistic structure into neural language models improves alignment with human brain activity patterns during language comprehension.

anc.t: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
anc.sig: Comprehensive empirical study of language model scaling laws, training dynamics, and architectural choices that established benchmarks for training efficient large language models at billion-parameter scale.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated using LLMs as autonomous agents to generate diverse adversarial prompts at scale, establishing a practical workflow for systematic red teaming without manual human effort.

anc.t: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
anc.sig: Established red teaming as systematic method for identifying language model vulnerabilities and demonstrated scaling behaviors of safety issues with model size, enabling targeted harm reduction.

anc.t: Explore, Establish, Exploit: Red Teaming Language Models from Scratch
anc.sig: Establishes a systematic three-phase framework (explore, establish, exploit) for discovering adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs through iterative red teaming without requiring pre-existing attack knowledge.

anc.t: Visual Adversarial Examples Jailbreak Aligned Large Language Models
anc.sig: Demonstrated that aligned LLMs can be jailbroken using adversarial visual inputs, bypassing safety guardrails without direct text attacks.

anchor.t: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
anchor.sig: Launched the circuits program — reverse-engineering networks into human-understandable features and connections — the basis of mechanistic interpretability.

anc.t: Open Sesame! Universal Black Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
anc.sig: Developed transferable adversarial prompts that reliably jailbreak diverse LLMs without model access, establishing black-box jailbreaking as a universal vulnerability requiring defensive mechanisms.

anc.t: Defending Against Alignment-Breaking Attacks via Robustly Aligned LLM
anc.sig: Proposes defense mechanisms to make LLM alignment robust against adversarial attacks that attempt to override safety constraints through prompt injection and jailbreaking techniques.

anc.t: Catastrophic Jailbreak of Open-source LLMs via Exploiting Generation
anc.sig: Demonstrates critical vulnerability in open-source LLMs where exploiting the generation process itself enables catastrophic jailbreaks, establishing generation-based attack vectors as a distinct exploitation class beyond prompt injection.

anc.t: Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
anc.sig: Systematically catalogued adversarial attack methods exposing LLM vulnerabilities including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and adversarial examples, establishing taxonomy for robustness research.

anc.t: Exploring Vulnerabilities and Protections in Large Language Models: A Survey
anc.sig: Comprehensive taxonomy of LLM vulnerabilities (prompt injection, jailbreaking, poisoning, backdoors) and mitigation strategies, establishing systematic framework for adversarial robustness research.

anc.t: Prompt Infection: LLM-to-LLM Prompt Injection within Multi-Agent Systems
anc.sig: Demonstrated that LLMs in multi-agent systems are vulnerable to prompt injection attacks from other LLMs, establishing cross-agent adversarial manipulation as a distinct security threat requiring novel defenses.

anchor.t: Model Evaluation for Extreme Risks
anchor.sig: Argued dangerous-capability and alignment evaluations must gate frontier deployment — the framing behind today's evals, red-teaming and safety cases.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Formalized the problem of removing learned information from trained models without full retraining, establishing key algorithms and evaluation metrics for machine unlearning.

anc.t: Machine Unlearning of Features and Labels
anc.sig: Proposes machine unlearning methods to remove both feature information and label information from trained models, enabling selective forgetting of learned associations while retaining utility.

anc.t: Hard to Forget: Poisoning Attacks on Certified Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Demonstrates that certified machine unlearning methods are vulnerable to data poisoning attacks, establishing that formal unlearning guarantees must account for adversarial training data manipulation.

anc.t: On the Necessity of Auditable Algorithmic Definitions for Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Establishes framework requiring precise algorithmic definitions and auditable procedures for machine unlearning to enable verification of data removal claims.

anc.t: Towards Adversarial Evaluations for Inexact Machine Unlearning
anc.sig: Proposed adversarial evaluation framework to rigorously test machine unlearning methods, establishing concrete metrics and attack strategies for verifying successful data removal from trained models.

anc.t: Adversarial Training for High-Stakes Reliability
anc.sig: Demonstrates that adversarial training improves model robustness in high-stakes domains by exposing failure modes through generated adversarial examples, establishing reliability-focused threat modeling for critical applications.

anchor.t: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
anchor.sig: Demonstrated automated, transferable jailbreak suffixes that defeat alignment training — the reference point for adversarial-robustness and jailbreak research.
